title: A Reasoning-Based Robotic Bolt Fastening System with a Dexterous Hand
authors: Tao Tao, ChunYun Zhang, HuaiDong Zhou, FuChun Sun
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Robotic bolt fastening with dexterous hands requires effective integration of perception, reasoning and manipulation. This paper proposes a reasoning-based robotic bolt fastening system that employs a dexterous hand as the end-effector. The system integrates 6D pose estimation with CAD model topology information to accurately obtain the target bolt poses. In addition, a knowledge base for bolt fastening scenarios is constructed to provide structured prior knowledge. Based on this, the system leverages vision-language models (VLMs) to perform task reasoning and execution planning. During task execution, tactile feedback from the dexterous hand is utilized for closed-loop adjustments, improving grasp stability and manipulation reliability. Experimental results on a real robotic platform demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed system in practical bolt fastening tasks.

title: RACER: A Caching Layer based Optimization for Accelerating Reinforcement Learning Workloads
authors: Kailash Gogineni, Yongsheng Mei, Karthikeya Gogineni, Peng Wei, Tian Lan, Guru Venkataramani
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: Reinforcement Learning (RL) learns optimal decision-making policies from experiential (transition) datasets and maximizes the RL agent’s cumulative rewards. In order to improve the RL training efficiency, prior works have studied how to selectively sample certain critical transitions that ultimately lead to better policies and rewards. However, RL workloads still face significant challenges from a systems perspective, particularly when the agent iteratively accesses batches of data from transition datasets, whose growing sizes continue to challenge the memory hierarchy. This results in frequent and costly memory transfers between caches and Dynamic Random Access Memory (DRAM), which negatively impacts the overall training time.In this paper, we propose RACER, our novel caching layerbased optimization for RL training workloads. Firstly, recognizing that the RL agent repeatedly accesses large transition batches from growing datasets, we design a storage-cache that prioritizes critical transitions to fit within the hardware cache hierarchies. This design reduces the memory access times by sampling from a subset of critical transitions and minimizes the costly memory trips to DRAM. Second, we demonstrate how to smartly leverage key metrics (viz., temporal difference error and advantage weighting) to quantify the importance/relevance of transitions during policy optimization and to identify the transition data that would need to be spilled out of (or filled into) the caching system. We also introduce dynamic optimizations to our caching system that minimize the prospect of discarding the critical transitions. Our performance evaluation across three state-of-the-art RL algorithms, under various task environments, and on three different systems, demonstrates that RACER achieves significant optimization time improvements to the RL transition data sampling phase (a speedup of $6 \times$) and end-to-end training time (up to $2 \times$) with comparable rewards.

title: Optimizing Data-Driven Solutions for Power Quality Management in Africa: Addressing Unbalanced Datasets with Machine Learning
authors: Lavenda Shipichira, Opondo Broono
abstract: Energy security is a critical issue in Africa, where reliable power supply is essential for development. This paper discusses optimization techniques for processing power quality data using machine learning (ML) to enhance energy security. By addressing unbalanced datasets and improving the detection of power disturbances, we aim to ensure stable and reliable electricity in African power systems. In recent years, ML applications have garnered significant interest from power system researchers, with their success heavily dependent on extensive and diverse datasets for training and validation. However, power systems typically operate in quasi-steady-state conditions, resulting in measurements that offer limited novel information for ML development. This paper proposes a data mining approach utilizing optimization techniques to filter root-mean-square (RMS) voltage profiles and identify unusual measurements within triggerless power quality datasets. We create balanced datasets with equal representation of event and non-event observations, allowing ML algorithms to extract valuable insights from rare but critical events. The proposed framework is validated using both synthetic signals and field data measurements, demonstrating its effectiveness in enhancing power system stability and energy security in Africa.

title: Queue Monitoring and Occupancy Estimation Using Image Processing
authors: Manjula Gururaj Rao, Priyanka H, Moulya N Shetty, Neel Patel, Sarthak R Gouda, Shriya Alva
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Crowd queue management is a serious matter in high-flow areas such as schools, transportation centers, and public-service junctures. Traditional queue-management methods widely depend on human observation or paper tokens, which face huge challenges in crowds with drastic changes, causing lengthy waiting times and lowering user satisfaction. Herein, the authors propose a vision-based queue monitoring system with automatic estimation of waiting time.The proposed methodology combines real-time human detection with frame-level analysis; for object detection, YOLOv8 is used, while image processing is done using OpenCV. It is further optimized with frame skipping and filtering of the region of interest to reduce the computational load. The waiting time is estimated from the observed queue length and the average service rate. The system implemented at a college canteen gave an accuracy of around 90% under varying illumination and cluttered background conditions, hence showing ways compact AI- enabled vision systems could improve flow and user experience in everyday scenarios.

title: Noisy Pixels, Credible Decisions – A Robust, Edge-Ready Neuro-Symbolic Architecture for Rural (African) Clinics
authors: Christian Kutabuna Kubakisa, Kyandoghere Kyamakya, Witesyavwirwa Vianney Kambale, Isaac Lukusa Kayembe, Divin Kayeye Kabeya, Hima Deepthi Vankayalapati
abstract: Deploying robust and interpretable medical-image analysis in real-world settings remains challenging, especially in rural African clinics where imaging devices are low-cost, maintenance is irregular, and connectivity is intermittent. In such environments, images are frequently corrupted by noise, motion blur, low resolution, and other hardware- and workflow-induced artefacts that conventional deep-learning systems—typically tuned on clean, curated datasets—do not handle well and can rarely explain. This paper introduces a deployment-aware hybrid neuro-symbolic architecture that couples (i) CeNN-inspired convolutional modules for localised, noise-tolerant feature extraction, (ii) a symbolic reasoning layer that enforces anatomical and pathological consistency via rule-based logic, (iii) a Bayesian trust estimator that quantifies predictive uncertainty and triggers safe fallback pathways, and (iv) an anomaly-handling component that detects corrupted regions, models device-shift artefacts, and initiates self-adaptive compensation. The full stack is explicitly designed for edge execution under tight latency, memory, and energy constraints, while exposing human-readable rationales aimed at clinicians and community health workers. We detail the system-level design, edge-oriented optimisation choices, and an evaluation roadmap based on public medical imaging datasets, robustness and interpretability metrics, and ablation studies comparing individual components to the complete pipeline. This conceptual contribution is intended as a blueprint for future quantitative validation and sustainable deployment in mobile radiography, tele-ultrasound, community retinal screening, and low-cost dermatoscopic workflows in low-resource health systems.

title: Design of a Triple Band-Notched UWB MIMO Antenna with Mutual Coupling Reduction Using H-Shaped DGS
authors: Elijah Joseph, Pradeep Kumar, Thomas J.O Afullo
abstract: With the growing demand for compact antennas capable of operating over wide frequency ranges, ultra-wideband (UWB) systems continue to face significant interference from narrowband services such as WiMAX, WLAN, and X-band radar. In this work, a compact dual-port UWB multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) antenna is presented with integrated triple-band notches to suppress these interfering signals. An H-shaped defected ground structure is employed between the two radiating elements to mitigate mutual coupling and improve isolation. The proposed antenna consists of two orthogonally arranged circular Minkowski-shaped radiating elements on an FR-4 substrate, each loaded with rectangular slot resonators to achieve notched responses targeting WiMAX (3.2–3.5 GHz), WLAN (5.1–5.8 GHz), and X-band (8.4–9.96 GHz) interference. The H-shaped DGS, etched in the common ground plane, effectively suppresses surface currents, resulting in improved isolation while maintaining a wide impedance bandwidth. Simulated results demonstrate an operational range from 2.79 GHz to 11.96 GHz with isolation exceeding 18dB, peak gain of 5.8dB, and an envelope correlation coefficient (ECC) below 0.012 across the passband. The radiation patterns remain stable, with near-omnidirectional E-plane coverage and complementary H-plane response, while the notched bands effectively suppress undesired radiation. These results confirm that the proposed design is compact, interference-resilient, and suitable for portable UWB MIMO applications.

title: Explainable Deep Reinforcement Learning for Scalable and Trustworthy Autonomous Decision Systems
authors: Lalit Sachdeva, Utkarsh Anand
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Autonomous decision systems are increasingly being implemented in the critical fields of transportation, industrial robotics, and intelligent infrastructure, where reliability, safety, and transparency are of primary importance. One of the most powerful approaches to these systems has been Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL), which allows an agent to acquire the best behavior by experiencing the environment. Nevertheless, the conventional DRL models are frequently characterized by low interpretability and low safety assurances, which restrict their usage in situations involving safety concerns. To overcome these challenges, this article introduces Scalable Explainable and Trustworthy Deep Reinforcement Learning (SET-DRL), a novel framework that would bring together transparency, trustworthiness, and computational scalability. The main goals of the research of SET-DRL are to: (i) understand the interpretability of DRL models by giving us human-understandable reasons behind choices, (ii) guarantee safe decision-making methods by safety constraints, uncertainty-conscious decision-making, and out-of-distribution detection, and (iii) facilitate scalable implementation in edgecloud systems by distributed training and real-time inference. The evaluation has been the most important as it indicates that SET-DRL performs better than baseline DRL models, providing an opportunity to increase the success rates between 82% to 88%, and explainability is greatly increased. Perturbations of fidelity and stability of explanations are increased by more than 10%, and safety constraint violations decreased by 75%. Results of scalability testing indicate a nearly linear increase in throughput with an increase in the number of workers concurrently, and confirm the scalability to large-scale, real-time operation. These findings indicate that explainability and trust frameworks can be incorporated into DRL to offer clear, credible, and scalable autonomous systems solutions to real world scenarios.

title: Incorporating Meteorological Data and Pesticide Usage for Crop Yield Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: Chhatre Shital Vitthalrao, K. Shilpa, Pillutla Naveen Reddy, Rukkaya Sultana, Rapolu Sai Rani, Puram Arun
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The honest estimation of crop yield is an essential part of contemporary agricultural planning, as it allows the making of right decisions related to food security, resource management, and mitigation of risks. Traditional statistical methods have a tough time dealing with the intricate and nonlinear interactions existing among weather, agricultural inputs, and crop output. In order to overcome this constraint, the authors of this paper introduce a yield prediction system based on machine learning that not only adds meteorological and pesticide usage data to the forecast but also increases the accuracy thereof. The proposed solution handles both types of features, featuring crop and region as the categorical and average temperature, precipitation, and pesticide usage as the numerical ones. The categorical inputs are changed into the matrix form using One-Hot Encoding technique, while the numerical ones are standardized through feature scaling method. The Random Forest regression model is applied to the task of discovering non-linear relationships between the features and giving the output of quantity of yield in continuous form. The model is made available through the web-based application underpinned by Flask that allows for real-time prediction based on the user inputs. The experimental evaluation uses historical data about agriculture and climate, and is enriched by exploratory data analysis and correlation studies whose purpose is to check how the yield of a crop is affected by each of the factors. The findings prove that the model proposed is very effective in capturing the differences in yields among various crops and areas, thus providing reliable predictions. The fusing of the factors of environment and pesticide powerfully fortifies the model above that based on the input parameters alone. The system that researchers created functions as a precise agricultural decision-making tool which first enables current operations and then allows for future expansion through new data acquisition.

title: Deep Learning Model for Early Lung Nodule Classification
authors: Chitra K, Nagaveni KM, Harsha DV, Princy Leona P, Charitha R, SowmyaRani M E
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Lung cancer remains the leading cause of cancer related mortality worldwide, with about 1.8 million deaths each year, mainly due to late stage diagnosis. Low dose Computed Tomography screening supports early lung nodule detection, but manual review of 200 to 400 CT slices per patient in high volume clinical settings is time consuming and error prone. This study presents a deep learning framework based on transfer learned ResNet50 for automated five class lung cancer classification from axial CT images. The pipeline includes DICOM preprocessing, lung windowing, data augmentation, U Net based lung segmentation, and classification into normal, adenocarcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma, and large cell carcinoma classes. Experimental results on a prepared lung CT dataset show strong and stable classification performance. The model learns discriminative visual patterns from lung tissue and nodules, enabling reliable separation of healthy and cancer affected scans. Evaluation includes class wise performance analysis, confusion matrix assessment, and training convergence analysis to measure generalization across image categories.

title:
California PM
                    <sub>2.5</sub>
                    : A Ground- and Satellite-Based Data Analysis and Policy Investigation
authors: Lennon Chrisman, Ayan Dalmia, Clare Patchett, Uriah Sacdalan, Sneha Verma, Zaina Merchant
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Particulate matter, namely PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf>, is an air pollutant linked to widespread respiratory health concerns. This study analyzes PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf> trends in California using policy review alongside Python-based analysis and visualization of EPA ground-level monitor data and the USHighPM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf> comprehensive dataset. We identify a long-term decline in PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf> from 2000 to 2024, despite notable outliers caused by extreme wildfire events, supported by both data sources. Our results also show various temporal, geographical, and seasonal PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf> trends, integrating multiple datasets in the analysis. We show that PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf> reduction requires targeted policy approaches that focus on specific emission sources.

title: SPHINX: An Agentic AI System for Automated Scientific Discovery in Computational Biology
authors: Aditya Sengupta, Manolis Kellis
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: The rapid growth of biomedical data and analysis techniques has created a critical research bottleneck, leading to inefficient discovery workflows. We introduce SPHINX (Scientific Progressive Hypothesis Iteration and Organized Novel In-silico eXperiments), a multi-agent AI system designed to accelerate discovery by automating computational biology. SPHINX integrates specialized agents for literature synthesis, hypothesis generation, data analysis and visualization, and in-silico experimentation within a “Lab-in-the-Loop” framework. Through rigorous case studies in Alzheimer's Disease and immunology, SPHINX replicated findings and performed complex analyses by autonomously reasoning, writing code, and self-correcting errors, demonstrating its end-to-end capability to accelerate biomedical research.

title: A Hybrid QGWO–PSO Based LET-Bounded RSU-Assisted QoS Routing Scheme for VANETs
authors: Sowdagar Fowzia Sultana, Karamtot Krishna Naik
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Vehicular ad hoc networks, have a lot of mobility, which makes links fail a lot. This means that the routes need to be figured out again and again, which lowers the quality of service (QoS). This work suggests a per-flow routing framework that limits routing choices using a mobility window based on analytical Link Expiration Time (LET). This makes sure that paths are possible and stable. Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), and Grey Wolf Optimization (GWO) are reformulated to operate under LET-bounded routing constraints. A LET-adaptive Hybrid Quantum Grey Wolf–Particle Swarm Optimization (QGWO–PSO) algorithm is proposed to balance path stability and QoS performance. The routing objective jointly considers end-to-end delay, jitter, recomputation overhead, and packet delivery ratio, while selectively utilizing RSU-assisted links. Simulation results show that the hybrid approach achieves higher throughput and packet delivery than PSO, ACO, and GWO, with comparable delay and jitter. While GWO attains higher minimum LET, the proposed hybrid method provides a superior QoS–stability trade-off under dynamic VANET conditions.

title: Research on Object Detection Using YOLOv5 in Haze Conditions
authors: Li Wei, Gao Jiajun
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Under clear, unobstructed conditions, models like YOLOv5 can accurately detect objects in images. However, in extreme weather such as smog, rain, or snow, detected objects are prone to being obscured or blurred, leading to a significant drop in accuracy. To enhance object detection accuracy in smoggy conditions, this paper proposes an improved YOLOv5 model incorporating the Transformer module, CMA module, and S1 attention module. The integrated YOLOv5 model achieves a 3.03% improvement in average precision and a 4.45% increase in recall rate. This substantially enhances object detection accuracy in hazy conditions, providing valuable insights for future research in this field.

title: Probing English and Spanish Syntax in Language Models
authors: Caroline E. Witty, Chen Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Language models must handle syntactic complexity in multiple languages with different sentence structures to be useful in real-world domains such as medicine, law, and education. Prior studies show that models tend to encode syntactic roles, but this work focuses on English only data so it cannot be applied to languages with different syntax rules. We created a Spanish minimal-pair dataset based on previous benchmarks by translating English captions and reordering them from Subject-Verb-Object (SVO) to Object-Verb-Subject (OVS) using GPT-4o. We verified the dataset is comparable to previous benchmarks using a compositionality benchmark test. Then, we probed word embeddings from a range of text encoders using logistic regression to predict whether a word is a subject or an object. On embeddings from monolingual models the classifier achieved average F1 scores of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 4}$</tex> for English SVO, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 0}$</tex> for Spanish SVO and 0.80 for Spanish OVS data, but dropped to 0.47 for combined Spanish SVO and OVS data. Contrastingly, given embeddings from models trained on multilingual data, the classifier achieved average F1 scores of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 6}$</tex> for English SVO, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 6}$</tex> for Spanish SVO, 0.92 for Spanish OVS, and 0.87 for Spanish SVO and OVS combined. Our research aligns with prior work demonstrating that language models encode syntactic roles, while also expanding this work to multilingual contexts. Specifically, we add a metric to ensure syntactic roles are encoded rather than word order, supporting the applicability of language models in languages with freer word order.

title: Prompt Engineering for Competitive Programming: Improving o4-mini's Accuracy on Codeforces Tasks
authors: Sunmay Padiyar, Aaroosh Kurchania, Daivya Singh, Mihai Boicu
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Generative AI tools such as OpenAI's o4-mini are increasingly used to solve programming tasks, yet their effectiveness in competitive programming remains underexplored. This study evaluates four prompting techniques—No Prompt, Zero-Shot Chain-of-Thought, Prompt Chaining, and Structured Chain of Thought—on 90 randomly selected Codeforces problems rated 1600–2400. These problems were released after May 31st, 2024, the knowledge cutoff date for o4-mini. Code was submitted to the official Codeforces grader, and accuracy was measured based on the number of problems accepted. Structured Chain-of-Thought achieved the highest accuracy (56.67%, p = 0.0139), showing a statistically significant improvement over No Prompt (44.44%). Prompt Chaining followed closely (55.56%, p = 0.0249) and showed strong potential, though its improvement fell just short of statistical significance over No Prompt. Zero-Shot Chain-of-Thought (42.22%) did not improve from the baseline. These results demonstrate that carefully structured prompts can substantially improve LLM performance on advanced, reasoning-intensive programming tasks.

title: RL-Enabled LoS Blockage Avoidance for Visible Light Robot Localization
authors: Lyall Sathishkumar, Rathikadevi Mani, Saravana Ram Srinivasan, Xiao Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: Modern warehouse automation requires scalable, collision-free, adaptive navigation for multi-robot fleets in dense indoor environments. Conventional SLAM or fixed-path methods suffer from high computational cost, limited flexibility, and multi-robot interference under dynamic logistics conditions. This paper presents RLORN, a Reinforcement Learning-enabled Optical Robot Navigation framework combining LED-guided positioning with multi-robot reinforcement learning in a physics-based PyBullet simulation. The system uses ceiling-mounted LED anchors for self-localization guidance, while robots learn obstacle avoidance, path planning, and delivery tasks via policy learning. We model the task of mitigating line-of-sight blockage for continuous visible light-based self-localization as a Markov Decision Process, and extend it to a multi-agent formulation to enable coordinated decision-making under shared warehouse constraints. The simulation supports ray-cast perception, velocity control, collision dynamics, and realistic physical interactions. Extensive simulations evaluate task success rate, collision frequency, convergence, and scalability. Results show stable policy convergence, fewer collisions than baseline methods, and enhanced multi-robot coordination efficiency. The proposed framework offers a scalable, simulation-based approach for LED-assisted warehouse robots and supports real-world deployment with decentralized learning.

title: An Experimental Analysis of Data Augmentation and Pretraining for Mammogram Image Classification
authors: Sharmila C, Chidananda Murthy M V, Sunil S. Harakannanavar, Keshava Murthy T G
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Breast cancer continues to be one of the major health challenges for women worldwide and detecting it at an early stage greatly increases the chances of successful treatment. Mammography remains the standard imaging technique for identifying tumors at their earliest development. In this study, an attempt is made to classify mammogram images as benign or malignant by applying deep learning-based methods. The MIAS dataset is used for model training, while the larger CBIS-DDSM dataset serves as a source for pretraining to strengthen feature learning. Since MIAS contains a limited number of images, several augmentation and preprocessing techniques—such as rotations, horizontal and vertical flips, Gaussian blurring, CLAHE contrast adjustment, and median filtering—are incorporated to enrich the dataset and improve image quality. Two backbone architectures, ResNet18 and ResNet50, are examined for the classification task. The experiments indicate that ResNet18, when paired with MoCo-based pretraining and the enhanced augmentation pipeline, reaches a validation accuracy of about 65%. In contrast, the same model without these improvements performs noticeably lower at around 52%. The study highlights that effective preprocessing and representation-learning strategies can substantially enhance model performance, even when only a small amount of annotated medical data is available.

title: A Decentralized Proof Framework for Digital Authorship and Ownership
authors: Rohith John Jacob, Shane Jacob Sebastian, Varsha S Panicker, Vishnu Satish, Jerrin Sebastian
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Digital art marketplaces have been expanding rapidly, resulting in a greater focus on issues of ownership verification, copyright infringement, and content tampering. Current blockchain non-fungible token (NFT) systems only keep ownership data on the blockchain, with the media files stored off the blockchain, resulting in a lack of trust in the ownership of assets secured by NFTs. This work proposes a framework for verifying the ownership of digital art in a decentralized fashion, using invisible frequency domain watermarks, cryptographic hash functions, and artificial intelligence (AI)-based semantic checks. This results in a method for embedding ownership directly into the image being protected by an NFT, with the cryptographic fingerprint of the image stored on the blockchain for easy access. An AI-based semantic verification layer provides assurance that ownership can only be confirmed by performing consistent content checks. As has been verified through experimentation, the framework developed will allow owners of digital images to preserve ownership of their images and to securely and decentralized verify ownership.

title: Open-Source Client Modules Supporting Time Transfer with Satellite- and Decrypted Network-Based Precise Point Positioning [Roadmap for Measurement and Applications]
authors: Runzhi Zhang, Rui Tu, Jihai Zhang, Xiaochun Lu
abstract: Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) now provides global users with high-precision positioning, navigation, and timing services. Precise point positioning (PPP) technique has been developed and utilized for international atomic time link computations. However, PPP relies on high-precision satellite products to correct satellite-related errors in measurement observations output by a GNSS receiver. To support applications, precise satellite products were provided either via network transmission or through direct satellite broadcast in real-time. In recent years, network-based products, which are encrypted, internationally standardized, and transmitted in binary format, and satellite-based products, which are fragmented, customized, encoded, and transmitted in binary format, have made it more difficult for users to reliably and continuously recover them. At present, real-time open-source PPP software does not provide these two supports, which limits the application of real-time PPP, particularly for stable and continuous real-time PPP time transfer research and implementation. In this study, two supporting software packages have been developed and released as open source, designed to be embedded into mainstream open-source C/C++ PPP software. RTStreamHub is a support package for receiving and processing encrypted network-based products, designed to ensure reliable, real-time reception of securely transmitted satellite binary products. HASPPP is a dedicated software for Galileo satellite-based PPP, providing integrated support for recovering complex satellite-based products and implementing PPP, while also offering interfaces for seamless integration into mainstream C/C++ software for PPP time transfer applications. The real-time PPP time transfer precision of both network- and satellite-based products was analyzed using the PPP model embedded within the software HASPPP. Taking fiber-optic link results as a reference, the PPP time transfer precision is 125.9 ps based on network-based satellite products and 133.8 ps based on Galileo satellite-based products. The performance of these software packages has been validated, providing support modules for mainstream C/C++ PPP software.

title: An Intelligent Home Interior Design Suggestion System Using Deep Learning
authors: Sunitha M R, Shreeya D, Druthi Jain S C, Shimsha K P
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This electronic document is a "live" template and already defines the components of your paper [title, text, heads, etc.] in its style sheet. *CRITICAL: Do Not Use Symbols, Special Characters, Footnotes, or Math in Paper Title or Abstract. (Abstract) The increasing demand for personalized home interiors has exposed the limitations of traditional design recommendation approaches. This paper presents An Intelligent home interior design suggestion system that employs deep learning to generate context-aware and personalized design recommendations. The proposed system analyses room images and user preferences using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for style recognition and feature extraction, along with a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) for realistic design visualization. The system performs room segmentation, furniture identification, and spatial analysis to recommend suitable colour palettes, furniture arrangements, and layout optimizations. A modular web architecture enables seamless interaction and real-time visualization. The system enhances design accuracy, accessibility, and decision-making while reducing dependence on professional interior designers.

title: Mitigating Context Loss in Legal RAG: A Metadata-Aware Chunking Strategy for Hierarchical Indian Statutes
authors: Venkatesh S Bati, Abhigna C B, Samarth H Sindhya, Kalpa R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems hold much promise for legal question answering; however, they often fail on statutory corpora as naive document segmentation removes crucial context. Meaning in hierarchically structured texts like the Indian Income Tax Act is encoded across acts, chapters, sections, provisos, and explanations, whereas token- or window-based chunking conventionally used ignores this structure and detaches qualifiers from their governing sections, exacerbating the "Lost in the Middle" problem. This paper proposes Smart Indexing, a deterministic, metadata-aware chunking and context-injection strategy that preserves statutory structure by embedding hierarchical metadata, namely Act, Chapter, Section ID, and section title, directly into each fine-grained chunk. Each chunk is thereby rendered semantically self-contained, allowing vector embeddings to carry legal identity besides semantic content. A novel parent-child retrieval architecture first selects small metadata-rich clauses for precision and then expands them to their parent sections in order to reconstruct full statutory context for generation. The approach has been implemented and evaluated within LegalLens, a privacy-preserving legal RAG framework powered by a local, quantized Llama 3.1 model. Experimental results demonstrate that metadata-driven context injection improves retrieval precision and recall for proviso and condition-sensitive statutory queries while reducing hallucinations without increasing context window size or model complexity, placing hierarchy-aware metadata injection as a practical, scalable basis for robust legal RAG systems.

title: An EfficientNetV2B3-Based Deep Learning Model for Accurate Diagnosis and Classification of Liver Tumors
authors: Anbalagan S
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Diagnosis of liver tumor is critical in timely intervention and better patient outcomes. Here, a deep learning model named EfficientNetV2B3 was created to help conduct automated diagnosis and classification of liver tumors based on medical imaging data. The model was trained and fine-tuned on a labelled liver image dataset and its performance was examined based on important metrics, which included accuracy, loss, ROC curve, and confusion matrix analysis. The ROC-AUC was 0.53, which implied low discriminative capacity, and the training and validation accuracy was close to 50, which hinted at possible underfitting. The loss analysis indicated that there were slight overfitting tendencies and the confusion matrix indicated a bias in prediction towards a single class, which could be attributed to the imbalance in the data. Although the results were modest, the research demonstrates the potential of the EfficientNetV2B3 architecture on the medical image classification task and the significance of the data augmentation and hyperparameter optimization to improve the accuracy of the diagnosis in the further studies.

title: Design, Modeling and High-Performance MTPA Control of an Oil-Submersible PMSM
authors: Yunlong Peng, Tingting An, Liangfang Zhang, Yanliang Xu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This paper investigates the design, modeling, and high-performance maximum torque per ampere (MTPA) control of a unit Oil-submersible permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) for electric submersible pump systems. First, the stator and rotor topologies, pole-slot combination, stator slot geometry, and permanent magnet materials of the oil-submersible motor are studied, based on which the design scheme of the unit motor is developed and the corresponding motor parameters are presented. Then, considering the operating characteristics of the oilsubmersible motor, an improved nonlinear mathematical model is established in which the inductance and flux-linkage parameters are influenced by core saturation and cross-coupling effects. Finally, a simulation study on the high-performance MTPA control method is carried out by implementing the improved motor model in simulation software, thereby verifying the effectiveness of both the motor modeling approach and the highperformance MTPA control method.

title: Detection of the Upper Surface Pose of Yarn Cones for Robot Gripping
authors: Jian He, Yujie Chen
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To address the challenge of accurately measuring the center position of the spindle during robotic picking operations, this paper proposes a 3D vision-based algorithm for detecting the upper surface of yarn cones. This algorithm can accurately estimate the center position and orientation of the spindle, thereby guiding the robotic arm to insert it and complete the picking task.First, based on the image data captured by the 3D camera, the circular contour of the top surface of the yarn cone is extracted and optimized. Next, the least squares method is used to fit this circular contour, yielding a mathematical expression for the top surface's circular contour. Building on this, a pose estimation model is constructed to calculate the normal vector of the circle's center and the spatial center coordinates of the top surface using local point cloud data. The experimental results demonstrate that the detection model proposed in this paper exhibits good robustness, and its detection accuracy meets operational requirements: the average detection error for the yarn cone's axis vector is less than 1.3°, and the average detection error for the distance from the center of the upper end face to the axis is less than 1.24 mm, ensuring that the robot can reliably perform yarn cone grasping operations. Importantly, this model can detect yarn cones in any orientation with greater efficiency than point cloud processing; it not only offers a new perspective on yarn cone center detection but also provides valuable insights for related object detection.

title: Section-Weighted Hybrid Approach for Legal Case Retrieval
authors: Rajith Arulanandam, Nisansa De Silva
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Finding truly analogous precedents requires capturing legal reasoning beyond surface word overlap. We present a two-stage, section-aware framework for legal case retrieval that first segments raw judgments into facts, issues, decision, and reasoning using a deterministic large language model (LLM) offline. In Stage 1, we combine parallel lexical (BM25) and semantic (dense ANN) whole-document searches via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) to form a high-recall candidate pool. In Stage 2, we perform fine-grained, like-for-like comparisons (e.g., query reasoning vs. candidate reasoning). To address the scale mismatch between unbounded lexical scores and cosine similarities, we apply query-wise Z-score normalization before aggregating signals with learned section weights. For the top results, the system returns the relevant section text with a concise, grounded rationale and party-stance labels. We evaluate on a jurisdiction-scale benchmark, demonstrating consistent gains over strong lexical and neural baselines while maintaining high candidate coverage.

title: Best First Search based Control of Residential Appliance Load Demand
authors: Anoohya Runkana, Kandala Prapthi, Nagaraja Chitimi M., Rashmi M. R., Hoong Pin Lee, Rachana M. Hullamani
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The increasing use of rooftop photovoltaic (PV) cells in residential buildings requires effective demand management to utilise the available power effectively. This paper presents a household appliance load demand control method based on the Best First Search (BFS) algorithm, implemented in MATLAB. The proposed approach schedules household appliances by considering the available <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P V}$</tex> power, demand by the appliances, and user-defined priority levels. The demand control problem is solved by using BFS algorithm, which explores possible appliance combinations and selects the best combination that operates within the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P V}$</tex> power limit while considering a greater number of appliances and its priorities. To represent realistic household time of the day-based priority variations are also included. Simulation results show that the proposed BFS based method effectively utilises available PV power with maximum number of appliances being supplied at all the times.

title: IoT enabled Smart Classroom Management System using Raspberry Pi
authors: Sneha Murudi, Soujanya U, Manoj Kumar M, Sushma M, Shraddha C
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Traditional classroom management practices such as manual attendance recording and manual control of classroom resources are time-consuming and inefficient. This paper presents a software-based Smart Classroom Management System designed to automate attendance tracking and improve classroom efficiency. The proposed system uses a standard webcam to capture live video, from which student faces are detected using computer vision techniques implemented with the OpenCV library and Haar Cascade classifier. Attendance records are automatically generated and stored using a web-based application developed with Flask. The system was evaluated under controlled classroom conditions involving 30 students and achieved an attendance accuracy of approximately 98%. Although the system is lightweight and cost-effective, its performance may be affected by lighting variations and facial occlusions such as masks. The results demonstrate that the proposed software solution can effectively reduce administrative workload and support an enhanced learning environment.

title: Study on Classroom Temperature Distribution in Hybrid Cooling System at Different Air Conditioner Setpoints
authors: Siti Zubaidah Bahrin, Nor Asiah Muhamad, Law Kah Haw
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Air-conditioning (AC) is essential to maintain comfortable indoor environments, particularly in classrooms, resulting in higher energy consumption in educational buildings compared to neighboring countries. This study investigates the effectiveness of fan-assisted cooling as a hybrid strategy to reduce AC energy use. Experiments were conducted in two classrooms: Room 1 with a single fan and Room 2 with double fans, across AC setpoints from 26 deg C to 22 deg C. Hourly temperature and energy consumption data were collected and analyzed. Results show that fan-assisted cooling can significantly lower peak indoor temperatures and total energy use, with the magnitude of temperature reduction dependent on sensor location and airflow distribution. Notably, the double-fan configuration achieved the largest reductions, highlighting the importance of fan placement and room-specific airflow patterns. Daily energy analysis revealed that Room 1 achieved savings with AC and 1 fan at higher setpoints (26–24 deg C), while at lower setpoints (23–22 deg C), hybrid cooling sometimes consumed more energy than AC alone. In Room 2, the double-fan configuration resulted in consistently higher energy use across all setpoints, highlighting the influence of room-specific conditions and fan placement. These findings demonstrate that hybrid cooling strategies can enhance indoor comfort while reducing energy demand in educational buildings.

title: End-to-End Warehouse Automation: A System-Level Review of Robotics, Order Fulfillment, and Intelligent Software System
authors: Sai Preetham R V, Vishwanath Koti, Impana R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The rapid growth of e-commerce and on-demand fulfillment has intensified the need for efficient, scalable, and intelligent warehouse operations. In response, warehouse automation has evolved from isolated mechanized solutions to complex cyber-physical systems integrating robotics, software intelligence, and real-time control. While significant research has been conducted on individual components such as automated storage and retrieval systems, autonomous mobile robots, order picking technologies, and optimization algorithms, existing studies largely remain fragmented and lack an end-to-end perspective. This review presents a comprehensive analysis of end-to-end warehouse automation systems, covering physical automation technologies, order fulfillment mechanisms, software architectures, and intelligent planning and control frameworks. Thirty peer-reviewed research works are systematically analyzed and classified based on their role within the warehouse automation pipeline, spanning task allocation, scheduling, path planning, system integration, and performance evaluation. The review highlights key trends, comparative insights, and limitations in current approaches, with particular emphasis on software coordination and system-level integration. Finally, open research challenges and future directions are identified, including unified planning frameworks, scalable decentralized control, and the need for standardized evaluation benchmarks, providing guidance for future research and industrial deployment

title: SEE Evaluation of a Serverless Computing Architecture Under 14-MeV Neutrons
authors: David Pacios, Mohammadreza Rezaei, José Luis Vázquez-Poletti, Sara Ignacio-Cerrato, Juan A. Clemente
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: This paper presents the effects of 14-MeV neutrons on a NVIDIA Jetson Nano running a serverless computing architecture. SDCs and DUEs were observed in its CPU and GPU when executing a serverless-based FFT function.

title: Self-Supervised Representation Learning for Data-Efficient Crop Yield Estimation from Multi-Temporal Remote Sensing Data
authors: M. Jeyanthi, SK.Piramu Preethika
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Crop yield estimation is an important part of agricultural planning, food security and policy making. Traditional methods of predicting yields typically draw on a lot of ground-truth data which are very expensive and time-consuming to collect especially over extensive areas. This study handles this issue of lack of data at higher resolution for estimation of crop yield by contributing to develop a framework called Self-Supervised Representation Learning (SSRL) which makes use of Multi-Temporal remote sensing imagery to learn rich spatiotemporal embeddings without need for huge datasets for their trainings. The framework combines 3D Convolutional Neural Networks and Temporal Transformers for extracting features from the data, self-supervised pre-text problems such as Temporal Order Prediction, Masked Patch Reconstruction, Contrastive Learning for self-supervised pre-training and few-shots learning together with MAML algorithm for fine-tuning with limited data. Further, the embeddings are combined with ancillary agronomic data such as soil, weather and irrigation variables using a multimodal attention mechanism. Experiments on Sentinel-2 imagery for maize fields in India show Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.82 \mathrm{t} / \text{ha}$</tex>, Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.31 \mathrm{t} / \text{ha}$</tex>, R2 of 93.4 % and Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 4.9 % which are better than five state-of-the-art models namely Random Forest, LSTM, GRU, 3D-CNN, and Transformer based models. The results show that a self supervised learning approach coupled with multimodal fusion and few-shot adaptation is highly effective to perform accurate, scalable and data-efficient crop yield prediction. This methodology gives practical insights to the uses of precision agriculture, regional planning and sustainable resource management.

title: Leaf-Based Health Assessment using CNN for NPK Deficiency Detection and IoT for Soil Analysis
authors: Chester Jefferson I. Andaya, John Michael R. Reyes, Kyla Jane D. Jamito, Patrick R. Eva, Psalm Ashley B. Andal, Alice M. Lacorte, John Carlo G. Torres, Marvin L. Atanacio
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Mechatronics, Control and Robotics (ICMCR)
abstract: This study presents a hybrid system for precision nutrient management in bitter gourd (Momordica charantia), integrating convolutional neural network (CNN)–based leafimage analysis with Internet of Things (IoT)–enabled soil sensing for nitrogen (N), phosphorus (P), and potassium (K) deficiency detection. A dataset of 1,200 expert-validated leaf images was used to train and evaluate InceptionV3 and ResNet50 models, with ResNet50 achieving superior performance (F1-score up to 1.00) under a 70-20-10 training-validation-testing split. Parallel soil analysis employed NPK, pH, and moisture sensors calibrated against laboratory assays. Results demonstrate that CNN classification accurately identifies visual nutrient deficiencies, while IoT data provides real-time soil context. Integrating both modalities enhances diagnostic reliability, reduces misclassification, and enables data-driven fertilization recommendations. The combined approach supports precise, costeffective, and sustainable nutrient management, mitigating risks of under- or over-application. This study underscores the potential of AI–IoT hybrid systems in advancing site-specific agriculture and improving crop yield and environmental stewardship.

title: Fail-Operational Architecture for ADAS-Integrated Lighting ECUs: Achieving ASIL-B with Triple Modular Redundancy
authors: Abdul Salam Abdul Karim
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Intelligent lighting Electronic Control Units (ECUs) have been increasingly integrated in Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) to improve visibility in the night and provide reliable perception and general road safety. Because the lighting functions become safety-relevant, especially when dynamic driving and fault-prone mode is applied, maintaining fail-operational behavior with standardized compliance with functional safety is of critical importance. This is the architecture of a fail-operational ADAS-integrated lighting ECU architecture, which sets the goal of Automotive Safety Integrity Level-B (ASIL-B) on Triple Modular Redundancy (TMR) usage. The suggested architecture uses three parallel lighting control channels, which use a majority-voting mechanism to identify and mask single point faults in real-time. A detailed time-correlated data set at a fixed sampling rate of 20 ms of raw sensor data, redundancy channel data, voter data, and diagnostic data is produced in control and injected fault conditions both with computation faults, sensor dropouts and temporary bit-flip errors. The overall accuracy of supervised fault classification over the generated dataset is 97.4 percent and the class-wise Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC) is of 0.97-0.98, showing the presence of strong discriminative capabilities in operational and fault condition. The diagnostic coverage of all the analyzed fault types is more than 90, which meets the requirements of the ASIL-B and proves the strength of the offered fail-operational design. The findings confirm the usefulness of TMR and redundancy-based data monitoring and safety monitoring to next-generation ADAS lighting ECUs that have been designed under safety-critical conditions.

title: EnViD: A Predictive Vision Framework with Temporal Contrast for Efficient and Robust Video Analysis in Dynamic Visual Fields
authors: Sritej Padmanabhan
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Video-analysis applications and autonomous unmanned vehicles (drones, etc.) require fast and resource-efficient perception and control to perform in dynamic, unpredictable real-world environments. Comprehending visual reality requires the efficient acquisition of common-sense knowledge over several regularities in the visual world, e.g., object spatial relationships, temporal dynamics such as motion & rotation, and illuminations on objects in view. In the computer vision domain, a paramount challenge has resided in the performance of vision systems within dynamic environments such as glare and shadows, particularly in Single Object Tracking (SOT). This research specifically takes interest in improving telehealth applications such as disease diagnosis and monitoring. Prevailing methods, while sophisticated, are hindered by large datasets and high computational needs, with their performance being compromised under varying ambient conditions, which inevitably appear in real-world conditions. Considering these challenges, a novel event-based predictive vision framework (EnViD) was developed to better capture such real-world dynamics more efficiently. EnViD builds sophisticated and meaningful high-to-high associations, in comparison to high-to-low mappings. This generates semantically rich features, which can be associated with more abstract, low-dimensional concepts through a predictive vision framework assembled in a hierarchy. EnViD was tested on an NVIDIA Jetson Nano with a custom dataset simulating real-world visual conditions like glare, occlusion, and motion blur. EnViD demonstrated consistent improvements in tracking precision and robustness compared to classical frame-based methods. However, the predictive vision framework alone still suffers from high energy use, which is unrealistic for embedded applications. Therefore, a temporal contrast signal processing method similar to the eye's retina was utilized to reduce the computational burden of typical frame-based vision. By processing only per-pixel polarity changes, an attention-like mechanism can bring focus to the critical components of the frame. This resulted in a reduction of data usage by ∼92%. However, utilizing event-based vision in combination with a predictive hierarchy result in swift signal loss. Therefore, a novel microsaccade signal generation technique was developed to reintroduce signal from signal feedback, similar to the human eye. This technique provides a possible explanation for why microsaccades are utilized in the human visual system, validated in silico. EnViD's increased efficiency and performance in dynamic conditions make it suitable for many vision tasks. To assess its performance for telehealth video-analysis tasks, it was applied to an algorithm for monitoring a Parkinsonian hand tremor. The EnViD module was adapted as a predictive tracking backbone that localized and stabilized the hand region in each frame under dynamic visual conditions, created in a custom dataset. This region was then passed into a MediaPipe-based hand landmark detector to extract fine-grained tremor motion signals. By narrowing inference to EnViD-predicted regions, the system reduced landmark dropout and tracking jitter by approximately 5%, yielding more reliable tremor quantification in the presence of the mentioned ambient conditions. This research introduces EnViD as a promising and novel neuromorphic architecture for improved performance of vision systems (i.e. telehealth camera input, autonomous vehicles, etc.) in dynamic conditions. Future applications include expanding this tool to multi-object tracking and additional contrasts beyond temporal, for increased efficiency.

title: Personal Healthcare Assistant Using AI
authors: Shruthi G, Anand Tilagul, Deepak B L, Chandana M
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) has enabled the development of intelligent healthcare systems that assist individuals in managing their health more effectively. This paper presents the design and implementation of a Personal Healthcare Assistant using AI, an interactive system capable of analyzing user symptoms, providing preliminary health advice, and recommending lifestyle improvements. The assistant employs NLP techniques to understand user queries, a symptom-checking algorithm to predict possible conditions, and a machine learning model for personalized health recommendations. By integrating with wearable devices and cloud-based medical databases, the system ensures continuous health monitoring and timely alerts. The proposed model aims to bridge the gap between patients and healthcare providers, offering accessible, affordable, and real-time health support. Experimental results demonstrate that the assistant can provide accurate and context-aware responses, improving user engagement and promoting proactive healthcare management.

title: FPGA Realization of an Operand-Aware Dual-Mode Hybrid Arithmetic Unit for 64-Bit Embedded Systems
authors: K Uma Maheswari, Kamatham Susmitha, Chinthakayala Manjeera, B Eramma, Peddinti Deven Kumar, Palla Chandana
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This research analyzes the realization of an operand-aware dual-mode hybrid arithmetic component on the FPGA technology with applications in 64-bit embedded processors. The arithmetic component is designed to support its operation, depending on the values of the operands and the corresponding opcode options, to accomplish arithmetic, logical, and multiplication operations. This is achieved with the support of the operand analysis module and the control logic system, enabling the selective operation of the calculation paths and thereby preventing any wastage because of switching processes and enhancing overall resource usage. The system also integrates the selective tile hybrid multiplier logic that can support high-precision 64-bit multipliers with an output that goes up to 128 bits, while maintaining a normalized footprint and power consumption level. The arithmetic component is modeled with the use of the Verilog HDL and implemented with the Xilinx Vivado 2023.2 platform and validated with simulation and reported to utilize minimal resources and control the power within the integrated chip.

title: Mechatronics-AI Collaboration: A Flexible Automated Testing Framework for Smart Home Devices Based on Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Bizhi Yu, Chaoxia Zhang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Modern smart home HMIs (Human-Machine Interfaces) are highly complex. Traditional testing methods depend on predefined coordinates and rigid fixtures. Consequently, these approaches suffer from poor flexibility and long setup cycles. This paper presents an AI-integrated mechatronic framework for flexible testing. At the perception layer, a multimodal large language model (MLLM) is utilized. A coarse-to-fine visual localization algorithm is also proposed. This setup achieves zero-shot, precise UI localization using unstructured product manuals. At the execution layer, a visual-to-physical mapping model is constructed. Additionally, a Hooke's law-based biomimetic touch mechanism is developed to prevent screen damage from rigid robotic hits. Tests indicate up to 90.0% localization accuracy on thermostat displays. The proposed system realizes a complete closed loop: cognition, perception, flexible execution, and visual feedback. This framework significantly enhances testing adaptability and provides a new method for product quality control.

title: An Explainable ML Framework for Predicting Booking Cancellations in the Hospitality Sector
authors: Nidamanuri Srinu, Yakkanti Jyothi, D. Bujji Babu
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Hotel cancellations is one of the primary pain points of the hospitality industry because it distorts demand indications and leads to revenue losses approximated at approximately 20 percent. However, prediction of cancellations in booking has been an understudied field especially with regards to the behavioural forces behind cancellations. The gap in this paper will be filled by proposing a novel method of predicting hotel booking cancellations based on stacked generalization and Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI). In particular, the integration of linear, tree-based, non-linear and deep learning schemes into one meta-model led to the improvement of the accuracy rate up to 96 %. Furthermore, this article concentrates on interpretability, where the driver aspects of behavioural factors that lead to cancellation are found to be location, type of room and consumer segments. Such a strategy may offer hoteliers not only with very precise forecasts but also with marketing insights that will enable them push strategy to reduce loss caused by cancelling. The findings of the study can be effectively used as a solution to the problems associated with predicting booking cancellations and at the same time achieving a balance between forecast prediction and the capacity to make practical implications. The primary objectives of this research are to develop an accurate booking cancellation prediction model and to provide interpretable explanations for managerial decision-making. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed stacked ensemble model achieves an accuracy of up to 96%, outperforming individual machine learning classifiers. Explainability analysis using SHAP reveals that lead time, room type, customer segment, and booking channel are the most influential factors driving cancellation behavior.

title: Light Weight MAMBA Based Deepfake Detection Model
authors: Sruthi Biju, R S Prasanth
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The rapid growth of deepfake generation techniques has raised concerns regarding digital misinformation, fraud and privacy breaches. The majority of deepfake video detection models currently in use rely on resource-intensive CNN-Transformer architectures, which restricts their practical implementation in real-time or edge environments. In this paper, a lightweight spatiotemporal detection framework based on Mamba-driven temporal learning and MobileNet-based feature extraction is proposed. By compressing high-level feature representations, a Vector-Quantized Autoencoder (VQ-AE) improves the ability to distinguish between authentic and fraudulent video patterns. Spatial artifacts within individual frames and motion inconsistencies between sequences are captured by two parallel VisionMamba branches. For the final prediction, a lightweight classifier processes the fused spatiotemporal representation. The suggested method is ideal for low-power deepfake detection applications since experimental evaluations show increased inference efficiency with competitive accuracy.

title: Vehicle Speed Estimation and Classification Using a Low-Cost Continuous-Wave Doppler Radar
authors: Dylan Kuming, Mohammed Yunus Abdul Gaffar, Lawrence Stanton
abstract: This paper presents a low-cost continuous-wave (CW) Doppler radar system for vehicle speed estimation and classification. A 24GHz radar module was integrated with an AD620 amplifier and an ASUS Xonar sound card, with data processed offline in MATLAB. The processing pipeline comprised Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) spectrogram generation, Order Statistic Constant False Alarm Rate (OS-CFAR) detection, Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DBSCAN), and Joint Probabilistic Data Association (JPDA)-based multi-target tracking (MTT) using a constant-acceleration (CA) Kalman filter. Classification used a ramp-duration feature extracted near the zero-Doppler point and normalised by the estimated vehicle speed. Experimental validation showed mean speed errors below 1km/h against GPS ground truth and 99% classification accuracy in preliminary controlled and live traffic data, validating the system’s promising performance and affordability for traffic monitoring.

title: Data-Driven Predictive Maintenance Using Fuzzy Clustering Approaches for Oil Temperature Forecasting in 8E Electrical Locomotives
authors: Rofhiwa C. Mufamadi, Stephen O. Oladipo, Udochukwu B. Akuru
abstract: The South African rail sector is vital to the national economy but often faces reliability challenges due to recurring malfunctions in electric locomotives such as the Class 8E. This study presents a predictive maintenance framework for forecasting oil temperature using an adaptive neuro fuzzy inference system (ANFIS) supported by fuzzy clustering methods. Real time operational data, including line current, voltage, ambient temperature, and oil temperature, were collected from 8E locomotives at Impala Platinum Mine between August and October 2024. Models were built using subtractive clustering (SC), grid partitioning (GP), and fuzzy c-means (FCM) techniques. The model using SC with a radius of 0.3 achieved the best performance with RMSE of 0.1637, MAPE of 0.3545, MAE of 0.1168, and NSE of 0.9937. The proposed model offers a reliable and cost-effective solution for early fault detection, enabling timely maintenance and improved operational uptime in the electric rail sector.

title: Unified AI-Driven Multimodel Framework for Real-Time Lecture Understanding and Study Support
authors: P J Kiruthiga, Kolaparthi Sri Venkata Harshith, Koya Sahith, Mandhava Lakshmi Kavitha, K Lakshmi Deekshitha
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The significant increase in the use of digital learning resources like online lectures, recorded classes, and digital study materials has led to students facing a very large amount of information, thus making it difficult to get the main insights quickly. The authors of this paper introduce an AI-Powered Multimodal Lecture and Study Companion that aims to make the learning process easier by integrating automatic text extraction, smart summarization, and uninterrupted content delivery all in one platform. The system is built on a web interface that is based on Streamlit, and it takes inputs from two main sources: (i) YouTube video links, where the platform first captures and then transcribes the audio into accurate text, and (ii) PDF documents, where text that is part of the document is extracted directly. The application uses the Google AI Studio API for sophisticated natural language processing, which results in the generation of brief and contextually relevant summaries of content that has been extracted while retaining the important and specific concepts and terms that are needed for proper comprehension in academia. The users are given access to the produced text and can also get the summarized document in PDF form for studying offline. The system like structure gives priority to processing in real-time, very little interaction with the user, and that it is ready to take on the future multimodal developments like question generation and topic clustering, in a large scale way. This platform, which provides a digital companion that is effective, produces better understanding, encourages active learning, and supports the usage of modern educational methodologies, by shortening the time and mental effort to be spent on reviewing long lectures and study materials.

title: A Variegated Look at Direct-to-Cell Satellites in the Wild
authors: Wei Liu, Jingyi Lan, Yuanjie Li, Bowen Sun, Yekai Dong, Yufeng Wang, Hewu Li, Qian Wu, Zeqi Lai, Jun Liu
