title: Dynamic Beam Position Partitioning and Layout Adjustment in LEO Beam Hopping Systems
authors: Lu Wang, Jifeng Liu, Yao Zhou, Tingting Wang, Cheng Wang, Weidong Wang
abstract: In Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite communication systems, beam-hopping (BH) technology significantly enhances spectrum resource utilization by dynamically activating different beam positions (BPs) across distinct time slots. However, in addition to the fact that BH decisions can affect system performance, the number of BPs can also impact the system latency and their layout influences system throughput performance. Therefore, this paper proposes a dynamic BP partitioning and layout adjustment method for LEO satellite communication systems. First, user adjacency is analysed via a KD-tree to determine initial BP centers, and a minimum enclosing circle algorithm is applied to maximize user coverage under radius constraints. Then, a virtual force field algorithm is proposed to adjust the BP layout by modelling the interactions between overlapping beams. Simulation results demonstrate that, compared to existing methods, this approach reduces algorithmic complexity while enhancing system latency and throughput performance.

title: Resolving the Buildup Dynamics of Noise-like Pulse in Mamyshev Oscillator
authors: Xiangzhen Huang, Ning Li, Tiantian Zhou, Xinrui Chu, Jia Cheng, Lili Miao, Chujun Zhao
abstract: The Mamyshev oscillator (MO) operating in the noise like pulse (NLP) regime can produce laser pulses with broad bandwidth and high pulse energy, which is crucial for many applications in optical communication, supercontinuum generation and micromachining. However, experimentally verifying the buildup dynamics of NLP in MO remains a significant challenge. Here, the all-fiberized ytterbium-doped MO has been designed to deliver NLP with 3-dB bandwidth exceeding 140 nm experimentally, and the buildup dynamics of NLP in the MO has been characterized by the time-stretch dispersive Fourier transform (TS-DFT) technique. Unlike the NLPs from conventional mode-locked fiber laser, the generation of NLP in MO is from the survived seed pulse in MO, and the transition from external seed pulses to stable NLP operation in MO involves four ultrafast phases, including seed pulse injection, amplified seed pulse, NLP generation, and stable NLP state. The numerical simulation results further validate the NLP buildup mechanisms in MO. The results can deepen the understanding of the NLP operation in MO, and may support the generation and manipulation of the pulse dynamics in MO.

title: Personalized Lattice Influence Maximization: Approximation Guarantees and Robustness
authors: Jun Wang, Qixin Zhang, Hongbin Zhang, Yu Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Recently, personalized influence maximization, which generalizes classic influence maximization by wisely allocating personalized discounts to users, showed its superiority in achieving cost-effective marketing plans over traditional influence maximization techniques. However, under continuous cost reaction scenarios, it is hard to obtain the approximation guarantee. To this end, we first propose a personalized lattice influence maximization (PLIM) problem, which can be approximately guaranteed through a greedy algorithm from an innovative perspective, i.e., match item selection with a knapsack constraint (MIS-K). Furthermore, to address the practical challenges associated with accurately learning user purchase probabilities, we extend the proposed PLIM framework in the form of robust optimization. We develop a robust algorithm that achieves a solution-dependent approximation guarantee and analyze the computational hardness of approximating RPLIM. By introducing an innovative Re-arrangement Sampling technique, we demonstrate that sampling can effectively mitigate uncertainty in purchase probabilities, enabling a strong robust ratio of (1 −ε)(1 − 1/e) with high probability. Experimental results on multiple benchmark datasets confirm the effectiveness and efficiency of our proposed methods.

title: Temperature-Insensitive Pressure Sensor Based on Bending-Induced Strain Gradient in Fiber Bragg Gratings
authors: Wei-Chen Li, Yu-Chung Chang, Wen-Fung Liu
abstract: high-sensitivity, temperature insensitive fiber Bragg grating (FBG) pressure sensor based on bending-induced nonuniform strain is demonstrated. External force applied to the loading plate is converted into controlled bending deformation of an elastic steel plate, generating an axial strain gradient along the embedded FBG that produces significant broadening of the Bragg reflection spectrum. Unlike conventional sensors that rely on Bragg wavelength shifts, this unique configuration employs spectral bandwidth as the primary sensing metric. This bandwidth-based approach provides a sensing mechanism that is expected to reduce temperature sensitivity. Through systematic optimization of the steel plate thickness, adhesive con-figuration, and support spring stiffness, the maximum sensitivity configuration achieves a bandwidth sensi-tivity of 0.181nm/N, which yields an equivalent pressure sensitivity of 9.05 nm/MPa. To prioritize mechani-cal durability and repeatability, the final design configuration provides a bandwidth sensitivity of 0.091nm/N, equivalent to 4.55 nm/MPa, with enhanced structural integrity. Experimental results confirm that the pro-posed sensor offers high sensitivity, superior repeatability, and robust structural stability, making it a prom-ising candidate for compact, high-dynamic-range force-based pressure sensing applications.

title: Enhancing Drug-Induced Liver Injury Prediction via Multi-Representation Molecular Images with Grad-CAM Explainability and Functional Group Attribution
authors: Hoang Phi Yen Duong, Nghia Trong Vo, Tuan Thanh Nguyen, Trung Q. Duong
abstract: Drug-induced liver injury (DILI) constitutes a critical challenge in pharmaceutical development and accounts for over 50% of acute liver failure cases. This study employs deep learning (DL) methodologies for DILI prediction utilizing convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and multi-representation molecular imaging. We employ ResNet (18, 34, 50) and EfficientNet (B0-B3) architectures on three molecular image representations, such as normal images (NM), heatmap 1 (HM1) derived from Crippen logP contributions, and heatmap 2 (HM2) extracted from bioconcentration factor analysis. Experimental outcomes on 475 compounds using 4-fold cross-validation demonstrate that heatmap representations substantially outperform conventional molecular images. ResNet-34 attains optimal performance with HM2, achieving an AUC of 0.8853 and a Recall of 0.8410. This significantly enhances performance compared to conventional images (AUC: 0.8604, Recall: 0.8295). ResNet-34 and EfficientNet-B0 with HM1 exhibits superior functional group identification capabilities with a Recall of 0.8499 and 0.8682. Grad-CAM visualization illustrates that HM1 effectively emphasizes specific functional groups while HM2 facilitates comprehensive molecular analysis. Functional group analysis indicates that nitro groups (93.75%), sulfones (92.31%), and urea derivatives (91.67%) are associated with the highest DILI risk, while sulfur-containing moieties broadly serve as toxicity indicators. In contrast, iodine-containing compounds and ketones exhibit notably lower toxicity rates. Our multi-representation methodology exhibits competitive performance while delivering enhanced interpretability through explainable AI techniques. This framework presents considerable potential for pharmaceutical toxicity assessment and diminishes dependence on animal testing protocols.

title: PreSFC: Predictive SFC Migration via Multi-slot Mobility Forecasting in MEC Networks
authors: Ji Li, Songtao Guo, Quanjun Zhao, Guiyan Liu, Liang Liu
abstract: Network Function Virtualization (NFV) is a foundational technology for Mobile Edge Computing (MEC). It delivers network services by chaining Virtual Network Functions (VNFs) into sequential Service Function Chains (SFCs). One of the most critical challenges in MEC is how to provide continuous and stable services to high-mobility user, such as intelligent vehicles and drones. However, current mobility-aware SFC migration methods remain constrained by either post-hoc reaction or myopic prediction horizons, failing to reconcile the divergent timescales of network services and user mobility, thus resulting in suboptimal resource allocation and service delivery. In this paper, we first formulate the predictive mobility-aware SFC migration problem as an NP-hard Integer Linear Programming (ILP) problem. Aiming to mitigate service disruption for mobile users in MEC networks, we propose PreSFC, a predictive SFC migration framework that integrates multi-slot mobility forecasting with fine-grained network state tracking. We first design Gformer, a deep learning-based long sequence time-series forecasting model, which operates on short time slots (less than 200 ms) to sensitively capture network dynamics while predicting over multiple slots (e.g., 50 slots) to effectively track user mobility. This dual-scale design explicitly addresses the temporal disparity between mobility patterns and service requirements. Based on the predictions, we further propose an Optimal Sub-period Partitioning Migration (OSPM) algorithm to determine migration timing and locations. Extensive simulations show that our approach reduces the maximum and average downtime by approximately 55% and 40%, respectively, compared to benchmark methods.

title: Probabilistic Sparse Attention and Adaptive Domain Modeling for External Defect Detection in Transformers
authors: Ning Sun, Jiabao Zhang, Sicheng Zhu, Zihao Wang, Hucheng Xu, Penghui Liu, Guangan Ding, Ying Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: The images of the external equipment of the transformer obtained by the cameras and other devices have complex backgrounds, and the small targets with minor defects or small images have low resolution, resulting in a decrease in the recognition rate. To address the environment with complex feature backgrounds, this paper proposes an adaptive detection network for external transformer defects. The network architecture incorporates a specialized stage structure, an enhanced transformer layer, an efficient layer aggregation mechanism, and a real-time detection transformer decoder. Initially, raw images of transformer equipment are utilized as input. The primary module, designated as the probabilistic sparse transformer layer, integrates a novel encoder module as the core channel mechanism. This module includes a patch embedding layer, a sparse multi-head probabilistic attention layer, a residual connection structure, a layer normalization operation, and a feedforward layer. This design efficiently extracts irregularly varying feature information under limited fault samples while globally capturing data anomalies induced by external defects. Additionally, the incorporation of an adaptive activation function augments the expressive capacity of nonlinear mappings. The piecewise linear properties of the function prevent extreme fluctuations in output values, thereby mitigating gradient vanishing issues while enhancing expressive capability. Furthermore, the integration of domain-adaptive batch normalization technology reduces feature distribution discrepancies between source and target domains. This study uses real engineering data sets, and the results show that DEIM-PPSformer, based on DEIM, increased mAP@0.5 by 3.1%, reaching 92.1%. mAP@0.5-0.95 increased by 5.8%, reaching 71.6%. This indicates that the proposed method has a significant advantage in detecting defects in external transformer equipment.

title: VTMedSeg: Geometry-guided Vision-Text Model with Concept-aware Fusion for Medical Ultrasound Image Segmentation
authors: Huan Wan, Wujian Xu, Yiwen Zou, Jinshan Zeng, Xin Wei
abstract: Medical ultrasound image segmentation is a vital diagnostic technique for identifying abnormalities, including tumors, cysts, and inflammation. To obtain accurate segmentation efficiently, many methods have been proposed. Amongst, vision–text models have shown promising progress; however, their performance is limited by the lack of high-quality paired text descriptions in ultrasound datasets. To address this issue, we propose VTMedSeg. In VTMedSeg, a geometry-guided text generator is developed to automatically synthesize medical descriptions by using connected component analysis and multi-dimensional geometric analysis. Then, the text features of these descriptions and the visual features of the corresponding ultrasound images are effectively integrated into a concept-aware cross-modal fusion module through multi-scale cross-modal alignment and medical knowledge graph modeling. Extensive experiments on four public medical ultrasound datasets demonstrate that VTMedSeg outperforms state-of-the-art vision–text models across multiple metrics.

title: Modelling Predictive Uncertainty for Reliable Traffic Flow Forecasting Using UA-STTFF Framework
authors: Hemavathi N, R Sumathi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) rely on precise and reliable traffic flow forecasting (TFF) to alleviate congestion, optimise routing, and allow safe traffic management. Nevertheless, most current spatiotemporal forecasting models do not well model complicated spatial interactions between intersections and changing time dynamics, and do not pay much attention to predictive uncertainty, which is essential in decision-making in the presence of variable traffic conditions. To overcome these difficulties, the present paper introduces an Uncertainty-Aware Spatiotemporal Traffic flow Forecasting (UA-STTFF) framework that simultaneously trains spatial dependencies with the help of graph convolutional networks and time dynamics with the aid of gated recurrent units (GRUs). The structure also uses probabilistic output heads and Monte Carlo (MC) dropout to enable the uncertainty-aware traffic forecasting. The proposed UA-STTFF is strictly tested on simulated multi-intersection time-series data of traffic. Detailed ablation experiments show that the spatial graph convolution and the temporal GRU block are fundamental in the enhancement of predictive accuracy, whereas probabilistic heads and MC dropout considerably enhance the representation of uncertainty. The experiments have demonstrated that UA-STTFF outperforms a deterministic spatiotemporal graph neural network baseline by 26.4%, 25.6%, and 26.4% in mean absolute error (MAE), root mean square error (RMSE), and mean absolute percentage error (MAPE). Further, the model has a prediction interval coverage probability (PICP) of 93.7%, which is a good prediction of uncertainty. Visualising uncertainty at the node level also allows the identification of intersections that are likely to be highly risky, which can be used in risk-conscious traffic control. In general, the suggested framework eliminates the significance of uncertainty-sensitive modelling in dependence on credible and extensive ITS.

title: Heavy Ion-Induced Single-Event Effects in eGaN HEMTS
authors: Arefa Billa, Patrick Maloney, Grant Mayberry, Tianfang Liu, Benjamin Bolton, Hector Parra, Fahim Ahmed, Joyprokash Debnath, Daniel M. Fleetwood, En Xia Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: This study investigates the Single Event Effects (SEE) enhancement-mode GaN High Electron Mobility Transistors (eGaN HEMTs) under off-bias conditions induced by heavy ion irradiation with varying incident angles. Two commercially available eGaN devices with different voltage and current ratings were subjected to 16 MeV and 20 MeV ion beams to assess their susceptibility to SEE-induced degradation. Significant sensitivity to single-event leakage current (SELC) and single-event burnout (SEB) was observed under xenon (Xe) irradiation at high drain bias. Additionally, low-level SELC events were recorded during argon (Ar) irradiation at elevated drain voltages and high incident angles. The results highlight the importance of ion species, angle of incidence, and bias conditions in determining SEE vulnerability in commercial eGaN power transistors.

title: Resource Allocation for Multi-Domain Network Slicing: A Transfer Learning Approach
authors: Jihong Zhao, Yingtong Wang, Lin Wen
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of 5 G technology, network slicing has emerged as a critical solution to accommodate diverse service demands. In multi-domain environments, mobile network operators must manage multiple radio access networks (RANs) domains independently, increasing operational complexity and cost. This paper proposes a resource allocation method based on transfer learning (TL) to enhance the adaptability of scheduling policies across RAN domains. A resource allocation policy is initially pre-trained using the proximal policy optimization (PPO) algorithm in a source domain. The learned policy is then transferred to the target domains via policy fine-tuning and distillation. To ensure stable convergence, a kullback-leibler (KL) divergence regularization term and a transfer decay mechanism are introduced. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method improves the average training efficiency by $44.81 \backslash\%$ and reduces the training steps by ${4 0} \backslash \%-{5 3} \backslash \%$ compared to training from scratch, effectively improving resource allocation and reducing operational costs in multi-domain scenarios.

title: An Effective Classification of Potato Leaf Diseases Using Hybrid Deep Learning Schemes
authors: T.M. Saravanan, S.Hari Prasath, M.K. Lingeshwaran, P.T. Vishwas, Moureeshvar Sa
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Potato is one of the most widely grown food crops that contains essential source of nutrition and revenue for many farmers throughout the world. However, a number of leaf diseases that arise and spread under specific environmental conditions have a substantial impact on productivity and can lower crop yield. In order to manage the illnesses and therefore manage the crop, it is crucial to identify and categorize them. This project's goal is to create a Deep Learning-based application that can recognize and categorize seven potato crop leaf diseases: bacterial, fungal, nematode, virus, phytophthora, and healthy. In order to produce comparison evaluations and determine which model is most suited for the disease classification system, the built system makes use of four Deep Learning methodologies: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN), Artificial Neural Network (ANN), Recurrent Neural Network (RNN), and RESNET. A total of 3,076 real-world photographs of potato leaves that have been scaled, standardized, and labeled make up the dataset. To effectively provide an objective assessment of model performance, these altered images were divided between training and testing sets, 80: 20 each. The RESNET model demonstrated the ability to learn spatial information and obtained the highest overall performance, with an accuracy of 97% on the testing set.

title: Advancements in Brain Tumor Analysis and Early Detection through MRI Imaging and Explainable AI Techniques
authors: Raghavendra Patil G E, Baba Fakruddin Ali B H, Swathi N, Jeevitha S R, Mehar Afroz, Umamaheswaran A
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This research uses deep learning and Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) methodologies in conjunction with Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) to classify and diagnose brain tumor early. The technique begins with the preprocessing of MRI images, which includes scaling, normalization, and enhancement of contrast, in order to prepare the data for modeling training. This work classifies brain tumor using architectures in InceptionV3 and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) in combination with transfer learning. The project uses XAI strategies like Grad-CAM and saliency maps, which visually highlight and show which areas are influencing model predictive accuracy, to promote transparency and clinical confidence. The project made use of cloud-based solutions to host the MRI image viewer and implementation of the system to manage large datasets efficiently. This multidisciplinary methodology enables health care providers to make accurate decisions while providing interpretable data through deep learning and XAI approaches to improve diagnostic precision. The case study demonstrates how deep learning, explainability, and complex image processing can be effectively combined to properly detect and classify brain tumors.

title: Comprehensive Voice-Based Pain Level Classification Utilizing Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)
authors: Andrew Y. Lu, Wei Lu
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Accurate classification of pain level is a persistent challenge in both clinical and non-clinical settings. Traditional approaches such as self-reporting, physiological signal monitoring, and facial expression analysis often suffer from limitations related to accessibility, equipment cost, and professional support. In this work, we propose a lightweight framework for real-time pain level classification using vocal signals. The proposed framework exploits the unique spectrogram features of voice signals to train Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) models. Our framework has been validated through simulations in Jupiter Notebook and a Raspberry Pi based prototype. Experimental results show that our approach outperforms existing methods by more than 20 % in accuracy. Our case studies also examined the impact of sample size and label imbalance on classification accuracy, showing that biased training leads to overfitting and a significant decrease in classification accuracy. Our hardware prototype, built from commercial off-the-shelf components for under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0}$</tex> USD, achieves real-time processing speeds ranging from approximately 6 to 22 seconds. In addition to CNN models, our experiments demonstrate that other machine learning algorithms such as Artificial Neural Networks, XGBoost, Random Forest, and Decision Tree have also proven applicable within our pain level classification framework.

title: Smart Farming: Eggplant Leaf Disease Detection through Fine-Tuned DenseNet201 Deep Learning Model
authors: Khushdeep Kaur, Husanpreet Kaur, Avneet Kaur, Varun Gupta, Shobhit Tomar
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: In the majority of developing countries, farmers use eggplant that is readily infected by different diseases that affect leaves. The early and correct detection of these diseases will help in provision of food, sustainable agriculture and cost reduction. A precise fine-tuning of the DenseNet201 and a deep learning methodology allowed scholars to differentiate the eggplant leaves state of a healthy plant, insect pest disease, leaf spot disease, mosaic virus disease, small leaf disease, white mold disease and wilt disease. Overall, 3552 images of open-source Kaggle platform were selected and divided into the following subsets: training, validation and testing: 70, 15 and 15 percent. The given system demonstrated a 86% hit rate with test data and a high score in precision, recall and F1scores on most of the classes. The analysis of the confusion matrices and the training curves show clearly that there is no error rate or the model is firm. Artificial intelligence in plant pathology can help find a faster solution to plant diseases, responsible eating patterns, and fight climate change and hunger. The study satisfies the objectives of sustainable development by contributing to the effectiveness of the farming process, by facilitating the new ideas in the process of finding the solutions and effective use of the resources. The recommended method can enable farmers and other agriculturalists to identify diseases at an earlier age, shift toward low-pesticide practices and achieve greater yields.

title: Revolutionizing Cloud Security through Federated Learning for Next-Gen Healthcare Systems in South Africa
authors: Ramahlapane Lerato Moila, Mthulisi Velempini
abstract: Our study presents an innovative federated learning (FL) approach secured by a hybrid zero-trust cloud architecture, specifically designed for the healthcare system in South Africa. Amid increasing cybersecurity threats and growing privacy concerns in hospitals, our approach enables collaborative model training across decentralized medical institutions without the sharing of raw patient data. This study used the CSE-CIC-IDS2018 dataset to simulate distributed environments and evaluate the performance of various algorithms, including logistic regression, random forests (RF), and deep learning, in both local and federated environments. Simulation results show that while the deep learning model performs well during local training, it degrades under global aggregation due to data heterogeneity. RF and Logistic Regression (LR) demonstrate stronger, more stable performance during FL. Compared with existing studies, our proposed scheme achieves higher accuracy—96% in LR and 97% in RF, improved scalability, and optimized computational efficiency, positioning it as a viable and secure foundation for next-generation healthcare systems in South Africa.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Aerodynamic Shape Optimization: A Machine Learning Approach to Accelerating Aircraft Design
authors: Akshay Lakkur, Benjamin Schmidt, Linus Feng, Aarav Dixit, Chloe Lui
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Aerodynamic Shape optimization (ASO) is a computationally challenging but critical component of modern aircraft design, which involves modifying an airfoil to minimize the drag-to-lift ratio. Traditional methods for this problem utilize a simulation-based approach known as computational fluid dynamics (CFD) to solve the Reynolds-Averaged Navier-Stokes (RANS) equations and extract pressure values, which can be used to calculate lift and drag. These values are then iteratively updated to better suit turbulence conditions. The problem with this approach lies in the time complexity of iteratively computing solutions to the RANS equations required for ASO to converge. To exponentially speed up the process of ASO, we propose a novel algorithm that replaces a CFD solver with a physics-informed neural network (PINN), along with a comparison between two parameterization techniques to optimize an airfoil, both utilizing a PINN instead of a CFD solver. The model shows a speedup of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 1, 3 1 0. 2 4 \%}$</tex> with the risk of only a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 3 \%}$</tex> decrease in accuracy compared to a CFD solver.

title: An Effective CNN Multiresolution Framework for Covid-19 Diagnosis using Chest X-RAY
authors: Kodumur Likitha, G. Fayaz Hussain, Thogata Manasa, Golla Sailaja, Akula Vyshnavi
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The COVID-19 made fast and accurate diagnostic systems four top priorities to aid in early detection and treatment. Chest X-ray images demand manual examination, and such analysis needs expert knowledge that is not always rapid, thus automated screening is incredibly useful. The present work suggests the adoption of a successful CNN-based multiresolution model to classify COVID-19 with the help of chest X-ray to either provide positive or negative results. The framework is analyzed over a set of spatial resolutions to address both local texture abnormalities and larger lung structural alterations and has better feature representation than individually analyzed singleresolution models. The transfer learning strategy based on the pretrained convolutional networks with multiresolution fusion phase are used, which enhances the diagnostic accuracy, at the same time retaining the computational efficiency. The experimental analysis of publicly available shares reveals that the suggested system has excellent accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, precision, recall, and F-measure when it comes to binary prediction of COVID-19. A well-balanced combination of complexities and processing time of the model delivers an effective and costefficient screening tool that is viable to be implemented in clinical practice, which leads to reduced diagnostic assistance and better patient-care management.

title: Brain Tumor Detection and Classification from MRI Scans Using Deep Learning
authors: Prashanshi Yadav, Sumit Shukla, Om Pandey, Nikita
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Brain tumors pose a significant threat to human life, where delayed or inaccurate diagnosis can have serious consequences for patient survival. Recent studies indicate that machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) techniques combined with magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) have become the most widely used approaches for automated brain tumor detection, segmentation, grading, and multiclass classification. A wide range of methods, including handcrafted texture descriptors, hybrid ViT-PCA-RF pipelines, entropy-based features, and advanced deep learning architectures such as CNNs, ResNets, CRNNs, WGAN-augmented networks, and transfer learning models including VGG, Xception, and EfficientNet, have consistently demonstrated high diagnostic accuracy, with many studies reporting performance in the range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$95-99 \%$</tex>. Despite these advancements, several challenges remain, including data imbalance, overfitting, limited interpretability, and the dependence on expert-annotated MRI datasets. This review consolidates recent computer-aided diagnosis (CAD)-based approaches, their architectural designs, and performance outcomes. It also highlights emerging trends in explainable AI, hybrid feature fusion, and robust data augmentation techniques, while outlining future research opportunities for developing clinically reliable and fully automated brain tumor diagnostic systems.

title: PCB Board Defect Sorting System Based on Improved Detection Algorithm
authors: Mengqi Chen, Yu Yan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Aiming at the practical requirements of PCB industrial board defect detection, this paper proposes an improved YOLOv8-based object detection algorithm for PCB automated inspection systems. Firstly, the CBAM attention mechanism is introduced into the original YOLOv8 network structure to enhance the mode's ability to extract critical defect features and improve feature representation. Secondly, the Wise-IoU loss function is incorporated to optimize the bounding box regression process, thereby improving localization accuracy and overall detection performance. Experimental results show that, compared with the original YOLOv8 model, the proposed method achieves significant performance improvements in PCB defect detection tasks: the precision increases from 79.1 % to 89.6 %, representing an improvement of 10.5 %. In addition, after integrating the improved model into the PCB automated inspection system, it can accurately identify and locate various types of defects such as missing holes, mouse bites, and open circuits. In summary, the proposed algorithm and its system application provide an efficient and reliable solution for PCB industrial automated inspection, and also offer valuable references for the intelligent development of other industrial defect detection tasks.

title: Computation–Accuracy Trade-Off in Service-Oriented Model-Based Control
authors: Hazem Ibrahim, Julius Beerwerth, Lorenz Dörschel, Bassam Alrifaee
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: Representing a control system as a Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA)—referred to as Service-Oriented Model-Based Control (SOMC)—enables runtime-flexible composition of control loop elements. This paper presents a framework that optimizes the computation–accuracy trade-off by formulating service orchestration as an A<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">★</sup> search problem, complemented by Contextual Bayesian Optimization (BO) to tune the multi-objective cost weights. A vehicle longitudinal-velocity control case study demonstrates online, performance-driven reconfiguration of the control architecture. We show that our framework not only combines control and software structure but also considers the real-time requirements of the control system during performance optimization.

title: Research Review on UAV-Based Air-Ground Small Target Detection and Tracking
authors: Shaoqiong Duan, Shixiao Wu, Xi Wang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: UAV air-to-ground small target detection and tracking is the core enabling technology for low-altitude intelligent perception, widely used in security monitoring, energy inspection, emergency search and rescue, agricultural monitoring and other engineering fields. Current research still faces key challenges: inherent feature scarcity of small targets, strong clutter interference from complex backgrounds, limited on-board computing resources, and insufficient detection-tracking synergy. Aiming at the deficiencies of existing reviews (inadequate elaboration on lightweight algorithm adaptation for UAV scenarios, insufficient discussion on detection-tracking collaborative optimization, and lack of systematic quantitative evaluation of state-of-the-art algorithms), this paper first clarifies the core scientific issues and five key technical bottlenecks in this field. Secondly, it systematically analyzes the core mechanism, technical advantages and limitations of various algorithms from six core technical dimensions: backbone network optimization, multi-scale feature fusion and enhancement, attention mechanism introduction, loss function improvement, lightweight model design, and detection-tracking collaborative optimization. Subsequently, it completes the quantitative performance comparison of mainstream improved algorithms based on the VisDrone2019 dataset. Finally, it dissects the core deficiencies of current research and prospects key future breakthrough directions.

title: The On-Robot Computing Needs of Embodied AI
authors: Shaoshan Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: Embodied AI spans a broad range of robotic forms, including mobile robots, manipulators, quadrupeds, humanoids, and other task-specific machines, rather than humanoids alone. Across these embodiments, a common systems challenge remains: intelligence must ultimately be supported by efficient on-robot computation. This article examines the shared on-robot computing needs of embodied AI through four tightly coupled layers: actuator and servo loops, multimodal sensing, whole-body and core robotic control, and foundation models. We argue that the central bottleneck is not only model capability, but also how onboard compute is architected, distributed, and coordinated under strict constraints in latency, power, thermal budget, reliability, and cost. In particular, embodied AI workloads are fundamentally heterogeneous, ranging from deterministic real-time control and sensor fusion to optimization-heavy robot functions and data-intensive model inference. These diverse requirements cannot be addressed by scaling foundation models alone. Instead, progress in embodied AI will increasingly depend on cross-layer co-design of algorithms, compute architectures, memory and communication systems, and robot form factors, so that robotic platforms can achieve responsive, robust, and energy-efficient intelligence in real-world deployment.

title: Clinical XAI: A Tutorial on Concepts, Methods, and Modalities from Linear Models to LLMs
authors: Munib Mesinovic, Tingting Zhu
abstract: None

title: A Non-Contact Volume Measurement System using a RGB-D Camera
authors: Jin Seong Son, Jae Hong Shim
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Mechatronics, Control and Robotics (ICMCR)
abstract: In recent years, the demand for three-dimensional (3D) scanners for shape reconstruction and volume measurement has grown significantly in the fields of manufacturing and quality inspection. However, most commercial 3D scanners rely on expensive equipment or complex robotic systems, making them cost-prohibitive and difficult to access for general users. To address these limitations, this study proposes the design and implementation of a low-cost 3D scanning system that integrates an RGB-D camera with a two degree of freedom (DOF) rotational structure. The proposed system consists of an RGB-D camera mounted on a servo motor enabling horizontal rotation and a turntable that rotates the target object. These two components operate independently, allowing the system to capture point cloud data from multiple perspectives and minimize occluded areas. The acquired point clouds from multiple viewpoints are aligned and post-processed to reconstruct the complete 3D geometry of the object. A volume estimation algorithm is then applied to automatically calculate the object's volume. Experimental results show that despite its simple mechanical configuration, the proposed system achieves accurate shape reconstruction and consistent volume measurement.

title: Predicting Student Performance in Architectural Visualization: A Random Forest Comparison of Core and Elective Student Groups
authors: S. M. Monowar Kayser, Mridul Sikder, Anindya Rudra, Sayon Chakraborty
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This investigation examines whether student performance patterns in "Architectural Visualization" differ between core students (Multimedia and Creative Technology, MCT) for whom it is mandatory and elective students (Environmental Science and Disaster Management, ESDM) who self-select the course. Using a dataset of 65 students (37 MCT, 28 ESDM), department-wise Random Forest models were developed to identify key performance drivers. Cross-validated models (82.2% accuracy for ESDM, 59.3% for MCT, exceeding baseline by 2. 3 × and 2. 4 × ) revealed distinct learning pathways: ESDM students rely more on continuous engagement (Attendance: 22.1% importance), while MCT students depend on major examinations (Final Exam: 17.8% importance), despite achieving statistically similar final outcomes (p = 0. 605). This indicates students from different academic backgrounds achieve similar outcomes through distinct learning pathways. The analytical framework provides educators with insights for developing targeted pedagogical interventions for both core and elective student groups.

title: Moodify AI: An Intelligent Air-Drumming System Using Computer Vision and Artificial Intelligence for Real-Time Musical Interaction
authors: Subrahmanya H M, Medha M, Nithushree, Vidhya Shree M, Deepika B O
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Traditional drum kits are expensive, bulky, and space-consuming, limiting accessibility for beginners and casual learners. This paper presents Moodify AI, a marker less, vision-based air-drumming system that uses OpenCV and MediaPipe for real-time hand-landmark extraction and a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) classifier for gesture recognition. Unlike sensor-based or wearable systems, the proposed approach requires only a standard webcam and performs velocity-based gesture detection to simulate dynamic drum intensity. Experimental evaluation shows an average gesture recognition accuracy of 92% in well-lit conditions and 87% under low-light environments, with an average latency of 150–250ms, making it suitable for real-time musical interaction. The system demonstrates a low-cost, portable, and scalable alternative to hardware-dependent virtual percussion systems while enabling expressive gesture-controlled music performance.

title: Fake Review Detection Based on LOFD-EGCNN Model
authors: Weixue Shen, Ming Li, Jing Wang, Linlin Fan
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: To address the shortcomings of poor data adaptability and insufficient feature utilization in fake review detection for Chinese e-commerce scenarios, this study proposes the LOFD-EGCNN model. The model integrates the LOFD feature weighting algorithm with a gated convolutional network, enhances domain-specific features through emoji semantic mapping and special punctuation retention, and realizes adaptive fusion of textual semantic features and statistical features via a gating mechanism. Experimental results show that the model outperforms mainstream deep learning methods in recognition performance on specific product review datasets and maintains favorable generalization in cross-domain transfer scenarios. Adapted to the typical characteristics of Chinese fake reviews including emotional exaggeration and symbol intensity, the model can provide technical support for optimizing the review audit systems of e-commerce platforms and facilitate the construction of a credible e-commerce information ecosystem.

title: AgroSSL-Net: A Self-Supervised Deep Learning Framework for Crop Health Monitoring
authors: Sharmila G
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Accurate crop health monitoring is essential for early disease identification, pest infestation detection, and nutrient deficiency assessment. Traditional deep learning (DL) models struggle with feature generalization due to limited labeled agricultural datasets. This study proposes a model AgroSSL-Net, a new self-supervised learning (SSL) framework to enhance feature learning from unlabeled crop images. This novel Modality-aware Contrastive Learning Network MoCLRNet method leverages contrastive learning and momentum-based representation learning to extract robust features. A fine-tuned CNN backbone with multi-head self-attention (MHSA) is employed for feature refinement, ensuring improved classification accuracy. Experiments on benchmark agricultural datasets demonstrate that MoCLRNet outperforms existing models, achieving an accuracy of 92.1% for disease classification, 91.8% for pest detection, and 90.6% for nutrient deficiency assessment. The proposed method also improves feature extraction efficiency, reducing computational complexity while enhancing robustness. These findings imply that AgroSSL-Net is an efficient approach for real-time, automated crop health monitoring in smart agriculture.

title: Enhanced Methane Production and Real-Time Nutrient Assessment in Automated Biogas Systems
authors: K. Sheikdavood, Vishnu Bharathi J, Vennila V, Subiksha K, Subisha P
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: One sustainable method of converting trash into renewable energy is the anaerobic digestion of organic waste to create biogas. However, due to insufficient process monitoring and limited control over operating parameters, conventional small-scale digesters usually have unpredictable digestion conditions. Although numerous studies have proposed automation in biogas systems, particularly in small household digesters, the specific contribution of controllable parameters to methane improvement and nutrient quality remains inadequately defined. By suggesting an automated biogas digestion chamber that separates and evaluates the impact of real-time regulation on system performance, this work closes this gap. It features parameter driven operational control and a structurally optimized geometry. The recommended system integrates a microcontroller-based feedback mechanism with embedded sensors for temperature, gas pressure, and slurry level to maintain constant anaerobic conditions throughout the digestion cycle. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that controlled thermal stability and pressure regulation significantly improve methane uniformity when compared to a non-automated setup. Furthermore, the nutrient profile of the output bio-slurry is assessed by integrating a real-time inline NPK sensor module, which enables an immediate evaluation of fertilizer quality without the need for laboratory-based analysis. The framework for evaluating energy and nutrients together provides quantifiable information about the stability of methane yield and the efficacy of nutrient recovery. books about automation. The results validate that the proposed automated chamber enhances process reliability and resource usage, making it suitable for scalable home and community-level biogas applications. They also contribute a parameter-specific performance study to the existing body of biogas automation literature.

title: Uplink User Association and Resource Allocation in Integrated Terrestrial and Non-Terrestrial Networks
authors: Denise Joanitah Birabwa, Sunday O. Oladejo, Godfrey Kibalya
abstract: The integrated terrestrial and non-terrestrial network (ITNTN) envisioned in 6G introduces uplink performance challenges due to limited user equipment power, heterogeneous channels, variable delays, and differing resource capacities across the ITNTN nodes. This paper addresses joint user association and resource allocation in the uplink of the ITNTN by formulating a nonlinear fractional programming problem aimed at maximizing global energy efficiency (EE). The problem is transformed into a multi-objective optimization problem (MOOP), which is then reformulated into a weighted-sum single-objective optimization problem (SOOP) to balance data rate and power consumption. To solve it, we propose JUAA-PSO, a novel particle swarm optimization algorithm that efficiently associates users and allocates resources to maximize data rate while minimizing network power usage. Simulation results show that JUAA-PSO performs within 3.1% and 4.4% of the ILP solution in spectrum efficiency and EE, respectively.

title: A Multimodal Deep Learning Approach for Emotion-Aware Speech Recognition
authors: Deepak. K. S, Elwin Gonsalves, Deepak.R, H.S.Veeresh, Ashwini Kodipalli, K.C.Narendra
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The following research proposes an integrated Deep Learning pipeline that unifies speech recognition, natural language understanding, and emotion-aware analysis to achieve human-like comprehension of spoken interactions. The system initiates with the fine-tuning of OpenAI's Whisper model by LoRA-based parameter-efficient training on the LibriSpeech ASR dataset for high-quality, lightweight speech-to-text transcription. The model performance was evaluated against metrics such as WER, CER, and BLEU score for linguistic fidelity. Subsequently, the resultant text is subjected to several steps involved in a multi-stage Natural Language Processing framework about Glove embedding, an Autoencoder, and then a Bidirectional GRU-based RNN for extractive summarisations, while a Keyword CNN highlights semantically important terms. The most relevant sentences, identified by the RNN, are passed to advanced transformer models such as Google Gemini and T5 for abstractive summarization, yielding coherent, context-rich summaries of conversations. In parallel, the system includes an emotion recognition module trained on the RAVDESS dataset using MFCC features extracted with torch audio and modelled through both MLP and GRU architectures. By analyzing pitch variation and RMS energy, the system maps detected emotions into expressive "vibe" categories such as enthusiastic, frustrated, hesitant, or calm, providing an additional emotional layer to dialogue understanding. Together, these components create a comprehensive AI framework capable of transcribing speech, understanding its semantic essence, summarizing key information, and interpreting emotional tone, forming a foundation for the next generation of emotionally intelligent and context-aware conversational systems.

title: Structural Damage Assessment and Repair Estimation Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Pushpanjali S, Nikhil J, Lakshit, Girish A M, Divya S, R Jayanthi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Rapid structural assessment dictates the trajectory of post – disaster recovery, safety inspections, and maintenance logistics. Conventional building evaluations depend upon manual surveys – processes notoriously sluggish, subjective, and hazardous within unstable environments. This research introduces an automated framework that identifies structural compromises from imagery and calculates approximate remediation expenses through machine learning-based classification and regression architectures. The system integrates image preprocessing, feature extraction, and deep learning-driven detection with rule–based estimation to produce repair recommendations characterized by heightened consistency and precision. Findings demonstrate that this computational methodology accelerates institutional decision–making and significantly diminishes the manual labor typically demanded by large–scale structural diagnostics and multifaceted engineering analyses. Additionally, the system performance is quantitatively validated using accuracy, confidence scores, latency, and memory utilization metrics to ensure practical deployment feasibility.

title: Characterization and Compensation of Deadband Nonlinearities in a Scaled Hydraulic Excavator for Human-Machine Interface Research
authors: Eli Williams
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Our goal is to create a novel simulated teleoperation training environment for novice excavator operators. We employed a 1/12-scale hydraulic excavator model and observed certain operational differences between the scaled version and the real excavator, namely two dominant effects: (i) static deadband due to the model's rotary spool valve's neutral position, which prevents gradual motion onset, and (ii) dynamic deadband, in which the model excavator joint velocity persists despite decreasing joystick input. An empirical, sensor-driven process is introduced to quantify and compensate both phenomena using only joystick-to-PWM commands and IMU-measured angular velocity. Static deadband is mapped by incrementally sweeping PWM from neutral to identify the minimum effective command per joint and direction; dynamic deadband is characterized via a timed reversal step input to determine the minimal directionspecific <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \text{PWM}$</tex> needed to terminate or reverse motion. Implementing joint and direction-specific offsets (static) together with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \text{PWM}$</tex> pulses (dynamic) restores bidirectional low-speed modulation, reduces reversal-onset delay, and yields a motion response that more closely tracks real excavator behavior. While the method improves the scaled model response, it does not linearize the inherent kinematic nonlinearity of the piston-linkage geometry, and calibration values remain condition-dependent. Overall, the results show a replicable process that measurably improves control fidelity on scaled hydraulic excavators, enhancing the usefulness of such platforms for HMI research and training.

title: Secure-BSM: Blockchain-Assisted Collaborative Misbehavior Detection in Vehicular Networks
authors: M M Imran, Yi Zhu, Shiyong Lu
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) communication relies on periodic Basic Safety Messages (BSMs) to support collision avoidance and cooperative driving, yet remains vulnerable to attacks such as False Data Injection (FDIA), spoofing, replay, Denial-of-Service (DoS), and Sybil attacks. Existing misbehavior detection approaches often exhibit significant limitations, as centralized authorities suffer from high revocation latency and infrastructure dependency, while standalone machine-learning and plausibility methods struggle with high false positive rates due to a lack of collaborative verification. We propose Secure-BSM, a blockchain-assisted architecture that reformulates vehicular misbehavior detection as a collaborative consensus problem to address these limitations. Shifting away from fixed-infrastructure models, Secure-BSM employs local attack detectors first, followed by decentralized validation where vehicles act as lightweight consensus validators. This dynamic approach utilizes a local Pocket Ledger for rapid consensus and a global Root Ledger for tamper-resistant revocation. By aggregating evidence via smart contracts and enforcing limits through Verifiable Rate-limit Tokens (VRTs), the system minimizes false positives and mitigates DoS attacks. Evaluation using SUMO traffic simulations across six attack types demonstrates that collaborative consensus improves detection precision by over 10% and reduces false positives by 77%–85% across various attack scenarios, while the VRT mechanism contains DoS flooding in under 0.2 seconds.

title: Research on Gear Deviation Patterns Based on Rolling Inspection Technology
authors: Shitao Wang, Zhaoyao Shi, Bo Yu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: With the increasing performance demands on gears for new energy vehicles, it has become essential to extract and decouple the gear error information contained in transmission error (TE) and radial composite deviation (RCD). However, a quantitative relationship between TE and RCD has not yet been clearly established. To address this issue, a numerical model of double-flank meshing is developed to reveal their intrinsic relationship. Based on this model, the effects of crowning and pressure-angle deviation on the relationship between TE and RCD are further analyzed.

title: An Ultra-Fast 3P0V Multi-Level Cell Programming Algorithm for Inference Context Memory Storage (ICMS)
authors: Jiacen Guo, Xiang Yang
abstract: This paper introduces a novel programming scheme, 3P0V multi-level cell (MLC) without program verification, designed as a caching layer for TLC/QLC SSDs. The proposed approach simultaneously enhances system burst performance and doubles effective cache capacity. Owing to the elimination of program-verify overhead, the scheme achieves ultra-high write bandwidth: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 \times$</tex> than that of mainstream TLC, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30 \times$</tex> that of mainstream QLC, and even 14% higher than mainstream SLC programming algorithms. Despite its aggressive programming speed, the proposed MLC 3P0V method demonstrates robust reliability and unexpectedly strong data-retention characteristics, enabled by a favorable interplay between fast programming dynamics and charge-loss mechanisms. As a result, this technique provides a compelling solution not only for conventional SSD architectures but also for emerging inference context memory storage (ICMS) SSDs.

title: FindTogether: AI-Powered Missing Person Detection and Collaboration System with Social Dashboard
authors: Anupama B. S, Aaryan Dewangan, Pratik Gautam, Impana K S, Sidhant Munjral
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Missing-person identification is a time-sensitive problem in which delays in analysing available evidence can significantly reduce the chances of successful recovery. The widespread use of surveillance cameras and mobile devices has resulted in large volumes of video data that may contain useful clues; however, manual inspection of this data is slow, inconsistent, and difficult under real-world conditions such as crowding, occlusion, poor lighting, and non-frontal views. This paper presents FindTogether, an AI-assisted platform for missing person detection and collaborative investigation that combines deep facial embeddings with clothing-based appearance descriptors and performs asynchronous video processing in a cloud environment. A YOLO-based person detector is used to identify individuals in video frames, after which facial and clothing features are extracted and fused into a unified similarity score to generate ranked candidate matches. An investigator dashboard supports human verification of AI-generated results, enabling efficient and transparent decision-making during missing-person investigations.

title: How State Space Machines can help African Speech Language Identification
authors: Howard Prioleau, Saurav K. Aryal, Jeremy Blackstone
abstract: This paper investigates advanced modeling techniques tailored for the identification of African language from audio data. We leverage state-of-the-art deep learning architectures, specifically the Audio Spectrogram Transformer (AST) and Mamba, a state space model optimized for efficient sequential data processing. Using a meticulously curated dataset comprising six African languages and performing a comparative analysis with traditional machine learning models, our aim is to evaluate the effectiveness and computational efficiency of these advanced approaches. Although AST achieves perfect accuracy in all languages tested, AfriMamba offers an impressive accuracy of 97. 9% along with nearly double the training speed and six times faster inference, demonstrating its transformative potential.

title: Deep Learning for Soil Type Detection: A CNN Approach to Agricultural Insights
authors: J. Brahmaiahnaik, KastalaKarthik, ThummalaPavankumar Reddy, VallemAnkammarao, Karibandi Venkata Sai Lakshmi Harika, Maddala Vijayalakshmi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: In land management, agriculture, and environmental monitoring, soil variety is essential. Conventional techniques for classifying soils depend on expert interpretation and laboratory examination, both of which are labor-intensive and subject to human error. In this work, we suggest a Convolution Neural Network (CNN)-based deep learning approach for soil type identification. Images from four distinct soil types make up the data set, which has been pre-processed and enhanced to enhance generalization. To efficiently extract spatial data, a custom CNN architecture with separable convolution layers and residual connections was set up. To address dataset imbalances, an adaptive learning approach with class weight balancing was used to train the model. The suggested method demonstrated its effectiveness in correctly categorizing soil types with a validation accuracy of 95.00%. By automating soil classification, this effort will lessen the need for manual inspection and provide a scalable and effective solution.

title: Degradation State Assessment of Rolling Bearings by Integrating Mechanism Knowledge with Multi-Scale Temporal Convolutional Networks
authors: Yuting Zhu, Yao Liu, Jiangyu Zhu, Chuanghui Lei
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: As a key component of rotating machinery, the health status of rolling bearings is directly related to the reliability of equipment operation. Traditional method of rolling bearing degradation state assessment, based on signal processing, statistical features, and multivariate data fusion, heavily relies on expert empirical knowledge. Furthermore, the features extracted may not necessarily exhibit optimal performance, limiting the effectiveness of degradation assessment. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a rolling bearing degradation state assessment method that integrates mechanism knowledge with multi-scale temporal convolutional networks.This approach combines expert-driven feature extraction and selection with deep learning-based adaptive feature learning, enhancing the bearing degradation feature extraction capability through the multi-scale temporal convolutional network, thereby improving the model performance. Experiments are conducted using XJTU-SY rolling bearing full lifecycle degradation data to validate the proposed method. The effectiveness and superiority of the method are confirmed through comparative analysis with two classical degradation assessment models and ablation experiments.

title: Solar-Powered Blackspot Alert System
authors: G.N. Keshava Murthy, Dhruthi P, Meghana D V, Saba, Shifa Iman
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Road accidents are a serious issue worldwide, especially at dangerous spots like sharp curves, blind spots, potholes, steep slopes, and areas known for frequent accidents. Traditional warning methods, such as static signboards, fall short because they do not give real-time alerts to drivers. This paper introduces a Solar-Powered Blackspot Alert System aimed at improving road safety through real-time hazard detection and driver alerts. The system uses infrared sensors to detect vehicles and measure speed. Ultrasonic sensors identify humps, potholes, and obstacles. Arduino UNO and Nano microcontrollers handle data processing, while RF modules enable wireless communication. Drivers receive alerts through an LCD display, buzzer, LED indicators, and a voice alert module. Solar energy allows the system to work continuously, even in remote areas without access to grid power or the internet. Test results show that the system performs reliably in real-time conditions and increases driver awareness, which helps lower the chances of accidents in blackspot zones.

title: AI-Powered Cyber Defense Desktop Application with Real-Time Behavioral Analysis, Threat Intelligence
authors: Shrihari M R, Ajay N, Chethan M, Charvick D raj, Dhanush p s
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The rise of sophisticated cyberattacks has outpaced the capabilities of conventional security systems, prompting the need for intelligent, autonomous, and real-time defensive solutions. This paper presents the design and development of an AI-powered Cyber Defense Desktop Application equipped with real-time behavioral analytics, multi-model machine learning–based anomaly detection, network monitoring, user authentication via email-based OTP, and automated threat alerting. The proposed architecture integrates simulated ensemble machine learning algorithms including Isolation Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) anomaly prediction, and behavioral deviation analysis to compute a composite threat score. Real-time telemetry is collected from system resources, network connections, and operational patterns to derive risk levels ranging from NORMAL to CRITICAL. Additional capabilities include threat intelligence lookup, automated HTML email reporting, weekly summary generation, and an interactive security dashboard. Results indicate significant improvements in early threat identification and risk estimation, demonstrating the practicality of AI-driven endpoint defense. This paper provides a complete analysis of system architecture, algorithm design, security mechanisms, implementation details, performance evaluation, and recommendations for future enhancements.

title: A Study on Digital Twin-Driven Predictive Maintenance Methods for High-Speed Winding and Baling Equipment
authors: Rui Yang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: The application of artificial intelligence and advanced intelligent algorithms has significantly promoted the development of predictive maintenance in industrial equipment. In this study, a digital twin-driven predictive maintenance framework is proposed for high-speed winding and packaging equipment, with a focus on capturing multi-physics coupling characteristics and fault propagation behavior. Specifically, a hierarchical virtual-physical mapping mechanism is established to construct the digital twin, in which physical components, operational states, and dynamic parameters are continuously synchronized within a unified information space. Multi-source sensor data, including vibration signals, temperature responses, load variations, and rotational speed, are collected and processed through a series of steps, including noise filtering, time alignment, feature extraction, and feature-level fusion. This process enables the construction of a unified and dynamically updated health state representation of the equipment. Based on the fused state representation, dynamic parameter updating strategies and degradation modeling methods are integrated into a time-series prediction framework to perform fault prediction and remaining useful life (RUL) assessment. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a fault prediction accuracy of 95.2%, outperforming the LSTM model by 1.7%, while reducing the MAE of RUL prediction to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2. 1}$</tex> cycles, approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 0 {\%}}$</tex> lower than that of the baseline model. These results indicate that the proposed framework exhibits strong accuracy, robustness, and stability under complex operating conditions.

title: ADD-Net-MRI: An Explainable Deep Learning Approach for Early Alzheimer’s Detection
authors: Mahesh Kumar M, Tippannagari Harshitha, Dhulla Bharath Kumar, Avula Praveen, Gopalolla Poojitha, Katari Jaswanth
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Early and accurate identification of Alzheimer’s disorder (AD) is crucial for powerful medical intervention, mainly in the course of the slight cognitive impairment (MCI) level. Structural magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) offers precious neuroanatomical records; but automated analysis remains tough because of elegance imbalance, inter-problem variability, confined generalization, and inadequate explainability. In these paintings, we suggest ADD-Net-MRI, a generalizable and explainable deep mastering framework for early and multi-level Alzheimer’s disorder class using structural MRI. The framework employs a light-weight convolutional neural community trained from scratch to stability diagnostic performance and computational efficiency. To deal with elegance imbalance, concern-degree SMOTETOMEK sampling is integrated throughout schooling, at the same time as a standardized preprocessing pipeline ensures reproducibility and robustness throughout datasets. Model interpretability is carried out using Grad-CAM– primarily based visual factors, permitting clinically significant insight into sickness-associated mind regions. Extensive problem-level pass-domain experiments demonstrate that ADD-Net-MRI achieves competitive class performance, improved sensitivity for early-degree AD detection, and stronger explainability with reduced computational complexity. These outcomes advocate that ADD-Net-MRI can function an effective laptop-aided diagnostic tool to support early Alzheimer’s disease assessment.

title: Macro Block Optimization Floor Plan and Placement at Advanced 7nm Node Using Synopsys ICC2
authors: Nagaraja. V, Praveenkumar Chittipolu, Manjula M, Sp. Lavakishor Reddy, T. Indu, V. Lavanya
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: To fulfill the performance requirements of modern integrated circuits, physical design at advanced. Area and Time management is a necessity in. Overcrowding and Energy. The implementation sequence for a. This paper deals with a digital design using Synopsys IC Compiler II (ICC2) on 7 nm CMOS technology. a.
