title: Multi-Axis Vision Transformer with Optimised Temporal Convolution for Oral Diagnosis
authors: I. Sherif Baig, K. Vivekanandan
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Oral cancer now represents a major worldwide public health threat. Cell mutations in the lips or mouth cause oral cancer. The prevalence and incidence frequencies of oral cancer are rising globally, despite progress in research and therapy. There has been no substantial improvement recently in the recognition of oral cancer, leaving it a long-standing challenge in biomedicine. In oncology, artificial intelligence has made remarkable achievements in recent years. Computer-aided diagnostic systems are enhanced by using machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) when provided with larger training datasets. This article presents a Histopathology Image-Based Oral Cancer Detection Framework Using a Transformer and a Temporal Convolutional Network (HIOCD-TTCN). The aim is to support pathologists in the early, accurate detection and treatment of oral cancer. Initially, image pre-processing uses the Gaussian filter (GF). Furthermore, the multi-axis vision transformer (MaxVit) method is used to extract meaningful image features. For classification, a temporal convolutional network is employed to effectively learn complex representations from the extracted features. Finally, the chicken swarm optimisation algorithm (CSOA) is used to select the parameters. The comparison analysis of the HIOCD-TTCN methodology showed a superior accuracy of 96.30% compared to other models on the NDB-UFES dataset.

title: Fault Analysis and Improvement of the Load Reducer for Bolter Miner
authors: Xiaoxia Liu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: The load reducer of a bolter miner is characterized by high load-bearing capacity, strong reliability, and complex internal transmission and external structures. However, factors such as load shocks, harsh underground operating conditions, and difficult maintenance severely affect its reliable operation. This paper addresses issues including static seal oil leakage, dynamic seal oil leakage, spline gear rolling, torque shaft fracture, and misalignment between input and output shafts caused by deformation of the motor connecting plate. Comprehensive improvement measures are proposed, focusing on material selection, heat treatment processes, manufacturing and assembly, as well as maintenance of the internal components of the load reducer, aiming to enhance its reliability and service life. Through loading tests on a transmission test bench and underground industrial trials, the precisely designed load reducer has demonstrated adaptability to impact and heavy-load conditions, meeting all performance requirements for the bolter miner.

title: Defect Detection of Photovoltaic Cells Based on Improved YOLO11n
authors: Zijian Zhou, Jiaojie Li, Shiyuan Yu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To address the challenges of low-contrast, tiny, and slender defects in photovoltaic electroluminescence (EL) images, this paper presents an improved lightweight model based on YOLO11n. Traditional detection methods often suffer from severe feature information loss during feature fusion and insufficient localization accuracy for slender micro-defects, leading to frequent missed and false detections. To alleviate these issues, the content-aware dynamic upsampling (DySample) module is introduced to enhance multi-scale feature fusion and preserve fine-grained details of tiny defects. Meanwhile, the Focaler-Shape-IoU loss is adopted to optimize bounding box regression and improve localization accuracy for irregular and slender defects. Experimental results on the PVEL-AD dataset show that the proposed model achieves 92.6% mAP@0.5 and 71.4% mAP@0.5:0.95, which are 1.2% and 0.8% higher than the baseline YOLO11n, respectively, with only a slight increase of 0.01M in parameters and unchanged GFLOPs. The proposed model effectively improves detection accuracy while maintaining high efficiency.

title: Correct Wrong Path Simulation
authors: Chrysanthos Pepi, Krishnam Tibrewala, Bhargav Reddy Godala, Sankara Prasad Ramesh, Alberto Ros, Daniel A. Jiménez, Gilles A. Pokam, Paul V. Gratz
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: Modern OoO CPUs employ deep pipelines with high branch misprediction recovery penalties. Instructions speculatively executed along mispredicted paths can significantly alter microarchitectural state. During design space exploration, architects often rely on trace-driven simulators, which are significantly faster than execution-driven models but trade accuracy for speed. Despite this benefit, trace-driven simulation often fails to adequately model the effects of wrong-path execution because traces are typically collected only from the correct-path. While prior work can accurately model wrong-path effects on the instruction stream, it often makes unrealistic assumptions when modeling the impact on the data stream. In this work, we examine the effects of wrong-path execution and present an infrastructure for enabling its modeling in a tracedriven simulator. Our analysis shows that wrong-path execution extensively affects structures on both the instruction and data sides, yielding performance variations ranging from $-3.6 \%$ to $85.7 \%$ compared to a baseline that ignores these effects. To benefit the research community and enhance the accuracy of simulators, we provide our traces and tracing utility. We aim for this to encourage industry to provide wrong-path traces generated by internal simulators, enabling fast academic research without exposing industry proprietary IP.

title: LightAD: A Lightweight Vision-Based Autonomous Driving System
authors: Yunge Li, Zhaodong Zhou, Shaibal Saha, Ali Irshayyid, Keer Chen, Lanyu Xu, Jun Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: Autonomous driving systems deployed in the real world suffer from excessive hardware redundancy and prohibitive deployment barriers. To address these challenges, LightAD is proposed as a lightweight, vision-based autonomous driving system framework tailored for simple, low-speed environments such as confined work zones and low-speed logistics. Diverging from industrial-grade solutions that rely on costly proprietary sensors and high-performance computing, LightAD implements system-level simplification. By leveraging consumer-grade hardware and lightweight algorithms for perception, planning, and control, LightAD significantly reduces costs and integration complexity while satisfying necessary autonomous driving functionality. Field trials at the Intelligent Ground Vehicle Competition on a physical vehicle confirmed the feasibility of LightAD and revealed practical implementation lessons. These results indicate that by combining low-cost general-purpose hardware with lightweight algorithms, LightAD effectively supports fundamental autonomous driving functionality in simple, well-defined, and low-speed environments, offering a feasible engineering pathway for cost-effective deployment of autonomous driving.

title: Evaluating the Risk of Storing and Transporting the Hydrogen Energy in Oman Using Multi-Criteria Decision Making
authors: Sara Al Hosni, Sina Sayardoost Tabrizi, Reza Babazadeh, Mehdi A. Kamran
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Hydrogen energy is widely recognized as a key component of the global transition toward low-carbon energy systems; however, its large-scale deployment remains constrained by safety risks associated with storage and transportation. These risks are particularly critical in high-temperature regions such as the Sultanate of Oman, which is emerging as a major hydrogen producer. Despite growing interest in hydrogen safety, existing studies largely focus on technical failures while overlooking the interdependent and systemic nature of risk factors. To address this gap, this study proposes a hybrid multi-criteria decision-making approach based on the integration of the Decision-Making Trial and Evaluation Laboratory (DEMATEL) and the Analytic Network Process (ANP) to evaluate the Risk of Storing and Transporting Hydrogen (RSTH) in Oman. The proposed framework models causal relationships among five major risk dimensions (personnel, storage, transportation, environmental, and management risks) using expert-based assessments. The results reveal that management-related factors, particularly the effectiveness of feedback mechanisms, dynamic monitoring, and standardized management, act as primary causal drivers within the RSTH system. These factors exert significant influence on downstream technical, human, and environmental risks, indicating that many technical failures are consequences rather than root causes of risk. The findings highlight the critical role of integrated management strategies in reducing hydrogen-related risks and demonstrate that improving management structures can lead to a systemic reduction in overall risk levels.

title: Traffic Sign Detection Using CNN in Adverse Imaging Environments
authors: Prathibha R, Yogaraja G S R, Amrutha N, Bhavya Shree D, Charitha N R, Harika K S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Traffic sign detection plays a very important role in modern transportation technologies, especially in systems designed for autonomous driving and driver assistance. These signs provide timely guidance that helps vehicles make safe and informed decisions on the road. Earlier recognition methods relied heavily on manually designed features, which often struggled when signs appeared blurred, partially hidden, or affected by changes in lighting or weather. In this work, a CNN-based architecture is proposed for the detection and classification of traffic signs using the Indian Traffic Sign Computer Vision dataset. The results indicate that CNN-based detection systems are well-suited for real-world deployment in driver assistance systems and autonomous driving, contributing to enhanced road safety and efficient traffic management.

title: Intelligent IoT-Driven Predictive Indoor Air Quality Management Framework for Educational Environments using Multi-Sensor Integration and Cloud-based Machine Learning Analytics
authors: Sarmisha Mary G, Shifani J, Hemalatha R
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The deterioration of the indoor air quality (IAQ) in schools is a major causative health problem and interferes with the efficiency of learning. The paper will describe an IoTbased IAQ management system based on the ESP32 to monitor in real-time, where the MQ2, MQ7, and MQ135 sensors are required to identify the presence of smoke, carbon monoxide, and dangerous gases. The sensor data is sent to a cloud solution where MATLAB is used to analyses the sensor data and machine learning models predict the trends of IAQ and preempt anomalies. The system makes automatic warning and suggestions to assure the best air quality, which allows effective prompt interventions. Complex analytics are used to detect patterns, correlations and source of pollutants enabling administrators to adopt specific action strategies. The experimental validation proves to be very accurate to detect pollutants and predict them. This open-source framework is scalable and provides real-time, intelligent decision support that can result into healthier school environments and provide the future basis of school IAQ optimization.

title: Performance Evaluation of Reliability Indices in Islanded Microgrids Under Renewable Uncertainty
authors: Ali Q. Al-Shetwi
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This paper presents a reliability assessment of a standalone hybrid microgrid designed to supply a 5 MW community load using photovoltaic (PV), wind turbine, battery energy storage system (BESS), and a backup diesel generator. The system operates in off-grid mode, requiring high reliability to ensure uninterrupted supply. Six key reliability indices are evaluated: Loss of Load Probability (LOLP), Loss of Load Expectation (LOLE), Expected Energy Not Supplied (EENS), System Average Interruption Duration Index (SAIDI), System Average Interruption Frequency Index (SAIFI), and Customer Average Interruption Duration Index (CAIDI). A baseline system is first analyzed without optimization, followed by comparative reliability improvement using three metaheuristic algorithms: Genetic Algorithm (GA), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), and Grey Wolf Optimizer (GWO). Simulation results show that optimization significantly reduces unserved energy, with EENS decreasing from 420 MWh/year in the baseline case to 95 MWh/year under GWO. Similarly, LOLE improves from 62 hours/year to 14 hours/year and both SAIDI and SAIFI are noticeably reduced, reflecting a lower impact on customers. The results highlight the potential of metaheuristic approaches in enhancing the reliability of off-grid hybrid microgrids for rural and remote applications.

title: A Multi-Level Debiasing Framework for Mitigating Cultural Bias in Reviews Sentiment Analysis
authors: Bhavya Sree S, Lena Merin Viju, Hemanth K S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Online review sentiment analysis systems are common platforms used to derive insights out of online reviews, but commonly, they are biased in culture because of language variation in linguistic expression, intensity of emotion and rating behavior is also varied between different geographical areas. Biases may cause biased sentiment predictions especially the underrepresented cultural groups. In an attempt to reduce cultural bias in airlines review sentiment classification, the Multi-level Debiasing Framework presented in this paper combines interventions on the data, model, and prediction levels. We first compare the performance of baseline classifiers such as Logistic Regression, Linear Support Vector Machine, and MultinomialNaive Bayes using Indian domestic airline review data and Western airline review data, and measure cultural bias by using measures of boundary probability (BP), true positive rate (TPR) gap, and false positive rate (FPR) gap. The findings of this experiment indicates that there are significant differences in favor of the Western reviews in the scenario of baseline models. The suggested unbiased framework integrates culturally balanced sampling of data, linguistic tailored enhancement, sample weighting, and culture-sensitive probability calibration. It was proven that the offered method helps to decrease BP by more than 90 per cent and significantly decrease the difference in TPR and FPR and retain comparable classification rates. Culturally consistent sentiment prediction across groups is also confirmed by qualitative analysis. The findings demonstrate the usefulness of multi-level mitigation in constructing just and culturally resilient sentiment analysis models and provide useful implications on utilizing equitable NLP solutions in an international setting.

title: ChaosRetina: Chaos-theory Based Feature Extraction for Multi-Label Retinal Disease Classification
authors: Sindhu P Menon, Subash S, S A Shanjith, Harshith K N, Yashas P
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The early-stage retinal diseases usually manifest in subtle and hard-to-recognize visual patterns, which are challenging for even the experienced clinicians to identify successfully. Despite the success of CNNs in automatic retinal image analysis, they struggle to recognize faint and irregular pathological cues, especially in a multi-label and heavily imbalanced context. Contribution: This paper introduces ChaosRetina, a two-stage deep learning architecture which incorporates chaos theory–inspired feature transformation speaker identification module into traditional CNN architectures. The proposed ChaosFEX constructs deep features by using iterative logistic map dynamics allowing complex minuscule details to be acknowledged after being suppressed by traditional activations. Experiments on the RFMiD multi-disease fundus dataset show that the proposed method achieves AUROC of 0.957 in binary disease detection and macro-AUROC of 0.869 in multi-label classification, outperforming multiple CNN based baseline methods. Extensive ablation studies show consistent improvement from ChaosFEX, especially for rare diseases. The findings indicate that chaos-based nonlinear transforms can be effective combined with deep learning representations for medical image analysis.

title: LaViCoN: Language and Vision Cooperative Navigation for Assistive Mobile Robots
authors: Tony Yang, Ren Zhong, Weisong Shi
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: This paper presents LaViCoN: a lightweight modular framework that enables indoor robots to perform real-time navigation from natural language commands such as "go to the door" or "go around that chair." The system focuses on navigation toward reference items or objects indoors and heavily prioritizes real-world deployment. LaViCoN consists of three main modules: natural language understanding (NLU), visual perception, and robot action. Since large end-to-end vision-language models require massive amounts of training data and are essentially black boxes, LaViCoN focuses on breaking down the task into interpretable parts for a relatively lightweight deployment. To enable NLU, a custom-modified BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) architecture designed for slot-filling is fine-tuned on a dataset that is synthetically created in this work by using GPT-4o, achieving a performance of 93% semantic frame accuracy. To allow the robot to visually perceive its environment, the system uses YOLOv8 object detection. The robot action module performs specific movements (approaching objects, avoiding obstacles, navigating between objects, etc.) through mapping the different slot values received from the NLU module to objects detected by the visual perception module. Lastly, LaViCoN is tested on a physical vehicle indoors with 20 novel indoor navigation tasks, achieving an average of 87% task success rate. The results show that a resource-efficient system like LaViCoN can be practical and achieve competitive performance in the real world, which offers an alternative to solutions that require vast resources or data.

title: Service Orchestration in the Computing Continuum: Structural Challenges and Vision
authors: Boris Sedlak, Víctor Casamayor Pujol, Ildefons Magrans de Abril, Praveen Kumar Donta, Adel N. Toosi, Schahram Dustdar
abstract: The computing continuum (CC) integrates different layers of processing infrastructure, from edge to cloud, to optimize service quality through ubiquitous and reliable computation. Compared to central architectures, however, heterogeneous and dynamic infrastructure increases the complexity for service orchestration. To guide research, this article first summarizes structural problems of the CC, and then, envisions an ideal solution for autonomous service orchestration across the CC. As one instantiation, we show how active inference—a concept from neuroscience—can support self-organizing services in continuously interpreting their environment to optimize service quality. Still, we conclude that no existing solution achieves our vision, but that research on service orchestration faces several structural challenges. Most notably: to provide standardized simulation and evaluation environments for comparing the performance of orchestration mechanisms. Together, the challenges outline a research roadmap toward resilient and scalable service orchestration in the CC.

title: AI in the Antenna Design Life Cycle: An AI-Assisted Systematic Review
authors: Ike Mafa, Simon Winberg
abstract: The increasing integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in antenna design presents opportunities for optimizing the design process. This paper presents a comprehensive AI-assisted systematic review of the use of AI in the antenna design life cycle. A Systematic Mapping Study (SMS) is first employed to map the landscape of AI applications in antenna design, identifying key research areas, trends, and gaps. The SMS categorizes studies into three primary domains: AI for Optimization, AI for Synthesis, and AI for Performance Prediction. In the second phase, a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) is conducted to critically evaluate and synthesize empirical findings from the selected studies. The SLR examines the various AI techniques used in each step of the lifecycle. The results from both methods provide a comprehensive overview of AI’s current applications in the field, highlighting key challenges and opportunities for future research. This hybrid approach, combining AI-assisted SMS and SLR, offers both a broad mapping of AI applications and a deep, critical analysis of their real-world effectiveness, making it a valuable contribution to the field of AI-enhanced antenna design and AI-assisted systematic reviews.

title: AI -Driven Cloud Storage Latency Reduction
authors: Kshama L, Rida Fathima, Varsha S, Dhanyashree, Manjula L, Pratyaksha S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: systems support data-intensive applications such as real-time analytics, multimedia streaming, and IoT platforms. Despite advances in cloud infrastructure, latency remains a major challenge, particularly under dynamic workloads where traditional static and rule-based optimization techniques fall short. This paper proposes an AI-driven framework for cloud storage optimization that integrates real-time monitoring, predictive analytics, and intelligent resource management. Machine learning models analyze latency metrics to detect performance trends, predict congestion, and enable proactive optimization. Experimental evaluation using simulated workloads demonstrates a latency reduction of up to 28% during peak periods, along with improved scalability and system stability. These results highlight the potential of AI-based approaches to transform cloud storage management into a proactive and self-optimizing process.

title: Hybrid CNN Architectures for Bengali Pitha Recognition: A Deep Learning Approach
authors: Dewan Rita Faria Prachi, Pabon Shaha, Muhammad Minoar Hossain, Yadab Sutradhar, Md. Moradul Siddique, Md. Sadiq Iqbal
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The Bangla terms pitha, pithe, and peetha refer to traditional native foods of Bangladesh and certain regions of India, particularly in areas where Bangla is the predominant language. Pithas hold a significant place in Bengali and Bangladeshi culture, encompassing a diverse range of traditional delicacies. Accurately classifying different types of pitha is essential for advancing the food industry and preserving cultural heritage. The goal of this research is to create an effective model for image-based classification of traditional foods, specifically focusing on various types of pitha. With the rapid progress of deep learning, remarkable advancements have been achieved in computer vision and related fields. Building on this, the present work provides a comprehensive evaluation of CNN-based techniques for recognizing and classifying pitha images.In this study, several deep learning architectures—ResNet50, DenseNet121, InceptionV3, and MobileNetV2—were implemented alongside two hybrid combinations: MobileNetV2 + DenseNet121 and InceptionV3 + DenseNet121. The hybrid model achieved a robust performance with an accuracy of 97.00% across diverse testing scenarios, underscoring its potential for real-world applications. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of CNN-based methods in food image classification, contributing to both technological innovation and the preservation of cultural heritage. Furthermore, this research supports future developments in educational tools, automated food labeling systems, and digital food preservation initiatives.

title: MEMS-Based Gas Sensing via Broadband Dielectric Relaxation Spectroscopy
authors: Satish K. Verma, Mahesh Menon, Aravind N. Warrier, Andreas Mrosk, Markus Gardill, Harald Schenk
abstract: Reliable and selective gas sensing demands a low-cost, energy-efficient, single-unit multi-gas sensor robust to ambient condition variations. This paper presents a novel broadband dielectric spectroscopy-based gas sensing platform. The sensor leverages strong localized electric fields (> 106 V/m) across microscale gaps under a hybrid bias (0.5 V ac + 5 V dc), inducing polarization within the gas-domain at ambient temperature. It employs MEMS based parallel comb electrodes for the detection and characterization of gas-phase analytes, demonstrated here with acetone vapor. An acetone specific loss peak at ∼350 Hz, attributed to gas-phase dielectric relaxation, scales with acetone concentration with sensitivity of 33.7m/ppm. This mechanism is promising for a miniaturized, low power, label-free, frequency-resolved dielectric sensing framework, scalable for multi-analyte, CMOS-integrated, chip-scale sensor systems.

title: Lightweight Pause Detector-Based ECG Sensing Device for Intelligent Cardiac Health Monitoring
authors: Achinta Mondal, M. Sabarimalai Manikandan, Ram Bilas Pachori
abstract: Sick sinus syndrome and heart blocks are types of heart rhythm disorders (arrhythmia) that are characterized by anirregular rhythm associated with pauses in the electrocardiogram (ECG) signal. The pauses in the ECG signal indicate a delay or complete interruption of electrical impulses from the atria to the ventricles, associated with life-threatening cardiac arrhythmias, with the severity and specific ECG patterns. Therefore, in this letter, we propose a lightweight automatic pause detector-based ECG sensing device by considering the resource constraints of affordable wearable cardiac health monitoring devices. The proposed pause detector consists of preprocessing, bipolar sequence addition, zero-crossing rate, and a decision rule that is integrated with a resource-constrained ECG computing platform. The proposed system achieved a sensitivity of 100% and a specificity of 99.75% across a wide variety of ECG morphologies, including local interval segments and beat-to-beat intervals. Further, evaluation results demonstrated that the pause detector can reduce the false R-peak detection rate by 86.06%-96.19%, leading to reliable R-peak detection without a search-back algorithm. On the Arduino Due (ARM Cortex-M3 CPU, 84 MHz clock, 96 kB SRAM, 512 kB flash) platform, interfacing with the ECG sensor, Bluetooth, and a Smartphone, it consumes energy of 58.85 mJ with a latency of 121 ms for processing a 9 s ECG signal. The proposed pause detector can be integrated with a resource-constrained ECG sensing device to detect the sinus node dysfunctions and heart blocks, and also improves the accuracy and reliability of R-peak detectors.

title: The Flashover characteristics and arc development patterns of train roof insulator under ultra - high altitude
authors: Yujun Guo, Ningning Su, Limei Hong, Ye Cao, Chuan Zhang, Xueqin Zhang, Guoqiang Gao, Guangning Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: With the continuous expansion of railway networks, trains are increasingly operating in extreme environments exceeding 4000 m in altitude, where roof insulators face the challenge of significantly reduced air pressure. Therefore, the flashover characteristics and arc propagation behavior of roof insulators are investigated under ultra - high altitude conditions based on tests in an artificial climate chamber. First, flashover tests were performed on four types of roof insulators with different shed structures under varying air pressure and contamination levels. Subsequently, the effects of different shed structures on flashover voltage and arc development under low - pressure conditions were analyzed. Last, the effective utilization rate of creepage distance, denoted as kE, is proposed to quantify the influence of the arc - floatation effect in ultra - high altitude environments. It is indicated that the flashover voltage of roof insulators decreases more significantly under ultra - high altitude conditions, as air pressure characteristics impact index n exhibits an initial increase followed by a decrease when the equivalent salt deposit density (ESDD) increases. The effective utilization rate of creepage distance kE decreases following a power-law trend with reducing pressure. If the creepage distance is sufficient, appropriately increasing the shed spacing and large shed diameter can mitigate the impact of the arc - floatation effect, improving insulation performance in ultra - high altitude environments. Conclusion above can provide valuable references for the design and performance evaluation of roof insulators intended for use in such demanding conditions.

title: CoDA: Color Distribution Probing for Efficient and Generalizable AI-Generated Image Detection
authors: Zexi Jia, Zhiqiang Yuan, Xiaoyue Duan, Jinchao Zhang, Jie Zhou, Anil K. Jain
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: AI-generated image detection faces a persistent trade-off between generalization and efficiency: lightweight artifact-based methods often degrade on unseen generators or domains, whereas more robust large-scale models are computationally expensive. Meanwhile, existing benchmarks mainly focus on cross-model evaluation in photorealistic settings, leaving cross-domain robustness underexplored. To address this gap, we introduce FakeForm, a large-scale benchmark with approximately 370,000 images across 62 diverse domains for both cross-model and cross-domain evaluation. Motivated by this broader setting, we revisit color-distribution probing as an efficient complementary cue for AI-generated image detection. We observe that, especially for photographic content, real photographs tend to exhibit smoother and more stable color patterns, whereas synthetic images often show characteristic color imbalances introduced by neural generation. Based on this observation, we propose CoDA, a compact 1.48M-parameter detector built on a Noise-Quantization Probe, together with a theoretical analysis linking probe responses to color non-uniformity. Experiments show that CoDA achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard benchmarks and the best results on the challenging cross-domain evaluation of FakeForm, while remaining highly competitive in cross-model photorealistic settings. These results suggest that persistent generative artifacts can provide a practical foundation for efficient and robust AI-generated image detection. The models and FakeForm benchmark will be made publicly available.

title: MMA++: Effective Multi-Modal Adaptation for Vision-Language Models_supp1-3691448.pdf
authors: Xiaohua Xie
abstract: Large scale pre-trained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have shown good generalization capabilities across diverse downstream tasks. However, adapting such large-scale models to few-shot generalization scenarios remains challenging due to the trade-off between preserving general knowledge and incorporating task-specific information. In this paper, we propose MMA++, an advanced and effective Multi-Modal Adapter framework for parameter-efficient VLM adaptation. Unlike prior works that independently inject adapters into each modality or uniformly across layers, MMA++ performs a dataset-level analysis to identify discriminative and generalizable features, and selectively applies adapters to the higher layers of both vision and text encoders. To bridge the modality gap, we further propose a shared feature projection space that enhances alignment between modalities. Beyond architecture design, we identify the fusion scale $\alpha$—which controls the strength of adapter integration—as a key factor in few-shot generalization. We empirically and theoretically demonstrate that $\alpha$ should not be static, but adapted based on training data size. To reduce the effort of tuning this value across different datasets, we propose the $\alpha$-consistency framework, consisting of: (1) a consistency training strategy under varying fusion scales; and (2) an $\alpha$-decoupling strategy that uses a larger fusion scale during training and a smaller one at inference to account for sample size mismatch. We evaluate MMA++ on a wide range of few-shot generalization tasks, including base-to-novel generalization, cross-dataset transfer, and domain generalization. Our method consistently achieves leading performance.

title: Beamforming Algorithm and System Optimization Based on True-Time-Delay and Phase Cooperative Control for Broadband Optically Controlled Phased Arrays
authors: Ting An, Limin Liu, Yunfeng Jiang, Yunjuan Zhang
abstract: To address the contradiction between hardware non-idealities and broadband beam squint effects in microwave photonic beamforming systems, this paper proposes a beamforming algorithm framework based on true-time-delay and phase cooperative control. In this paper, we decouple and analyze the discrete tuning characteristics of optical true-time delay lines and the continuous phase tuning capability of electro-optic phase modulators, a mapping model from device constraints to system performance is established. A three-layer algorithm architecture is proposed, including discrete true-time delay solving, improved delay-and-sum algorithm, and robust MVDR algorithm. On this basis, a true-time-delay and phase cooperative control strategy is constructed, utilizing true-time delay to eliminate beam squint and electro-optic phase modulator to compensate for time-delay quantization residual errors. Simulation results show that the cooperative control reduces the beam pointing error from 0.86° to 0.06°, suppresses the sidelobe level to −31.5 dB, and achieves a squint coefficient lower than 0.01 ° /GHz. This study provides theoretical and algorithmic support for high-precision, low sidelobe, wide-angle scanning of broadband optically controlled phased array systems.

title: Optical Remote Sensing Image Super-Resolution via Adaptive High-Pass Filtering and Window Attention Mechanism
authors: Qikai Zhou, Baoxiang Huang, He Gao, Milena Radenkovic, Ge Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Remote sensing image super-resolution (SR) aims to enhance image resolution, critical for various applications. Conventional convolutional neural networks (CNNs) often struggle to recover fine textures and sharp edges, and are limited in adaptively combining local and global features. To address this, we propose Adaptive High-Pass Spatial Gate Transformer Network (AHGTNet), a novel cascade network for optical remote sensing image SR. AHGTNet integrates an Adaptive High-Pass Enhancement Block (AHEB) and a Residual Gate Window Attention Blocks (RGWB). The AHEB dynamically extracts and emphasizes high-frequency features, crucial for detailed textures and edges, by generating adaptive filtering weights and applying grouped high-pass operations. Subsequently, the RGWB efficiently models both local details and long-range dependencies, facilitating a comprehensive integration of contextual features. Extensive experiments on three optical remote sensing datasets demonstrate AHGTNet's effectiveness. Compared with state-of-the-art methods, our model consistently achieves competitive, and often leading, reconstruction quality in terms of PSNR and SSIM. Furthermore, AHGTNet shows favorable performance on real-world SR tasks, adeptly recovering detailed textures while preserving overall scene coherence.

title: Innovation-Domain Decision-Directed Phase Tracking for Wiener Phase Noise in Fast Rayleigh Fading
authors: Ura Klongklaew, Nithiroth Pornsuwancharoen, Phichai Youplao
abstract: This letter proposes an innovation-domain decision-directed phase tracking (ID-DDPT) architecture for coherent detection over Rayleigh fading channels with temporally correlated phase evolution and Wiener phase noise. By reformulating phase tracking into the innovation domain, replacing the unbounded cumulative phase with its stationary increments, the proposed method converts a non-stationary estimation problem into a stable low-complexity filtering problem. A closed-form expression for the steady-state residual phase error variance is derived under the locked-regime assumption, along with an analytical optimal smoothing factor. Modeling the residual phase distortion as an effective signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) attenuation yields a tractable bit error rate (BER) approximation for BPSK over Rayleigh fading. A first-order error-propagation analysis further characterizes the impact of decision errors and provides insight into the onset of cycle slips. Simulation results demonstrate that ID-DDPT outperforms DBPSK and a complexity-equivalent scalar Kalman tracker (SKT), achieving near-coherent performance with O(1) per-symbol complexity and minimal pilot overhead.

title: Ultra-low-power Monostatic Backscatter Platform with Phase-Aware Channel Estimation and System-Level Validation
authors: Hanyeol Ryu, Sangkil Kim
abstract: This paper presents a novel phase-aware channel-estimation (CE) method and an experimentally validated hardware–software platform for an ultra-low-power monostatic backscatter link. Conventional phase-stationary least squares (LS) and linear minimum mean square error (LMMSE) estimators incur deterministic bias when residual phase-slope is present across pilot symbols, a backscatter-specific impairment that is particularly consequential in monostatic configurations. To address this limitation, this paper introduces a lifted pilot model augmented with a linearized residual-phase-slope regressor, yielding closed-form phase-aware LS and LMMSE estimators with minimal additional computation. A double-Rician fading model characterizes the cascaded forward and backscatter hops, and a resource-optimal pilot allocation rule is analytically derived to balance CE accuracy against payload efficiency. The prototype integrates a semi-passive tag, a software-defined radio (SDR) reader, and a 2×1 planar Yagi–Uda array providing 7 dBi gain and over 30 dB isolation at 2.4–2.5 GHz. The receiver combines pilot-aided carrier frequency offset (CFO) compensation, the proposed lifted estimators, and zero-forcing (ZF) equalization for symbol recovery over the cascaded backscatter channel. It achieves 500 kbps at 1 m, with tag-side power consumption of 10 μW for the radio-frequency (RF) switch and 11.2 mW including the general-purpose microcontroller unit (MCU), corresponding to 20 pJ/bit and 22.4 nJ/bit, respectively. Measured error vector magnitudes (EVMs) are 2.97% for on-off keying (OOK) and 4.02% for binary phase-shift keying (BPSK). Bit error rate (BER) measurements and over-the-air (OTA) full-color image reconstruction confirm reliable multimedia-capable operation and provide practical codesign guidance for future Internet of Things (IoT) deployments.

title: Structure from Noise: Confirmation Bias in Particle Picking in Structural Biology
authors: Amnon Balanov, Alon Zabatani, Tamir Bendory
abstract: The computational pipelines of single-particle cryo-electron microscopy (cryo-EM) and cryo-electron tomography (cryo-ET) include an early particle-picking stage, in which a micrograph or tomogram is scanned to extract candidate particles, typically via template matching or deep-learning-based techniques. The extracted particles are then passed to downstream tasks such as classification and 3D reconstruction. Although it is well understood empirically that particle picking can be sensitive to the choice of templates or learned priors, a quantitative theory of the bias introduced by this stage has been lacking. Here, we develop a mathematical framework for analyzing bias in template matching-based detection with concrete applications to cryo-EM and cryo-ET. We study this bias through two downstream tasks: (i) maximum-likelihood estimation of class means in a Gaussian mixture model and (ii) 3D volume reconstruction from the extracted particle stack. We show that when template matching is applied to pure noise, then under broad noise models, the resulting maximum-likelihood estimates converge asymptotically to deterministic, noise-dependent transforms of the user-specified templates, yielding a structure from noise effect. We further characterize how the resulting bias depends on the noise statistics, sample size, dimension, and detection threshold. Finally, controlled experiments using standard cryo-EM software corroborate the theory, demonstrating reproducible structure from noise artifacts in low-SNR data.

title: Stepwise Dynamic Parameter Identification of Serial Robots with a Nonlinear Friction Model
authors: Haonan Ma, Kun Chen, Peng Xu, Bing Li
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Dynamic parameter identification is essential for achieving high-performance robot control. Conventional identification methods are mostly based on linear friction models and therefore cannot accurately capture joint friction characteristics. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes a stepwise dynamic parameter identification method. To enhance the accuracy of friction modeling, the sign function is replaced with an arctangent function, an exponential velocity term is introduced to describe viscous friction, and two additional coefficients are incorporated to characterize the Stribeck effect of Coulomb and static friction. To reduce identification errors, a reciprocating S-curve trajectory is employed for the preidentification of nonlinear friction parameters. An iterative weighted estimator that can accommodate the nonlinear friction model is then developed, and the pre-identified results are used as its initial inputs. Experiments on a UR16e robot demonstrate that the proposed method improves joint torque prediction accuracy by nearly 22.35% compared with conventional methods.

title: Smart Disease Prediction and Medicine Recommendation System
authors: Ushashree R, Aishwarya M, Deepthi, Hamsaveni D, Jayanth Kumar P N, Akash Kamath
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The healthcare sector increasingly depends on technological advancements to provide rapid and reliable assistance for individuals facing medical issues. Central to this transformation is the Intelligent Disease Prediction and Medicine Recommendation System a platform where users can enter their symptoms through an easy-to-use web interface to identify potential health conditions. Utilizing the Random Forest algorithm, which is particularly effective in analyzing intricate symptom patterns and producing accurate predictions, it is trained on curated datasets that encompass diseases, related symptoms, standard treatments, preventive measures, dietary advice, and lifestyle modifications. Users can input their symptoms, and within moments, the system generates a primary diagnosis along with customized recommendations regarding medications, precautions, dietary practices, and daily routines to enhance self-management and awareness. Developed using Python, Flask, and publicly available machine learning libraries, it operates seamlessly in any web browser, ensuring affordability and simplicity without the need for complicated installations. Acknowledging its limitations, the system consistently indicates that its outputs are to be considered as preliminary guidance only, emphasizing the importance of consulting healthcare professionals to guarantee accuracy and safety. Initiatives like this highlight the potential of machine learning in facilitating early health assessments and recommendations, particularly in underserved areas, while also incorporating safeguards against oversights such as individual patient characteristics or rare diseases.

title: Thermoelectric Energy Generators for Harnessing Excess Thermal Energy: Thermoelectric Energy Sources for Sustainable Usage
authors: Varun Akella, Amogh Basker, Camryn Evans, Kyuri Lim, Abigail Mokrzycki, Dhruhi Patel, Victoria Rough, Gideon Sun, Paul Belony
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Sustainable thermoelectric energy generation has the prospect of addressing current and future energy crises by utilizing the waste heat emitted by everyday appliances and equipment. The vast majority of literature has been directed towards semiconductor doping to achieve high figures of thermoelectric merit (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Z T$</tex>) for efficient energy conversion. By contrast, there is far less research on practical thermoelectric systems that are affordable, especially for underprivileged communities that cannot afford costly-yet-efficient devices such as solar panels. To address this, we present an alternative energy device employing the Seebeck and Peltier effects. Using thermoelectric generator modules (TEGs) configured as a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times 3$</tex> array of nine thermoelectric coolers (TECs) set between two <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15.24 ~\text{cm} \times 15.24 ~\text{cm}$</tex> copper plates (overall module dimensions are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15.4 ~\text{cm} \times 15.4 ~\text{cm} \times$</tex> 15.0 cm, and temperature differences between hot and cold tap water, we achieved a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \mathrm{T}=66 ~\mathrm{K}$</tex> and a peak open-circuit voltage of 3.5 V across the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.0238 ~\mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> module surface area. This work offers insights into the design and development of this low-cost TEG and its promise for improving household energy efficiency in underpriveledged communities.

title: Orientation–Frequency Fields and Periodic Prior Reranking for Robust Cross-Modal Tire Indentation Retrieval
authors: Xiaosong Hu, Ziqi Sun, Ying Liu, Zhijie Xu, Yushan Pan, Nan Xiang, Weidong Zhang, Yu Hao
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Cross-modal tire indentation retrieval refers to the task of retrieving corresponding tire surface images from a gallery using indentation images as queries. This task is inherently challenging due to factors such as low contrast, directional misalignment, local deformation, and other environmental disturbances. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel framework that integrates representation learning with geometric priors to enhance matching robustness and accuracy. During training, Orientation-Frequency Fields (OFF) are employed to guide the backbone network in capturing discriminative features related to tread orientation and pitch. During inference, a Periodic Prior Reranking (PPR) module is introduced, which performs normalization, period estimation, cyclic crosscorrelation for phase alignment, and period-wise tokenization for structural consistency verification, with the results fused through similarity scoring. To improve practical applicability, a phase-correlation-based coarse filtering module is incorporated to reduce the candidate search space. In addition, feature and periodic caches are adopted to eliminate redundant computations during retrieval. Finally, mask fallback, de-duplication, and tail completion strategies are integrated to further enhance robustness under challenging real-world conditions. On the test dataset, the proposed method achieves a Hit@10 accuracy of 75.81, outperforming the baseline model by a margin of 5.59. These results demonstrate that the proposed framework provides an effective solution for cross-modal tire indentation retrieval.

title: Secured Cloud Data Storage using Blockchain
authors: A Bhuvanesh, Arpita Belure, Cheemala Lavanya Reddy, Yellati Jyothi, Bharathi M, Ambika S R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Cloud computing provides scalable on-demand resources but faces significant security challenges, including reliance on centralized third-party identity providers, data breaches, and single points of failure. To address these vulnerabilities, this paper proposes a Blockchain-based Secure Cloud Identity and Access Management (BSCIAM) framework that integrates decentralized identity verification with secure cloud data storage. The proposed architecture utilizes Ethereum smart contracts to manage user authentication and access control tokens, eliminating the need for centralized intermediaries. Furthermore, the system incorporates the Interplanetary File System (IPFS) for decentralized file storage, ensuring high data availability and integrity. A key feature of this framework is the implementation of client-side encryption using the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) 256-bit algorithm, which ensures that data is encrypted before transmission and storage, thereby preserving strict confidentiality. The system was implemented using the Sepolia test network for blockchain interactions and a React-based frontend for user operations. Experimental results and performance analysis demonstrate that the framework successfully mitigates centralized risks, optimizes gas consumption for smart contract execution, and achieves low latency in encrypted file retrieval, providing a robust solution for privacy-preserving cloud computing.

title: KubeOrch: Visual Template-Based Kubernetes Configuration Management
authors: Aruna S, Mohit Nagaraj, Kshitij N. Kulkarni, Rishiraj Chirchi, Naman B. Parlecha
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: KubeOrch is a visual platform that replaces manual YAML authoring for Kubernetes with a template-based, drag-and-drop workflow composed of four subsystems: a Visual Composer, a Template Engine, a Parameter Inference Engine, and a Validation Engine. A user study with 20 participants compared KubeOrch against raw YAML and Helm on five deployment tasks. KubeOrch reduced average completion time by 84.6% versus YAML, lowered configuration errors from 8.0 to 1.1 per session, achieved a 99.1% validation success rate, and reduced NASA-TLX cognitive load by 78.4%.

title: Research of FastSLAM Based on Butterfly Optimization Algorithm with Hybrid Strategies
authors: Shuai Yuan, Jiarui Liu, Haoyan Li, Zhaoqing Zhang
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Mechatronics, Control and Robotics (ICMCR)
abstract: To address the issue where the classical Fast SLAM algorithm utilizes a motion model as the proposal distribution, leading to a deviation of particle distribution from the actual posterior probability distribution, this paper proposes an improved FastSLAM algorithm combined with a hybrid-strategy based Butterfly Optimization Algorithm. First of all, the STC (Sine-Tent-Cosine) chaotic mapping is introduced into the initialization stage of the Butterfly Optimization Algorithm to enhance population diversity. In the next place, an adaptive inertia weight strategy is introduced in the global search phase to balance the global exploration and local exploitation capabilities of the algorithm. In order to prevent the algorithm from falling into local optima, an improved strategy based on Cauchy mutation and lens opposition-based learning(OBL) is introduced. Finally, the improved Butterfly Optimization Algorithm is utilized to handle the prediction step of FastSLAM, which optimizes the particle poses and generates the new proposal distribution. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm effectively enhances the accuracy of mobile robot localization and mapping, providing an efficient solution for FastSLAM research.

title: Semantic Preservation in LLM-Based Cultural Narrative Generation for Marketing Communication Contexts
authors: Pagon Gatchalee
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed to transform culturally embedded narratives into digital storytelling formats. While these systems demonstrate fluency and stylistic flexibility, less attention has been paid to how generative processes reshape narrative structure, symbolic specificity, and interpretive framing. This paper examines semantic preservation in LLM-based cultural narrative generation through a literaturebased thematic analysis of LLM-focused research spanning bias analysis, creativity modeling, narrative reconstruction, and knowledge-augmented heritage applications. The analysis identifies recurring patterns of structural compression, salience redistribution, pragmatic drift, symbolic attenuation, and circulation-driven stabilization. These patterns suggest that semantic preservation cannot be reduced to surface-level correctness or lexical similarity but instead reflects layered representational dynamics that operate across structural, interpretive, symbolic, and distributional contexts. Building on these findings, the study formalizes a semantic preservation evaluation framework designed to guide discourse-aware assessment, knowledge-augmented generation strategies, and culturally grounded benchmarking in LLM research. By repositioning semantic preservation as a structured evaluation problem, this work contributes a conceptual foundation for culturally robust generative modeling and discourse-aware assessment in marketing communication contexts involving culturally sensitive narrative transformation.

title: Analog In-Memory Computing for Large Language Model Inference: Opportunities and Challenges
authors: A. Vasilopoulos, H. Benmeziane, J. Büchel, W. Simon, A. Singh, I. Boybat, J. Luquin, P. Narayanan, H. Tsai, G. W. Burr, A. Rahimi, M. Le Gallo, V. Narayanan, A. Sebastian
abstract: Recent advancements in large language models (LLMs) have shifted the primary bottleneck of AI hardware from compute to memory capacity and data movement. Analog in-memory computing (AIMC) offers a promising path to address this challenge by enabling matrix-vector multiplication directly within memory arrays, significantly reducing data transfers associated with model weights. In this paper, we discuss the role of AIMC in LLM inference workloads from a holistic systems perspective. We analyze the architecture of modern LLMs and identify which operations are well-suited for AIMC. We further discuss key challenges and opportunities in memory technologies, algorithms, system architecture, and heterogeneous system composition that must be addressed to enable AIMC as a practical accelerator for future AI inference infrastructure.

title: Research on the Oscillation Mechanism of Induction Motor V/f System
authors: Min Wu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This paper investigates the three primary causes of instability in induction motor V/f control systems: the inherent unstable regions at medium-to-low frequencies, resonance within the DC-link filter, and the dead-time effect of the inverter. By establishing a mathematical model of the inverter-fed induction motor system, the study analyzes how various parameters-such as motor constants and DC-side inductance/capacitance-affect the system's inherent stability. Through impedance analysis, the paper clarifies the mechanism of oscillations triggered by energy exchange between the motor and the DC-link components. Additionally, it examines how error voltages and zero-current clamping caused by dead-time effects lead to discontinuous power supply and subsequent system oscillations. This research provides a theoretical foundation for improving the stability and performance of V/f speed control systems.

title: Intelligent Fruit Detection Using Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Manoj Kumar, Urmila Pilania, Roshi Saxena, Jai Gupta, Drish Bhalla
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Accurate fruit detection is a fundamental task in computer vision with significant applications in agriculture, retail automation, and assistive technologies. In the proposed work authors utilized Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model for fruit detection from the images. For training the CNN model Fruit-360 dataset from Kaggle has been utilized. The CNN technique is capable of retrieving deep feature and from these features it learns discriminative visual patterns. The model is capable of identifying and classifying fruit images into many categories. The dataset used contain verity of labeled images which are taken under controlled conditions. The proposed work is evaluated based on performance metrics including: accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score. With an accuracy of 74% on the test dataset, precision of 75.6%, and recall of 74%, experimental results confirm the effectiveness of the suggested model and are deemed adequate for real-world uses. The web app is also developed for the real-time detection and classification of fruit images. In future authors will integrate CNN model with neutral language processing for answering the questions of the users.

title: Communication System for the Deaf and Mute
authors: Divya Lingineni, Prasanna DRL, Revanth Kumar Addanki, SaiPraneetha Singarapu, Jaya Bharath Reddy
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The paper proposes a new and efficient full-duplex communication system that fills the gap between Deaf and Mute (D-M) and Non-Deaf and Mute (ND-M) people without the need for ND-M people to learn sign language. In contrast to other models that only support unidirectional translation or translation in a single language, this system allows for real-time bidirectional translation in multiple sign languages, namely Indian Sign Language (ISL) and American Sign Language (ASL). The system also supports Speech-to-ISL/ASL, Text-to-ISL/ASL, ASL- to-ISL, and ASL-to-Speech translation. The system is developed using Machine Learning (ML), Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), and Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to provide high accuracy in gesture recognition and context-aware translation. The system is cost-effective, scalable, and can be applied in various settings such as education, healthcare, and public service sectors. The system is modular and can be easily extended to incorporate other sign languages. Experimental evaluations show over 96% accuracy with real-time responsiveness. The system and codebase are openly available to encourage replicability and wider adoption, thereby promoting digital inclusivity for the D-M community. Future enhancements include dataset expansion, full-word recognition, and collaborative deployment with accessibility-focused organizations.

title: A Control Method for Electronic Expansion Valves Based on Superheat Variations and its Application in Cold Storage Temperature Control
authors: Shuai Ye, Shan Jiang, Mingxue Sun, Yahong Li
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Cold storage refrigeration systems inherently suffer from strong non-linearity, time-varying parameters, and significant response lag. Conventional PID controllers often react too slowly to these dynamics, leading to temperature overshoot and insufficient safety margins. Conversely, advanced model-based predictive controls (such as DMC) demand highly precise models and complex computations, limiting their practical engineering application. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a novel, rule-optimized predictive control strategy for electronic expansion valves (EEV). By forecasting the dynamic trends of evaporator superheat online, the controller preemptively adjusts the valve aperture before the superheat deviates from its setpoint. This predictive action is integrated with a multi-rule coordination mechanism that evaluates the current superheat state, cold store temperature deviations, and mechanical valve constraints. Experimental validation confirms that this method effectively prevents the superheat from dropping below safe thresholds while significantly mitigating fluctuations caused by inherent system delays. Compared to traditional feedback techniques, the proposed strategy accelerates the cooling response, enhances temperature stability, and offers superior engineering adaptability through its simplified, model-independent implementation[1]

title: Multi-level In-Memory Computing with 3D Flash Memory using Activation-Time-Modulated Sequential Multi-Block Access and Tightly Integrated Current Control Cell (CC Cell)
authors: Kana Kudo, Yuta Aiba, Kazuma Hasegawa, Xu Li, Yuichi Sano, Tomoya Sanuki
abstract: We show that multi-level approximate search can be performed by in-memory computing using 3D flash memory. In our previous work, we proposed the in-memory computing method using the current control cell (CC cell), which is fully compatible with conventional 3D flash memory, and demonstrated 1-bit and 128-dimensional approximate search with one cell per bit. In this work, we propose a novel approximate search method of activation-time-modulated sequential multi-block access with tightly integrated CC cell. (1) To enhance accuracy, we optimized the CC cell Vth distribution tightly and maintained normal Single Level Cell (SLC) operation in the data region. (2) To perform computation with multi-level data, we applied new key data placement. Assigning key values with different bit depths to each cell, we modulate the activation time according to the bit depths. The linearity between activation time and the number of multi block accesses allows bit depth representation. Suppressing the increase in storage capacity enables a large amount of source data to be stored as key vectors. We successfully demonstrated approximate search with sufficient accuracy using 3-bit and 128-dimensional data. Additionally, energy consumption is significantly reduced when compared to the normal TLC read operation, and no additional WL control circuit is required. This technology is a promising candidate for energy harvesting in-memory computing of multi-level data using conventional 3D flash memory.

title: Explainable AI Framework for Transparent and Fair Recruitment Decision-Making in Automated HR Systems
authors: Revati Ramrao Rautrao, M. Shailaja, N. Roopalatha, K. Kalaivani, Feras Almarashdi, M. Karthik
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Human Resource hiring using Artificial Intelligence has revolutionized the traditional hiring practices, one that has not only increased the effectiveness of candidate evaluation and the efficiency of candidate selection. Current literature where graph-based and deep learning models are used is characterized by fairness, explainability, and biases mitigation challenges, which often result in opaque decisions that are difficult to trust. The suggested HR-GNN (Human Resource Graph Neural Network), which takes advantage of graph neural networks, represents the connection among candidates, job descriptions, and skills with fairness restrictions and SHAP-based clarifying modules. The methodology is implemented in Python and builds on heterogeneous graphs, which are created and the results produced are fed to relational learning to enhance both prediction accuracy and transparency. HR-GNN has an accuracy of 92.5% and F1-score, precision, fairness, and explainability of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$91.9,0.92,0.72$</tex>, and 0.74 respectively, compared to traditional machine learning (78.5% accuracy) and deep learning (85.2% accuracy). The presented framework will allow HR professionals to make ethical, transparent, and biasconscience hiring choices to help them comply with the regulations, enhance trust in an organization, and enable the responsible use of AI-based recruitment systems in the actual HR setting.

title: Multi-Path Regulation of Regenerative Braking Energy for Electric Drive Tracked Vehicles
authors: Zifan Song, Jiangtao Gai, Yaoheng Li
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Under severe operating conditions such as highspeed emergency braking and sustained downhill braking, electric tracked vehicles are highly susceptible to thermal fade of the mechanical brakes caused by excessive frictional loading. Although regenerative braking can effectively alleviate the mechanical braking burden, the limited charging power capacity of the onboard battery pack prevents the regenerated electrical energy from being adequately absorbed and dissipated, readily leading to overvoltage on the high-voltage DC bus and consequently preventing the electric brake from fully exploiting its inherent braking capability. To address this issue, this paper proposes a multi-path braking energy dissipation strategy for electric tracked vehicles. The proposed approach overcomes the conventional limitation of relying solely on battery energy storage as the single absorption channel, and introduces a multidimensional energy dissipation architecture encompassing traction motor self-heating through maximum-loss drive control, active cooling fan power absorption, and engine compression braking. Simulation results demonstrate that, with the multi-path dissipation mechanism engaged, the system is capable of safely absorbing hundreds of kilowatts of excess regenerative power, substantially relieving the constraints on electric braking output and reducing the thermal loading on the mechanical brakes. This study effectively extends the energy absorption boundary of the vehicle electrical system, fully unlocks the braking potential of the electric drivetrain platform, and significantly enhances both the electromechanical compound braking safety and the electrical network stability under extreme operating conditions.

title: A TD3 Approach Based on Two-Sided Evolutionary Fusion for Robotic Arm Path Planning
authors: Feng Zhang, Haiyan Liu, Chengjie Fan, Shuai Yuan
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Mechatronics, Control and Robotics (ICMCR)
abstract: A Two Side Evolutionary Fusion-Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TEA-TD3) method is proposed focused on low planning efficiency along with long and tortuous paths for robotic arm path planning. Firstly, TEA-TD3 utilizes the Improved Cross-Entropy Method (ICEM) to alternately optimize the actor network and the critic network of TD3, thereby obtaining a better critic policy and improving the overall convergence speed. Secondly, hybrid distribution evolutionary perturbation mechanism and elite individual recycling mechanism are proposed. These mechanisms balance the scope of initialization perturbations for the actor network population and reintroduce the best critic network individual from the previous generation into the current population, thereby enhancing the overall explorative capability and stability of the method. Finally, the robotic arm conducts path planning experiments in the Robot Operating System (ROS)-Gazebo simulator. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the ICEM-TD3 and TD3 methods in terms of success rate, average path length, average distance to the target, and average steps to completion.

title: TeleQNet: Defense-Grade Messaging with Quantum Teleportation
authors: Sandhia G K, M. Ranjani, Sandeep Raj Saravanan, Himadhri Hariharasudhan, Santhosh P
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The rapid advancement of quantum computing raises concerns about the long-term security of existing cryptographic systems. In response, this paper presents TeleQNet, a secure communication framework that combines classical encryption with quantum-inspired techniques. The system integrates Quantum Key Distribution based on the BB84 protocol and a simulated quantum teleportation mechanism implemented using Qiskit. To strengthen security, a layered encryption approach is adopted using the Hill Cipher followed by AES-256 encryption. The framework is implemented as a web-based system using React and FastAPI, with an AI-based monitoring module that tracks parameters such as teleportation fidelity, channel noise, and communication latency. Experimental results show that the system achieves high reliability, with teleportation fidelity exceeding 99%, encryption accuracy around 99.5%, and average latency below 120 ms. However, the current implementation is limited to simulation and does not involve real quantum hardware. The results indicate that combining quantum-inspired methods with classical techniques can be a practical approach for building future secure communication systems.

title: A 1781 GOPS Compute-In-Memory CIM Macro with a Novel Dual-Port 8T SRAM for Implementing Multiple Logical Operations
authors: Cheena Singhal, Abhishek Goel, Kartik Shahi, Palak, Sparsh Mittal, Sudeb Dasgupta
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: We propose a novel compute-in-memory (CIM) enabled dual-port 8T (DP-8T) SRAM bitcell design, which can simultaneously perform two Boolean operations, such as NAND and OR, in a single cycle. By contrast, conventional single-port SRAM bitcells are limited to performing only one Boolean operation per cycle. We use additional logic gates to realize other Boolean operations such as XOR/XNOR and OR/AND, along with a half-adder. The proposed 64x128 design achieves a throughput of 1780.86 GOPS with an energy efficiency of 12 fJ/bit over an operating frequency range of 746.3 to 869.5 MHz. The robustness of the proposed architecture is validated by demonstrating correct functionality of all supported logic operations under worst-case process, voltage, and temperature (PVT) corners. The proposed work achieves an accuracy of 99.6% for a binarized neural network (BNN) on the MNIST dataset.

title: HLS Driven Obfuscation of DSP Hardware IP Designs using Non-invertible Arbitrary Function and Reconfigurable Intertwined Obfuscation Logic
authors: Anirban Sengupta, Nabendu Bhui, Vishal Chourasia, Ayush Kumar Singh
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: Digital Signal Processing (DSP) hardware intellectual property (IP) designs are pivotal to the proliferation of consumer electronics (CE) systems such as camera, camcorder, smart watch, smart TVs, IoT etc. Such DSP hardware IPs is typically designed using high level synthesis (HLS) framework. However, with the globalization of integrated circuit/system-on-chip (SoC) design supply chain, emergence of threats such as reverse engineering (RE) has become a matter of grave concern. This paper presents novel HLS-driven functional obfuscation of DSP hardware IP designs using non-invertible arbitrary function and reconfigurable intertwined obfuscation logic. The proposed approach thwarts RE attack by hindering various forms of threats such as key-sensitization attack, removal attack, different versions of SAT attack and ML attack. Results of the proposed approach revealed significantly greater obfuscation strength (causing reduced probability of attacks) than prior approaches.

title: Automated Screening of Diabetic Retinopathy from Retinal Images
authors: G.Prabhakar, Jukanti Sneha, K.Maneesha, Kadari Rachana, Kandala Harini, Jalli Nikhitha
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is still one of the most serious challenges to vision worldwide, but early diagnosis is often obstructed by a lack of advanced eye care resources and the insidious presentation of early disease. Although Artificial Intelligence has achieved great success in medical imaging, most current models are still challenged to effectively combine the strengths of deep feature learning with the capability to identify very small, multi-scale lesions such as microaneurysms. In this paper, we propose a Hybrid Multi-Scale Fusion Framework that is specifically tailored to address the gap between high-performance research models and more practical clinical applications. Our proposed model combines the unique strengths of the InceptionV3 model’s diverse receptive fields and the powerful residual learning of ResNet50. To further break through the limitations of most current models, we also developed a Customized Concatenation Mechanism and a specialized SW (Swish-based) Activation Layer, which maintains essential spatial information that is usually ignored by most current models.

title: Non-Invasive Blood Group Classification using Fingerprint Images and Deep Residual Learning
authors: K Shilpa, J Revanth Kumar, E Sravanthi, Gousiya Begum, V S Manoj Kumar, M Sireesha
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Knowing blood type is essential in case of a medical emergency, transfusion organizations, and the overall handling of the healthcare system. The classical techniques for blood typing require blood samples, are time-consuming, and imposes a laboratory-grade infrastructure. To mitigate these problems, a novel blood type classification system that combines fingerprint images and deep residual learning is proposed in this paper. A pretrained ResNet-based convolutional neural network is employed to sort fingerprint images into the eight blood type classes (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{+}, \mathrm{A}^{-}, \text{AB}^{+}, \text{AB}^{-}, \mathrm{B}^{+}, \mathrm{B}^{-}, \mathrm{O}^{+}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}^{-}$</tex>). The model takes advantage of the residual connections to effectively learn even the most subtle ridge and texture patterns of the fingerprint images. Imaging preprocessing and data augmentation methods are applied to augment generalization. The experiments rest on nearly 6,000 fingerprint images, divided into training, validation, and test sets. The proposed method achieves a 77.5% accuracy on the test set, with an even distribution of class-wise precision and recall across the blood group categories. The results indicate that fingerprint biometrics possess undisclosed discriminative information that is relevant to blood group classification. The proposed method offers a low-cost, non-invasive, and fast alternative in the preliminary blood group identification test which is very advantageous in case of emergencies or hospitals with limited resources.

title: Enhancing Offline Maritime Translation Safety: A Retrieval-Gated Generation Approach with Entity Constraints
authors: Linfeng Wu, Enjiao Zhao, Wei Zhou
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Maritime communication faces a critical dilemma: it demands a near-zero tolerance for errors to ensure safety, yet relies on offline edge deployment where computational resources are strictly limited. To resolve this conflict, this paper proposes a retrieval-gated generation framework for bidirectional maritime translation. The system prioritizes a domain knowledge base for standardized phrase recall, utilizing a neural model as a fallback only when retrieval confidence is insufficient. By integrating similarity-based gating with entity-consistency verification, our method minimizes high-risk errors in numbers, headings, bearings, and distress keywords. Experiments on an in-domain dataset demonstrate that the system improves BLEU scores from 14.84 to 16.24 for Chinese-to-English translation and from 21.56 to 23.88 for English-to-Chinese translation. Furthermore, analysis of knowledge-base hit rates confirms that the proposed approach effectively balances deterministic safety with the efficiency constraints of edge environments.

title: Woman-in-the-Loop: AI-Driven Mentorship Matching System
authors: Angela Santos, Nina Klimenkova, Roee Shraga
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Despite the proven impact of good quality mentorship, the process of finding the right mentor remains an underexplored challenge. This is especially evident for underrepresented groups such as women. To address this issue, we propose an AI-driven mentorship matching system that is refined iteratively via Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) feedback to enable personalized and equitable pairings. We designed a survey-based data collection process, followed by automated filtering to validate completeness and quality. To address lack of data, we generated synthetic data profiles based on survey structure. We evaluated the quality of generated datasets, comparing the realism, diversity, naturalness, and coherence. We employ a natural language processing (NLP) model, BERT, to assess mentor-mentee compatibility using both classification and regression. Future work will include obtaining and utilizing larger, more diverse datasets, integrating more HITL, developing matching algorithms, and launching the official survey.

title: Novel Full Ferroelectric-based Continuous-Time Ising Machine with Ultra-Low Hardware Cost for Real-Time Combinatorial Optimization
authors: Weikai Xu, Qianqian Huang, Ru Huang
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: In this work, for the first time, a novel full ferroelectric (FE)-based continuous-time Ising machine is proposed with significantly reduced hardware cost. By utilizing the FE polarization to compensate process variation, a FE-based latch is proposed for spin implementation, which can randomly generate spin-up or spin-down states, eliminating the need for extra bulky calibration circuits. Moreover, by leveraging the multi-level storage and gate controllable structure of FeFET, the spin interactions with in-situ precision extension can be efficiently implemented, further reducing hardware cost.
