Based on the above design, the max-cut problem is solved with enhanced success probability and reduced hardware cost, showing its great potential for solving the real-time combinatorial optimization problems.

title: Research on the Pragmatic Functions and Phonetic Realization of Pause Signals in Natural Conversation in the Mongolian Khalkha Dialect
authors: Cheliger
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: To examine the pragmatic functions and phonetic realization of pause signals in natural conversations in the Khalkha dialect of Mongolian, this study analyzes 174 minutes of conversational data (22,744 prosodic words and 1,103 turns). Statistical analyses show that the 1,700 phonetic pauses have a mean duration of 0.480 s and a right-skewed distribution, with most pauses concentrated between 0.00 and 1.28 s, indicating clear quantitative linguistic regularities. Pauses are classified into three types: (1) pauses between prosodic words, (2) pauses between turn-constructional units (turn-holding), and (3) pauses between turns (turn-yielding). Inter-turn pauses have the longest mean duration (0.540 s), with a maximum of 4.032 s. Pause duration differs significantly between pauses between prosodic words and the other two types ($\mathbf{p}{\lt}0.05$), whereas the difference between the latter two types is not significant. Turn-yielding pauses are significantly longer than turn-holding pauses, which may reflect cognitive preparation during turn transition. This study provides empirical evidence for research on speech prosody and conversational interaction.

title: Investigating the Suitability of High-Performance Linux-Based System-on-Modules for Lunar Applications
authors: L. Coïc, G. Giuffrida, E. Le Goulven, S. Yjjou, A. Marino, P. Garcia, L. Turchi, G. Magistrati, A. Cowley
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: Characterization campaigns conducted on two System-on-Modules destined for lunar surface applications are presented. Their TID and SEE sensitivities were investigated under irradiation. Outgassing, Offgassing and Lifetest campaigns were also performed in this ESA project.

title: Precision-Aware Lightweight Deep Learning Framework for Automated Skin Cancer Classification from Dermoscopic Images
authors: Goldy Verma, Komuravelly Sudheer Kumar, Pankaj Negi, Pawan Kumar Mishra, Neha Sharma, Rupesh Gupta
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Skin cancer is among the most frequent and deadlier conditions in the field of dermatology, and early and correct diagnosis is of much significance in enhancing the rates of survival among the patients. In this work, the systematic review of the literature shows that automated classification of skin cancer based on dermoscopic images with the help of a deep learning-based system has been developed effectively. A narrowed down version of MobileNetV2 is used to classify both the benign and malignant skin lesions with low computational complexity. The methodology suggested allows combining efficient data preprocessing, augmentation, transfer learning, and optimal fine-tuning to improve the feature representation and generalization ability. A publicly available dataset of dermoscopic images was experimentally evaluated and the model performance evaluated in terms of standard evaluation measures. The average classification accuracy of the proposed approach is 96% which is a better result than some commonly used deep learning models. Strong learning and low overfitting are observed by the close correspondence between training and validation results. The proposed model is also easily scalable to work with clinical decision support systems and resource-constrained healthcare settings and is lightweight, which makes it suitable to the work of a real-time system because of high accuracy and low resource requirements.

title: Identification of Position-Dependent Geometric Errors in Five-Axis Machine Tools Based on an Attention-Mechanism CNN-LSTM
authors: Biting Lei, Hanyue Bao, Simi Li, Huaijie Wang, Xiong Fu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: With the increasing requirements for processing accuracy of complex free-form surface parts in aerospace and automotive manufacturing, the position-dependent geometric error (PDGE) of five-axis machine tools has become an important factor affecting processing quality. Traditional PDGE identification methods use expensive measurement equipment, such as laser interferometers, R-test, and ball bar instruments. However, these cannot accurately reflect the real processing state. This paper proposes a five-axis machine tool PDGE reverse identification method that integrates a geometric error model with the attention mechanism CNN-LSTM. First, a geometric error model from the tool end to the workpiece end is constructed. Based on sensitivity analysis, 30 PDGE items are decomposed into 15 suberror models to reduce the complexity of error coupling identification. Next, a sequence input integrating position features, mean-zero features, and difference features is designed. The CNN-LSTM-Attention algorithm is used to achieve a nonlinear mapping from the simulation contour error to each axis PDGE. Additionally, a PDGE supervision term and a simulation contour error constraint term are introduced to construct a loss function for joint optimization of the prediction results. This improves identification accuracy. The results show that the deviation between the predicted contour error of each axis and the simulation contour error is less than 2.93% of the simulation contour error. The proposed method achieves high-precision and interpretable PDGE reverse identification without the need for professional measurement equipment. This provides a feasible technical path for PDGE identification, integrating geometric error models and deep learning.

title: Using Transformer Encoders for Light Curve Classification in Telescope Pipelines
authors: Avni Bansal, Dhruv Roongta
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Modern time-domain surveys such as the Zwicky Transient Facility (ZTF) and the upcoming Vera Rubin Observatory generate millions of light curves per night. Identifying and flagging the scientifically interesting observations for follow-up is a time-sensitive and non-trivial problem. Traditional models, including tree-based classifiers and convolutional neural networks (CNNs), are widely used in this context. However, they have limited success, and they often struggle with the irregular sampling, multi-band structure, and long-range dependencies inherent in astronomical time series. They also often systematically miss transient and semi-periodic sources. In this work, we implement and benchmark a Transformer-based architecture for sequence-to-label classification of light curves using the PLAsTiCC dataset. Our Transformer model takes raw light curve data as inputs without the need for resampling or handengineered features, and outperforms both a baseline CNN and a hybrid CNN+Transformer model in overall accuracy and perclass recall. In particular, the Transformer does well on rare and irregular classes such as kilonovae, M-dwarf flares, and active galactic nuclei, where traditional models often fail. We also find that the CNN+Transformer hybrid counterintuitively performs poorly, due to information loss in the convolutional front-end. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of selfattention mechanisms for irregular time-series classification and suggest that Transformer-based models offer a scalable and generalizable approach for next-generation telescope pipelines.

title: Infrared Thermal Bushing Fault Diagnosis Based on Adaptive Roi Extraction and Deep Learning Fusion Model
authors: Xianfeng Zhu, Shaobo Liu, Changdong Zhu, Yi Du, Songlin Guo, Zihan Teng, Zixin Jiao, Zhixian Zhang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Accurate fault diagnosis of transformer bushings via infrared thermal imaging is challenging due to complex background interference and the limited capability of singlearchitecture model. This paper proposes a bushing fault diagnosis framework integrating adaptive region-of-interest (ROI) extraction with a CNN-Vision Transformer (ViT) fusion model. An adaptive ROI extraction method combining infrared thermal distribution with geometric structural constraints is first designed to precisely isolate the bushing region. A Swin-CNN-ViT fusion architecture is then constructed, where the CNN branch extracts local hotspot features and the Swin Transformer branch models long-range spatial dependencies, with the two fused via weighted feature aggregation for classification into normal, overheating, insulation defect, and contamination states. Experiments on a 5,600-image infrared power equipment dataset show that the proposed method achieves 95.0 % validation accuracy, a 6.5 percentage-point improvement over the ResNet50 baseline, with faster convergence and fewer missed detections in defect categories.

title: Fourier-Denoising for Noisy Hamiltonian Systems: Enhancing Symplectic Dynamics Learning
authors: Chloe Ho
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Hamiltonian Neural Networks (HNNs) allow for modeling physical and dynamical systems, but their performance deteriorates when trained on noisy real-world data. We demonstrate that such noise disrupts the symplectic structure HNNs are designed to preserve, leading to unstable trajectories and energy drift. Through error analysis, we quantify the impact of noise on learned Hamiltonian gradients and propose a low-pass Fourier filtering approach to remove high-frequency artifacts prior to training, reducing the mismatch between the true and learned dynamics. Our preliminary findings indicate that this frequencydomain preprocessing substantially enhances HNN robustness, yielding both short-horizon accuracy gains and long-horizon stability. In this position paper, we argue that such denoising strategies are paramount for bridging the gap between idealized Hamiltonian models and noisy, real-world data, ensuring reliable, physically meaningful performance in practical applications.

title: An Ideal Approach to 3D NAND Flash Design for Ultra-High IOPS SSDs (>100M) with Low-Latency (<=us), Fine-Grained (Page Size=512B), and a Novel MB-level Embedded DRAM to support On-Chip NAND Managements
authors: Hang-Ting Lue, Teng-Hao Yeh, Chin-Shan Yuan, Keh-Chung Wang, Chih-Yuan Lu
abstract: Recently, there has been a growing demand for ultra-high IOPS (Input/Output Operations Per Second) SSDs (Solid-State Drives) with fine granularity (small page size <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=512 ~\mathrm{B}$</tex>) to meet the requirements of various AI computing applications. Beyond the commonly referenced “low-latency” SLC NAND design, this paper proposes several solutions to further enhance the performance of nextgeneration SSDs: (1) An ultra-small plane design utilizing “CuA+CbA” (CMOS under Array and CMOS bonded Array) co-integration to optimize layout efficiency of minimized word line and bit line lengths; (2) Small block size <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<1 \text{MB})$</tex> to reduce WL capacitance loading, enabling faster WL setup times; (3) Double-pitch M1 bit lines (BL) combined with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{4}$</tex> period page buffer (PB) sensing circuits to support true Page_Size=512B small granularity for each plane; (4) We develop a MB-level embedded DRAM inside NAND Flash chip using 3T1C cell without process change, where the transistors are realized from existing peripheral low-voltage NMOS devices, and the capacitors (a few fF) are constructed using existing through-array vias (TAV). This internal eDRAM serves as a versatile cache, supporting on-chip managements such as write buffering and block address mapping tables. We anticipate that these innovative design techniques can improve the 3D NAND technology for Memory-Class Storage (MCS).

title: Emotion Detection From Facial Expressions using AI and ML
authors: J. Karthika, Kuncham Lalitesh Sampath Vinay, Kuruva Siva Kumar, M. Vanathi, B. Sandhiya, R.S. Amshavalli
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Real-time emotion recognition enhances user interaction by allowing systems to interpret and respond to facial cues instantly. The suggested system approach combines deep learning with traditional machine-learning methods to achieve reliable emotion detection in changing environments. The central part of the system is MobileNet convolutional neural network that was chosen because it balances speed and accuracy well hence the suitability of real time use. Thereafter, one more model, a Random Forest (RF) classifier, is included and trained on manually identified features, including facial landmarks and gradient-based features. This is a hybrid approach that makes the model more robust and allows flexibility in how it is deployed in different computing environments. The application is implemented in Flask web framework and takes live video streams recorded with the help of a webcam. It extracts significant facial features and classifies expressions into categories such as happiness, sadness, anger, surprise, and neutrality. Flask responds to and delivers model predictions to the front end without complications, and the response is fast. The system can be used in the mood-adaptive system, educational tools, and personalized marketing platforms because it provides highperformance and real-time emotional feedback through a hybrid model that merges deep-learning strategies with conventional classification algorithms.

title: Smart Signal Booster for Cellular and Wi-Fi Communication
authors: S Bhargavi, Abhijith J V, Abhishek H K, Govardhan V K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: In today’s digitized world, dependable access to Both cellular networks and internet services are needs. However, many remote and rural communities continue to suffer from weak signals. and poor infrastructure, which prevents access to basic resources such as education, healthcare, and emergency services. This growing digital divide requires innovative and affordable solutions that guarantee dependable connectivity reaches even the most Isolated regions. The proposed paper directly addresses this. Challenge by improving network coverage and providing internet Access where it is critically lacking. The system employs a tech An ontologically advanced way of amplifying weak cellular signals and redistribute them via a Wi-Fi network, intended for simplicity, portability, and sustainability with renewable energy sources or battery power. Initial tests showcase significant Signal improvements, reaching as high as 4-bar strength in remote locations. The Such solution supports rural development, disaster re Lief, and on-the-go connectivity, driving inclusion to progress in underserved areas.

title: Smart Kinetic Envelope: Modeling and Empirical Testing of IoT Systems for Tropical Climates : A Low-Cost Innovation Approach to Making the Global South More Energy Efficient
authors: M. Amsaveni, Nikitha, Adarsh, Shree Harsha, Yashwanth R, Govardhan Reddy
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Tropical cities face acute energy challenges driven by rapid urbanisation. Buildings globally consume over 40% of total energy, with air conditioning systems responsible for the dominant share in hot-humid climates such as those prevalent across India and the wider Global South. Conventional static building envelopes are fundamentally incapable of adapting to dynamic solar geometry, leading to chronic over-consumption of 40–50% beyond theoretical optima. This paper presents the complete design, mathematical modelling, prototype fabrication, and empirical validation of a Smart Kinetic Envelope (SKE) system that integrates IoT intelligence with origami-inspired panel mechanics. The system employs a Waterbomb Base tessellation to enable mechanically efficient, low-torque modulation of solar heat gain and natural ventilation.Control architecture is realised using ATmega328P microcontrollers, LM393 light-dependent resistor sensor modules, and MG996R metal-gear servo motors arranged in a fault-tolerant decentralised I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>C cluster topology. Maekawa’s Theorem and Kawasaki’s Theorem guarantee flat-foldability and single-degree-of-freedom actuation, while a dynamic thermal model couples the fold angle θ to both the effective U-value and the Solar Heat Gain Coefficient. EnergyPlus simulation of a 100 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> office zone in Chennai and physical wind-tunnel testing of a 1:1 prototype demonstrate a 28% reduction in annual cooling energy, a 31.7% reduction in peak cooling load, a 75% improvement in daylight glare control, and structural stability at gusts up to 60 km/h. Total installed cost is approximately USD 65 per m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, representing a 90% reduction relative to premium kinetic facade benchmarks.

title: Human centric conversational system in Tamil using Retrieval Augmented Generation
authors: Yogita Bhavanishankar, Vishnu Siva B, J Cruz Antony
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Living in rural and under-resourced communities, people are frequently relying on the use of simple telephone as there is a low access to the internet and low smartphone rates. Traditional Interactive Voice Response systems are menu-based, rigid and not very conducive to natural speech in local languages. This paper proposes an artificial intelligence-based bilingual voice-first application that translates Tamil and English while talking using a regular GSM phone. The design that is proposed combines multi-language speech recognition, generative text that is retrieval augmented, natural language understanding, and neural text-to-speech synthesis as part of a flexible telephony platform that can be configured to operate with little or no network connectivity. Local audio routing based GSM call simulation layer is used to eliminate the expensive third-party telephony APIs and minimize the cost of testing and deployment. Experimental assessments reveal a high degree of cross-accent speech recognition, appropriate response generation, and use as a result of conversations, as well as a high level of user-satisfaction. It provides the telephonic services with the much-needed accessibility increase and creates a feasible platform on which voice automation can be easily scaled in such areas like education, healthcare, customer care, and delivery of public services.

title: Image Enhancement for Classifying the Diabetic Retinopathy in Retinal Fundus Images
authors: Mahesh M R, Rohith S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The fundus images are improvised by introducing a complete framework for retinal image enhancement that addresses typical problems including uneven lighting and poor contrast. This system improves images by creative de-hazing methods and contrast augmentation, making subsequent analytic jobs more accurate. Images of the retinal fundus are essential for the diagnosis of several conditions affecting the eyes, such as hypertension and diabetic retinopathy. These issues are fully addressed by the retinal image enhancement architecture presented in the research. It uses cutting-edge methods to improve fundus picture clarity, including as contrast augmentation and de-hazing. By correcting non-uniform illumination, de-hazing techniques successfully lessen the effects of uneven lighting situations. By restoring the photos original sharpness, this restoration improves their suitability for analysis.

title: Multi-Model Deep Learning Computer Vision System for Brain Disorder Classification and Tumor Segmentation with Treatment Recommendations
authors: Sasirekha R, Narendiaran N, Nirenjana JV
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: This research study presents the design and implementation of a high-performance, modular frontend platform built using modern web technologies. The application takes advantage of server-side rendering, clientside hydration and React Server Components to create an efficient rendering pipeline with less latency and a better user facing experience. Build-layer manifests (including routing, middleware, dynamic chunk mapping, and font configuration) expression of an optimized method of deployment that entails the least client overhead and standardized performance traits. The architecture of the system includes the use of modular TypeScript configmanage, a custom document layout, and incremental compilation for maintaining system and operation throughout the development and production processes. With light weighted runtime behaviour, lack of client middleware and clean assets bundling, the platform offers strong foundation that can be used as a basis for secure, responsive and extendable web interfaces. This research is an analysis about the structure of the system, its performance implications, and the opportunities for it to be incorporated into large-scale Web solutions.

title: Trajectory Design of Angular Displacement for Centrifugal Actuator Based on Model Predictive Control and Research on Vehicle Lateral Vibration Reduction
authors: Haiyan Xu, Bing Xu, Yu Zhang, Yizhe Song, Yijue Sun
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Large vehicles are prone to significant vibration during driving, which impairs driving stability and the service life of components. To address this issue, this paper proposes an angular displacement trajectory optimization method based on Model Predictive Control (MPC) for effective suppression of vehicle lateral vibration. Meanwhile, combined with the structural characteristics of the body of large vehicles, a dynamic model of vehicle lateral vibration is established (ignoring the influence of wheel lateral movement), and a linear mapping relationship between the output force of the centrifugal actuator and angular displacement is clarified. The particle swarm optimization algorithm is adopted to solve the optimal angular displacement trajectory, ensuring that the actuator output accurately matches the demand for vehicle body vibration suppression. A simulation platform is built by MATLAB, with the mapping relationship between the corresponding disturbance force and angular displacement set based on actual engineering scenarios. Finally, the effect differences between passive vibration reduction and active control are compared to verify the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy, which provides a feasible engineering technical solution for lateral vibration suppression of large vehicles.Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed MPCPSO combined control strategy effectively reduces vehicle body vibration levels, with the vibration index decreasing from 0.88 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.57\left(~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}\right)^{2}$</tex> and achieving a vibration suppression rate of 35.2 %-significantly outperforming traditional passive damping solutions. The optimized angular displacement trajectory exhibits continuous smoothness, fully complying with the physical constraints of centrifugal actuators. This strategy enables real-time tracking of lateral disturbance forces, effectively enhancing ride comfort and improving structural durability. The findings provide reliable theoretical foundations and technical support for engineering applications of lateral active damping technologies in large vehicles.

title: Melody-Centric Cryptography: A Human-Centered Framework for Symmetric Key Generation
authors: Julia Lu, Qiaoyan Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Traditional methods for symmetric key generation rely on random alphanumeric strings, biometric data, or hardware tokens. While secure, these approaches often suffer from poor memorability, limited interpretability, and reduced accessibility in resource-constrained settings. In this paper, we propose a novel human-centered method for generating Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) keys from musical melodies. The pitch sequence of a user-selected melody is encoded into a structured string and then hashed using SHA-256 to produce a 128-bit or 256-bit AES key. To demonstrate the feasibility of this approach, we developed a web-based prototype that allows users to upload or input a melody, then perform AES encryption and decryption of text messages using the derived key. Encrypted messages can be easily copied and sent via email, enabling lightweight and portable secure communication. This system bridges humanperceivable artistic input with algorithmic cryptography, providing a cross-modal pathway for secure interaction. Preliminary use cases suggest that melody-based key derivation offers a promising direction for designing culturally adaptive, memoryfriendly symmetric encryption schemes. Future work will be focused on developing a mobile version to support real-time, on-device secure messaging.

title: Study and Analysis of Cyber Security Threats in Smart Home Technology
authors: Divya Kaushal, Aniza Flaquer
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Smart home technology enhances convenience but introduces significant cybersecurity risks due to interconnected devices. This study explores common threats, including unauthorized access, data breaches, and malware attacks, while assessing the effectiveness of current security measures. By analyzing case studies and emerging challenges, the research emphasizes the need for robust security frameworks and user awareness to ensure the safety of smart home ecosystems.

title: Reviving Ancient Immunity: Harnessing Molecular De-Extinction and Scalable Algal Biofactories of Computationally-Mined Antimicrobial Peptides to Eradicate Livestock Antibiotic Resistance
authors: Hosamulhaq Brula
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: The overconsumption of antibiotics in livestock drives bacterial resistance, threatening global food security and human health. This unchecked trend imposes escalating economic and sustainability burdens with prevalent mortality and diminishing feed efficiency. In the absence of antibiotic discovery for decades, antimicrobial peptides (AMPs) offer broad-spectrum, membranedisruptive mechanisms but face emerging resistance and prohibitive synthesis costs. This study presents a molecular de-extinction strategy employing the APEX deep-learning pipeline to mine 10.3 million peptides reconstructed from extinct proteomes. Three lead candidates (Elephasin-1.5, Mylodonin-2, and Staph-2) were validated, exhibiting minimum inhibitory concentrations (MICs) of 2-8 μM against Clostridium perfringens, 1-4 μM against Enterococcus cecorum, and 16 μM against avian pathogenic Escherichia coli (APEC), with no hemolysis up to 64 μM. By accessing peptide diversity beyond modern pathogenic evolutionary exposure, these de-extinct AMPs circumvent prevailing resistance mechanisms. For scalable, costeffective deployment, Chlamydomonas reinhardtii chloroplasts were engineered to function simultaneously as photosynthetic biofactories and oral delivery vehicles. Algae-produced AMPs can be freeze-dried into feed, protecting peptides from gastric proteolysis and ensuring targeted intestinal release while preserving MIC potency. This green bioprocess leverages phototrophic cultivation to obviate complex chemical synthesis and sustain circular bioeconomics. Collectively, this end-to-end platform, from extinctome-inspired discovery to algal biomanufacturing, mitigates livestock-driven antibiotic resistance, thereby safeguarding human health and preserving a $1.3 trillion food industry. Moreover, it establishes a blueprint to harness this vast, untapped sequence space for rapid discovery and delivery of novel therapeutics across various disorders.

title:
ZnO/Al
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    Heterojunction Based Optoelectronic Neuro-inspired Memristor
authors: Saransh Shrivastava, Yu-Hang Poh, Chen-Wei Chang, Ke-Yue Wen, Stephen Ekaputra Limantoro, Tseung-Yuen Tseng
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: In this work, we propose a ZnO/Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> heterojunction-based optoelectronic memristive synapse (OMS). It shows the analog-type resistance switching behavior with a large memory window of ~1498. The long-term potentiation/ depression behavior of biological synapse is emulated by OMS with small non-linearity, which is further utilized to simulate convolution neural network to classify the face images of Yale dataset. The same OMS also responds to violet light of 405 nm with an excitatory change in the device current, which further follows the persistent photo conductive phenomenon under dark and exhibits the emulation of synaptic functions such as short to long term memory transition and paired pulse facilitation. All these findings indicate the OMS’s potential applications in optoelectronic neuro-inspired applications.

title: Optimized Recognition of Dermal Lesion Analysis Based on CNN and Vision Transformer
authors: P. Arivubrakan, C. Arun Kumar, Peram Venkata Naga Durga Prasad
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: In recent years, skin disease has played an important role in humans, to diagnose the disease based on the inputs. With the increasing demand on the automation diagnosis, deep learning methods are very much familiar nowadays. Skin cancer is the most common and rapidly enhancing forms with variety of cancer in today's world, with millions of humans are identified every day. The performance metric analysis for skin cancer, which is in the dataset by integrating the two important models of deep learning, such as Convolution Neural Networks (CNN) and Vision Transformer (ViT) is used. The ViT model achieved an accuracy of 87 %, while the ensemble method of CNN+ ViT, which obtained 91 % accuracy. The analysis emphasises the importance of choosing the right model selection based on the available dataset characteristics, and also it provides insights into designing an effective dermal lesion classification system.

title: Smart Energy Management in Industry 4.0 Using IoT-Enabled Sensing and LSTM Prediction Models
authors: K. Arunasakthi, V. Akilandeswari, C. Maharajothi, G. Danya, D. Renuga
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Industry 4.0 enables the creation of intelligent and networked manufacturing systems in which real-time monitoring and decision-making through data are required for enhancing efficiency. Nevertheless, despite the progress, industrial energy consumption remains a significant problem due to inefficient machine use, the lack of continuous monitoring, and the lack of predictive intelligence. In this paper, a low-cost and IoT-based multi-sensor solution for real-time monitoring of industrial energy consumption and short-term forecasting is proposed. The proposed solution combines temperature, current, vibration, pressure, and flow sensors with an edge computing system to monitor machine-level operational data. The electrical energy consumption is calculated from real-time data and analyzed using a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network to forecast future energy consumption and detect any unusual operating conditions. Sensor data is transmitted to a cloud platform using the MQTT protocol, which enables scalable data storage, processing, and predictive analytics. Experimental results are derived from a time-series dataset that simulates industrial operating conditions. The LSTM model performs with a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 0.108 kW and a Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of 0.164 kW, ensuring the accuracy of short-term energy prediction. The experiment outcomes validate that the proposed framework successfully detects energy inefficiencies, enables predictive maintenance, and enables optimal machine scheduling.Through the use of IoT sensing and deep learning analytics, this research provides a cost-effective, scalable, and intelligent solution for energy-efficient smart manufacturing systems.

title: Design of a Digital Twin Interactive System for a Radio Control Wheeled Vehicle
authors: Qi Li, Wen-kai Wu, Zhi-qi Lang, Hong-cheng Jiao, Tao Jing, Han-tao Zhao
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This paper takes a radio control wheeled vehicle proposed by the project team in the early stage as the research object, constructs digital twin entity including 3D models and data models in order to develop a digital twin interactive software. By activating the real-time correlation between 3D models and data models, valuable data exchange can be achieved between the digital twin entity and the physical entity, and valuable data can be used to drive both to refresh their operating status.

title: Predicting the Total Crop Production in Area and Advising Best Alternative Crop
authors: Chandan M, Aditya, Anurag Vishwanath Gadduge, Chidamabaram M, Baba Fakruddin Ali B H, Raghavendra Patil G E
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The project presents "Agri Smart," an intelligent decision-support system that predicts total crop production and recommends the best alternative crop by combining machine learning with real-time IoT sensing for precision agriculture. The system fuses soil parameters (N P K, pH, moisture) with live weather data (rainfall, temperature, humidity) from APIs and feeds them to a Random Forest regression engine that achieved around 93% validation accuracy, significantly outperforming basic linear and single-tree models for yield prediction. A dual microcontroller architecture using Arduino Uno for sensing/relay control and ESP8266 for MQTT-based Wi Fi communication enables an automated irrigation loop that triggers a pump within about 2 seconds when soil moisture crosses critical thresholds. On top of this, a web dashboard and regional-language voice assistant convert complex analytics into simple, vernacular audio alerts so that non technical farmers can access predictions and irrigation status without needing to read dashboards. Overall, Agri Smart demonstrates that a low cost, full stack IoT ML framework can bridge current gaps in real time adaptability, automation, and usability, offering a practical blueprint for scalable smart farming solutions.

title: Multi-Agent Interaction: Creating Websites with Ease
authors: VimalaDevi R, Prathibha A, Lokitha G S, Yashaswini, Srivatsa N, Vishal Kumar Jha
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Website development is traditionally a complex and time-consuming process requiring specialized skills across various domains such as design, coding, content management, SEO, and deployment. This paper presents a novel approach to simplify and automate the process through the use of multi agent systems (MAS), where intelligent, autonomous agents collaborate to handle different aspects of website creation. Each agent is specialized—for example, in UI/UX design, backend development, database configuration, or search engine optimization—and works in coordination with others to fulfill user-defined requirements. Leveraging principles of distributed artificial intelligence, these agents communicate, negotiate tasks, and make real-time decisions to generate complete, responsive, and optimized websites with minimal human intervention. The system incorporates natural language interfaces for ease of use, allowing even non-technical users to describe their needs in plain language, which the agents then interpret and act upon. Machine learning techniques may also be embedded to allow agents to learn from past projects, improving their performance and adaptability over time. This collaborative, modular, and fault-tolerant approach significantly reduces development time and errors while increasing scalability and accessibility. A prototype implementation demonstrates the feasibility of this model, showing substantial improvements over traditional methods in terms of speed, quality, and user experience. Overall, this research illustrates how multiagent interaction can revolutionize web development by making it faster, more intelligent, and accessible to a broader range of users, ultimately democratizing the creation of digital platforms across industries.

title: Characterization and Performance Analysis of Aging Effect on Enterprise HDDs
authors: Ava Dezhban, Mohammadamin Ajdari, Hossein Asadi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: Hard Disk Drives (HDDs) are still a popular choice in data centers for cloud storage, due to lowest cost per terabyte and decent performance. Previous works have studied parameters related to disk failure and also proposed prediction models to predict the time that a disk fails. However, the impact of disk aging on performance has remained a big mystery. In this paper, we empirically study over 30 enterprise HDDs and analyze how different parameters related to aging (e.g., the amount of previous disk writes and power-on hours) affect the performance. We specifically provide six findings, summarized as four major insights. For example, we reveal that a new disk, after disk being used for over eight moths, shows up to $3.9 \times$ higher write latency. At the same time, no latency difference exists between disks older than two-years of age. Overall, a single used disk (with two-years of age) alongside three new disks in a RAID array worsen the I/O latency by 17%. Our findings can help storage architects and admins to (1) ensure high service-level-agreements for HDDbased storage systems, (2) detect used enterprise disks compared to fairly new disks even when disk S.M.A.R.T information are not available or have been altered deliberately to cause misleading information.

title: Real-Time Smart Path Recommendation System for People’s Safety
authors: Nehaa Sanchithi H, Nidhi Chandru, Meghana A, Manikantha K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Navigation applications have become an essential part of daily travel; however, most existing systems are designed to prioritise speed or distance rather than user safety. This approach often overlooks real-world risks, such as accident-prone roads, unsafe neighbourhoods, poor lighting conditions, and time-dependent vulnerabilities. To address this limitation, this paper presents SafePath, a real-time smart path recommendation system that focuses on delivering safer navigation experiences instead of merely faster routes.The proposed system integrates GPS-based location tracking with OpenStreetMap road data and historical accident information to estimate route safety. A modified risk-aware A* pathfinding algorithm is introduced, where safety risk is treated as a dynamic cost alongside physical distance. Machine learning–based risk prediction is integrated using a lightweight on-device model to evaluate road segments based on accident history, contextual factors, and time-of-day variations. In addition to route planning, the system provides continuous safety monitoring, local crime alerts, and an emergency SOS feature to support users during critical situations.Experimental evaluation conducted using real accident data from Bangalore demonstrates that SafePath can recommend routes that significantly reduce exposure to high-risk areas while maintaining reasonable travel efficiency. The results highlight the importance of incorporating safety intelligence into navigation systems and show that the proposed approach can effectively enhance personal security in urban environments.

title: The Impact of Rain–Fog Coexistence on Quantum Positioning
authors: Xuchang Qu, Min Nie, Guang Yang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: To investigate the impact of rain-fog coexistence on quantum positioning performance, we analyzed-using raindropsize distribution functions and extinction coefficients-how visibility and propagation distance in such compound conditions affect photon energy, positioning error, quantum-channel fidelity, and bit-error rate. For a $\mathbf{1 0 ~ k m}$ link, the energy of a single photon drops to $1.87 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~J}$ when visibility is 0.7 km and to $1.59 \times 10^{-6} \mathrm{~J}$ when visibility falls to 0.6 km. While visibility decreases from 0.7 km to 0.5 km, and the photon energy is kept at $10 \times 10^{-5} \mathrm{~J}$, the positioning error grows from 0.826 cm to 1.017 cm. Although the exact decline differs slightly among channels, the fidelity of the quantum communication channel invariably decreases as visibility worsens. At a 1 km range, the channel bit-error rate rises to 0.24 when visibility is 0.50 km and to 0.14 when it is 0.55 km. These results demonstrate that rain-fog coexistence markedly degrades quantum positioning performance. Consequently, system parameters must be adjusted in real time according to the prevailing visibility and propagation distance to maintain the stability and reliability of quantum positioning systems operating in rain-fog environments.

title: Integrating Federated Learning and Hyperspectral Imaging for Early Detection of Tomato Disease
authors: Lucas Zhou, Mohammad Amini, Yiannis Ampatzidis, Mostafa Reisi Gahrooei
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Bacterial spot of tomato (BST) causes significant crop loss for farmers in the U.S. However, current detection methods lack the speed and accuracy needed to effectively control the disease. In this study, we propose a federated learning (FL) framework, implemented using the Flower platform, for early BST detection using hyperspectral imagery, providing a scalable and privacy-preserving alternative to centralized models. We compare centralized, federated (FL), personalized federated (PFL), and local learning versions of logistic regression (LR), support vector machines (SVM), and neural networks (NN) for early detection of BST using hyperspectral imagery. When implementing FL, multiple aggregation strategies were assessed. Results show that FL and PFL approaches achieve accuracy up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 2. 8 {\%}}$</tex> for NNs, closely matching the centralized model's performance of 93.3%, without the need to share the raw data. FL was key for simpler, computationally efficient linear models, boosting accuracy from a local-only baseline of 74.0% to over 90%. These findings demonstrate that distributed, communicationbased computing enables scalable and privacy-preserving disease classification, suitable for real-world agricultural deployment.

title: CPU Bottlenecks in Rack-Stand Brain-Computer Interfaces: A Phase-Aware Characterisation
authors: Victor Kariofillis, Iris Uwizeyimana, Sara Ahmad, Tanvi Manku, Raghavendra Pradyumna Pothukuchi, Abhishek Bhattacharjee, Natalie Enright Jerger
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: Rack-stand brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) remain the dominant platform for exploratory and clinical BCI research, yet their CPU behaviour is poorly understood [1]–[7]. We characterise four rack-stand BCI pipelines—seizure detection [8], movement intent [9], speech decoding [10], and neural-data compression [11]—using peer-reviewed implementations and redistributable datasets on a commodity x86 CPU. Using offline replay of recorded neural streams, we combine end-to-end runtime across three core-frequency operating points with phase-aware Top-Down execution-time and memory-boundedness breakdowns [12]. The results show that rack-stand BCIs do not form a single bottleneck category, that channel count can change CPU demand by about $3 \times$, and that a single “memory-bound” label is too coarse because different cache and memory levels dominate in different stages.

title: Haptic Mouse for Non-Visual Navigation with Hybrid IMU-Optical Tracking
authors: Juan Pablo Robles Pérez, Donggu Lee, Melisa Orta Martinez
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Exploring spatial aspects of digital interfaces remains challenging for people who are blind, despite advances in screen readers, particularly when these interfaces include graphical content. Previous research has demonstrated that absolute positioning combined with haptic feedback can significantly enhance performance in shape recognition tasks. However, most existing systems often rely on expensive graphical tablets, restricting their accessibility and scalability. In this work, we present advancements in a low-cost haptic mouse system by replacing the graphical tablet with an integration of an optical sensor and an inertial measurement unit (IMU). This combination enables driftcorrected absolute positioning, independent of device orientation. Additionally, we introduce a design employing linear resonance actuators to deliver directional vibrotactile cues to the user's palm, enhancing positional awareness and orientation during exploration. To evaluate the system, we performed controlled displacement tests to measure positioning accuracy, quantified as the deviation from expected trajectories (pixels per centimeter). Preliminary results demonstrate millimeter-scale resolution and consistent positional mapping across trials. This research contributes to accessible human-computer interaction by eliminating dependence on costly hardware for absolute positioning, providing a scalable solution suitable for educational applications (e.g., STEM-related graphical exploration) and general digital interface navigation for blind and visually impaired users. Future studies will involve user-centered evaluations with blind participants to further refine the haptic feedback design and validate the usability of the system.

title: Enhancing SONOS-Type Flash Memory for Nonvolatile Analog Computing-in-Memory via Precise Multi-Level Weight Control and Improved Retention
authors: T. Kobayashi, Y. Taniguchi, H. Ikegaya, T. Tamatsu, M. Yamaguchi, K. Yanagisawa, Y. Uji, T. Yabe, S. Yoshida, Y. Kawashima, T. Shimozato, H. Kobori, T. Kato, F. Owada, S. Noda, H. Yoneda, K. Okuyama, T. Hosomi, K. Oto, S. Koshina, T. Morie
abstract: This paper demonstrates the advantages of SONOS-type flash memory for non-volatile analog computing-in-memory (nvACiM) applications. For nvACiM, high-density weight states per cell and precise weight control are essential. We implement an iterative multi-step programming sequence in SONOS memory to achieve 256 -level weight control, validated through both simulations and single-bit cell measurements. To address the critical requirement of weight stability, we introduce a combination of band control thermal treatment during the ONO formation process and shallow trap charge reduction during the programming sequence; this approach significantly enhances the thermal retention of weights. These techniques were applied to nvACiM test chips, demonstrating 32-level weight programming with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2-\text{nA}$</tex> spacing in the subthreshold region of memory MOSFETs, enabling extremely low power operation. Furthermore, linear multiply-accumulate (MAC) operations were successfully verified.

title: Histopathology-Based Deep Learning for Melanoma PDX Drug-Response Tracking
authors: Sahil Kapadia, Aria Merrill, Sagar Kapadia
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Longitudinal assessment of tumor response remains a cornerstone of translational oncology, yet most pre-clinical studies depend on costly molecular assays, under-utilizing the diagnostic power of routine histopathology. We propose a deep-learning pipeline that classifies patient-derived xenograft (PDX) whole-slide images from the WM4237 melanoma model into six treatment stages (T0–T4, TC) using H&E tissue alone. Patch-level embeddings are extracted via the TRIDENT pipeline and encoded by three complementary foundation models (Musk, Hoptimus-1, Conchv1.5); a feature-level ensemble is then classified with a multilayer perceptron trained under focal loss and stratified splits. On patch-held evaluation the ensemble attains 0.98 accuracy (macro F1=0.98), whereas slide-held testing—which simulates unseen specimens—yields 0.86 accuracy with strong F1-scores for resistant T4 (0.86) and control TC (0.84) tumors. Transitional stage T3 shows the largest generalization loss (F1=0.68), pinpointing a biological window where morphology is most heterogeneous. Overall, histology-derived deep learning captures time-resolved treatment response while substantially reducing reliance on molecular assays, offering a rapid, interpretable, and lowcost alternative for large-scale therapeutic monitoring.

title: Meaning in Motion: Tracking Temporal Semantic Drift through Incremental Representation Spaces
authors: Chandra Kiran Guntupalli, Thomas Böhme, Murray Patterson, Mario M. Kubek
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Language constantly adapts to social and cultural change, causing words to shift in meaning over time. Understanding how such semantic drift occurs is essential for developing language models that remain accurate and contextually aware. This work presents a framework that incrementally fine-tunes a pretrained contextual encoder on time-ordered data, enabling it to learn evolving patterns of usage without full retraining. To ensure consistent comparison across time, each model’s internal knowledge is represented as a semantic similarity matrix, providing a shared geometric space for measuring how meanings move and reorganize as the model adapts. By combining principles of incremental learning and temporal language modeling, this approach offers an efficient way to observe how meanings stabilize or shift, demonstrating that incremental adaptation captures meaningful trends in linguistic evolution while maintaining temporal coherence.

title: Si Ultrathin Body Nanosheet by Sophisticated Wet Etching
authors: Wei-Jen Chen, Tao Chou, Chun-Yen Tseng, Chih-En Fan, Yi-Ming Mei, C. W. Liu
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: The ultrathin Si nanosheet (T<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">body</inf>~4.3nm) is achieved by sophisticated wet etching and CVD epitaxy for channel release and S/D doping, respectively. The ultrathin nanosheet features reduced SS of 69 mV/dec and enhanced I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf> ratio of 1.5x10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">8</sup>. The reduced SS is attributed to strong SS degradation immunity of the ultrathin body, while the enhanced I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf> ratio results from widened bandgap due to quantum confinement effect. The 1.9X I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf> enhancement at 77 K indicates that the improved mobility originates from limited phonon scattering. The ultrathin nanosheet with reduced SS and high I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">ON</inf>/I<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">OFF</inf> ratio is suitable for low power applications.

title: FLAIR: A Framework for LLM-Driven Assertion Generation and Incremental Refinement
authors: Utkarsh Choudhary, Shivam Shukla, Sneh Saurabh
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: Hardware verification using SystemVerilog Assertions (SVAs) is critical in modern design flows, yet translating natural language properties into SVAs demands significant effort and is prone to errors. We present FLAIR—a Framework for LLM-based Assertion generation and Incremental Refinement—that automates this process by integrating Google’s Gemini 2.0 Flash with Cadence JasperGold. FLAIR supports three models: a Base Model for direct generation, a Reactive Model leveraging formal feedback for iterative refinement, and a Proactive Model that systematically enriches prompts through syntactic, semantic, temporal, and sampled-value analysis. Experiments on 14 RTL designs of varying complexity show that the Proactive Model achieves correct-by-construction assertions with 92.2% success rate, substantially reducing designer effort and eliminating manual intervention.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning-Based Active Thermal Routing for Energy Storage Converters Under Renewable Uncertainty
authors: Jiawei Huang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Objective:This paper proposes a deep reinforcement learning (DRL)-based active thermal routing strategy for energy storage converters operating under renewable energy uncertainty. The proposed approach aims to address power fluctuations while improving thermal management performance and system reliability.Methods: A comprehensive co-simulation model integrating renewable energy generation, energy storage converters, and thermal routing dynamics is developed in the MATLAB/Simulink environment. Nonlinear and intermittent power profiles are generated to train a DRL agent based on the DDPG algorithm, enabling adaptive control of fan speed and coolant flow.Results: The proposed strategy is evaluated across multiple performance metrics, including temperature prediction accuracy, precision retention, energy conversion efficiency, fault warning time, response latency, and remaining useful life (RUL). Compared with conventional PID control, the DRL-based method improves temperature prediction accuracy by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5-8 \%$</tex> under varying load conditions, maintains precision retention above 90%, and enhances energy conversion efficiency by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1-2 \%$</tex>. In addition, the proposed strategy advances fault warning by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-5 \mathrm{s}$</tex>, achieves stable response performance, and extends RUL by 5-10 h.Conclusion: The results demonstrate that the proposed DRL-driven thermal routing strategy effectively mitigates the impact of renewable power fluctuations and achieves robust thermal regulation. The approach provides a self-adaptive and scalable solution for intelligent thermal management in complex and dynamic energy storage systems, with significant potential to improve both energy utilization and device longevity.

title: Soft Error Evaluation of 12nm FinFET Technology Based on COTS GPU Neutron Testing
authors: L. Artola, A. Urena, C. Elash, L. Chen, G. Hubert
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: This work presents the experimental SEE evaluation of TU104 COTS GPU designed in 12nm FinFET technology under neutron irradiations. SEU and SEFI events are discussed and compared with SEU modeling to consolidate the technology susceptibility acknowledge.

title: A Hybrid Classical–Quantum Variational Learning Framework for Breast Mass Classification
authors: Bhavaling Alias Pratima Khot, Mahadevaswamy S, Raviraj H. Havaldar, S. S. Ittannavar, Vijayalaxmi R. S, B P Pradeep Kumar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: While quantum machine learning (QML) is a burgeoning field for solving intricate computational problems, its utility for actual medical diagnosis—where hardware is currently limited—is still being debated. This study explores the viability of a hybrid classical–quantum system for identifying breast masses via a Variational Quantum Classifier (VQC). Using a real-world breast cancer dataset, we established a binary task to separate benign from malignant cases. We handled clinical variables through standard encoding and normalization, then applied Principal Component Analysis (PCA) to compress the data into a format manageable for a low-qubit system. The quantum model, built on a parameterized variational circuit, was executed via a quantum simulator and benchmarked against a standard Support Vector Machine (SVM). Our findings show that the classical SVM outperformed the quantum approach (82.08% vs. 52.50%). However, the VQC still surpassed the threshold of random chance, even when restricted to only four qubits and minimal circuit depth.

title: Minimal Linux-Bootable RV64 Core: Microarchitecture Design and SoC Simulation
authors: Pei-Shan Hsiao, Yi-Teng Zhuang, Chung-Ho Chen
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: We present Luna Core, a compact RV64IMA processor capable of running Linux on a six-stage in-order pipeline. The design improves efficiency through a decoupled frontend, out-of-order load/store unit (LSU), and pipelined virtually indexed, physically tagged (VIPT) caches, enabling high throughput without full out-of-order complexity. A custom simulation framework accelerates Linux bring-up by 6×, supporting fast full-system validation. Luna Core boots Linux and FreeRTOS, passes the riscv-tests suite, and synthesizes in 16 nm FinFET technology with a 0.28 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> area footprint, demonstrating its suitability for lightweight embedded and edge SoCs.

title: Early Identification and Segmentation of Brain Tumors Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Anand Tilagul, K S Anand Kumar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Brain tumors pose a serious medical challenge due to their complexity, high mortality, and difficulties in early detection. Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is the primary tool for noninvasive diagnosis, while advances in deep learning greatly improved tumor detection, classification, and segmentation. This survey reviews key brain tumor datasets (BRATS, TCGA, REMBRANDT, OASIS, Kaggle/ Figshare) and traces the progression of deep learning techniques from early CNNs to U-Net variants, 3D volumetric models, attention-based methods, and transformer-enhanced frameworks such as Swin UNETR and CycleFormer. It also highlights insights from recent BraTS challenges, focusing on ensemble learning, multi-modal fusion, pediatric-specific models, and strategies for robustness. Finally, the study emphasizes trends in domain adaptation, uncertainty estimation, and explainability, underscoring the importance of developing accurate, generalizable, and clinically reliable brain tumor segmentation models.

title: The xPU-athalon: Quantifying the Competition of AI Acceleration
authors: Alicia Golden, Carole-Jean Wu, Gu-Yeon Wei, David Brooks
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: The push for greater efficiency in AI computation has given rise to an array of accelerator architectures that increasingly challenge the GPU’s long-standing dominance. In this work, we provide a quantitative view of this evolving landscape of AI accelerators, including the Cerebras CS-3, SambaNova SN-40, Groq, Gaudi, and TPUv5e platforms, and compare against both NVIDIA (A100, H100) and AMD (MI-300X) GPUs. We evaluate key trade-offs in latency, throughput, power consumption, and energy-efficiency across both (i) end-to-end workloads and (ii) benchmarks of individual computational primitives. Notably, we find the optimal hardware platform varies across batch size, sequence length, and model size, revealing a large underlying optimization space. Our analysis includes detailed power measurements across the prefill and decode phases of LLM inference, as well as quantification of the energy cost of communication. We additionally find that Cerebras, SambaNova, and Gaudi have 10-60 % higher idle power than NVIDIA and AMD GPUs, emphasizing the importance of high utilization in order to realize promised efficiency gains. Finally, we assess programmability across platforms based on our experiments with real profiled workloads, comparing the compilation times and software stack maturity required to achieve promised performance.

title: Forecasting for the Future: AI-enhanced Emissions Modeling in the Built Environment
authors: Kelsi Naidoo, Simon Lucas Winberg, Mogamat Yaaseen Martin
abstract: The real estate sector is responsible for nearly 40% of global greenhouse gas emissions, making it a critical focus area for climate action and digital innovation. However, traditional carbon accounting systems remain fragmented, manual and retrospective, limiting their ability to support real-time strategic decisions. This paper presents a machine learning-powered emissions forecasting tool integrated within the GCX DASH platform. Developed in Python using ARIMA, LSTM, and linear regression models, the tool forecasts carbon trajectories while enabling interactive scenario testing through a custom Streamlit dashboard. The system allows users to apply different decarbonization levers and track their projected emissions reductions against SBTi net-zero targets, using exponential decay modeling as a benchmarking method. Applied to two anonymized real estate datasets, the system achieved a minimum Root Mean Squared Error of 91.24 tCO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> using XGBoost, outperforming conventional models and offering actionable insights for sustainability planning. By merging robust forecasting models with a real-time user interface, the tool reimagines emissions management for the built environment and exemplifies the transformative role of information technology in advancing net-zero strategies.

title: Effect of Stirring Time on the Oxidation After AC Breakdown Voltage Test of Insulation Oils
authors: Erra Aerwenny Roslan, Nor Asiah Muhamad, Ang Swee Peng
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: The AC breakdown voltage (BdV) test is widely used to assess the dielectric strength of transformer insulation oils. However, the electrical discharge and associated stirring process may affect the oxidation state of the oil. This paper investigates the effect of stirring time during the AC BdV test on the oxidation behavior of insulation oils. Pure vegetable oil (PV) and mineral oil (MO) samples were subjected to breakdown voltage testing with varying stirring times which include 0 s (no stirring), 30 s, 60 s, 90 s, 120 s and continuous stirring (t’). Oil samples collected before and after the breakdown test were analyzed using ultraviolet-visible (UV-Vis) spectroscopy to evaluate changes in oxidation level. Oxidation was assessed through peak absorbance characteristics and area under the absorbance curve (AUC) analysis over the wavelength range of 200 nm to 500 nm. The results show that stirring time does affect the oxidation response before and after BDV for both oils, with PV having higher oxidation compared to MO. Best stirring times were identified for each oil, minimizing oxidation variability and improving repeatability. The findings indicate that excessive or insufficient stirring can promote oxidation during electrical stress, emphasizing the importance of selecting an appropriate stirring duration to improve the reliability and consistency of the BdV test while minimizing oil degradation.

title: From Specification to Layout: An AI-driven Automation Design Flow for Analog Circuits
authors: Fang-Yi Liu, Hsin-Yi Lee, Tai-Cheng Lee
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: An AI-driven design and layout automation methodology is presented and validated on multiple analog circuits in a 28-nm CMOS technology. The flow comprises three main steps: 1) automatic layout dataset generation using DRC/LVS-compliant parameterized cells via Cadence SKILL, guided by circuit knowledge, 2) training a layout-level neural networks (NN) model to predict circuit performance, and 3) implementing a design iteration loop to refine circuit parameters toward target specifications. The effectiveness of the proposed methodology is demonstrated through the layout generation of a ring oscillator, a two-stage op amp, and a ring amplifier based on target specifications. Compared with manual schematic-to-layout design, this simulation-independent approach achieves up to a 150x speedup.

title: Techno-economic analysis of PV-integrated EV charging stations for a university campus microgrid
authors: Mohammad Reza Maghami, Jacqueline Lukose, Ka Fei Thang, Antik Debnath, Mooya Mabvuto, Ali Akbar Firoozi
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: The increasing adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) and renewable energy technologies requires sustainable solutions for campus energy systems. This study investigates the integration of rooftop photovoltaic (PV) generation and EV charging stations within a campus microgrid. The proposed system supports a campus load of approximately 7,350 kWh/day with a peak demand of 863 kW, while EV charging demand is estimated at 145 kWh/day with a peak load of 44 kW. Solar PV systems installed on building rooftops generate up to 1.2-10.4 GWh annually, depending on the penetration level. The techno-economic analysis indicates that the system Net Present Cost (NPC) ranges from RM 9.99 million to RM 27.4 million, with a Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE) between RM 0.282/kWh and RM 0.776/kWh under different solar deployment scenarios. Increasing EV charging demand from 0.9 to 1.1 load variation slightly increases NPC from approximately RM 9.75 million to RM 9.78 million, while reducing the cost of energy from RM 0.2757/kWh to RM 0.2753/kWh due to improved system utilization. The results show that integrating rooftop PV significantly reduces grid electricity purchases, from 1.91 GWh/year to 0.51 GWh/year, highlighting the potential of renewable energy integration to enhance sustainability and reduce grid dependency in campus microgrids

title: An Adaptive Multi-Agent Framework for Energy Optimization in Green Data Centers
authors: Rakshitha N T, Bandavya G, Vijaya Kumara Y M, Lavanya J, Vidya V, Sahil A Gowda
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Data centers account for approximately 1–2% of global electricity consumption, with projections reaching 8% by 2030. Traditional management systems rely on reactive control mechanisms that cannot adapt to dynamic conditions or renewable energy fluctuations. This paper presents an intelligent framework using multi-agent deep reinforcement learning to optimize energy use while maintaining service quality. The system coordinates three specialized agents controlling cooling infrastructure, workload scheduling, and renewable energy storage. Graph neural networks model thermal dynamics, replacing expensive computational fluid dynamics simulations. Unlike existing solutions that optimize subsystems independently, this integrated approach discovers synergistic strategies across thermal management, computation, and energy storage. We incorporate carbon-awareness directly into optimization objectives, enabling intelligent workload shifting to periods of high renewable availability. Safety constraints through Lagrangian-based reinforcement learning guarantee thermal limits and service agreements are maintained. This framework provides a blueprint for achieving carbon-neutral data center operations while preserving performance and reliability.

title: Multimodal Sentiment Analysis via Dynamic Mixture of Modal Experts and Cross-modal Gated Fusion
authors: Yi Du, Tian Sun, Cai Xu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: With the surge of social media image-text pairs, multimodal sentiment analysis (MSA) is key to capturing users’ emotions, but existing methods face three issues: poor robustness to missing/low-quality modalities [17], the “modality balance dilemma” (ignoring sample-specific modality importance), and coarse-grained cross-modal interaction [18] (failing to align image emotional regions with text keywords). To address these, this paper proposes DMoME-CmGF: a robust MSA method that uses dual feature queues for modality distribution modeling (recovering missing modalities and quantifying low-quality contributions), a dynamic expert-mixture routing network for sample-level modality weighting, a region-word fine-grained alignment/gating module for deep cross-modal interaction, and single-modal context enhancement/knowledge distillation to retain modality-specific information. Experiments on MVSA-S, MVSA-M, TumEmo, and CMU-MOSI show DMoME-CmGF outperforms state-of-the-art methods across complete/missing/lowquality scenarios: e.g., 4.9% higher accuracy than MVAN on MVSA-S (modality destruction rate 1.0), and 48.2% accuracy on CMU-MOSI’s 7-classification task, proving its effectiveness and robustness.

title: Mitigating String Current Deficit in Future-Generation 3D NAND through a Co-Design Strategy for Verify Level and Bitline Voltage
authors: Wei Cao, Xiang Yang, Henry Chin, Deepanshu Dutta
abstract: The scaling of modern 3D NAND has been driven by the rapidly increasing number of wordline layers to achieve higher bit density and lower bit cost. One expected challenge of this scaling approach is the string current deficit as the poly-Si channel length is growing while electron mobility is difficult to improve at the same pace. Increasing bitline voltage is a straightforward knob. However, it is found in this work that simply increasing bitline voltage does not increase string current as much as expected, and moreover, it can even decrease string current. To address this issue, we propose and demonstrate a verify level and bitline voltage co-design rule to recover the string current deficit for future 3D NAND.

title: Intelligent Appointment Scheduling and Resource Optimization in Smart Healthcare
authors: Shivakumar. E, Lingapuram Sreenivasulu, A.Poojitha, Anchi Charan, Nanamagari Madhan Kumar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: With the rapid growing of smart health care system, efficient appointment scheduling with limited medical resources is a necessity. Traditional scheduling policies (often based on a simple rule or heuristically designed by manual planning) do not account for the variation in patient arrivals, uncertain consultation time length, no-show pattern and heterogeneous clinical resources constraint. The corollary effect of these restrictions is prolonged patient waiting times, underemployment of medical workforce and ancillary services and sub-optimal delivery of care. To address these challenges, an intelligent appointment scheduling and resource optimization approach is introduced for smart healthcare environment in this paper. The system proposed in this work combines AI based analytics to jointly schedule patient appointments and allocate healthcare resources. Using historical clinical history along with real-time patient tracking, and resource capacity estimates, the system dynamically schedules flows to minimize wait time and optimize resource usage. Predictive models are used to forecast service times and no-show probabilities, while an optimization engine optimizes across multiple objectives (e.g., patient satisfaction, workloads and hospital efficiency). Experimental analysis on both synthetic and real-world healthcare datasets demonstrates that the proposed scheduling policy SincereSpace reduces per-patient wait by 35-42%, enhances healthcare utilisation (i.e., physician, machine) by 28-33% and drops appointment cancellations by 25% as against existing state-of-the-art-of scheduling schemes. The results show that the intelligent data-driven scheduling achieves higher operational efficiency and patient centricity of the smart healthcare system. The proposed framework provides a scalable and flexible solution to the next generation healthcare facilities, while providing improved realtime decision making and level of service.

title: Improved Power Transformer Fault Diagnosis Through Optimized Feature Selection in DGA Using SVM and KNN Techniques
authors: Given Sipho Malesa, Lutendo Muremi, Vikash Rameshar
abstract: This study enhances power transformer fault diagnosis by applying optimized feature selection in Dissolved Gas Analysis (DGA) with Support Vector Machine (SVM) and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) algorithms. We evaluated these models across four distinct transformers for accurate identification of arcing, partial discharge (PD), thermal faults and normal condition. Both SVM and KNN achieved high overall accuracy (up to 96.54% and 96.66% respectively). Notably both algorithms showed exceptional precision and recall for "arcing" faults (especially in transformer 1 and 3), and improved consistency for "normal" and "arcing" classes in Transformer 2 and 4, highlighting the benefits of optimized feature selection. While "PD" and "thermal’ faults were more challenging, these findings underline the effectiveness of DGA-based machine learning for transformer fault classification, indicating areas for further refinement.

title: Text Detection for Screen Content Images with Complex Text Characteristics
authors: Yanchao Gong, Fanhua Zeng, Maoyuan Gong, Ping Feng, Kaifang Yang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Screen content images (SCIs) generated by computers are widely used in remote education, screen sharing, online meetings, etc. Current text detection (TD) methods mainly target natural images captured by cameras that contain natural scene content. Compared to text in natural images, text in SCIs exhibits complex characteristics in back-ground, density, orientation, contrast, etc., which results in lower efficiency of current TD methods in processing SCIs. To address this issue, a matched TD method for SCIs, referred to as TD-SCI, is proposed. First, an information entropy driven constrained optimization problem is constructed and further solved by combining outlier method and simulated annealing to eliminate the influence of complex background on TD. Then, lightweight operations of corner detection, line detection, and morphological operations are further organically integrated to eliminate the influence of density, orientation, and contrast on TD. Compared with state-of-the-art methods, the TD-SCI has a higher F1 score and lower training and ap-plication costs.

title: An Intelligent System for Remote Care of Parkinson Disease
authors: Chinchana, Mangala P Shetty, Elrusha Milina D’sa, Spoorthi Shetty
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Parkinson App is an Android application designed to assist individuals living with Parkinson’s disease through intelligent, user-friendly digital support. The app integrates multiple modules to address the daily challenges faced by patients. The Parkinson’s Detection module leverages sensor data and voice analysis to aid in early identification and monitoring of Parkinsonian symptoms, offering users insights into potential signs of the disease. The Reminder module provides personalized scheduling and notifications for medication, therapy sessions, and daily routines, promoting adherence to treatment plans. The Doctor Connect module bridges the communication gap between patients and healthcare professionals, enabling remote consultations, report sharing, and continuous medical guidance. Parkinson App aims to enhance the quality of life for Parkinson’s patients by combining technology, accessibility, and care into a single mobile platform. It serves not only as a daily health companion but also as a tool for early detection and streamlined patient-doctor interaction. The novelty of the proposed work lies in bringing symptom observation, routine management, and patient–doctor communication together within one integrated and easy-to-use mobile application, offering a practical and accessible approach to digital health support for Parkinson’s care.

title: Face Detection In Real World Environment Using Deep Learning Model
authors: Bhanumathi S, Seshaiah Merikapudi, Nandini S, Manjunath S, M. Krishna Rani, Gayathri V
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Face Detection in Real-World Environments Using Deep Learning Models focuses on developing a robust face detection and recognition system capable of performing reliably under challenging conditions such as varying lighting, different face orientations, and partial occlusions.
