The system employs the Local Binary Pattern Histogram (LBPH) algorithm for facial recognition, taking advantage of its efficiency in real-time applications and its strength in capturing local texture features. OpenCV is used for face detection, while pre-trained deep learning models are integrated to improve accuracy and overall system robustness. The application includes a user-friendly interface developed using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript on the front end, and Django on the backend for handling user authentication, data storage, and face recognition operations. Key functionalities include user registration, login, a recognition dashboard, and secure logout options. The proposed system offers a dependable and provides an efficient solution for face detection and recognition, making it suitable for a wide range of real-world applications in security, surveillance, access control, and personalized authentication systems.

title: The K-BLEND Framework: A Hybrid Genomic Workflow Integrating Blast with a Kmer-Based Neural Ensemble
authors: Laksh Agarwal, Juan Koothur, Juexin Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: The proliferation of next-generation sequencing has created a pressing need for tools that can navigate the tradeoff between the accuracy of alignment-based methods and the throughput required for large-scale analysis. This paper argues that the optimal solution is not a choice between methods, but their intelligent integration. We introduce K-BLEND (Kmer BLAST-Leveraging Ensemble for Neural Diagnostics), a blueprint for a two-stage hybrid system. Our findings, based on classifying 11,000 SARS-CoV-2 genomes, reveal a nuanced, data-dependent performance relationship. A regression analysis identifies an analytical crossover point at approximately 643 genomes, after which the K-BLEND MLP ensemble becomes the more accurate tool, eventually achieving a perfect F1-score of 1.0. This finding provides the empirical basis for a workflow that uses BLAST for initial discovery and transitions to the ensemble for rapid surveillance. The K-BLEND framework thus offers a comprehensive solution, harnessing both the precision of alignment and the operational efficiency of machine learning.

title: An Intelligent System for Personalised Compliance Training Recommendation
authors: R. Seeta Rama Swamy, Deepak Sinha
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Compliance training is a fundamental component of governance, risk, and compliance (GRC) programs. However, in many organizations it remains ineffective due to the widespread use of uniform training that does not consider employee roles, behavioural risk exposure, or historical compliance performance. Such uniform approaches often lead to low engagement, limited behavioural improvement and reduced regulatory effectiveness. This paper proposes an AI-assisted, risk-based personalized compliance training recommendation system that models compliance training as an adaptive recommendation problem. The proposed framework integrates dynamic risk scoring, content-based filtering, decision tree classification and rule-based constraints to generate targeted and explainable training recommendations for individual employees based on their profiles, violation histories, and training outcomes. The system is designed to support continuous adaptation while maintaining auditability and regulatory alignment. A simulation-based experimental evaluation using a synthetic enterprise dataset compares the proposed approach with conventional generic training strategies across multiple metrics, including compliance violation reduction, risk score improvement, high-risk employee identification accuracy and training efficiency. The results demonstrate that the proposed system achieves improved risk mitigation and violation reduction while requiring a reduced number of training assignments per employee, indicating its effectiveness as a scalable and measurable compliance risk management approach.

title: Automated Fish Feeding System for Aquaculture
authors: S. Sharmila Devi, P. Srinath, S. Monish, K.N.V. Vivinrakul
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: A new fish-feeding system is proposed here, incorporating an intelligent and automatic fish-feeding mechanism using Internet of Things, along with real-time monitoring and live streaming. In this proposed design, an ESP32-CAM is utilized to watch over fish activities round the clock, streaming live images to both a computer and a smartphone interface. The fish-feeding process is further performed by a servo motor, which feeds fish only when they demonstrate adequate activities. This design has adopted IoT connectivity, which allows remote control and observation using a smartphone application. Additionally, remote monitoring using live streaming has also been incorporated. The power management is carried out with a help of a transformer, a bridge rectifier, a capacitor, along with voltage regulators.

title: Full-Stack Learning Management System with AI-Driven Course Generation
authors: Prathibha A, Vimala Devi R, Pruthviraj H M, Sandeep B Maradikolli, Srujan C
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This project is an all-inclusive smart learning ecosystem, which, through advanced Artificial intelligence is able to accelerate academic and professional growth. It is built using a modern technology stack with Next.js being used for a responsive front-end and Supabase for secure backend authentication and data management. This platform offers a single interface for students and professionals to skill up in an easy way. The app uses AI models to give the user personalized help, thus, educational support of the highest quality becomes accessible and interactive at any time of day. The platform mainly includes three different AI-based tools: Cognix Assist, Cognix Builder, and Cognix Interview. Cognix Assist acts as a specially designed educational support unit, providing users with immediate answers to their questions and individualized study directions, which eventually lead to the grasp of complex subjects. With the help of AI-guided prompts, Cognix Builder creates the professional resume of the user that meets the industry standards. Besides that, Cognix interview is well-equipped with a real-world environment; it offers the users the possibility to rehearse mock interviews, and as a result, they are able to get feedback from the trainers instantly, which is also actionable, and thus they can further their communication skills and Become interview-ready. In addition, the project is not only about individual AI tools but also about the users capability to interact with each other without interruption and in real-time. Owing to the integration of Stream Chat and Stream Video SDKs. Thus, it is possible to have a very convenient and chat room fully fueled with features, such as uploading files and showing the person who is writing through the typing indicator, and in addition, there are very good quality video and audio calling features for the sessions, where it is either one-to-one or group that the participants can use. By uniting these real-time communication channels with intelligent content generation and secure payment processing , this project becomes a complete platform that not only connects learning with reliable practice but also professional communication.

title: CFACO-Net: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework with Dual-Backbone Transformer Fusion and Metaheuristic Feature Selection for Aluminum Wheel Defect Detection
authors: K. Rajavadhani, B.Sheela Rani
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: In modern automotive manufacturing, ensuring the structural integrity of aluminium wheels is critical for safety and quality. Traditional inspection methods are slow and error-prone, while existing automated systems struggle with defect localization, generalization, and efficiency. To overcome these issues, this study proposes CFACO-Net, a novel hybrid Deep Learning (DL) model for accurate and real-time aluminium wheel defect detection. The model integrates ViGo-YOLO for fast rim detection, DualNeXt-SwinCF with DCS-TNN for rich local-global feature extraction, and Chaotic-Fuzzy Ant Colony Optimization (CFACO) technique for optimal feature selection. Moreover, a lightweight MLP is used for achieving efficient classification. In addition, the efficiency of the devised model is tested on the CAWDEC dataset, the CFACO-Net system achieves better performance than existing models by achieving 99.55 % accuracy and 99.35 % precision while delivering a dependable and adaptable solution that enables intelligent quality control in smart manufacturing environments. Overall, the evaluation results demonstrate that CFACO-Net effectively improves quality control procedures while it decreases inspection mistakes and enhances productivity in smart manufacturing systems.

title: Eight Myths on Software Engineering and GenAI
authors: Jenna Butler, Brian Houck, Margaret-Anne Storey, Travis Lowdermilk, Steven Clarke, Emerson Murphy-Hill
abstract: None

title: Steering Committee
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)

title: Wavelet-Decoupled Representation with Rotation-Domain Prior Gating for PolSAR Ship Detection
authors: Shibo Chang, Haoying Feng, Ping Lang, Hao Chang, Jian Dong, Xiongjun Fu
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Polarimetric synthetic aperture radar (PolSAR) provides multi-channel scattering responses, offering richer cues for ship detection. However, existing data-driven methods primarily focus on spatial-domain feature modeling and underutilize physically motivated priors embedded in polarimetric scattering mechanisms. To address this, we propose a Wavelet-based Rotation-Prior Gated Network (WRPGN). First, via equivalent polarization-basis rotation, we construct a rotation-domain polarimetric prior map. This map exploits polarimetric information to enhance signal-to-noise ratio, yielding reliable spatial confidence for subsequent gating. Second, we introduce a High-Frequency Prior-Gated (HFPG) block into the feature pyramid. It decomposes features into low-frequency structural components and high-frequency details, and maps the rotation-domain prior to learnable gating weights that selectively modulate the details. These modulated details are then residually fused with the structural components to reconstruct multi-scale representations that are both physically interpretable and highly discriminative. Experiments on the FPSD dataset demonstrate the effectiveness and competitive performance of the proposed method.

title: Spatiotemporal Ensemble Deep Learning for Sea Surface Temperature Forecasting
authors: Lalita Chaudhary, Shakti Sharma, Gaurav Kumar Dashondhi, Sudipan Saha, Arnab Muhuri
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Sea Surface Temperature (SST) in the Bay of Bengal exhibits rapid variability driven by freshwater discharge, monsoon winds, and frequent atmospheric disturbances. Accurate short-range forecasting of this field is important for marine operations and climate-related studies over the region. This letter presents an ensemble deep learning framework that integrates a Long Short-Term Memory network, a Gated Recurrent Unit, and a transformer-based TimeSformer branch. All three components are trained using satellite-derived SST fields from the SEVIRI Indian Ocean product to capture complementary temporal and spatial characteristics of the signal. The individual forecasts are combined using a weighted fusion strategy to generate SST predictions for a fourteen-day horizon. The proposed approach is evaluated over the Bay of Bengal and compared against several widely used deep learning models, including CNN-Long Short Term Memory (LSTM), ConvLSTM, Bi-LSTM, Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), ViT-LSTM, TimeSformer, and the earlier DualSeq model. The ensemble framework achieves the lowest prediction error, with a root mean square error of 0.55°C, a mean absolute error of 0.42°C, and an R-squared value of 0.96. Compared with DualSeq, the root mean square error is reduced by approximately eleven percent, indicating improved skill in short-range SST forecasting for this dynamically evolving region.

title: Prioritizing Expert-Like Transitions for Reward-Free Offline Imitation Learning
authors: Zhiliang Lin, Zhuangzhuang Chen, Guanming Zhu, Li Wang, Jianqiang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Offline imitation learning (IL) enables embodied agents of AI-driven automation control systems to acquire policies from demonstrations without interacting with the environment. However, collecting extensive expert demonstrations that rely on a manually designed reward function is labor-intensive, and even impractical for dynamic real-world settings. To mitigate the reliance on large amounts of reward-annotated expert data, previous works have made great efforts by leveraging both expert and suboptimal demonstrations. In this paper, we reveal that existing methods still suffer from suboptimal results for two reasons: (i) reward signals in suboptimal demonstrations are not reliable, due to these rewards derived from inexperienced annotators, thus can not reflect the actual rewards, and (ii) those suboptimal demonstrations, which are significantly distant from expert behaviors, result in low reconstructed rewards. Moreover, motivated by the recent diffusion-based foundation model, we propose Expert-Like Transition Imitation Learning (ELTIL), a novel generative trajectory augmentation method that effectively leverages diffusion models. ELTIL re-designs the reward function based on expert proximity, and then proposes amplified return conditioning for achieving high-quality trajectory generation. More specifically, instead of relying on environment-provided rewards or manually designed rewards, ELTIL first serves as a good reward labeler for transitions by measuring their distance to expert states. These re-designed rewards then serve as guidance in the diffusion process, with amplified returns contributing as conditioning values to bias generation toward expert-consistent behaviors. Extensive experiments on diverse D4RL benchmarks demonstrate that ELTIL consistently improves the performance and stability of reward learning and offline IL methods, maintaining reliability under varying noise levels, reward sparsity, and dataset distributions. These characteristics make ELTIL well-suited for deployment in robust and reliable automated control systems such as robotic manipulation and autonomous driving.

title: Foreword
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)

title: Robust Generalized Maximum Likelihood Estimator Inspired Physics-Informed Learning for Distribution System State Estimation
authors: Gang Cheng, Junbo Zhao, Jamie Lian
abstract: Current data-driven methods for distribution system state estimation (DSSE) have not fully utilized the integration of physical information to enhance statistical and numerical robustness. This paper proposes a physics-informed learning approach for robust DSSE inspired by the generalized maximum likelihood (GM)-estimator. It addresses two critical issues: enhancing robustness against gross errors or bad data, and adapting weights to leverage points for better numerical robustness. First, the GM-estimator is employed to embed power flow equations into the loss function to automatically mitigate the impact of bad data, thereby improving the physics-informed learning model's statistical robustness. Second, the projection statistics algorithm calculates appropriate weights for each measurement, including the leverage points. This mitigates the impact of the domination of leverage points on the gradient calculation and convergence within the physics-informed learning model for DSSE. The effectiveness and robustness of the proposed methodology are validated on the standard IEEE 13- bus system and a real-world three-phase unbalanced 759-node distribution system.

title: Full-aperture Imaging of curve-trajectory SAR Based on Hybrid Parametric and Non-parametric Autofocus
authors: Yi Xiong, Buge Liang, Yanghao Jin
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: SAR high maneuverability platform is one of the future development needs. However, the high maneuverability of the platform can lead to non-linear flight trajectories, which can result in severe two-dimensional spatial variability of the signal, imposing the traditional full aperture frequency domain algorithms to fail. Spurred by these limitations, this paper introduces a hybrid parametric and non-parametric full aperture imaging algorithm. The proposed algorithm uses the Two-step and range migration algorithm (RMA) for range cell migration correction (RCMC). RMA is followed by a hybrid parameterized nonlinear chirp scaling (NCS) algorithm and a non-parameterized phase gradient autofocus (PGA) algorithm to correct second-order and third-order azimuth spatial variant characteristics. Finally, a resampling (RS) algorithm corrects the remaining fourth-order azimuth spatial variant characteristic. Extensive simulation imaging results effectively validate the effectiveness of the algorithm.

title: A Novel SVPWM Strategy for Asymmetric Cascaded H-Bridge Inverters Considering Power Balancing and Current Backflow Elimination
authors: Xiaogang Wang, Ziyan Zhou, Ru Yang, Yingyi Liang, Xiaobo Meng
abstract: The Asymmetric Cascaded H-bridge (ACHB) inverter under SVPWM faces three key issues: high modulation complexity with poor scalability, power imbalance, and current backflow. Power imbalance results in uneven thermal stress, increased junction temperature, reduced device lifespan, and degraded reliability. Current backflow causes dc-link voltage fluctuations and extra power losses. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel SVPWM strategy. The strategy combines a hybrid high-low frequency modulation architecture with the staircase modulation principle and presents a novel voltage vector selection method, effectively reducing the computational complexity of the modulation algorithm and improving its scalability to higher-level topologies. In addition, based on the SVPWM strategy, a space vector power balancing control strategy is proposed. By investigating the intrinsic relationship between output power and space vectors, the strategy dynamically switches output modes, accurately monitors and proportionally distributes power among cells within the same phase, thereby enhancing the active power output capability of the inverter and system reliability. Furthermore, a strategy is designed to eliminate current backflow, so as to avoid unnecessary power losses while maintaining the quality of the output waveform. Both simulations and hardware experimental tests demonstrate the effectiveness and superior performance of the proposed SVPWM strategy compared to conventional modulation schemes.

title: Minimizing Person-Specific Variations using Cepstral Features and Supervised Clustering for Improving Stress Identification Accuracy
authors: H Haritha, C Santhosh Kumar, A Anand Kumar, K T Sreekumar
abstract: The escalating prevalence of stress disorders necessitates innovative and automated diagnostic tools. In electrocardiogram (ECG)-based stress identification systems (SIS), person-specific physiological variations do not contribute to stress-related information and often degrade classification performance. This work focuses on improving SIS performance by minimizing non-informative, person-specific variations in ECG signals through cepstral processing and supervised clustering. Person-specific characteristics in ECG signals are manifested in the frequency domain as multiplicative factors. By extracting cepstral features, these multiplicative components are transformed into additive factors using logarithmic scaling and subsequently decorrelated using the discrete cosine transform (DCT). Cepstral mean subtraction (CMS) and cepstral smoothing are then applied to further minimize person-specific variations and enhance the performance of SIS. To reduce residual intra-class dispersion, supervised clustering of deep features was performed. ECG signals from 104 participants (53 meditators and 51 individuals with clinically diagnosed stress disorders) were analyzed. The baseline system using raw spectral features achieved a mean accuracy of 71.62%, sensitivity of 52.48%, specificity of 88.74%, and AUC of 0.804. The final system incorporating cepstral smoothing and supervised clustering achieved a mean accuracy of 91.18%, sensitivity of 87.10%, specificity of 91.25%, and AUC of 0.922, representing an improvement of 19.56%, 34.62%, 2.51%, and 0.118, respectively. The proposed approach was further validated on the publicly available Wearable Stress and Affect Detection (WESAD) dataset, and similar performance improvements were observed under induced stress conditions. These results emphasize the importance of mitigating person-specific variations through cepstral processing, leading to a robust and generalizable ECG-based stress identification framework.

title: GA-Avatar: Geometry-Aware Human Avatar Modeling Based on Gaussian Splatting From Monocular Videos
authors: Lei Shi, Sen Peng, Zhiyang Deng, Wenjing Zhang, Rong Jin, Xiao Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: The reconstruction of realistic 3-D digital humans from monocular videos plays a crucial role in socially intelligent systems, enabling human–machine interaction, behavioral analysis, and immersive communication in virtual environments. However, achieving geometrically consistent and visually faithful avatars under limited views remains challenging. Existing Gaussian-based approaches lack effective geometric constraints and resulting in structural drift and appearance degradation in novel poses. To address the above problems, we propose GA-Avatar, a dual-branch reconstruction network via decoupled geometry and appearance modeling, which improves the geometric accuracy as well as rendering quality. First, we construct a high-resolution mesh in canonical space, bind Gaussian primitives to mesh vertices, and achieve personalized geometric adjustments by optimizing Gaussian positions. Based on canonical features of the Gaussians, we design a dual-branch modeling network for geometry and appearance, learning vertex deformation and color features, respectively. To improve the modeling capabilities of nonrigid deformations, we introduce pose-dependent deformation modules in both the geometry and appearance branches. In the optimization stage, we proposed photometric loss and geometric optimization based on depth and normal constraints, which effectively enhanced the structure preservation and appearance consistency of the model in different poses. We conduct rich experiments and comparisons, and GA-Avatar outperforms existing reconstruction methods on multiple datasets. GA-Avatar provides a foundation for computational modeling of human appearance and behavior in intelligent systems.

title: An Efficient Approach with Theoretical Guarantees to Simultaneously Reconstruct Activity and Attenuation Sinogram for TOF-PET
authors: Liyang Hu, Chong Chen
abstract: In positron emission tomography (PET), it is indispensable to perform attenuation correction in order to obtain quantitatively accurate activity (tracer distribution) in the body. Generally, this is carried out based on the regulated or synthetic attenuation map obtained by computed tomography or magnetic resonance imaging respectively. However, except for errors in the attenuation correction factors obtained, the additional scan not only brings in more radiation dose and/or increases the scanning time but also leads to severe misalignments induced by the body motion during and between the two sequential scans. To address these issues, based on Maximum Likelihood estimation, we propose a new mathematical model for simultaneously reconstructing the Activity and Attenuation Sinogram, referred to as MLAAS, from time-of-flight (TOF)-PET emission data only. Particularly, in the proposed model, we make full use of the exclusively exponential form of the attenuation correction factors, and consider the constraint on a total amount of the activity in some mask region. Furthermore, we analyze the well-posedness of the model, including the existence, uniqueness and stability of its solution. We propose an alternating update algorithm to solve the model, and also analyze its convergence. Finally, numerical experiments with various emission data demonstrate that the proposed method MLAAS is of numerical convergence and robust to noise, and outperforms some state-of-the-art methods in terms of accuracy and efficiency, and enables TOF-PET to have the capability of autonomous attenuation correction.

title: Dependent Task Offloading Based on OPQ-SubTransformer and Genetic Optimization for Vehicular Edge Computing
authors: Ronghui Cao, Hanyun Xiao, Tan Deng, Xingyu Du, Ying Li, Xiaoyong Tang
abstract: The development of intelligent driving and vehicle-road collaboration has increased the computational pressure on in-vehicle systems, and VEC is considered an important solution to address this issue. However, current research on edge task offloading often treats tasks as a whole without performing fine-grained optimization. A few studies decompose tasks into dependent subtasks for offloading, but the complex prioritization and dependency relationships make task scheduling and management more complicated. Meanwhile, the trade-off between energy, delay, and completion rate further complicates the optimization objectives. To address these issues, this paper constructs a joint optimization framework based on fine-grained task partitioning, utilizing a hierarchical optimization strategy to globally minimize cost. Based on this framework, the paper designs a task offloading strategy generated by a SubTransformer neural network with an Order-Preserving Quantization (OPQ) mechanism, which fully considers task priority and subtask dependency relationships. Additionally, an improved genetic algorithm (GA) is integrated to achieve adaptive allocation of compute resource blocks (CRBs) on edge servers, thereby explicitly handling task priority constraints and resource competition during scheduling. This two-tier mechanism holistically addresses constraints like task priority and subtask dependencies, achieving collaborative multi-objective optimization. Experimental results show that, compared to baseline methods, the proposed solution reduces cost by 11.75%, delay by 11.38%, and energy by 11.06%.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning Enabled Persistent Surveillance with Energy-Aware UAV-UGV Systems for Disaster Management Applications
authors: Md Safwan Mondal, Subramanian Ramasamy, James D. Humann, James M. Dotterweich, Pranav Bhounsule
abstract: Integrating Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) with Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGVs) provides an effective solution for persistent surveillance in disaster management. UAVs excel at covering large areas rapidly, but their range is limited by battery capacity. UGVs, though slower, can carry larger batteries for extended missions. By using UGVs as mobile recharging stations, UAVs can extend mission duration through periodic refueling, leveraging the complementary strengths of both systems. To optimize this energy-aware UAV-UGV cooperative routing problem, we propose a planning framework that determines optimal routes and recharging points between a UAV and a UGV. Our solution employs a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) framework built on an encoder-decoder transformer architecture with multi-head attention mechanisms. This architecture enables the model to sequentially select actions for visiting mission points and coordinating recharging rendezvous between the UAV and UGV. The DRL model is trained to minimize the age periods (the time gap between consecutive visits) of mission points, ensuring effective surveillance. We evaluate the framework across various problem sizes and distributions, comparing its performance against heuristic methods and an existing learning-based model. Results show that our approach consistently outperforms these baselines in both solution quality and runtime. Additionally, we demonstrate the DRL policy's applicability in a real-world disaster scenario as a case study and explore its potential for online mission planning to handle dynamic changes. Adapting the DRL policy for priority-driven surveillance highlights the model's generalizability for real-time disaster response. More details are available on our website: https://sites.google.com/view/ecucps.

title: Robust Fine-Grained Oriented Ship Detection for Remote Sensing imagery via Controllable Generative Pretraining_supp1-3694663.docx
authors: Xiaoping Liu
abstract: Fine-grained ship recognition in remote sensing imagery is essential for maritime applications. However, its development is hindered by two challenges: (1) the limited granularity of existing ship detection datasets, and (2) the disturbance of complex maritime conditions as well as the arbitrary ship orientations and distributions. To address the first issue, we annotated a large-scale fine-grained ship instance detection dataset (LAFI), comprising 48,717 ship instances worldwide with 49 categories. To tackle the challenges of marine disturbance and diverse ship status, we proposed a controllable generative knowledge-driven ship detection framework (COSD). It employs a controllable generative model guided by ship-marine knowledge to generate millions of synthetic images that not only preserve ship structures but also cover diverse sea and weather conditions for robust pretraining. Furthermore, a heterogeneous feature alignment decoder is designed to align multi-modal metrics of orientation and distribution features in the latent space, allowing for accurate representation of diverse ship status. Extensive experiments on two benchmark datasets showed that our method respectively increased 0.093 and 0.129 mean average precision (mAP) over SOTA methods, particularly in scenarios involving small, densely packed and arbitrary oriented ships.

title: Optimizing Power Infrastructure Reinforcement using Distributed Generators: An Economic Loss-Based Analysis of Three Installation Scenarios
authors: Gayathri Bhaskar, Sivasankari GS, Narayanan K, Mithulananthan Nadarajah
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Distributed generators (DGs) have emerged as an effective solution for supporting load growth in modern distribution systems by supplying power close to demand centers. When optimally placed and sized, DGs can reduce power losses, improve voltage profiles and enhance the load hosting capacity of the network. This study investigates the role of DG integration in managing increased demand by evaluating the economic implications of different DG installation strategies. The maximum allowable load increase in the system is determined while considering ampacity and power flow constraints. Based on this analysis, three DG installation scenarios are examined to assess their impact on economic losses associated with infrastructure reinforcement. The results demonstrate that strategic DG deployment can significantly influence the cost-effectiveness of accommodating load growth within existing distribution networks. This study provides valuable insights for distribution planners seeking economically efficient approaches to support future load expansion.

title: Mango Leaf Disease Classification through EfficientNetB5 based Deep Learning Framework
authors: Bhoomika, Sripelli Jagadish, Tashi Negi, Garima Jaitly, Neha Sharma, Sheifali Gupta
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Mango (Mangifera indica L.) is economically; this denotes that it is one of the most important fruit crops in the tropics and whose yield and quality is extremely influenced by a number of foliar diseases. The early and proper identification of these diseases is the key to the effects since it guarantees healthy crops and economic agricultural activities. This research paper suggests EfficientNetB5 deep learning model that is fine tuned for classification of 3 classes of mango leaves diseases automatically into 8 different categories namely Anthracnose, Bacterial Canker, Cutting Weevil, Die Back, Gall Midge, Healthy, Powdery Mildew & Sooty Mould. The dataset is an open-source of Kaggle with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4, 0 0 0}$</tex> high-quality images, consisting of 500 samples of each class, and organized in training (80 %), validation (10 %), and testing (10 %) groups of images. Transfer learning, data augmentation, and regularization were used to fine-tune the model in order to improve the feature extraction and the generalization. In experiments the great accuracy of 97 % was obtained, the precision, recall and F 1 -score were all 0.97. Minimal misclassifications have been indicated by the confusion matrix that depicts outstanding discriminative precision among a visually similar disease. The convergence of the training and validation plots proved to be constant with minimum loss. Altogether, the suggested EfficientNetB5-based context has a high level of reliability and efficiency and can be seen as an effective instrument to utilize a timely mango leaf disease recognition and encourage a precision agricultural approach and sustainable farming habits.

title: On the Compressibility of Fine-Tuned Attention: A Tensor Decomposition Study of PEFT Weight Structure
authors: Kasun Dewage, Shankhadeep Mondal
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) methods like LoRA rest on the premise that adaptation lives in a low-rank subspace. An increasingly popular line of work takes this further, using tensor decompositions such as Tucker and Tensor-Train to parameterise even more compact updates. Yet surprisingly little is known about whether the resulting fine-tuned weights actually possess the low-rank tensor structure these methods presuppose. We investigate this question through a careful empirical study of RoBERTa-Base fine-tuned on eight GLUE tasks. After stacking all attention projections into a single third-order tensor, we examine its spectral profile, geometric properties, and behaviour under decomposition. Three findings stand out. First, the merged attention matrices have high effective rank (above 520 out of 768), with gradually decaying singular value spectra that lack any clear low-rank cutoff. Second, naive post-hoc Tucker and Tensor-Train compressions are devastating: even a gentle 1.5 times reduction can wipe out more than 40 percentage points of accuracy. Third, the weight-change tensor, while sparse across projection types, carries task-specific geometric signatures that PCA and CKA analyses make plainly visible. These observations challenge the casual assumption that attention weights are nearly low-rank and point toward decomposition-aware training as the right path for tensor-based PEFT.

authors: David M. Hiemstra, Nelson Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: Proton induced SEU cross-sections of the SRAM which stores the logic configuration and certain functional blocks of the Versal® Adaptive SoC Processing System and Programmable Logic are presented. Upset rate in the space radiation environment is estimated.

title: A High-Performance Modular Linear Actuator With Enhanced Compensation of Cogging Torque and Friction
authors: Junyang Wang, Jinhong Dong, Xueai Li, Fenglei Ni, Kui Sun, Jinjun Xia, Baoshi Cao, Hong Liu
abstract: This article presents a compact linear actuator featuring a high force-to-weight ratio enabled by integrating all the drive, transmission, and sensing control modules. A four-guide-rod design enhances bending and torsional stiffness with low mass. An iterative method collects and compensates cogging torque of the linear actuator, ensuring reduced speed fluctuations, improved motion stability, and rapid convergence. Position-dependent friction is addressed by a hybrid Stribeck-neural network strategy, which captures residual effects to enhance low-speed smoothness and tracking accuracy. The integrated framework combines model-based and data-driven strengths to deliver precise, robust, and high-performance linear actuation and effectively mitigate dynamic disturbances.

title: Enabling Fast Polar SC Decoding with IR-HARQ
authors: Marwan Jalaleddine, Mohamad Ali Jarkas, Jiajie Li, Warren J. Gross
abstract: To extend the applications of polar codes within next-generation wireless communication systems, it is essential to incorporate support for Incremental Redundancy (IR) Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request (HARQ) schemes. For very high-throughput applications, Successive Cancellation (SC) decoding is particularly appealing for polar codes owing to its high area efficiency. In this paper, we propose modifications to SC decoders that employ special nodes to accelerate decoding. Our modifications enable the use of polar IR-HARQ with SC decoding for high throughput applications. Compared to the unmodified SC IR-HARQ scheme, our proposed approach allows us to achieve up to 66% reduction in node traversals with polar codes. Simulation results confirm that the proposed special node modifications do not cause any degradation in FER performance compared to the unmodified state-of-the art special nodes.

title: AI-Driven Agricultural Portal for Optimized Crop Management
authors: Ms.A.P. Narmadha, Pindi Jaya Ramireddy, A V Pranay Kumar
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Modern agriculture faces challenges in maximizing productivity and ensuring sustainable practices, which necessitate the integration of advanced technologies for real-time decision support. Climate uncertainties, pests, soil erosion and fluctuating markets are increasingly having impacts on agricultural production and require informed decisions by farmers. This paper proposes a solution to all these challenges by introducing a unified platform that integrates machine learning-based analytics, secure data management, and digital trading services. The suggested system is based on supervised machine learning models, which help in crop selection, yield forecast, rainfall forecast, and fertilizer prescription using historical yield data, soil parameters, and weather data. Moreover, pest and disease identification by the use of images helps farmers to detect and intervene on time. To guarantee the privacy and integrity of the data, the platform will store and transmit data via Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) and the integrity of the data will be ensured with the help of Secure Hash Algorithm (SHA-256), which prevents any manipulations with data by any third party. A trading module based on blockchains enables transparent and direct farmer-to-consumer processes, eliminating the need for intermediaries to ensure better profitability. The experimental analysis proves that the suggested system has about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 - 1 8 \%}$</tex> higher predictive accuracy and operational efficiency than the traditional methods. The findings illustrate that incorporating intelligent prediction models into secure data infrastructure is feasible in supporting sustainable and data-driven agricultural decision-making.

title: Areca Nut Recognition Using Machine Learning
authors: Pooja Bharamagoudra, Geetha R Bharamagoudar, Manjula
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Accurate identification and grading of Areca nuts are essential for maintaining quality standards and improving efficiency in agricultural practices. Traditional manual inspection techniques are often inefficient, require significant human effort, and are susceptible to errors. To overcome these limitations, this study proposes an automated Areca nut recognition system based on machine learning approaches. The developed framework employs effective feature extraction techniques combined with supervised classification algorithms to differentiate between high-quality and defective Areca nuts. Performance evaluation is conducted using multiple machine learning models, including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Support Vector Machines (SVM), and Random Forest classifiers, with assessment based on accuracy, precision, and computational efficiency. The proposed system supports farmers and agro-processing industries by minimizing manual intervention, improving quality evaluation, and optimizing the overall Areca nut processing pipeline.

title: A Clinical Feature-Guided Deep Learning Framework for Robust Dementia Severity Assessment using the Clock Drawing Test
authors: R.K. Resheka, R. K. Vigneshwar, P. Aurchana, Nithiya Baskaran
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Early identification of cognitive impairment using the analysis of the Clock Drawing Test is critical for timely dementia intervention, yet manual scoring remains insensitive to subtle spatial disorientations. Most of the existing automated systems still rely purely on convolutional neural networks, which overlook the clinically meaningful patterns that neurologists use for interpretation. These convolutional approaches often misclassify moderate and severe impairment when drawings contain digit misplacement, distortions, or hand- angle deviation. To address these challenges, the proposed work presents a clinically-guided hybrid framework that combines deep visual embedding with more than 25 handcrafted clinically-inspired features capturing stroke structure, digit morphology, and radial symmetry, which are fused and classified using an ensemble of logistic regression and random forest models. For this work, the clinical data were taken from the Shulman dataset, which contains drawings classified based on the Shulman scale (0-5). The proposed hybrid model classifies the data into three classes based on severity, namely mild/ normal, moderate, and severe dementia. The hybrid model showed a remarkable improvement over the convolution method, achieving an overall accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 6 \%}$</tex> and an AUC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 8 4 - 0. 9 8}$</tex>. This hybrid approach not only enhances performance but also provides interpretable insights into the clinical reasoning, making it suitable for real-world cognitive screening. This work demonstrates the achievability of fusing clinical knowledge with modern feature learning to improve dementia classification.

title: Mechanism Analysis of the Impact of Distributed Photovoltaic Integration on Distribution Network Overcurrent Protection*
authors: Runze Yin, Feng Zhang, Junshui Hu, Jingxuan Zhang, Huahua Jiang, Kunlin Shu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: With the continuous increase of distributed photovoltaic (PV) penetration in distribution networks, the fault characteristics of distribution networks have undergone significant changes, and traditional overcurrent protection faces severe challenges. This paper systematically investigates the impact mechanism of distributed PV integration on distribution network protection. Firstly, based on the different control strategies of Class A and Class B inverters, fault steady-state calculation models for distributed PV are established, revealing their controlled current source characteristics that are fundamentally different from synchronous generators. Secondly, three types of impact mechanisms of PV integration on overcurrent protection are deeply analyzed: the infeed effect causes unintended expansion of the protection range of the outgoing line section I, leading to the risk of mal-tripping; the outfeed effect results in reduced fault current detected by upstream protection, decreasing sensitivity or even causing failure to operate; the reverse overcurrent effect may cause mal-operation of downstream branch protection. On this basis, we quantify the system reliability risk under different PV penetration levels, discuss feasible engineering mitigation schemes, and verify the scalability of the conclusions in a standard IEEE test feeder. Finally, the influence patterns of these three effects under different PV capacities are verified through PSCAD simulations. Research shows that the weak-infeed characteristics and controlled phaseangle characteristics of PV fault currents are key factors affecting protection performance, providing a theoretical basis for subsequent protection adaptability assessment, risk management, and engineering optimization.

title: IoT for Social Safety: A Cloud-Based Fire Detection and Alert Solution
authors: Lohith C, Channaveerayya B H, G V Pradeep, Deepak T, Aditya Mahajan
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Fire incidents remain one of the most frequent hazards in residential, commercial, and institutional indoor environments, often causing substantial property loss and risk to human life due to delayed detection, limited warning mechanisms, and inadequate emergency response systems. Traditional fire alarm systems rely heavily on isolated smoke or heat triggers and local audible notifications, which are ineffective when occupants are away or unable to respond. With advancements in Internet of Things (IoT) technologies, opportunities now exist for developing distributed, intelligent detection platforms offering real-time alerts and automated safety actions. This paper presents an integrated IoT-enabled fire detection and alert solution incorporating flame and temperature sensors, multi-parameter threshold analysis, secure ID-based authentication, and automated emergency response via cloud-connected SMS and voice notifications using the ThingSpeak API. The system employs an ESP32 microcontroller for real-time sensing, dual-sensor fusion to minimize false alarms, and relay-based appliance shutdown to mitigate incident escalation. Extensive testing across 50 controlled scenarios demonstrated an overall detection accuracy of 94.1%, mean cloud alert delivery time between 2.1–5.2 seconds, and complete elimination of false alarms under healthy operating conditions. The proposed solution offers a reliable, scalable, and costeffective fire safety framework suitable for smart homes, offices, warehouses, laboratories, and educational institutions, enabling continuous environmental monitoring and rapid automated emergency response.

title: A Non-autoregressive based Multi-role Speech Synthesis Method
authors: DingYun-tao
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Non-autoregressive speech synthesis methods offer short synthesis time and high speech fidelity. Based on the Transformer speech synthesis model, this paper employs the Montreal Forced Alignment (MFA) technique for text and audio corpus, adds an enhancement module to strengthen prosodic feature representation, and achieves efficient and highly natural speech synthesis. The experimental results show that the method proposed in this paper performs better than the Transformer speech synthesis model, with the synthesized speech being closer to natural speech in terms of naturalness, clarity, and stylistic quality.

title: Experimental Benchmarking: CAN and I2C Protocols for EV BMS Communication
authors: Pavithra G, Anjana Joshi, Monica K M, M C Prabhavathi, Sharavani Math, Bhavyashree S Bhat
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This paper presents an integrated analysis of battery behavior under dynamic load conditions with a focus on state-of-charge (SoC) transitions, thermal response, and communication efficiency using CAN and I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>C protocols in electric-vehicle battery management systems (BMS). The study models realistic charging and discharging profiles, illustrating SoC rise under partial recovery, voltage stabilization, and decline during sustained load. Complementary temperature-response observations capture heating during high-current discharge and gradual cooling as load decreases, reflecting thermal dynamics critical for safety and performance. Simulink-based implementations of CAN and I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>C communication layers demonstrate comparative data-handling capability, latency, and robustness under varying operating conditions. Results indicate that CAN provides superior noise immunity and bandwidth for real-time monitoring, while I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>C offers compact integration suitable for low-power sensing modules. The combined modeling framework supports improved BMS design, enabling enhanced estimation accuracy, fault detection reliability, and overall system efficiency in EV applications.

title: High-Frequency Performance Analysis of a Compact Multi-Port Planar Antenna for Future 6G Networks
authors: S.Kavi Priya, V. Nagaraja, V.Sai Sampath Kumar Reddy, T. Revathi, C.Sai Prakash
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The fast development of the wireless communication systems to sixth-generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(6 \mathrm{G})$</tex> networks has boosted the need to have a compact and efficient antenna that can work at a high frequency and its multi-port performance is reliable. A small multi-port planar antenna is introduced in this work and its properties at high frequencies are discussed in the case of future 6G wireless services. The antenna suggested in this paper makes use of orthogonal planar arrangement, which minimizes mutual coupling but ensures stable radiation characteristics throughout the operating range. The main performance parameters, such as impedance matching, gain, radiation properties, and envelope correlation coefficient (ECC), are explored with the help of complete electromagnetic full-wave simulations. The antenna is shown to exhibit good impedance behavior, that is, with reflection coefficients less than -10 dB, a peak achievable gain of about 7.4 dB, and low ECC values across the board, which is an indicator of great diversity performance. The results obtained suggest that the designed compact planar antenna architecture has a potential to be a prospective solution in high-frequency multi-port systems in the next-generation 6G communication devices.

title: Spatiotemporal Drought Analysis Using Hybrid Evolutionary Algorithm-Optimized Deep Learning Architecture
authors: Vandana Sardar, Surabhi B P, Varshitha D, Harshitha B M, Likitha J
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The agricultural community in the semi-arid region of Bagalkot faces significant economic hazards due to the unpredictable occurrence of drought. The current system of monitoring is inefficient due to the use of global thresholds, which are inappropriate for classifying vegetation stress in the region. This paper proposes a region-specific decision support system based on a hybrid deep learning model. The core model combines a one-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN) to identify short-term meteorological variations with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) units to identify seasonal variations. Instead of using trial-and-error methods for hyperparameter selection, the paper proposes a hybrid optimization technique that combines Whale Optimization and Grey Wolf Optimization (WOA-GWO). The model is tested on new data and achieves an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 90.74% and an RMSE of 0.0296. Moreover, the severity levels were revised to show that a Vegetation Health Index (VHI) value below 36.10 represents severe drought in Bagalkot, while the global value is 20. This revised and optimized tool provides a localized approach for accurate agricultural risk assessment.

title: Prognostic Impact of Hypertension and Diabetes on PRKAA1, MAP4K4, RBP4, and ZFHX4 Expression in Endometrial Cancer
authors: Aditya Shah, Ishaan Anand, Katie Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Endometrial Cancer (EC) remains the most common gynecological malignancy and often co-occurs with metabolic disorders, including hypertension and diabetes. Although these associations are well established, their molecular and genetic understanding is much less established. In this study, we focus on four metabolism- and cancer-related genes in EC patients. Using TCGA data of 175 primary EC patients, we conducted KaplanMeier survival analyses on gene expression cohorts divided at median expression levels in UCSC Xena Browser. Overall, high PRKAA1 expression was associated with worse survival in the full cohort (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p<0.01$</tex>), despite its well known role as an AMPKmediated tumor suppressor, and its effect was also amplified in comorbid EC/hypertension patients (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.05$</tex>) - highlighting a novel insight that PRKAA1 may instead mark metabolic stress in EC rather than protect. MAP4K4, a kinase gene implicated in inflammation and glucose dysregulation, was significantly associated with poorer outcomes only in hypertensive patients (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p} =0.0155$</tex>), but not in other subgroups. On the other hand, RBP4 showed no prognostic impact in any of the cohorts, besides in diabetic patients <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p=0.0766)$</tex>, while the ZFHX4 gene was the only one to approach significance in metabolically healthy patients (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p} =0.0653$</tex>). Together, our findings reveal a context-dependent interplay between gene expression and comorbid metabolic states in EC prognosis, implicating precision medicine treatments.

title: Deep Learning-based Automated Defect Detection Approaches for Quality Assessment in Smart Manufacturing Industries: A Comprehensive Review
authors: G. R. Bharathi, Salini Suresh
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: In quality control, defect detection is an essential feature in industrial processes. Standard manual control techniques are costly, incompetent, and vulnerable to human errors. With current developments in computer vision (CV) and deep learning (DL), intellectual visual examination models present an assuring solution for automatically detecting defects. In the advanced industry, defect detection in manufacturing systems is among the major applications of artificial intelligence (AI). By using AI models, like DL, intellectual production methods have the potential to examine videos and images of production procedures, identify deviations, detect issues promptly, enhance product quality, and forecast maintenance requirements. This study presents a detailed investigation of DL based models for automated defect detection in smart manufacturing industries. All-inclusive reviews of existing machine learning (ML) and DL models are presented for detecting defects in industrial production systems. Additionally, a broad overview of industrial defect detection and the challenges involved in automated inspection is presented. Furthermore, experimental results reported in the surveyed studies are discussed to highlight the effectiveness and limitations of various methods. Finally, a conclusion on DL-based automatic defect detection in innovative manufacturing industries is presented.

title: A sub-1V, 82ppm/K Cryo-CMOS Voltage Reference based on ΔVth in 22nm FDSOI
authors: Matthias Eberlein, Johannes Weber
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: We present a precise CMOS reference in 22nm FDSOI for low-voltage cryogenic applications. The simple structure uses a self-biased amplifier whose differential input employs transistors with different threshold voltages. A resistive feedback loop scales the systematic offset from the threshold-delta, yielding a buffered output Vref. The temperature slope under cryogenic conditions is tuned by the sizing/current ratio of the input pair, which introduces a PTAT or CTAT component. Measurements from 4–75 K demonstrate operation down to VDD = 0.66 V and a stability of 82 ppm/K. With an active area of 594 μm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, the approach provides robust reference generation suitable for densely integrated cryogenic SoCs.

title: Discrete-Time Sliding Mode Control for Constrained Mechatronic Systems via an $r$-Order Difference Observer
authors: Shuyi Zhu, Yuhao Ji, Jiayi Mao, Jinliao Yan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This paper proposes a discrete-time sliding mode control (DSMC) strategy based on a finite-memory <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r$</tex>-order difference observer to address the ubiquitous issues of actuator input saturation and mismatched external disturbances in mechatronic servo systems. Traditional disturbance observers are highly susceptible to nonlinear coupling when facing actuator input truncation, which consequently leads to amplified steady-state errors or even the windup phenomenon. To circumvent this, an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r$</tex>-order difference disturbance observer is first designed based on a single-step physical measurement basis. Incorporating a cold-start data truncation mechanism, the observer explicitly derives the algebraic amplification upper bound of high-frequency measurement noise during the difference extrapolation process under the worst-case scenario, strictly adhering to the boundedinput bounded-output (BIBO) stability principle. Subsequently, by integrating the orthogonal decomposition of disturbances, a discrete sliding mode control law with mismatched disturbance feedforward compensation is formulated, where the physical saturation deviation is treated as a lumped bounded disturbance and suppressed via a robust reaching gain. Theoretical derivations confirm that the observation error dynamics are structurally decoupled from the control law, thereby ensuring the global uniform ultimate boundedness (UUB) of the closed-loop system. Subsequent simulations on a normalized mechatronic servo joint validate the robustness of this scheme across different noise levels and observer configurations.

title: Comparative Analysis of Regression Algorithms for Crop Yield Prediction
authors: Khushali Gupta, Rashi Bhati, Ayush Pandey, Roshi Saxena, Parneeta Dhaliwal
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Crop yield prediction plays an important role in agricultural planning, food security, and the efficient use of resources. With the availability of large amounts of agricultural and environmental data, it has become possible to estimate crop production more accurately. This study compares several regression-based models to predict crop yield using factors such as rainfall, temperature, soil type, crop type, fertilizer use, irrigation, and weather conditions. The dataset was prepared through data cleaning, encoding, and feature scaling before training different models, including linear regression, ridge regression, LASSO, decision tree, random forest, gradient boosting, XGBoost, and LightGBM. The models were evaluated using standard performance measures such as Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root mean square error (RMSE), and R-squared. The results show that linear and boosting-based models provided the best performance, achieving an R-squared value of around 0.91. These findings indicate that the proposed approach can effectively support better decision-making in agriculture, helping improve crop productivity, sustainability, and overall yield for farmers.

title: Early Detection of Heart and Stroke Analysis Facial Bio Marks and Location Based Medical Analysis
authors: Rakshana P, Padmapriya B, R Mohandas, Kandavel N, M Vinodhini, Karthikeyan P
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Stroke and heart attacks cause over 15 million deaths annually, with treatment delays often exceeding the critical “golden hour” in disadvantaged communities. This paper proposes a smartphone-based early detection system using facial video analysis with personalized baseline comparison. The system extracts 468 facial landmarks from 5 -second video recordings using MediaPipe Face Mesh, measuring facial asymmetry, drooping, abnormal eye movements, and skin color changes. Unlike population-based models, this approach compares each user's current facial features against their healthy baseline recorded during registration, enabling personalized risk assessment. An ensemble machine learning model combining Random Forest, XGBoost, and CNN analyzes facial features alongside medical parameters including blood pressure, cholesterol, and blood sugar levels. The 10MB TensorFlow Lite model operates offline on entry-level Android devices with 37 ms inference time, achieving 92% accuracy, 91% precision, and 89.7% recall. An adaptive triage system categorizes users into three color-coded risk levels: GREEN (healthy-personalized health advice), YELLOW (moderate risk-symptom questionnaire), and RED (emergency-automatic geolocationbased hospital routing with one-tap emergency calling). Simulation studies demonstrate up to 80% reduction in treatment delays compared to conventional diagnostic pathways. This accessible solution enables early cardiovascular risk detection without requiring internet connectivity, wearable devices, or laboratory infrastructure, making it particularly suitable for resource-limited and rural communities where timely access to emergency medical services remains challenging.

title: Adaptive NeuroDefense: Graph Neural Network and Reinforcement Learning-based Self-Healing Cybersecurity Architecture
authors: Manoj Kumar R, Srinivasan K, Rohidh S, Sibirajan D, Sakthivel G, Iniavan G
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Adaptive NeuroDefense is a cybersecurity architecture that uses artificial intelligence to predict and mitigate threats in a smart fashion combining Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and Reinforcement Learning (RL). The system represents the network behavior by means of problematic attack patterns as interlocked graphs. A counter-agent based on Deep Q-Network (DQN) makes independent choices of the best counter-measures. The self-healing mechanisms isolate the affected nodes and restore the services with minimal human intervention. It would enable real-time data streaming and automated incident response because it would be based on Kafka and Flask microservices. Broad comparison of NSL-KDD, CICIDS-2017 and UNSW-NB15 has an accuracy of 98.2 % and an average response time of 45 ms, both significantly better than the conventional methods. It is a dynamic, expandable solution to up-and-coming network-based cybersecurity problems.

title: Effects of Grid Impedance and Load on Transformer Frequency Response via in situ Measurement
authors: Stevan Engelbrecht, Johannes Cornelius Bekker, Fredrick Mukundi Mwaniki
abstract: This paper investigates, through simulation, the effects of grid and load impedance on the frequency response of a wideband model of a 16 kVA, 22 kV / 240 V distribution transformer. The aim is to provide insight into how network impedance influences in situ measurements for transformer condition monitoring. A Pseudo Random Impulse Sequence (PRIS) perturbation source delivers broadband excitation (1 - 100 kHz) while presenting high impedance at 50 Hz to protect the source from the grid. Case studies vary the Thevenin-equivalent grid impedance to represent strong, weak, and open-circuit conditions, and apply loadings up to 75% of rated capacity. Results show that grid impedance significantly affects frequency response, shifting the first resonance to lower frequencies as grid reactance increases. Load impedance has negligible effect but reduces signal-to-noise ratio at high-frequency resonances. The findings highlight the effect of network impedance in wideband transformer diagnostics.

title: Weak Magnetic Detection Method of Steel Wire Rope Based on the Principle of Spatial Magnetic Field Vector Synthesis
authors: Longfei Wu, Tong Wu, Cunlong Zheng, Lei Yi, Dapeng Huang, Guan Wang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This study introduces a novel weak magnetic detection approach for steel wire ropes based on the principle of spatial magnetic field vector synthesis. A circumferential fourloop excitation scheme is adopted. Simulation analyses are conducted on the leakage magnetic field characteristics of wire rope defects. The accuracy of the simulation model is verified through analytical calculation and physical experiment comparison. Furthermore, a complex-valued convolutional neural network (CVCNN) model is constructed to achieve defect localization and type identification. A complete physical experimental platform is built and a labeled dataset of actual defect signals is constructed. Experimental findings confirm that the proposed method effectively suppresses background interference noise, clearly characterizes defect-related information, and achieves higher detection precision and classification accuracy than existing mainstream comparison models. The minimum detectable defect size is clarified to guide engineering applications. Consequently, it provides an efficient and stable technical solution for the nondestructive testing of steel wire ropes in engineering scenarios.

title: Scalable Short-Term Solar PV Forecasting Using Hybrid Transformer-ELM Models under Heterogeneous Data Conditions
authors: Seyyed Kasra Mortazavi, Witesyavwirwa Vianney Kambale, Hussein Ahmad Ahmad, Kyandoghere Kyamakya
abstract: Accurate short-term photovoltaic forecasting is crucial for maintaining grid stability, but real-world datasets vary from clean, regularly sampled series to noisy, irregular records with numerous missing values. We propose a scalable hybrid framework that combines transformer-based temporal encoders (PatchTST and TS-BERT) with ridge-regularized Extreme Learning Machine (ELM) regressors. Notably, PatchTST+ELM performs best on clean, structured data, while TS-BERT+ELM is more robust for irregular and noisy data. Across five European national-scale PV datasets with meteorological covariates, the hybrid models reduce mean absolute error by 12–18% versus LSTM, GRU, and statistical baselines, gain a further 7–12% from weather features, provide 80% prediction intervals with 90–94% coverage, and achieve sub-3 ms inference with 5–7× faster updates than full retraining.

title: Proposal of a 3D Position Estimation Method by Region-based Matching of Stereo Images using a Two-Pass Scan Clustering Method
authors: Kazuma Kato, Hideki Toda
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Mechatronics, Control and Robotics (ICMCR)
abstract: Stereo matching is essential for obstacle detection and understanding road conditions in autonomous driving; however, matching between stereo images can be computationally expensive and sensitive to brightness changes. In this study, we perform stereo matching using regional features to reduce the matching load, and we propose an image segmentation method that mitigates the influence of brightness changes. We present results on stereo image pairs with complex backgrounds, showing that the proposed approach can detect corresponding points robustly in the presence of shadows and gloss, while suggesting efficient processing with reduced computational cost. As future work, we will further discuss improvements to the algorithm and its applications.

title: Redefining AI Architecture with NAND Flash
authors: Hao-Zhi Lee, Ming-Hui Wang, Yu-Hao Wang, Chia-Ming Hsu, Yi-Juan Du, An-Cin Li, Tso-Yuan Chen, Kai-Wei Tsou, Shiu-Ming Tsao, Jian-Ping Syu, Wei Lin
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: As Agentic AI accelerates data demands, Phison aiDAPTIV utilizes high-endurance NAND Flash as a memory expansion layer to optimize training, inference, and RAG applications. To overcome PCIe bandwidth bottlenecks, we introduce Computational Storage Devices (CSD), which offload tasks like weight updates and vector searches directly to the storage level. These cases demonstrate that CSD is a pivotal evolution for scaling efficient, AI-centric infrastructure.

title: SAATH:Smart Alert and Tracking Hardware
authors: Savitha MM, Supriya G, Varun Kumar G, Varun Kumar K A
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This project presents a Smart ID Card system designed to enhance personal safety and asset tracking using RFID, GPS, and ESP32 microcontrollers. The Smart ID Card includes an RFID tag for entry and exit logging, and a GPS module for continuous location tracking. One ESP32 controller within the card collects and transmits data, while another serves as a gateway to send information securely to the cloud via Wi-Fi. The system uses the Telegram messaging platform to deliver real-time alerts when an individual moves outside predefined safe zones or interacts with an RFID reader. This enables instant communication with guardians or security personnel. The proposed solution demonstrates how low-cost hardware and widely used communication tools can be combined to create a reliable, efficient, and user-friendly safety monitoring system.

title: A Table of Contents-Based Novel Architecture for Compute-Aware Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Harshit Kondle, Ruhaan Singh, Sai Satheshkumar, Saketh Nandam, Yuvaan Chandra, Anvit Koppella, John McKee, Mihai Boicu, Kamaljeet Sanghera
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems often embed entire corpora upfront, which is computationally expensive in constrained environments such as military deployments. We introduce a novel architecture, TOC-Based Load-Time Section Filtering (TLSF), which parses structured documents like DAFMAN 36-2664 using a hierarchical TOC and routes queries to relevant sections before embedding. Our pipeline comprises three modules: TOC parsing, probabilistic keyword-based TOC indexing, and lazy embedding. We evaluate TLSF across latency, compute cost, retrieval precision, and memory usage. Our results demonstrate 3.15x latency reduction and significant compute savings over brute-force RAG, while increasing retrieval precision by 19%. This approach is promising for low-resource natural language processing applications in policy-intensive domains.

title: LUCID: An Explainable Multi-Stage Framework for Lung CT Image Analysis and Clinical Outcome Interpretation
authors: Rachana.S, Kokila Paramanandam
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Lung cancer continues to be one of the most common causes of death globally, where timely detection and individualized treatment are key to better outcomes. The majority of the current medical image analysis platforms offer black-box predictions or correlations only. To address this, we introduce a multistage analytical pipeline that integrates lung CT segmentation, explainable AI (XAI), clinical data causal inference, and a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) chatbot to provide explainable and patient-specific explanations. We utilize two complementary datasets: the NSCLC Radiogenomics Archive (TCIA) for patient-specific clinical and survival data to facilitate causal modeling, and the Medical Segmentation Decathlon (Task 6) for expert-annotated lung CT scans to facilitate robust segmentation and interpretability. Our SE-Attention UNet segmentation pipeline achieved a Precision of 0.9144, a recall of 0.8647, an F1-score and Dice coefficient of 0.8888, and an IoU of 0.7999.Grad-CAM++ and attention maps improve interpretability by identifying clinically relevant tumor areas. Contrary to standard reports that only summarizes associations, causal inference gives actionable cause–effect knowledge e.g - smoking status highly increases the risk of mortality and recurrence, and radiation therapy and adjuvant therapy have protective roles. The relationships differ between subgroups, requiring the need for individualized care. Lastly, a domain-expert RAG chatbot combines segmented image outputs, causal results, and filtered medical knowledge to provide context-aware explanations for clinicians and patients. It integrates the outputs of imaging, causal reasoning, and interactive knowledge retrieval, this model extends existing models to provide a new, interpretable, and clinically meaningful basis for lung cancer diagnostic analysis

title: Structure-Aware Mamba Sequence Modeling for Image Super-Resolution
authors: Jian Xu, Jiaqi Liu, Xiaoli Meng, Jingang Shi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: This paper introduces a structure-aware Mamba sequence modeling approach for image super-resolution (SR). While Mamba has shown success in NLP with linear complexity and long-sequence modeling, its application to visual tasks faces challenges due to the two-dimensional structural constraints of images. To address this, we propose a lightweight structure-aware prior module that quantifies regional importance using gradient and local variance cues. Based on this prior, we design a dualbranch Mamba block (DCMB) that jointly models local structural details and global contextual dependencies, fused via an adaptive gating mechanism. Furthermore, an adaptive scanning path strategy is introduced to improve structural continuity and reduce computational redundancy. Experiments on Urban100 demonstrate that our method achieves a 0.34 dB PSNR gain with only 0.84 M parameters and improves inference speed by approximately 32%, validating the effectiveness of structureaware sequence modeling for efficient visual reconstruction.

title: Aero-Engine Remaining Useful Life Prediction Based on CNN-BiLSTM Bidirectional Temporal Modeling
authors: Jingzhi Luo, Lu Yang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Accurate prediction of the remaining useful life (RUL) of aero-engines is crucial for ensuring flight safety and enabling predictive maintenance. To address the coexistence of local degradation features and long-term temporal dependencies in sensor monitoring data, this paper proposes a hybrid prediction model that integrates a convolutional neural network with a bidirectional long short-term memory network (CNN-BiLSTM). The model extracts local spatial patterns from sensor sequences through the CNN layer and captures bidirectional long-term dependencies during the equipment performance degradation process via the BiLSTM layer, thereby achieving comprehensive modeling of degradation dynamics. Experimental results on the NASA C-MAPSS benchmark dataset demonstrate that the hybrid deep learning model achieves excellent performance on the FD001 subset, with a coefficient of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$({R}^{{2}})$</tex> of 0.8641 and a root mean square error (RMSE) of 10.27, significantly outperforming other models used for comparison. This work provides an effective technical approach for the intelligent health management of aeroengines.

title: A Global Vision-Based Mobile Obstacle Perception and Global Path Optimization Method for Unmanned Vehicles
authors: Shuang Yu, Liangxi Xie, Ming Xiao, Zihao Guo
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: In dynamic environments, unmanned vehicles are required to achieve safe and efficient autonomous navigation in the presence of dynamic obstacles such as workers and transport vehicles. This paper proposes a risk-aware global path planning algorithm based on the historical trajectories of dynamic obstacles under a global perspective. By collecting multiple historical motion trajectories of dynamic obstacles, the algorithm infers the probability of their possible motion routes according to the current states of dynamic obstacles, which is then applied to the global path planning of unmanned vehicles. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can generate effective dynamic obstacle avoidance trajectories and reduce potential collision risks. Compared with traditional methods, the method presented in this paper significantly improve safety of the planned paths.

title: Bridging Extreme Data Gaps: SAR-Optical Fusion Guided by Dual-Temporal SAR Differences
authors: Zheng Ge, Xiaoqing Wang, Jie Wang, Ming Tao, Peng Qin, Xiaopeng Chao, Tianwu Lin, Yuting Zhu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Continuous and complete optical observations are essential for agricultural monitoring, phenology analysis, and land-surface assessment.
