In subtropical and tropical regions, persistent large-scale thick cloud cover frequently causes extended periods with few available optical image pixels, making traditional cloud removal and optical reconstruction methods that depend on spatially and temporally adjacent cloud-free data inapplicable. Similarly, existing SAR-optical fusion and multi-temporal optical approaches require partially cloud-free target imagery. As a result, they become ineffective in scenarios with extreme cloud coverage (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\geq$</tex-math></inline-formula>80%) or during prolonged periods of optical data absence. To address this challenge, we propose TDSAR-DR, a dual-temporal SAR-difference-guided optical data reconstruction framework for reconstructing complete optical imagery at the target time without requiring any usable target-time optical observation as model input. Modulated Fusion (TV-SMF) module integrates reference optical information with time labels and temporal change cues extracted by a Time Difference Enhancement Module (TDEM). These dual-temporal SAR derived cues explicitly encode the direction and magnitude of land-surface dynamics, providing physically grounded guidance for a directionally informed and quantitatively consistent transformation toward the target time. Then, a SAR Guided Structural Refinement Module (SGR-M) exploits detailed SAR spatial cues to improve the structural fidelity and spectral consistency of the reconstructed target-time optical image, thereby producing robust results even when optical observations are missing for extended periods. Extensive experiments on subtropical farmland datasets show that TDSAR-DR achieves better qualitative and quantitative results under extreme cloud coverage, outperforming current SOTA SAR-optical fusion models. TDSAR-DR achieves a PSNR of 27.134 and SSIM of 0.947, surpassing other the state-of-the-art methods. More importantly, the framework enables reconstruct continuous cloud free time series, producing temporally consistent optical imagery when optical data are fully missing for long periods.

title: An Adaptive and Lightweight Hybrid Beamforming Design for Massive MIMO
authors: Maoxia Xu, Yuanhui Liang, Chan-Tong Lam, Qingle Wu, Benjamin K. Ng, Jin Hou
abstract: Traditional deep unfolding-based hybrid beamforming designs for Joint Communication and Sensing (JCAS) often suffer from prohibitive computational overheads derived from extensive step-size multiplications and are limited by fixed task weights, which prevent the system from adapting to dynamic channel and sensing environments. To address these challenges, this letter proposes a lightweight intelligent beamforming algorithm comprising two synergistic modules. First, at the algorithmic architecture level, we propose a Multiplication-Free Projected Gradient Ascent (MF-PGA) mechanism that transforms step-size scaling multiplications into hardware-friendly shift-and-add operations based on an Additive Powers-of-Two (APoT) discretization scheme, while preserving the full-precision gradient computation essential for beamforming accuracy. Second, at the optimization strategy level, we propose a meta-learning-based Balancer Neural Network (BalancerNN) that dynamically generates optimal task weights according to real-time channel conditions. Step-size parameter storage requirements are reduced by 87.5%, and all step-size-related multiplications are eliminated during inference. Furthermore, by mitigating the dominance of gradients under high signal-to-noise ratios through dynamic weighting, the method improves sensing accuracy by approximately 30%, achieving a balance between communication and sensing.

title: Reduced-scale test method for dynamic responses of bundle conductor line following ice-shedding
authors: Bo Yan, Xinrui Zhou, Wei Zong, Huachao Deng, Jin Li, Ming Hua, Xiaoqian Ma
abstract: A reduced-scale test method for dynamic responses of bundle conductor line following ice-shedding is proposed. In this method, hollow cylindrical electromagnets are used to absorb and release mass blocks modelling ice on and ice-shedding from sub-conductors, multiple cameras are utilized to capture the vertical, transverse and torsional vibrations, tension sensors to measure tensions in all sub-conductors, and three-component force sensors to measure the reaction force at hanging points. Without simplifying the bundle conductor line into a single one which was employed in previous works, the proposed reduced-scale model can satisfy the similarities of bending and torsion stiffness at the same time, model the torsional vibration induced by ice-shedding from sub-conductors, and record tension in each sub-conductor. Reduced-scale test platform for a twin-bundle conductor line was established and utilized to study the dynamic characteristics of the twin-bundle conductor line following partial, unzipping and asynchronous ice-shedding from sub-conductors. The method can be extended to investigate dynamic characteristics of bundle conductor lines following any pattern of ice-shedding.

title: CleanAll: Efficient Object Collection Route Planning from LiDAR Point Clouds Transmission in Cleaning Scenarios
authors: Muhammad Nawaz Khan, Sokjoon Lee, Ali Alqazzaz, Mana Saleh Al Reshan, Asadullah Shaikh, Mousa Alalhareth
abstract: Due to technological advancements, today’s world is rapidly changing from traditional and legacy approaches to smarter and more autonomous techniques. Intelligent systems and super-connectivity make possible the era of pervasive services, where these services can be used by anyone, anywhere, and at any time (AAA). These advancements have completely transformed modern living, increasing convenience and providing greater comfort. Robotic cleaning is one such revolutionized area where self-driving robots and automated systems are used to navigate and clean dirt. 3D mapping adapts changes based on visual conditions to detect and remove waste obstacles from the location. These robots are useful for collecting waste and cleaning the floor. In this research work, we have proposed a LiDAR-based cleaning robotic system called ”CleanAll: Efficient Object Collection Route Planning from LiDAR Point Clouds in the Cleaning Scenario.” It is an automotive system that calculates an optimized path from its 3D point-cloud data to collect some objects through its object collector. CleanAll moves over the optimized path using point cloud data along with a dual-antenna GNSS receiver and an IMU. A defined data set is already used to compare and detect object shapes, and an extended Kalman filter is used to obtain the robot’s position. CleanAll is more responsive, and its quick decision-making makes it a better option for cleaning scenarios where dirt and trash are scattered throughout areas. The optimized path used by the robot is not only useful in finding the shortest path, but it also saves time and consumes the minimum energy by following these short paths for trash collection. CleanAll is evaluated in three different scenarios, collecting all objects (scattered/line-by-line) with an average standard error rate of 0.4593. To assess performance on other parameters, it has been tested with two standard datasets, ACIN and IPA. The TPRs have 0.75, and the FPRs have been recorded as 0.40 in ACIN, while the TPRs in IPA have 0.7 and 0.4 in the same scenario. This means that CleanAll performs better using the ACIN data set, achieving higher TPRs with lower FPRs. All these experiments illustrate that CleanAll performs better using the ACIN data set, which aims to recognize and clean trash more precisely and efficiently.

title: Global Token-Driven Multi-Scale Forecasting with Dual-Attention Fusion for Multivariate Time Series
authors: Rong Chai, Zhiqiang Fan, Caiyi Yang, Yanchen Liu, Hong Chen, Qianbin Chen
abstract: Real-world multivariate time series often exhibit multi-scale temporal dynamics and intricate inter-variable dependencies, making long-term forecasting particularly challenging. In this work, we propose a global token-driven multi-scale forecasting framework with dual-attention fusion. To capture multi-scale periodic patterns, the input sequence is segmented into multi-scale patches based on candidate periods, with the dominant ones derived via fast Fourier transform. Global tokens are then introduced as shared representations to integrate information from the temporal and variable dimensions, and a multi-scale patch-token interaction module is designed to establish interactions between the patches and global tokens, enabling the capture and aggregation of temporal dependencies across different scales. A dual-attention fusion module, employing both self-attention and cross-attention mechanisms, is then proposed to capture intrinsic and context-aware variable correlation among variables. To integrate cross-variable and cross-scale information into patch representations, a global information fusion module is designed. Finally, a period-aware weighting approach is devised to adaptively fuse the multi-scale predictions. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves state-of-the-art performance across various real-world datasets.

title: Inter-class Enhanced Feature Correlation for Adaptive Online Knowledge Distillation
authors: Jianping Gou, Hongfang Zhu, Lan Du, Weihua Ou, Wu Chen, Zhang Yi
abstract: Online knowledge distillation has emerged as a powerful technique for training multiple student networks concurrently through online learning, fostering their performance enhancement. However, existing methods in this domain predominantly concentrate on single feature maps at the feature knowledge level, overlooking the utilization of relational knowledge during the online distillation training process. We argue that relational knowledge, which encapsulates rich and valuable information, merits exploration. We believe that leveraging relational knowledge in online knowledge distillation can significantly improve model performance and enhance model generalization. Thus, we introduce a novel online knowledge distillation framework termed Inter-class Enhanced Feature Correlation for Adaptive Online Knowledge Distillation (ICOKD), which emphasizes the construction of feature correlations from an inter-class perspective. Specifically, we estimate the correlation between different inter-class samples within the feature spaces of each network and facilitate the transfer of relational knowledge between them. Before computing feature correlation, we employ a feature enhancement module to enrich features, thereby providing richer information for the calculation of inter-class sample correlation. Subsequently, inter-class samples are selected from the enriched features within each student network to calculate feature correlation, enabling students to learn from each other. Moreover, we design an adaptive distillation module at the logits level to facilitate effective learning in each student. By integrating these three modules into a unified training framework, we promote the generalization of student models. Experimental evaluations on various datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed ICOKD method, achieving a test accuracy of 74.21% on CIFAR-100 for ResNet32. Our code is publicly avaliable at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/9jiujiu9/ICOKD</uri>.

title: The Application of Robotics and Automation in Smart Agricultural Manufacturing: Achieving Precision Operations and Maximizing Yield
authors: Yu Hai-Yang, Yang Tao
abstract: This paper introduces a novel approach to the application of robotics and automation within Smart Agricultural Manufacturing—an overarching industrial paradigm that elevates traditional precision farming into a fully autonomous, deterministically controlled production ecosystem. Rather than treating automated farming as a collection of isolated mechanical tasks, we introduce a scalable, hybrid multi-modal framework focused on robotic systems to optimize the entire production lifecycle, from automated soil preparation to robotic harvesting and post-processing. By integrating Cyber-Physical System (CPS) data streams-including real-time telemetry from autonomous vehicles, robotic actuator data, environmental sensors, and logistics-our system establishes a high-fidelity digital shadow of both the agricultural environment and the automated machinery operating within it. Tailored for large-scale robotic fleets, the framework utilizes hierarchical feature learning to segment critical autonomous operation phases.We propose a dynamic robotic task allocation algorithm, inspired by Dijkstra's shortest path, to engineer optimal ``field-to-factory'' automated workflows, minimizing operational latency and maximizing machine uptime. To handle the high-dimensionality of data from on-board robotic sensors (e.g., LiDAR, hyperspectral cameras), a novel geometric feature selection mechanism eliminates redundancy while preserving latent indicators of crop health and operational efficiency. These refined attributes are projected into a probabilistic state space, allowing individual robotic units or automated zones to exhibit distinct performance signatures. Central to our approach is the construction of an operational similarity graph, which facilitates community detection across the robotic network. These clusters reveal shared optimization strategies, such as superior robotic cultivation protocols and resilient automation configurations. Finally, a ranking-based recommendation engine synergizes local robotic sensor data with global cluster insights to drive prescriptive actions for the autonomous fleet. Validated on an extensive dataset of over 1 million records detailing robotic field operations, our framework achieves a superior average yield forecasting accuracy of 89.2% (a 14% improvement over leading baselines) and a Balanced Error Rate (BER) of 0.5665 for production clustering. These scale-independent metrics prove its efficacy in achieving precision operations and transforming traditional farming into a resilient, high-yield automated manufacturing system.

title: DetO-RAN: Dual-Timescale Resource Allocation for Flows in Deterministic Open RAN
authors: Kai Qiao, Hao Xiong, Qinding Wang, Zihao Zhang, Hongchao Wang, Weiting Zhang, Dong Yang
abstract: Deterministic transmission and computation are essential for open radio access networks (O-RANs) to support latency-critical and computation-intensive applications. How ever, existing O-RAN and time-sensitive networking integrations mainly focus on deterministic guarantees in wired fronthaul transmission, while lacking a unified mechanism to coordinate wireless transmission, wired transmission, and computation. This limitation makes it difficult to provide bounded end-to-end latency under time-varying wireless channels and increasing computational demands, which reduces flow scheduling success rates and causes resource wastage. In this paper, we propose a hierarchical deterministic O-RAN framework, named DetO RAN, which ensures deterministic transmission and computation for flows through a unified queuing and resource allocation mechanism. DetO-RAN introduces a three-queue model (i.e., wireless, wired, and computing queues) to support transmission and computation of flows, and establishes a closed-loop resource allocation process based on model training, inference, and updating. Based on this framework, we formulate a multi-objective optimization problem aiming to maximize the flow scheduling success rate and resource utilization. Due to the time-varying wireless channels, strongly coupled resources, and network dynamics, traditional optimization algorithms are inefficient. Thus, we decouple the problem into a radio resource block (RB) allocation subproblem as well as a time slot and computing re source allocation subproblem. Furthermore, we propose a meta learning-based dual-timescale resource allocation (ML-DTRA) algorithm to solve them. Specifically, ML-DTRA performs RB allocation via a graph neural network at a small timescale and makes time slot and computing resource allocation decisions via deep reinforcement learning at a large timescale. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that the ML-DTRA algorithm significantly improves the scheduling success rate by 12.7% and the resource utilization by 15.9% compared with state-of-the art benchmarks, showing its effectiveness in supporting end-to end deterministic transmission and computation while improving resource efficiency in dynamic O-RAN environments.

title: Three-Dimensional Geometric Information Based Grasping Points Computation and Trajectory Planning for Human-Mimic Robotic Arm
authors: Monika Vyas, Amit Kumar, Ashish Kumar Bhandari
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: The computation of grasping points and trajectory planning are crucial phases for autonomous grasping of unknown real-time objects using robotic arms, particularly for multi-fingered human-like manipulators. These operations should be accurate and fast enough to match the real-time requirements. Although deep learning techniques yield satisfactory grasping performance but they are often limited to trained object classes and require heavy on-board computation. Existing geometric grasp planning approaches typically rely on simplified grippers or object symmetry assumptions, which limit their applicability to low degree-of-freedom (DOF) hands. In this paper, we present a fast and accurate geometry-based novel method to compute grasping points for a human like five-fingers robotic arm to grasp any random object. The proposed method explicitly computes thumb and finger grasping points using visible surface geometry extracted from RGB-D data without assuming object symmetry or object-specific models. A subdivision-based trajectory planning and smoothing approach is adapted and integrated into the framework to guide the fingertips smoothly toward the computed grasping points for stable grasping. A deep learning–based instance segmentation module is used only for object detection and masking, whereas grasp synthesis and trajectory planning are entirely geometry-driven. The proposed method is implemented and experimentally validated on a 20-DOF in-house developed robotic arm using a Kinect RGB-D camera. Experimental results demonstrate approximately 12% reduction in cumulative grasping point error and 17% improvement in grasp execution time compared with state-of-the-art methods. Statistical validation using a two-sample t-test confirms the superiority of the proposed method with 95% confidenc

title: Terahertz Band for High-Altitude Platform Station Communications: Performance Analysis, Potential, and Challenges
authors: Mikail Erdem, Khalid Qaraqe, Mohamed-Slim Alouini
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: The Terahertz (THz) band (0.1-10 THz) attracts attention due to its massive bandwidth, potentially enabling ultra-high data rate with ultra-low latency. High-altitude platform stations (HAPSs) are envisioned as nodes of a non-terrestrial network (NTN) that complements terrestrial networks, particularly for connecting the unconnected and addressing temporary needs, such as disaster relief. THz HAPS communication is a promising solution for NTN, since HAPS is quasi-stationary in nature, located in the stratosphere, thereby avoiding beam misalignment and molecular absorption loss issues in the THz band. In this paper, we provide an overview of THz band HAPS communication, considering HAPS-to-ground, HAPS-to-HAPS, HAPS-to-satellite, and HAPS-to-unmanned aerial vehicle links. We present simulation results of outage probability, ergodic capacity, and spectral efficiency under beam misalignment fading and turbulence fading in comparison to millimeter wave and sub-6 GHz bands. In addition, we summarize existing THz HAPS studies to reveal ongoing efforts. Moreover, we discuss open issues and research directions for THz band HAPS-related links. Overall, this paper draws a clear picture of THz HAPS communication by reporting its potential and challenges.

title: An Energy Balancing Fuzzy based Routing Algorithm for Energy-Efficient IoT Networks
authors: Shilpa A N, Jayant Nandwalkar, P. Susheelkumar Sreedharan, Viji K, Rajeswari P, Sridhar N
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: We have seen the wide proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in every-day that has bought to the fore-front concerns regarding optimizing energy utilization and routing paths along with scalability factors in a constrained environment. In the world of IoT, with multitudes of autonomous sensing nodes continuously communicating, it’s a great challenge to maintain balanced energy consumption with reliable data communication path. It is observed that under dynamic conditions, the existing traditional routing cannot adapt to the uneven load distributions and cannot be relied on. Hence, we propose an enhanced fuzzy based multi-criteria-based routing algorithm F-EVM++ which is energy efficient and provides for stable IoT communication. In the proposed approach, we have integrated the Fuzzy MAIRCA (Multi-Attribute Ideal–Real Comparative Analysis) and Fuzzy Eigenvector approaches to evaluate the decision criteria such as residual energy, distance to the sink under varied network conditions. Further we have incorporated a dynamic cluster formation algorithm called as Adaptive DBSCAN (Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise) algorithm which takes into account the balance energy and the spatial distribution of sensing nodes. To facilitate the above algorithm, we make use of an Energy Balancing Feedback (EBF) mechanism that continuously monitors the residual energy and ensures a controlled re-clustering process to effect minimal node deaths and even-out the load distribution from time to time. The collaborative mechanism of all the three algorithms enables intelligent re-clustering and minimum-cost path selection for data movement in real time. We have implemented the above approach using MATLAB@2024b in a simulation network of 100 sensing nodes employing random distribution. The simulation results are quite encouraging and suggests enhanced energy utilization and a prolonged stable network lifetime in comparison to the existing approaches namely F-EVM, DECR and MT-MAC protocols.

title: Superior Performance of Online Learning in Breast Cancer Classification: An AI-Driven Approach for Diagnosis and Prognosis
authors: Umme Haani A Haliyal, R. Kokila
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Breast cancer is a tumor that arises in the breast that is commonly overlooked and has resulted in the death of thousands of women. Artificial intelligence has simplified the process of diagnosis and prognosis, which is further categorized into machine learning. This advancement has made the detection process quicker than traditional methods, which can potentially increase the risk of fatalities. Approximately 15 algorithms were trained and evaluated using the Wisconsin Breast Cancer Dataset (WBCD) to assess their accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score. The dataset includes 569 instances and 30 features, such as the radius, texture, and concavity. It also has a split of 62.7 % benign and 37.3% of malignant cases. The Stratified 10-Fold Cross-Validation were employed to ensure reliable performance estimation and quality assurance. Logistic Regression achieved the highest mean cross-validation accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97.54 \% \pm 1.95$</tex>, while the Passive Aggressive Classifier achieved a mean accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$96.67 \% \pm 2.41$</tex> and consistent high precision, recall and F1score. While batch learning models are performed well on static datasets, the Passive Aggressive Classifier has the advantage of online learning capability and low computational cost making it a better option for real time clinical decision support systems.

title: Benchmarking Machine Learning Models for Cloud Resource Forecasting: LSTM vs. SVR, Random Forest, XGBoost, and Prophet
authors: Lusani Mamushiane, Mduduzi C. Hlophe, Hlabishi Kobo, Albert A. Lysko, Joyce Mwangama
abstract: Effective cloud resource forecasting is essential for optimizing virtual machine (VM) provisioning in increasingly dynamic and resource-constrained environments, especially now with the advent of 5G/6G networks. This paper evaluates a set of machine learning and deep learning models (LSTM, SVM, Random Forest, XGBoost, and Prophet) for VM workload prediction using real-world data. The analysis emphasizes the trade-off between forecasting accuracy and computational complexity, two critical aspects for real-time and scalable deployment. Results show that while LSTM achieves superior accuracy, it incurs significant training overhead. Random Forest provides a more balanced trade-off, maintaining competitive accuracy with lower computational cost. These findings inform model selection in contexts like 5G/6G base station association, network slicing orchestration, multi-access edge computing, intelligent handover decisions, and dynamic service function chaining, where both efficiency and precision are vital for maintaining service-level guarantees under constrained resources.

title: Enabling Logic-in-Memory (LiM) Functionality with Junctionless FeFETs
authors: Neeraj Samant, Aarya Ameet Agavekar, Dhruv Ganesh, Rohit Kumar Nirala, Manish Gupta, Abhinav Kranti
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: Implementing logic (NOR/NAND) by utilizing the polarization of HZO ferroelectric (Fe) layer in field effect transistors (FETs) opens new possibilities for realizing logic operations within memory. Despite the relatively simpler fabrication process, junctionless (JL) Fe FETs exhibit a degraded State 1 and are unsuitable for implementing logic-in-memory (LiM). The drawback is addressed through a co-optimization of channel doping (10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup> instead of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">19</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup>) and gate workfunction (midgap 4.7 eV rather than 5 eV) which enables NOR/NAND operations in JL FeFETs and their adaptation in LiM modules.

title: Design of a 3D Braiding Online Tension Monitoring Spindle Based on Multi-Sensor Fusion
authors: Haowen Jiao, Keze Xu, Yujing Zhang, Zhuo Meng
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: In view of the problems of insufficient measurement accuracy, large system size, and delayed data acquisition in traditional yarn tension monitoring methods during 3D braiding, a tension monitoring system based on multi-sensor fusion technology and combined with the original slider-type spindle is designed. An adaptive tension fusion algorithm based on vibration perception is also proposed. Based on the stm32 main processor and BLE wireless transmission, various sensors such as laser ranging sensors, micro pressure sensors, and inertial modules are deployed to achieve online acquisition of spindle yarn tension data during 3D braiding. The Sigmoid function is used to continuously reconstruct the dynamic model of spindle pulley displacement-yarn tension mapping, eliminating the singularity of the observation matrix. Considering the inertial impact on the micro pressure sensor during spindle trajectory changes, an adaptive weight function is utilized to dynamically adjust the noise covariance in the AKF algorithm. The system is applied to an inner-loop 3D braiding machine, and tension is selected as the observed quantity for offline and online experiments. The results show that this tension monitoring system can accurately and real-time output yarn tension changes, achieving higher measurement accuracy and better robustness compared to single-sensor measurement systems and other measurement methods.

title: A Real-Time Heuristic 3D Bin Packing Algorithm for Robotic Mixed Palletizing with Kinematic and Stability Constraints
authors: Yongyong Sun, Kezheng Sun, Wu Yan, Yisong Cai, Zhaoyang Liao
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Mixed palletizing of multi-sized boxes is a critical operation in modern flexible manufacturing and smart logistics. However, traditional 3D Bin Packing Problem (3D-BPP) algorithms often fail in physical deployment because they prioritize theoretical volume utilization while neglecting the mechanical and kinematic realities of robotic execution. This paper proposes a real-time heuristic 3D-BPP algorithm specifically tailored for industrial robotic palletizing systems. By integrating a Guillotine Split space management strategy with strict physical guardrails-specifically, a minimum <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5 {\%}}$</tex> bottom support area constraint to prevent collapse, and a top-down kinematic clearance check to avoid end-effector collisions-the proposed algorithm effectively eliminates dangerous trajectories during the packing sequence. Furthermore, the algorithm is encapsulated within a highly extensible software framework, enabling millisecond-level inference on standard industrial PCs without requiring heavy GPU acceleration. Real-world implementation demonstrates that while the theoretical space utilization is marginally compromised, the algorithm achieves a near-perfect 99% industrial executability rate, successfully bridging the “Sim-to-Real” gap in robotic automation.

title: Smart Sidewalks: Exploring Piezoelectric Energy Harvesting for Sustainable Urban Transport Systems
authors: Dominic Bien M. Aquino, Margareth S. Barber, Mervin Anthony S. Millamina, Dante L. Silva, Jocelyn S. Buluran, Christ John L. Marcos
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This study investigates the performance of a piezoelectric tile prototype in converting footstep-induced mechanical energy into electrical energy within an urban pedestrian setting. A three-day experimental setup was deployed at the Mapúa University exit, combining real-time voltage monitoring with manual foot traffic counting during peak and offpeak hours. The results demonstrated that the tile system successfully generated voltage in response to varying pedestrian loads, with higher outputs recorded during peak times. Statistical analysis using four t-tests confirmed a significant relationship between foot traffic intensity and voltage output. Although the harvested energy was insufficient for high-power applications, it proved suitable for low-energy functions, such as pathway lighting and pedestrian sensors. Environmental impact assessment revealed modest but meaningful non-monetary benefits, including reduced grid dependency and increased awareness of sustainable practices. These findings support the potential of piezoelectric sidewalks as a supplemental power source and a step toward more intelligent, sustainable transportation infrastructure.

title: Fast Dual Path Hybrid FIR-IIR Filter Algorithm for Multi Target Separation in Array Signal Processing
authors: Wei Sun, Santuan Qin
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: In array signal processing applications such as audio and radar telemetry, multi-target separation and direction of arrival (DOA) estimation are critical tasks. Conventional methods typically employ a multi-stage FIR filter structure: signals of multiple targets at different frequencies are first separated to the baseband via Digital Down Conversion (DDC), then each target signal undergoes multi-stage FIR filtering to suppress interference from other targets, before final DOA estimation. However, multi-channel, multi-stage FIR filters consume substantial resources in FPGA implementations. To address this, in this paper, a dual-path hybrid FIR-IIR filtering algorithm is proposed. Leveraging the advantage of IIR filters achieving comparable filtering performance with fewer resources, the proposed algorithm replaces the final several stage in the multi-stage filter structure with an IIR filter. This approach significantly reduces FPGA resource utilization while maintaining the overall link performance. To tackle the issue of phase nonlinearity in IIR filters when handling wideband signals, the proposed dual-path method decouples the tap position for DOA estimation from the signal synthesis point. Specifically, DOA estimation is performed after IIR filtering, whereas signal synthesis is conducted before the IIR filtering stage. This design effectively prevents the synthesized signal from suffering from phase nonlinearity distortion, which can ensure accurate demodulation by subsequent receiver stages. The effectiveness of the proposed algorithm was verified through simulations involving the separation of four targets within a 200 MHz bandwidth, using a hybrid architecture of 3 FIR stages and 3 IIR stages. The results demonstrate that the proposed dualpath algorithm achieves target separation performance and DOA estimation accuracy comparable to the traditional method, while saves more than half of the DSP slices, underscoring its significant value for engineering applications.

title: Adaptive Multi-Resolution Attention Networks for Speech Emotion Recognition with Temporal Consistency Regularization
authors: M. V. Rajesh, Nalla Manasa Surya Subrahmanayeswari, Doddi Hari Sri Priyanka, Kommu Ratna Chandra, Kolli Leela Prasad, Illa Chandravirat
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Research Objective: Contemporary SER architectures exhibit two fundamental limitations: the application of uniform temporal resolutions across emotionally diverse utterances, and the absence of mechanisms ensuring prediction stability across temporal segments. This work introduces AMRAN (Adaptive Multi-Resolution Attention Network), a framework that dynamically determines optimal temporal granularity based on emotion-specific acoustic characteristics while enforcing cross-segment prediction coherence. The proposed architecture employs learnable gating mechanisms that assign differential weights to three concurrent CNN pathways, each processing WavLM-Large representations at distinct temporal scales (kernels: 3, 7, 15). We then apply stacked bidirectional LSTMs and multi-head attention mechanisms. A novel temporal consistency loss function discourages abrupt shifts in predictions between adjacent segments. The system was rigorously tested through 5-fold speaker-independent cross-validation across 14,169 combined utterances from RAVDESS, TESS, and IEMOCAP datasets. Experiments show three key findings. The model delivered a mean accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$64.97 \% \pm 9.66 \%$</tex>, with the strongest fold reaching 81.99%. The results reveal temporal preferences specific to emotions: high-arousal emotions such as anger and happiness gravitate toward fine-grained features (42% weight on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{k}=3)$</tex>, while low-arousal states such as sadness and neutrality showed a clear preference for coarse patterns (41% weight on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{k}=15)$</tex>. This finding validates our central hypothesis about adaptive resolution. The temporal consistency regularization mechanism successfully reduced prediction variance through smoother emotion transitions, contributing to an F1-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.6385 \pm 0.0984$</tex>. Notably, all three resolutions played substantial roles (Fine: 33.4%, Medium: 33.5%, Coarse: 33.1%), showing that fixed-scale architectures cannot capture the full diversity of emotion-specific temporal characteristics.

title: An Automated Brain Stroke Classification from Computed Tomography Scans using a Deep Denoising Autoencoder Network with Feature Extraction Technique
authors: Ramachandiran R, R. Karunia Krishnapriya, G. Sharmila, V. Kavitha, A. Sariga, S. T. Arokkiya Mary
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: A brain stroke is a severe health condition resulting from a lack of blood flow. The affected brain area loses normal function after a stroke, making early detection significant. Computed tomography (CT) plays a key role in detecting suspected stroke patients, but is currently prone to significant intra- and interobserver variation. CT are extensively applied for classifying abnormal brain tissue after haemorrhage and infarct in stroke. Manual lesion representation is now the conventional method, but it is either operator-dependent or time-consuming. Primary techniques for automatically identifying brain stroke on CT scans using deep learning (DL) are presented. The DL advances a computational method with multi-layered processing to gradually learn from raw input. Many DL frameworks are widely used to address image processing challenges. This paper proposes an Automated Brain Stroke Classification Using Deep Denoising Autoencoder with Feature Extraction (ABSCDDAEFE) model from CT scans. The aim is to detect brain stroke from CT images to support timely analysis and treatment planning. Initially, the adaptive Kalman filter (AKF) method is used for image pre-processing. Next, the EfficientNet-B5 method is employed for feature extraction. To detect and classify brain stroke, the stacked denoising autoencoder (SDAE) model is employed. Finally, the parameter selection of SDAE is implemented by using the cuckoo search (CS) approach. The comparison analysis showed a superior accuracy of 98.80 % over existing models on the brain stroke CT image dataset.

title: OpenVerse: An Intelligent Recommendation Platform for Google Summer of Code Preparation
authors: Sharvani G S, Prajwal Mundargi, Ramya G, Sahana Adiga V, Samarth G, Prarthana V Gunakimath
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: <copy979-8-3315-4777-6/26/$31.00 ©2026 IEEE>OpenVerse is an intelligent web-based platform designed to simplify and enhance the preparation process for open-source contributions, particularly for Google Summer of Code (GSoC) aspirants. The methodology integrates data analytics, predictive modeling, and personalized recommendation systems to guide users toward suitable organizations and projects. Historical GSoC datasets are collected and analyzed to identify trends such as recurring organizations, project domains, and commonly used technology stacks. Predictive analytics techniques are employed to forecast potential organization participation in future GSoC editions. The platform is implemented using a Flask-based backend with a Neon PostgreSQL database for efficient data storage and processing, while a React-based frontend provides an interactive and responsive user interface. OpenVerse further integrates GitHub analytics by displaying organization-level metrics and highlighting "Best First Issues," enabling users to initiate contributions directly. Performance evaluation using Google Lighthouse reports a performance score of approximately 78, accessibility of 95, best practices score of 92, and SEO score of 82, demonstrating a reliable, accessible, and user-friendly web experience.

title: In Vitro Micro-Propagation of Chrysanthemum (Poornima) Using Apical Meristem
authors: Ashwini D.S, Santosh Kumar Singh
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This Chrysanthemum, a decorative flower belonging to the Asteraceae family, has emerged as an important commercial crop for numerous farmers in Karnataka because of its strong market demand. However, the supply of high-quality plants has been challenging, complicating the mass production of genetically pure lines. To tackle this issue, we implemented tissue culture technology. We sterilized shoot tips as explants using various agents, including Bavistin, Streptomycin sulfate, Cetrimide, 2% sodium hypochlorite, 70% ethanol, and 0.1% HgCl2. The basal MS medium was enriched with different combinations of auxins and cytokinin’s to optimize shoot and root formation. The findings indicated that the medium containing MS medium along with 2 mg/L BAP, 0.2 mg/L NAA, and 50 mg/L ADS, resulted in the highest number of healthy shoots and an improved survival rate. Additionally, the medium with ½ strength MS and 1.0 mg/L IBA proved to be the most suitable for developing a healthy and viable root system. Acclimatization, which gradually introduces tissue culture plants to the natural environment, used a mixture of red soil, vermicompost, and coco peat in a 1:1:1 ratio. The micro propagated plants were assessed by using different DNA markers, and it was observed that the plantlets had no genetic variations.

title: A Comparative Study of Backbone Architectures for Document Analysis with DINO
authors: Liyakhanya Tabata, Clement N. Nyirenda
abstract: This study evaluates the integration of the DINO transformer-based detector with four modern backbones: ResNet-50, EfficientNetV2-S, Swin-T and MobileNetV3-L, within a document analysis pipeline. Models are benchmarked on a high-resolution synthetic dataset simulating real-world paperwork with diverse layouts, handwriting and fine-grained elements. The aim is to assess how backbone choice affects detection accuracy, efficiency and performance under resource constraints and limited data. All models are stress-tested using document artefact augmentations to reflect deployment conditions. Results show EfficientNetV2-S leads in accuracy with a high computational overhead. MobileNetV3-L performs well on small objects with a comparable computational performance. ResNet-50 and Swin-T offer the fastest inference. ResNet-50 is balanced for low-resource setups, while Swin-T delivers high throughput but struggles under noise. These findings highlight the role of backbone choice in real-world document detection.

title: Intelligent Laptop Assistance Chatbot using Retrieval Augmented Generation
authors: Varsha R, Manasa, Prarthana G, Rashmi S G, Shilpa K N
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The growth of e-commerce has led to an increasing demand for smart and scalable customer support systems. Traditional chatbots remain less effective since they are more prone to giving incorrect responses due to poor contextual understanding and brittle rule-based reasoning. This paper proposes the use of a Retrieval-Augmented-Generation based laptop assistance chatbot that effectively combines semantic search with large language models powers to provide smart, context-sensitive responses to troubleshoot laptops of top few brands . Product guides and support documents are chunked, processed, and stored in a vector database to enable real-time retrieval. Given a customer's query, the chatbot retrieves the most relevant chunks of documents and leverages a large language model to generate human-like answers. Moreover, the system supports smart recommendations of YouTube videos relevant to the user's question, which enhances guidance over troubleshooting and product usage. The proposed model was evaluated with the RAGAS metric, achieving a faithfulness of 0.81, answer relevance of 0.76, and context precision and recall of 0.74, to show strong factual grounding and contextual reliability. Overall, the system greatly improves response accuracy, reduces human-agent load, and enhances customer experience in resolving laptop related issues.

title: Detection of Health Conditions and Disease of Multi-Crops Using Deep Learning and Machine Learning Models
authors: Hamsa G R, Geeta Budni, Harshith Kolluru, Akarshak Sharma, Nalini N, P. N. Tengli
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Plant diseases pose a significant challenge to agricultural productivity and global food security, particularly in regions with limited access to expert diagnosis. This paper presents a lightweight and deployment-oriented convolutional neural networks for multi-crop plant disease classification, designed to operate efficiently in low-resource environments. The proposed approach utilizes a compact CNN architecture to classify leaf images into 38 healthy and diseased categories across various crops. The model is trained on the PlantVillage dataset using TensorFlow and Keras, with images resized to 224×224 pixels and processed in batches of 32. Regularization techniques and early stopping are employed to improve generalization and reduce overfitting. Experimental results demonstrate a validation accuracy of 91%, with consistent class-wise precision, recall, and F1-score values. The lightweight design enables fast inference and reduced memory usage, making the system appropriate for deployment on edge and mobile devices. The primary contribution of this work lies in system-level optimization, efficient training strategies, and deployment feasibility rather than architectural complexity.

title: Leveraging Large Language Models to Predict Long-Term Language Outcomes in Late Talkers
authors: Saketh Sundar
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Early identification of children likely to experience persistent language delays remains a gap in both research and clinical practice. Late talkers (LTs) are toddlers with delayed expressive language but otherwise typical development that show highly variable outcomes, with some catching up and others experiencing long-term difficulties. This exploratory study investigates whether large language models (LLMs) can classify early language delay as LT or typically developing (TD) and predict outcomes at school-age (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5; 6$</tex>) among LTs using spontaneous speech transcripts collected at ages 2;6 and 3;6. Using transcript data from the Ellis Weismer Corpus in CHILDES, we evaluate a transformer-based model against a traditional machine learning baseline (Random Forest) trained on common linguistic features used by clinicians such as mean length of utterance and lexical diversity. The LLM transformer model DistilBERT slightly outperformed the baseline, achieving moderately high accuracy in distinguishing LTs from typically developing peers and moderate success in forecasting long-term outcomes. The findings show that early child speech data contains features that transformer models can leverage to make accurate predictions, suggesting these models offer a promising but still preliminary path to automating developmental risk evaluation for late talkers.

title: ShadowGPT: Linguistic Fingerprinting for Robust Detection of AI-Generated Phishing Emails
authors: Yousef Abu Dayeh
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: This paper presents ShadowGPT, a novel machine learning system for detecting ChatGPT-generated phishing emails through linguistic fingerprinting. As ChatGPT-3.5 become increasingly accessible, malicious actors exploit them to create sophisticated phishing campaigns that evade traditional detection methods. Our approach extracts 4 4 distinctive linguistic features encompassing stylometric patterns, readability metrics, vocabulary diversity, syntactic structures, and semantic indicators. We developed a robust gradient boosting classifier trained on 6,749 emails comprising 831 ChatGPT-generated phishing samples, 1,660 human-written phishing emails, and 4,258 legitimate communications. The training process incorporated noise augmentation to ensure real-world resilience. ShadowGPT achieves 99.6% accuracy with a 95% confidence interval of 99.2% to 99.8% in binary classification while maintaining an exceptionally low 0.14% false positive rate. The system demonstrates remarkable robustness, maintaining 99% accuracy under 20% feature perturbation. Our analysis reveals that ChatGPT-generated phishing exhibits consistent patterns including moderate urgency scores (0.98 ± 0.73), elevated trust language (0.70 ± 0.81), complex readability (Flesch: 23.9 ± 26.1), and low vocabulary diversity (0.684). These findings demonstrate that ChatGPT-3.5-generated phishing possesses detectable linguistic fingerprints, enabling effective defense against this emerging threat.

title: SCARF-SLAM: Spatially Confidence-aware Aggregation for Federated Implicit Neural SLAM
authors: Gaofeng Rao, Mozi Chen, Mengchuang Hu, Shengkai Zhang, Kezhong Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: Rapid and accurate environmental reconstruction is a foundation for intelligent mobility systems, such as autonomous vehicles, mobile robots, and drones, to navigate and understand complex spaces. Neural implicit representations offer an effective way to model continuous 3D scenes for dense visual SLAM, but most existing methods depend on centralized training with a single agent, which limits their scalability in large environments. In realistic scenarios, multiple agents explore different parts of a scene, resulting in highly unbalanced observations that make model fusion non-trivial. In this paper, we propose SCARF-SLAM, a federated implicit neural SLAM framework that enables collaborative large-scale mapping through spatially confidence-aware aggregation. SCARF-SLAM leverages confidence derived from volume rendering to aggregate spatial implicit features across agents, while regulating the fusion of global decoder parameters according to their effective exploration. This unified aggregation strategy allows robust learning under heterogeneous data distributions without sharing raw sensor data. Experiments on large-scale datasets show that SCARF-SLAM achieves accurate reconstruction and s table SLAM performance, outperforming the existing NeRF-based methods and naive federated baselines.

title: Infrastructure-Guided Connectivity-Enhanced Road Crack Detection and Estimation
authors: Haosong Xiao, Yamini Ramesh, Rishabh Shukla, Swarat Sarkar, Chaozhe R. He
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: In this paper, we report the world’s first infrastructure-guided communication-enhanced road crack detection pipeline that is effective and implementable on passenger vehicles. We first design a customized communication protocol to transmit the region of interest from the infrastructure to the vehicle. With proper camera image processing (e.g., dynamic cropping and frame selection), the focused images are provided to the crack detection model. Leveraging state-of-the-art crack detection model backbones and a carefully prepared dataset comprising a forward-facing view with a crack, we train the model to improve crack-detection performance. We demonstrate the full detection pipeline on an experimental vehicle platform, showcase the detection effectiveness, and project future research directions.

title: AI-Powered Assistive System for Visually Impaired People
authors: Ishwarya. S, Pasala Sathvika, Sakthivel. S, Raghavi. K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The AI based assistive system, within this paper, is targeted to assist those with visual impairments in their daily activities like text reading, safe navigation, currency recognition, social interaction, etc. Unlike the existing assistive technologies which are limited to a single functionality or their location such as Indian currency recognition, the proposed technology brings a few AI technologies under the same framework. The combination of computer vision, natural language processing, and voice-based interaction is used to provide real-time assistance in the system. Obstacle and currency detection has been implemented using yolo based object detection models and dark net and facial and emotions recognition using deep face and opencv is used for social awareness. Environmental understanding and contextual description is done via BLIP, and printed and handwritten text recognition is done using EasyOCR. A conversational chatbot built with Dialogflow and Transformer models enables natural interaction with all the outputs made available using eSpeakNG based text-to-speech, for complete accessibility. Experimental observations indicates enhanced usability and beneficial assistance to support the potential for improved independence in daily activities of people who are visually impaired by integrating AI systems.

title: A First-Order Logic-Based Alternative to Reward Models in RLHF
authors: Chunjin Jiang, Xinhua Zhu
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) plays a crucial role in aligning large language models (LLMs) with human values and preferences. However, the quality and stability of the trained reward model largely determine the final alignment performance. Existing approaches such as Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) rely heavily on reward models to guide LLMs toward human-aligned behaviors. In this work, we propose a logic-similarity-based reward mechanism as an alternative to conventional reward modeling. Instead of relying on heuristic reward estimation, our method leverages formal logical consistency to steer model alignment with human preferences. Since real-world questions can be interpreted from multiple perspectives, to ensure that logic-based reinforcement learning does not cause model collapse, we introduce SGRPO, a supervised variant of the GRPO framework. S-GRPO incorporates an additional supervised component and jointly optimizes the generation term, KL-divergence regularization, and label-based objective during training. Experimental results demonstrate that S-GRPO consistently outperforms standard supervised fine-tuning (SFT) in both performance and robustness. Furthermore, it extends existing preference-learning frameworks such as GRPO and DPO, offering a more flexible and task-adaptive approach to alignment training. Our code is available at https://github.com/ChunjinJiang/sgrpo.

title: ZUNO-The Smart Assistant
authors: D Swathi Darla, Vishesh J, Ananya G Naik, Rajnandani Singh, Megha R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The Zuno Smart Assistant is a unified intelligent platform enhancing productivity, learning, and well-being. It integrates task management, academic planning, fitness tracking, and health support within a single assistant. Key features include SlideZen for slides generation(without watermark), PlanPulse a structured event planner and scheduler, Priorix a dynamic task prioritization engine, and RecoMate which generates personalized study plans. Additional modules include FitTrack for monitoring fitness and MailVox for sharing emails via voice commands. The InternGo feature further assists students in finding live internships within their domains of interest. Qualitative evaluations demonstrate significant improvements in task organization, study efficiency, and user engagement. By combining multiple intelligent modules into one platform, Zuno demonstrates the effectiveness of unified digital assistance for managing academic and professional life.

title: Enhancing Predictive Cybersecurity Analytics Through the Convergence of Machine Learning and Generative AI
authors: Subhash Chandra Mouli D, Mahammad Sohail Mulla, Kunnanan Reddy Chittepu, Mohammad Thoufik Shaik, Jagan Mohan Byneni, Bala Gangadhara Gutam
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Cyberattacks are dynamic and hence, it makes it. challenging to present-day protection strategies of networks, particularly in the context of new zero-day weak spots. and polymorphic invasions. Due to intrinsic dataset There are imbalances and lack of annotated instances of. atypical characteristics of attacks, machine learning as a matter of course. such complex threats are usually illusory to classifiers. A combination of predictive modelling and generative artificial. smarts will be proposed in order to address the limitations in this paper.
