title: Neutron Radiation Testing of Multiple System-on-Chip Devices Using a Unified Test Framework
authors: Jeffrey Goeders, Weston Smith, Jacob Bertrand, Ethan Hunter, Maria Kastriotou, Michael Wirthlin
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: This work presents neutron testing of various SoC devices using a unified test framework, which emphasizes measuring component-level failure rates. The framework is tested using four different SoC devices, with 16 boards in total.

title: Design and Evaluation of an Intelligent IoT-Based Pill Dispensing System for Elderly Medication Adherence
authors: Manjeet Kaur, Kamali Gupta
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The problem of medication adherence in geriatric healthcare is rather severe because age-related cognitive impairments, complicated pills intake, and absence of constant supervision often contribute to missed medications, overdosing, and medication errors. These problems have huge effects on the results of treatment and raise the costs of healthcare. In order to resolve this issue, the current paper provides the design and analysis of an intelligent Internet of Things (IoT)-based pill dispensing system that would allow automated medication delivery and adherence control and monitoring of the old age care. The proposed system will combine a time-controlled dispensing system, dose-intake sensors, and automatic locking to prevent overdose, and a cloud-based monitoring system that sends real-time alerts to the caregivers and health practitioners. Experimentally, the system was tested in simulated cases of elderly care to determine the accuracy of adherence, reliability, and stability of the system. Findings indicate that the adherence detection accuracy is high (96.8) and the true positive (96.8) and true negative (95.1) rates are high to determine the medication intake and missed doses respectively. The receiver operating characteristic analysis gave an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.982 and so this result is excellent to distinguish between adherence and non-adherence events. It demonstrated steady convergence in terms of training and validation performance, and a high accuracy of training and validation of 97.2 and 96.5, respectively, and validation loss of 0.11. The comparative analysis showed that the suggested system decreased the number of incidents of missed doses by 41.2 percent among IoT- only reminders and 58.7 percent among the traditional manual medication handling systems.

title: Forensic Face Sketch Construction and Recognition Using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Bhanumathi S, Monica B L, Nagashree M, Vandana J R, Yashaswini N
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Forensic face sketches are widely used in criminal investigations when photographic evidence of a suspect is unavailable. Traditional hand-drawn sketches depend heavily on eyewitness memory and the artistic skills of forensic experts, often resulting in subjective and inconsistent outcomes. This paper presents an artificial intelligence based system for automatic forensic face sketch construction and recognition. The proposed system converts a facial photograph into a forensic-style sketch using image preprocessing, edge detection, and stylization techniques. Local Binary Pattern Histogram (LBPH) is employed to extract discriminative facial features from both sketches and stored facial images. Similarity matching is performed using distance-based measures to retrieve potential suspects from a criminal database. The system is implemented using Python and OpenCV and provides a lightweight, repeatable, and computationally efficient solution suitable for real-world forensic environments.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Blockchain and Distributed Ledger Integration in IoT: Consensus Mechanisms, Security Challenges and Future Directions
authors: A Anitha, Nikita
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The fast evolution of large-scale Internet of Things (IoT) networks has made it possible to develop intelligent applications in smart cities, healthcare, industry automation, transport, and nextgeneration communication systems. However, the conventional centralized architecture and security mechanisms are seriously challenged regarding scalability, latency, energy efficiency, trust management, and resilience against cyber-attacks in large-scale and heterogeneous IoT networks.Blockchain and Distributed Ledger Technologies (DLTs) provide promising solutions with the help of decentralization, immutability, and secure data sharing. However, the conventional consensus algorithms such as Proof of Work (PoW) and Proof of Stake (PoS) are not appropriate for large-scale IoT networks because of their high computational and communication complexities. This review provides a comprehensive analysis of the latest blockchain-based IoT solutions, with emphasis on lightweight, scalable, and energy-efficient consensus algorithms for largescale IoT networks. The latest and most advanced consensus algorithms, such as Delegated Proof of Stake (DPoS), Practical Byzantine Fault Tolerance (PBFT) variants, sharding-based systems, Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG)-based blockchain solutions, and agent-based DLT systems, are analyzed. The peer-reviewed literature is analyzed using quantitative performance analysis criteria such as throughput, latency, scalability, energy efficiency, and security resilience. The results show that although the new emerging lightweight and distributed ledger architectures have shown substantial improvements in performance and scalability in large-scale IoT networks, there are still important research challenges to be addressed in security robustness, interoperability, real-time processing, and real-world deployment validation. The research gaps are identified, and future research directions are presented in this review to develop new lightweight, secure, adaptive, and scalable blockchain-based architectures for next-generation large-scale IoT networks.

title: Enhancing Accessibility Through Hand Gesture Recognition in Tamil and English Text Output System
authors: Anandhajothi Nalini R, Ishwarya M, Shanmugapriya S
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: This study presents a real-time hand gesture to text translation system for Tamil and English that uses context dependent text synthesis based on Large Language Models (LLM) and effective hand gesture detection based on YOLOv12. The system uses YOLOv12's powerful spatial localization capacity to instantly recognize dynamic hand gestures under a variety of settings, overcoming the communication barrier faced by hearingimpaired people. A. Without the requirement for gloss annotations, the LLM module supports accurate and natural language translation by converting these representations into fluent bilingual text. Tests reveal a notable increase in latency, linguistic coherence, and translation accuracy, indicating that the system is suitable for real-time. This solution promotes inclusivity for deaf and hard-of-hearing populations who speak Tamil and English by seamlessly combining cutting-edge computer vision and natural language processing technology to facilitate accessible communication in multilingual situations.

title: Cell-free mMIMO Channel Estimate and Performance using InceptionNeXt
authors: Ke Jiang, Wen Wang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Cell-free massive MIMO (CF mMIMO) is a fundamental $\mathbf{6 G}$ mobile communication architecture that depends on dispersed Access Points (APs) managed by a central processing unit. However, real-time resource scheduling is hampered by two problems: classic pilot-based estimating approaches have a large overhead, and Channel State Information (CSI) ages quickly due to dynamic wireless channels. By resolving the dynamic CSI prediction problem in CF mMIMO networks, this research suggests an enhanced InceptionNeXt-based approach to address the aforementioned issues. First, we define the core optimization objective as minimizing the cumulative CSI prediction error across consecutive time slots, with subchannel allocation and transmit power as decision variables. Second, we utilize the multibranch depthwise separable convolution of InceptionNeXt to extract the spatio-temporal properties of dynamic channels and model the channel features of CF mMIMO using historical CSI. Lastly, InceptionNeXt minimizes prediction errors by optimizing CSI prediction through hierarchical feature aggregation. Numerical results show that the proposed strategy lowers the deployment complexity of real-time services while achieving better sum rate performance and more accurate CSI prediction.

title: High-Performance Software-Defined Radio Spectrometer: A GPU Implementation for Multi-GHz Radio Astronomical Signal Processing
authors: Lwando Gomomo, Yunus Abdul Gaffar, Simon Winberg
abstract: This paper presents a GPU-accelerated polyphase filterbank (PFB) spectrometer enhanced with spectral kurtosis (SK) capability for real-time operation in radio astronomical signal processing. Modern wideband radio telescopes employ analog-to-digital converters (ADCs) that sample signals at multi-GHz rates, generating tens of gigabits per second (Gbps) of data per antenna that demand high-speed processing while operating in radio frequency interference (RFI)-contaminated environments. Existing solutions often separate FPGA- or GPU-based spectrometer backends and RFI detection systems, but this work unifies both within a single GPU pipeline. Using NVIDIA’s PyCUDA framework on a Tesla T4 GPU, the system employs a four-tap Hamming-windowed PFB for channelisation, an averager and a higher-order spectral moment estimator based on the SK algorithm for automated RFI detection in the measured spectrum. The implementation achieved 8.88 GSamples/s throughput with high spectral fidelity. Tests using radio telescope data confirmed accurate detection of stationary and transient interference while maintaining false-alarm rates only 1.3% above theoretical expectation. This research demonstrates that modern GPUs can enable efficient and flexible high-throughput spectrometer architectures for advanced digital signal processing (DSP) in radio astronomy.

title: Optimized Shoot-Through Pulse Generation in High Voltage Boost Z-Source Inverters: A Performance-Based PWM Technique Comparison
authors: Sweta Kumari, Rajib Kumar Mandal, SP Daniel Chowdhury
abstract: Z-source inverters (ZSIs) offer a unique single-stage power conversion with voltage boost capability via shoot-through (ST) states, enabled by specialized PWM techniques. This paper compares various ST PWM strategies, including Simple Boost PWM, Maximum Boost PWM, Constant Boost-Third Harmonic Injection PWM and Space Vector PWM, for performance optimization of high-voltage boost ZSI (HVB-ZSI) systems. A MATLAB/Simulink model was developed to evaluate each under identical conditions, focusing on output voltage quality, THD, Z-network capacitor voltage stress, switch stress, and inductor current ripple. Results demonstrate that while all PWM techniques successfully facilitate the ST condition, their performance varies significantly with respect to voltage stress and harmonic profile. This study provides valuable insights into selecting appropriate PWM control strategies for the next-generation ZSI-based power systems.

title: Novel Channel Backside Engineering for Highly Reliable QLC Operation in 3D Flash Memory
authors: Takayuki Gyakushi, Yukihiro Sakotsubo, Hikaru Arakawa, Tadashi Nakamura, Yosuke Mitsuno, Satoshi Shimizu
abstract: The MANOSON memory cell proposed in this paper has the potential to overcome the trade-off in data retention properties between fresh cells and program/erase (P/E)-cycled cells. This is achieved by effectively suppressing electron de-trapping enlargement with increasing P/E cycling stress. The advantage is realized through automatic tuning of the actual P/E window, enabled by an increase in Vth in neutral memory cells with P/E cycling. In this study, we characterize the intrinsic behavior of the memory cell and demonstrate the superiority of MANOSON memory cells in 4-bit-per-cell (QLC) operation in 3D flash memory.

title: Transformer Core Saturation T-Type Equivalent Model Based on Inrush Current Experiment
authors: Yizhuo Li, Lin Li
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Under the influence of lightning strikes and system surge currents, the magnetic flux of the power transformer core is prone to increase significantly and enter the saturation zone, causing the transformer's operating state to exhibit obvious nonlinear characteristics. To accurately describe the excitation characteristics of the transformer under transient and deep saturation conditions, a method based on extracting the transformer's excitation inductance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\psi$</tex> - <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$i_{L}$</tex> curve from the excitation inrush current experimental data is adopted, addressing the issue of insufficient accuracy of the traditional linear assumption of excitation inductance in inrush current analysis. By collecting and analyzing the waveforms of the primary side voltage and excitation current during the no-load closing test of a single-phase transformer, the excitation flux linkage is calculated using the voltage-time integration relationship and mapped to the corresponding excitation current, thereby obtaining the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\psi$</tex> - <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$i_{L}$</tex> curve that reflects the nonlinear saturation characteristics of the core. This paper takes a single-phase transformer as the research object, conducts excitation inrush current tests, and identifies and extracts the equivalent circuit parameters considering the core saturation characteristics. Based on this, a T-type equivalent circuit model of the transformer considering core saturation characteristics is established on the ATP-EMTP simulation platform. By comparing and analyzing the excitation inrush current waveforms calculated by the model and the experimental test results under the same conditions, the rationality of the model is verified. The results show that, under the condition of a power supply closing angle of 0°, there is a significant deviation between the peak value of the excitation inrush current calculated by the traditional T-type equivalent circuit model and the experimental measurement results; in contrast, the T-type equivalent circuit model considering core saturation characteristics has an excitation inrush current peak value calculation error of 16.8%.

title: Airfoil Trailing-Edge Noise Prediction Using Physics-Informed Neural Networks
authors: Kaining Yuan, Sathvik Malla, Varun Ramachandran, Vinamra Singhal
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Predicting airfoil trailing-edge noise accurately is crucial for sound-aware aircraft design, but conventional approaches still rely on time-intensive and expensive Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) simulations. This work introduces a Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) framework combined with transfer learning (TL), trained on two aeroacoustic datasets from NASA and the University of Bristol, to produce accurate and physically consistent sound pressure level (SPL) predictions across various airfoil shapes. The key input parameters are frequency, angle of attack, chord length, free-stream velocity, radius of microphones from the trailing-edge, Reynolds number, and Strouhal number, plus a compact spline-based airfoilshape encoding. Compared with a transfer-learning Deep Neural Network (TL-DNN), the TL-PINN achieves better accuracy and generalization, especially under extrapolation, achieving test RMSE below 2 dB. This approach offers a fast and scalable alternative to CFD-based aeroacoustic simulations.

title: A Reproducible, Data-Driven Framework for Safety and Functionality Verification of Level-4 Automated Buses Through Real-World Testing
authors: Doğacan Dolunay Acar, Kaan Çakin, Demet Tümkaya, Ergün Can, Cemre Kavvasoğlu, Kerem Par, Ali Ufuk Peker
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: Ensuring the safety of automated buses is a prerequisite for their deployment in real-world public transportation. This study focuses on the systematic verification and validation of an autonomous bus using established Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) functions, rather than introducing new functionalities. We propose a Functionality and Safety Verification, Analysis, and Assessment (FSVAA) framework that establishes a structured transition from the Operational Design Domain (ODD) to Dynamic Driving Task (DDT) elements and corresponding test scenarios. The framework is implemented on a full-size Level-4 automated bus equipped with ADASTEC’s flowride.ai platform. A total of 340 on-track tests were conducted across Automated Emergency Braking (AEB), Automated Trajectory Following (ATF), and Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC). System performance was evaluated using safety- and comfort-related indicators, including time-to-collision (TTC), time headway (THW), acceleration, and jerk. The results demonstrate consistent pass/fail decisions and support compliance with acceptance criteria required for progression toward open-road testing, providing a reproducible foundation for continuous safety validation of automated buses.

title: EduSense: Advancing Teaching with Audio-Visual Analytics
authors: Soumya G V, Renukadevi M N
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: In the recent years examining the specific manner by which teaching will influence learning, and it happens that the mechanisms most often used to check it (such as grade surveys, the occasional peer review) are rather biased and slow. EduSense is a novel AI-based installation that attempts to provide a more objective perspective because in real-time it constantly monitors facial expressions and the body angle in relation to each other. It integrates the best computer vision features (YOLOv8 to detect objects, MediaPipe to pose-track, DeepFace to detect face vibes, audio: Whisper to transcribe and OpenSMILE to tone. Detection of emotions and tracking of postures allow EduSense to record it all, such as the level of teacher emotions to his or her sitting position, indicating us not only with the presence of attention, but also with the body language in the mix. The system achieves 4 step pipeline and finally scores 89.4% on emotion recognition on seven emotions and 94 percent on posture orientation on four directions. These are comparatively high numbers in accordance with student feedback (r = 0.74) and even provide time stamp granularity that can be used to frame future instructional adjustments. In essence, it demonstrates how multimodal sensing would make the existing spot-and-comment format a derivative of data, as a continuous loop, and this is in line with UN SDG 4 and 9.

title: Static Obstacle Avoidance Of Autonomous Vehicles Using MATLAB Simulation
authors: Aluvala Sai Vinyaa, T. V. Smithab, N. Neelimac
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Autonomous driving is becoming important in intelligent transportation systems since it aims at making travel safer and more reliable, aligning with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goal SDG 11 "Sustainable Cities and Communities". This work points out the MATLAB-based simulation that illustrates how an autonomous car can avoid obstacles, turn, and change its speed without using physical sensors. In place of real-time sensor inputs, the system relies on algorithmic logic and a prior awareness about the surroundings. In this simulation, the vehicle spots obstacles and turns in case something is in front of it. I f something gets too close, it slows down. Due to the inbuilt responses, the vehicle stays safe and glides along its lane without much trouble. This simulation hence proves that without actual sensors, one can work things out, MATLAB simulations handle the testing and development of control so that the simulated vehicle maintains a stable path and avoids collisions, hence showing the potential of software-driven models for early-stage testing of self-driving systems-contributing further to SDG 9, "Industry, Innovation, and Infrastructure", by promoting innovative software-driven mobility solutions.

title: myMNIST: Benchmark of PETNN, KAN, and Classical Deep Learning Models for Burmese Handwritten Digit Recognition
authors: Ye Kyaw Thu, Thazin Myint Oo, Thepchai Supnithi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: We present the first systematic benchmark on a standardized iteration of the publicly available Burmese Handwritten Digit Dataset (BHDD), which we have designated as myMNIST Benchmarking. While BHDD serves as a foundational resource for Myanmar NLP/AI, it lacks a comprehensive, reproducible performance baseline across modern architectures. We evaluate eleven models, ranging from classical deep learning (CNN, LSTM, Transformer) to emerging paradigms including Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (FastKAN, EfficientKAN), energy-based models (JEM), and PhysicsEnergy Transition Neural Networks (PETNN). Using standard classification metrics, our results show that while CNNs remain a robust baseline ($F_{1}=0.9959$, Accuracy $=0.9970$), the PETNN (GELU) variant achieves near-parity ($F_{1}=0.9955$, Accuracy $=0.9966$), significantly outperforming LSTMs, Transformers, and KAN variants. We further quantify the performance gap between physics-inspired transitions and true energybased modeling. By introducing myMNIST as a standardized benchmarking framework for this resource and releasing these reproducible baselines, we aim to integrate Burmese digit recognition into the global ‘MNIST-family’ of research tasks and encourage the evaluation of frontier architectures on regional scripts.

title: Vital Sign Detection via Millimeter-Wave Radar Based on Improved VMD and Adaptive Threshold Screening
authors: Peizhan Yan, Zhonghua Liu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To address the challenge of heartbeat extraction being highly susceptible to strong respiratory harmonic interference in complex power operation environments, this paper proposes an enhanced monitoring method. Traditional VMD relies on manual parameter settings, which often leads to signal oversegmentation. In this study, an energy fluctuation criterion is introduced to adaptively determine the optimal decomposition layer K. Subsequently, a dual-stage screening mechanism—integrating frequency-band energy ratio and mutual information—is constructed to accurately isolate vital sign components from noise. Experiments demonstrate that the heart rate Mean Absolute Error (MAE) is 1.9 BPM at a distance of 1.0 m, showing robust performance under various conditions such as side-facing and post-exercise states.

title: A Knowledge-Driven Framework for Context- Aware Decision Support Using Lightweight AI Reasoning Models
authors: Siddharth Arora, Anupriya, Fahim Khan, Kruthika C G, Priya Dahiya, Neelam Goswami
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Decision support systems increasingly rely on data-intensive learning models that assume stable contexts, continuous data availability, and ample computational resources. The assumptions which have been widely invoked are not met in many of the real world contexts. Operation environments are evolving, data streams are never complete and making decisions has to be weighted by strict time and resource requirements. Current methods often put predictive accuracy above contextual reasoning and explainability thus constraining their application in dynamic settings. The paper presents a knowledge-based architecture of context-aware decision support which specifically combines structured domain knowledge with a lightly-weighted artificial-intelligence decision support structures. The suggested framework specifically simulates the states of context, decision rules and inference pathways so that the system may adapt its thinking process in response to changing environmental conditions. Instead of using deep and resource-intensive models, the framework adopts low complexity learning elements to sharpen the reasoning results without compromising on the transparency and computational efficiency. A prototype implementation is assessed through a case-based experimental study of simulating a dynamic decision situation. The findings indicate that the proposed method achieves competitive performance in making decisions compared with the exclusively data-based ones, and significantly decreases the computational cost and positively affects interpretability. These results indicate explicit knowledge representation with lightweight AI reasoning is a viable and scalable option that can be used in decision support systems that run in context-rich and resource-sensitive environments.

title: An Improved FMECA Method Based on Interval-Valued Intuitionistic Fuzzy Sets and VIKOR
authors: Sizuo She, Fanlong Zhu, Shuhao Weng, Yingkui Gu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: As one of the core subsystems of a diesel engine, the valve train significantly affects engine reliability. However, the traditional Failure Mode, Effects, and Criticality Analysis (FMECA) suffers from ambiguous evaluation information and an unreasonable calculation of the Risk Priority Number (RPN). To address these issues, this paper introduces interval-valued intuitionistic fuzzy sets (IVIFSs) to model expert ratings, thereby reducing the ambiguity associated with crisp numerical scoring. Then, an IVIFS-based Criteria Importance Through Intercriteria Correlation (CRITIC) method and the Best-Worst Method (BWM) are employed to derive combined weights for severity (S), occurrence (O), and detection (D). Finally, an IVIFS-based VIKOR approach is applied to rank the risks of failure modes. A case study involving 18 failure modes across nine major components of the valve train in a specific diesel engine indicates that the valve spring and camshaft are the most failure-prone components, providing useful guidance for maintenance planning and fault diagnosis.

title: Post-Quantum Cryptographic Algorithms Performance Evaluation of Secure Networked Systems
authors: Prathusha Prakash, Naga Deepak Pothuraju
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: It is also worth noting that the implementation of quantum computing has been a big threat to the classical cryptographic algorithms that are popular in secure networked systems. PostQuantum Cryptography (PQC) has come to play as one of the viable answers to this issue because it has the capacity to resist quantum-enabled attacks. In this paper, the author offers a detailed performance analysis of post-quantum cryptographic algorithms, lattice-based encryption which is based on the Learning with Errors (LWE) principle, and hashbased digital signatures which is based on SPHINCS+. An experimental environment was created that is controlled by using specific performance measures, such as time of key generation, the latency of encryption and decryption, amount of memory, and the size of keys and ciphertext. To determine the suitability of PQC in guaranteeing confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity over insecure channels, secure network communication was emulated. Through experimental findings, it has been shown that the lattice-based generation of keys has greater overhead in terms of computation and memory, but the encryption, decryption and signature processes have feasible limits. The results validate the practicability of the post-quantum cryptography mechanisms implementation in the practical secure network systems and the trade-off between the increased quantum resistance and the resource consumption.

title: STGCN-MA: A Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolutional Network with Mamba Attention for Cellular Traffic Prediction
authors: Wen Wang, Ke Jiang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: In the era of intelligent and heterogeneous wireless communication, accurate traffic prediction is crucial for efficient network management. However, due to the complex spatiotemporal correlations in cellular data, existing methods often have limitations in efficiently capturing both characteristics simultaneously. To address these issues, this paper proposes a spatiotemporal graph convolutional network based on Mamba attention (STGCN-MA), which is a novel prediction framework using an encoder decoder architecture. STGCN-MA integrates the advantages of the selective state space model in Mamba to achieve efficient computation and long-range dependency modeling. The encoder consists of two core modules, a Bi Mamba module that utilizes the linear computational complexity of Mamba to capture long-term temporal dependencies. And a novel Spatial Mamba Attention Graph Convolutional Network (MA-GCN) module that integrates Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) with Mamba based attention mechanism to effectively model dynamic and non local spatial correlations. Experiments on the real Telecom Italia Milan dataset show that the performance of STGCN-MA is significantly better than baseline models including long short-term memory (LSTM), Graph Wavenet, Timesnet, and Adaptive Graph Convolutional Recurrent Network (AGCRN), verifying the excellent accuracy and efficiency of this model in cellular traffic prediction.

title: Designing a Workplace Harassment Reporting Platform Framework using Sentiment Analysis
authors: Shekainah Kim A. Sardeng, Thelma D. Palaoag
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Subtle and multi-faceted workplace harassment can leave victims hesitant to report incidents. This study aims to design a workplace harassment reporting platform framework enhanced by sentiment analysis to better capture and understand employee experiences. The study adopted a systematic, iterative research design structured into three interconnected phases—data collection and gathering, data analysis, and framework design —to develop a comprehensive workplace harassment reporting platform framework using sentiment analysis. The methodology combined an initial literature review on harassment systems, reporting platforms, and sentiment analysis with in-depth document analysis of organizational policies, HR workflows, and technical options, alongside the development and refinement of a design prototype specifying user roles, reporting flows, and dashboard-based monitoring. Insights and drawbacks identified through these analytic and design activities were fed back into an iterative framework construction process, ensuring that the final platform framework is theoretically grounded, operationally feasible, and capable of integrating sentiment analysis for prioritizing and managing harassment reports. The designed framework provides a structured yet flexible platform that integrates sentiment analysis to enhance the detection and interpretation of both explicit and implicit harassment reports. The framework supports multiple reporting channels, ensuring immediate accessibility and confidentiality for victims, while enabling organizations to monitor incidents in real-time through automated emotional cue analysis and thematic categorization. The established framework supports the formulation of timely and context-appropriate organizational interventions predicated upon the resulting sentiment insights, thereby positioning the integration of sentiment analysis within a harassment reporting platform as a promising solution to improve the detection and effective monitoring of workplace harassment.

title: Enhanced LFSR-Based Frequency Divider with Multi-Stage Feedback Selection and State Extension
authors: Raghavendra K R, Manjunath H S, Kiran B Hegde, Veena M B
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Efficient frequency division is a critical requirement in modern digital systems, where power consumption, hardware efficiency, and adaptability play key roles. This work presents an optimized Linear Feedback Shift Register (LFSR) with state extension for frequency division, integrating multi-stage feedback selection and clock gating to enhance performance. The proposed method employs a carefully selected primitive polynomial-based LFSR to generate a maximum-length sequence with minimal hardware overhead. By introducing state extension, the sequence length is significantly increased, allowing for more precise frequency division. Instead of conventional counter-based approaches, the LFSR output dynamically controls the clock gating mechanism, optimizing power efficiency. This technique reduces switching activity and ensures an area-efficient implementation, making it well-suited for VLSI circuits, FPGA-based designs, and embedded systems. The proposed design is implemented and achieved low-power, high-performance frequency division

title: Adaptive Cyber Threat Detection Using Event-Aware LSTM Networks
authors: A. Venkata Ramana, C. Pallavi, M. Gangadhar, B. S. Uday Kumar, B. Lochan, U. Keerthi Priya
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Traditional signature-based IDS is no longer sufficient due to the sophistication of cyberattacks and increased network traffic. DL holds immense promise for automatic threat detection; however, existing models face difficulties in real-world dynamic scenarios. There still exist two major challenges: we cannot model irregular time intervals in network logs, and performance degradation due to concept drift when attack patterns and normal traffic change over time.This report proposes a strong framework for Adaptive Cyber Threat Detection using Event-Aware LSTM Networks. This model captures variable time gaps and better detects low-and-slow attacks with a time-decay mechanism, which is different from traditional methods assuming events are equidistant in time. The model is then combined with Event2Vec embedding to encode various log event types better than one-hot representations. An online drift detection module based on the ADWIN algorithm monitors the model’s performance and initiates its retraining in incremental steps during distributional shifts. This keeps the model adaptive over time. The experiments with CICIDS2017, UNSW-NB15 and simulated drift scenarios prove that the Event-Aware LSTM can achieve an accuracy of up to 99.5% and quickly adapts after a concept drift, hence is less vulnerable compared to the static DL models. This work enhances self-adjusting autonomous SIEM systems that would work dependably in cyber-physical surroundings that are unstable.

title: Smart Fruits Disease Detection System with Statistical Modelling and Feature Construction Using Machine Learning Models
authors: G. Ramkumar
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The Smart Fruits Disease Detection System is a technology of AI to perform image analysis for automatically detecting and classifying diseases in fruits by using machine learning methods. The Statistical Feature-Driven Segmentation and Fusion Model (SFDSFM) developed in this study is a methodology that combines statistical modelling with deep learning for accurately classifying and detecting chrysanthemum flowering stages. The Jinju Chrysanthemum Exhibition in November 2022, comprising 117 original images that were augmented to create a set of 11,808 images. The Generalised Extreme Value (GEV) distribution was utilized to generate a feature vector was employed to train a CNN-based YOLO detection network. The simulation parameter is used to calculate the SFDSFM model is a confusion matrix of the SFDSFM model achieved 1-57 classes in cm1 and 0-19 classes in cm2. SFDSFM Accuracy Calculation at epoch 40 of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$87-90 \%$</tex>, SFDSFM Loss Calculation achieves training loss of 1.8, and by the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$60^{\text {th }}$</tex> epoch, SFDSFM Precision Calculation of 87% of epoch 40, and SFDSFM Recall Calculation achieves 96.8% of 70 epochs.

title: Machine Learning Prediction of ADHD Medication Trends Reveals Transient Impact of the COVID-19 Pandemic in the U.S.
authors: Margaret Liu, Hannah Guo, Jingyan Han
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic and associated public health measures have affected mental health service utilization and psychotropic medication use. This study analyzed U.S. pharmaceutical sales from 2014 Jul to 2019 Dec to characterize pre-pandemic trends in attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) medication consumption. A machine learning model was developed to forecast expected consumption for 2020 and 2021 under non-pandemic conditions. The deviation between the actual and predicted sales under non-pandemic conditions generates the quantitative estimation of the pandemic's impact. Results showed that 3.3% of the U.S. population aged 6 and older were receiving ADHD medications with a stable rate from 2015 to 2019. Approximately 60 % of individuals diagnosed with ADHD were treated with medication, consistent with expected treatment rate. Our machine learning model identified a lower consumption rate of ADHD medication in 2020, indicating a transient disruption following the onset of COVID-19. However, by 2021, the actual consumption rebounded and aligned with forecasted trends, suggesting recovery of ADHD treatment patterns.

title: Retinal Vessel Segmentation Through Green Channel Enhancement and Comparative Deep Learning Architectures
authors: Sandhya B R, Nalini N
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The early detection of cardiovascular and ocular disorders depends heavily on retinal vessel segmentation. Low contrast, thin vascular architecture, and background noise make it difficult to accurately identify blood vessels from fundus images. The impact of preprocessing methods on segmentation performance is highlighted in this paper's through comparison analysis of deep learning-based segmentation architectures for retinal vascular extraction. To improve vessel visibility, three preprocessing techniques are assessed: Baseline processing, contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization (CLAHE), and green-channel extraction in conjunction with CLAHE. The superiority of green-channel CLAHE for retinal image enhancement is demonstrated by quantitative preprocessing evaluation using entropy (3.65) , contrast improvement index(0.264), and edge-based contrast measure (24.09). The three convolutional neural network architectures—U-Net, Residual U-Net, and Attention U-Net—are then trained and assessed using a patch-based method on a combined retinal vascular dataset. According to experimental findings, Residual U-Net gets the greatest Dice coefficient of 0.8193, IoU of 0.6959 and Accuracy of about 98.45% showing greater vessel overlap accuracy. Results from qualitative segmentation provide additional evidence of improved vessel continuity and background suppression.

title: AgroFlow- Smart Agriculture System for Crop Recommendation, Disease Prediction, Market Analysis and Farmer Supports
authors: Rajesh L, Sandesh Kumar T S, Vijayalaxmi V Huded, Vishwaprasad M Ganagi, Yathish H Gowda
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Agriculture is confronted with a ton of daily issues such as climate changes, plant infections, wild market prices, and lack of good advice to the farmers. Due to this reason, farmers are usually unable to make the right decision at the right time. In this paper, we discuss a smart farming system called AgroFlow, which is designed to assist farmers in AI and data analysis. The system provides crop recommendations using previous farm records, anticipates diseases to identify issues in time, and provides price data on the market to enable them to sell optimally. It also displays live weather information hence the tips are realistic. AgroFlow has a learning and support area and an artificial intelligence chatbot that allows a user to pose several questions and receive immediate responses. The entire system is developed as a user-friendly software platform so that farmers do not have technology issues with the use of it. The experimental results demonstrate exceptional performance across all modules. The crop recommendation model achieved a testing accuracy of 99.55 percent with the Extra Trees Classifier. The yield prediction model attained an R-squared score of 98.44 percent, indicating highly reliable harvest estimates. The disease detection CNN achieved 94.67 percent validation accuracy across 38 disease classes. System performance benchmarks confirmed responsive API behavior with crop prediction averaging 127 milliseconds response time and successful load handling up to 100 concurrent users.

title: Fusion of Heterogeneous and Multi-Location Sensors for Collective Perception
authors: Tiago Silva Pereira, Andreia Figueiredo, Pedro Rito, Susana Sargento
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: Autonomous Driving (AD) represents a significant advance in autonomous mobility, transforming the way vehicles operate and interact with their surroundings. While AD technologies promise improvements in road safety, traffic efficiency, and sustainability, their effectiveness depends on robust perception mechanisms and cooperation among all elements of the transportation system. This work presents a collective perception architecture supported by Multi-Access Edge Computing (MEC) and Multi-Sensor Fusion (MSF) algorithms capable of combining data from road sensors with mobile sensors information shared through Vehicle-To-Everything (V2X) communications. The solution integrates two complementary levels—Local Fusion (LF) and Global Fusion (GF)—allowing it not only to improve the accuracy and reliability of the individual perception of each Intelligent Transportation System Station (ITS-S), but also to extend its range beyond physical limitations. The proposed approach is integrated into a real smart city platform, an AD, and a connected vehicle. The fusion results show that the GF module achieves a precision of 75.59%, combined with a recall of 95.60%, yielding an F1-score of 84.36%, which demonstrates a high capacity to preserve true detections. These results show that the combination of MEC-based perception and information exchange via V2X offers a scalable and robust approach for collective perception systems in smart mobility scenarios.

title: Multi-level Dynamic Pricing for Electric Vehicles Charging to Balance Load in Distribution Networks Using Reinforcement Learning
authors: Leloko J. Lepolesa, Kayode E. Adetunji, Khmaies Ouahada, Zhenqing Liu, Ling Cheng
abstract: The global shift away from internal combustion engine vehicles toward electric mobility is widely encouraged as a way to lessen the environmental harm associated with fossilfuel dependence. Many research studies have been conducted to regulate the charging of EVs to avoid grid destabilisation. To enhance the stability and overall performance of modern power grids, this study introduces a new method that balances electrical demand across the neighboring distribution networks. Our strategy is based on dynamic distribution network-level EV charging pricing that is based on reinforcement learning (RL). Simulation results show that in addition to balancing the demand across the distribution networks, the proposed method improves the grid stability by minimizing the variations in load demand and power loss over time, attaining as high as 151.66% and 143.46% standard deviation percentage change on load and power loss respectively.

title: EcoScan - Smart Waste Classification using Computer Vision and Deep Learning
authors: Sheba Jebakani, Angelina Sharon Robert, K Harini, Janhavi M, Akash R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Automated classification of solid waste is essential for improving environmental sustainability and reducing health risks associated with manual sorting. This paper presents the design and implementation of a computer vision-based waste classification system leveraging deep learning techniques to accurately categorize various types of waste. The system utilizes Convolutional Neural Networks to extract and analyse image features, enabling efficient multi-class classification across diverse waste categories. Integrated into a user-friendly web application, the solution supports real-time image upload and provides automated predictions with confidence scores. The proposed approach demonstrates significant potential to enhance waste segregation processes by reducing human intervention and improving sorting accuracy. Future work will focus on optimizing model architectures and expanding deployment for practical applications in smart waste management.

title: A 38.21-TOPS/W SRAM-Based Digital CIM Macro for Bit-Level Sparsity-Aware MAC Operations
authors: Priyanshu Tyagi, Dhayan Dhananjaya Senanayake, Sparsh Mittal
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: We propose a 7T SRAM-based digital CIM macro that exploits input-bit sparsity. Our key idea is to leverage a novel shift-based weight mapping, where a weight vector is stored across successive rows of a sub-array with progressive leftward shift. During MAC operations, input bits are streamed sequentially via input lines (ILs). This IL-driven input streaming enables a sparsity-aware clock generation that disables accumulation cycles when the input bit is zero. It significantly reduces dynamic power consumption. Moreover, our design utilizes the adder tree only once, thereby reducing compute latency and improving throughput. The core structure is an 8×16 SRAM-based sub-array that supports MAC operations up to 8-bit precision. A 128×128 CIM macro, formed by 128 such sub-arrays, performs 1024 4b×4b operations per cycle at 0.75V with 4.68 mW power consumption, achieving 128 GOPS and 27.35 TOPS/W. At 75% input-bit sparsity, the energy efficiency further increases to 38.21 TOPS/W. The macro maintains high inference accuracy—92.5% for ResNet-18 and 91.7% for EfficientNetV2 on the CIFAR-10 dataset. The results show macro's suitability for edge inference.

title: AeroLogiSim: A Simulation Framework for Autonomous Drone Delivery Using PX4 And AirSim
authors: Raghavendra Patil G E, Niveditha H M, Elluru Sanghavi, Dinesh G, Darshan P, Baba Fakruddin Ali B H
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Drone Simulation using PX4 and AirSim focuses on creating a realistic and safe virtual environment to study and improve autonomous drone operations. Instead of experimenting directly with real drones, the project uses the PX4 autopilot system and Microsoft AirSim simulator to model flight behavior, test delivery scenarios, and evaluate how drones respond to different situations. This simulation setup allows drones to practice delivering medical supplies, consumer goods, and urgent packages across cities, rural regions, and hard-to-reach locations. It enables real-time flight monitoring, supports AI-based route optimization, and provides a cloud-enabled setup for testing multiple flight missions efficiently. The goal is to understand how electrically powered drones perform under different weather conditions, terrains, and regulatory constraints all without the risks or costs of physical testing. By exploring the system architecture, technical design, and challenges involved, this project highlights how PX4 and AirSim can help accelerate research, training, and development in drone-based logistics and autonomous flight systems.

title: AtriGuard: An IoT-Enabled Machine Learning Framework for Realtime AFib Detection
authors: Ujjwal Keshari, Utkarsh Kumar, Yatharth Choudhary, Paramita De
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The proposed work AtriGuard is a real-time monitoring of Atrial Fibrillation using machine learning and IoT which focuses on developing a low-cost, portable, and real-time cardiac monitoring system capable of detecting atrial fibrillation (AFib) at an early stage. The system integrates ECG sensing hardware, embedded processing, machine learning-based analysis, and IoT readiness to continuously monitor heart activity. The architecture uses AD8232 ECG sensor interfaced with an ESP32 microcontroller to acquire single-lead ECG signals in real time, that can help to monitor AFib outside clinical environment. The HRV features, mainly RMSSD of RR intervals are identified and the irregular rhythms associated with AFib are tried to identify by applying a classification model. The reliability and efficiency of the system is demonstrated by the experimental result generated by the implemented prototype. Integrating IoT and real time analytics in the system AtriGuard, makes it a scalable solution for early AFib detection that can act as a proactive cardiac care system beyond traditional clinical environments.

title: MediTag: A Colour-Coded Micro QR System for Batch-Level Medicine Tracking and Verification
authors: Ankita V Joshi, Esha Balaji, Akshaya R, V Rose Priyanka
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: A colour-coded micro-QR code system can be used for batch identification of medicine strips. The proposed colour-coded micro-QR code has two encoding methods; the batch number is encoded with a uniquely assigned coloured background, while the Medicine_ID is encoded with either numbers or letters in the conventional micro-QR code pattern. By using two methods of encoding, a greater amount of data can be encoded in each micro-QR code, and the same micro-QR code pattern can be used for multiple batches creating a new micro-QR code simply by changing the colour of the background. This is important for small, delicate blister medicine strips. In addition to colour coding the data, it is also important that the micro-QR code has high geometric accuracy for the data to be decoded accurately. The geometric guidelines for locating the micro-QR codes is having a pure black finder pattern, while all the data modules are colour coded. The proposed colour-coded micro-QR code system is composed of two algorithms, one for colour generation and micro-QR code encoding, and the second for decoding image data to obtain both text and colour data for verification.

title: Multi Layer Multi Sensor Autonomous Mobile Inspection Robot for Mining Sites in Oman
authors: D Satyanarayana, Abdullah Said Al Kalbani, Madan Kumar Sharma, Gopal Rathinam, Nadir Kamal Idries, Alaya Hamyar Al Azzani, Hiya Mohammed Al Risi, Shama Khalid Al jabri
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Mechatronics, Control and Robotics (ICMCR)
abstract: Mining sites are considered dangerous places that could severely hurt mining workers. For instance, the erosion of mining environments may cause unsafe conditions that threaten human lives. Therefore, it is essential to conduct an inspection of mining site prior to the arrival of employees to work. In this paper, we design a customized mobile robot that performs inspection of the site and ensures safety of workers in mining sites specifically made for mountainous terrains such as Oman. We propose a multi-layer mobile robot equipped with sensors to conduct inspection for Omani mining sites. The robot can perform various tasks under harsh conditions in mining sites, like mountainous areas and rocky soils that experience high heat. The simulation of proposed methodology is implemented with the sensors monitoring of environmental factors as well as worker safety.

title: CWE Knowledge-Enhanced Vulnerability Detection for Renewable Energy Grid-Connected Terminal Firmware
authors: Gang Qu, Liang Zhang, Xin Xu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Vulnerability detection in firmware of renewable energy grid-connected terminals is essential for power system cybersecurity. Existing deep learning methods either depend on large-scale pre-trained language models with hundreds of millions of parameters or fail to incorporate domain-specific vulnerability knowledge, limiting their applicability in resource-constrained industrial scenarios. This paper proposes CWE-KE-VD, a lightweight detection method that integrates Common Weakness Enumeration knowledge into the detection pipeline. The method extracts dual-channel features: code lexical features via TF-IDF with SVD dimensionality reduction, and 59-dimensional CWE knowledge features derived from a domain knowledge base covering 12 vulnerability categories. A dual-tower neural architecture with multi-head cross-attention and gated fusion adaptively combines the two representations. Experiments on the DiverseVul dataset containing 1 5 0, 0 0 0 C/C++ functions demonstrate that CWE-KE-VD achieves an F1-score of 0.483 and AUC of 0.819 with only 215 K parameters, outperforming gradient boosting ensemble methods and traditional neural baselines. Ablation studies confirm the effectiveness of each proposed component.

title: A Stacking Ensemble Framework Based Prediction of Total Average Efficiency for Planar Monopole Antenna in High Voltage Partial Discharge Diagnostics
authors: Md. Shafat Bin Islam, Mohamadariff Othman, Hazlee Azil Illias, M. A. Taher, S. M. Kayser Azam, Tarik Abdul Latef, Wong Jee Keen Raymond, Ali Othman, Ihsan Bin Ahmad Zubir
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Partial discharge (PD) monitoring is essential for the reliability of high-voltage equipment. Planar monopole antennas (PMAs) are effective and compact PD sensors but optimizing their total average efficiency using full-wave electromagnetic simulations is computationally expensive. This paper presents a data-driven machine learning framework to rapidly predict PMA efficiency from geometric dimensions. A dataset of 6,048 antenna designs was generated using CST Microwave Studio by varying six key structural parameters. Ridge Regression, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting were combined in a two-tier Stacking Ensemble. The ensemble outperformed individual models, achieving an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.9941 and a MAPE of 0.2393%. This surrogate modeling approach offers a fast and accurate alternative to traditional simulations, enabling efficient design and optimization of UHF printable antennas for PD diagnostics.

title: Wavelet Fourier Diffuser: Frequency-Aware Diffusion Model for Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yifu Luo, Yongzhe Chang, Xueqian Wang
publish: 2025 International Joint Conference on Neural Networks (IJCNN)
abstract: Diffusion probability models have shown significant promise in offline reinforcement learning by directly modeling trajectory sequences. However, existing approaches primarily focus on time-domain features while overlooking frequency-domain features, leading to frequency shift and degraded performance according to our observation. In this paper, we investigate the RL problem from a new perspective of the frequency domain. We first observe that time-domain-only approaches inadvertently introduce shifts in the low-frequency components of the frequency domain, which results in trajectory instability and degraded performance. To address this issue, we propose Wavelet Fourier Diffuser (WFDiffuser), a novel diffusion-based RL framework that integrates Discrete Wavelet Transform to decompose trajectories into low- and high-frequency components. To further enhance diffusion modeling for each component, WFDiffuser employs Short-Time Fourier Transform and cross attention mechanisms to extract frequency-domain features and facilitate cross-frequency interaction. Extensive experiment results on the D4RL benchmark demonstrate that WFDiffuser effectively mitigates frequency shift, leading to smoother, more stable trajectories and improved decision-making performance over existing methods.

title: Fused-STA: Automated Design Space Exploration of a Fused Systolic Tensor Array for Universal Deep Learning Acceleration
authors: Jooyeon Lee, Sangwoo Jung, Jaeha Kung
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Systolic array architectures have become the dominant solution for accelerating deep neural network (DNN) computations, yet designing optimal configurations remains challenging due to the vast design space spanning array dimensions, dataflow strategies, SRAM allocations, and tiling sizes. Existing tool-aided optimization frameworks constrain this design space by treating SRAM sizes or tiling sizes as fixed input parameters, while focusing only on conventional systolic arrays, limiting their ability to discover a truly optimal design. This article presents a comprehensive framework for systolic tensor array (STA) design space exploration that jointly optimizes array configurations, SRAM sizes, tiling sizes, and dataflow types. We introduce TensorSim, a cycle-accurate simulator with an ML-based synthesis prediction model, and TensorOptimizer, an automated multiobjective optimization framework. Evaluation with TensorOptimizer across 12 representative DNNs—from edge-level convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to billion-parameter large language models (LLMs)—reveals that output stationary (OS) dataflows outperform weight stationary (WS) dataflows by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.53\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> on edge workloads, while WS achieves <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.13\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> higher efficiency over OS on server-scale computations. Based on these insights, we propose Fused-STA, a reconfigurable architecture that dynamically switches between OS and WS modes within a unified hardware substrate. Fused-STA achieves over 91% of the oracle single-dataflow performance, specifically designed for a single network, providing <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.34\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.92\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> improvements over a tensor processing unit (TPU)-like baseline on edge-level and server-level benchmarks, respectively.

title: A review of factors affecting the dynamics of spur gear-rotor system
authors: Huijun Yao, Azrul Abidin Zakaria, Jawaid Iqbal Inayat-Hussain
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive review of the dynamic model applicable to spur gearrotor systems. The modeling process of 1-DOF, 2-DOF and multiple degrees of freedom (multi-DOF) systems is discussed. Among them, the 1-DOF or 2-DOF dynamic model is usually used to simplify analysis and is suitable for examining the basic vibration properties of the system. However, multi-DOF models, such as 4-DOF, 6-DOF and above, provide more comprehensive characterization of the system’s nonlinear dynamics, particularly in capturing the coupled bending-torsional-axial vibration interactions. In this dynamic model, the calculations of meshing stiffness (MS), transmission error (TE) and backlash are the key aspects. This paper examines TE generation mechanisms and their correlation with dynamic system behavior. Backlash-induced nonlinear effects become particularly pronounced during high-speed operations and under fluctuating loads, significantly altering the system's vibrational signature. In this paper, the effect of backlash on the system’s vibration response is also examined. Regarding time-varying mesh stiffness (TVMS) determination, we evaluate four principal approaches, namely analytical method (AM), finite element method (FEM), hybrid method (HM), and experimental method (EM). Furthermore, parametric studies reveal how operational variables, particularly rotational velocity, friction coefficient, and mesh damping, govern system dynamics. These factors collectively determine vibration amplitudes, spectrum characteristics and operational stability of the system. Finally, the main achievements and shortcomings of spur gear-rotor system dynamics are summarized, and the directions for future research are proposed, which should focus on multi-factor coupling effects, nonlinear active control strategies, and experimental validation.

title: IRL-based Co-channel Interference Optimization for UAV Cellular Network Communication Links
authors: Lu Zhang, Danyang Qin, Bojia Zhao, Jiaqiang Yang, Haoze Bie, Lin Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: In urban cellular networks, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) serve as critical aerial nodes and are extensively deployed. However, during mission execution, they are susceptible to co-channel interference (CCI) with terrestrial networks, which can adversely impact quality of service (QoS). This paper develops an optimization model for uplink communication links in spectrum-sharing cellular networks involving UAVs and proposes a joint optimization framework for UAV trajectory planning and transmission power control. Specifically, we introduce a Double and Dueling Deep Q-Network via Inverse Reinforcement Learning (D3QN-IRL) algorithm featuring a prioritized experience replay mechanism with replay time, designed to investigate the impact of varying ground user density on optimization problems. The proposed algorithm integrates expert demonstrations with Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL), guiding UAVs to avoid interference hotspots within dynamic jamming environments and autonomously reduce transmission power when approaching densely populated user areas. Through this process, the UAV is capable of progressively learning the optimal policy to achieve joint optimization of trajectory and power allocation. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach significant outperforms existing methods in terms of throughput, signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR), and interference management, validating its effectiveness and potential for deployment in complex wireless communication environments.

title: Development of Columnar Tin-Doped Gallium Oxide Films Using an RF/DC Co-Sputtering Technique of Dual-procedure for Hydrogen Sulfide Sensing Applications_supp1-3694920.docx
authors: Ting-Jen Hsueh
abstract: This study presents the development of a highly sensitive hydrogen sulfide (H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>S) gas sensor that features Tin-doped Gallium Oxide (Sn-d-Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>) nanorod films with a micro-electro-mechanical systems (MEMS) platform using a dual RF/DC co-sputtering process. The dual-procedure deposition and the high-thermal treatment allow controlled nanorod formation, enhanced carrier transport and improved electrical conductivity, compared with undoped and single-procedure films. Thermal calibration demonstrates that there is a highly linear relationship between applied voltage and operating temperature (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.9988), so there is accurate temperature control for the microheater. Structural analyses confirm Sn incorporation, increased surface area and a polycrystalline nanorod morphology. The optimized sensor, which is fabricated at a high-thermal temperature of 197.2 °C and operated at 282.8 °C, exhibits a strong response to H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>S and high selectivity against interfering gases. The sensor achieves rapid response/recovery times of 12.1 s and 32.2 s, respectively, and demonstrates stable detection at sub-ppm concentrations, with a calibration linearity (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) of 0.994. Aging tests show that dual-procedure deposition significantly suppresses impedance drift and improves long-term stability. These results show that the synergistic combination of MEMS integration, Sn donor doping and dual RF/DC sputtering increases sensing performance, reproducibility and the stability of Ga<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>-based gas sensors, which are eminently suited to practical environmental monitoring applications.

title: Arithmetic-Structured Koopman Lifting for Data-Driven Modeling and Control of Integer-Periodic Nonlinear Systems
authors: Tushar Jain
abstract: This letter develops an arithmetic-structured Koopman lifting framework for nonlinear systems with integer-periodic dynamics. The central idea is to organize lifted observables into divisor-indexed blocks of a cyclic phase variable, where each block groups all harmonic subspaces sharing the same primitive period. Interactions between active blocks motivate a conservative lcm-completion rule for state-phase products. For periodically parameter-modulated linear dynamics, an exact finite-dimensional bilinear lifted representation with explicit matrix structure is derived. For more general systems, the cyclic phase component remains exactly closed under the divisor-block organization, while state closure is approximate and appears as a projection residual set by the chosen base dictionary. A block-necessity result shows that, under the stated modulation assumptions, active state-phase divisor blocks cannot be removed without losing closure of the lifted dictionary, giving a mechanism-level minimality property of the arithmetic organization. A divisor-block sparsification procedure identifies active periodic mechanisms from data. Experiments on a torsional drivetrain show consistent recovery of planted hidden periodicities and improved prediction accuracy under matched-period conditions; closed-loop LQR cost reductions are small but consistent across seeds on the tested benchmark.

title: Communication-Efficient Zero-Order and First-Order Federated Learning Methods over Wireless Networks
authors: Mohamad Assaad, Zeinab Nehme, Merouane Debbah
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) is an emerging learning framework that enables edge devices to collaboratively train ML models without sharing their local data. However, FL faces a significant challenge due to the high amount of information that must be exchanged between devices and the aggregator in the training phase, which can exceed the limited capacity of wireless systems. In this paper, two communication-efficient FL methods are considered, where communication overhead is reduced by communicating scalar values instead of long vectors and by allowing a high number of users to send information simultaneously. The first approach employs a zero-order optimization technique with a two-point gradient estimator, while the second involves a first-order gradient computation strategy. The novelty lies in leveraging channel information in the learning algorithms, hence eliminating the need for additional resources to acquire channel state information (CSI) and to remove its impact, as well as in considering asynchronous devices. We provide a rigorous analytical framework for the two methods, deriving convergence guarantees and establishing appropriate performance bounds.

title: Scenario-Aided Robust Factor Graph Optimization for GNSS/INS Integration in Complex Environments
authors: Yueyang Ben, Chen Lv, Zhaoyang Wang, Qian Li, Yabo Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The integration of global navigation satellite systems (GNSS) and inertial navigation systems (INS) employs robust methods to ensure reliable navigation solutions in challenging environments, where the robustness parameter is used to balance accuracy and estimator stability. However, in time-varying GNSS conditions or prolonged harsh environments, a parameter–environment inconsistency may lead to incorrect rejection or excessive down-weighting of normal measurements, causing accuracy degradation or divergence. To address this issue, this paper proposes a scenario-aided robust factor graph optimization (RFGO) algorithm that adapts the robustness parameter to time-varying conditions. Scenarios are first classified based on GNSS/INS integrated error characteristics, and an observation model is constructed using GNSS signal features. Considering the latency of the integration scenarios induced by INS characteristics, a hidden semi-Markov model (HSMM) with state duration modeling is employed for scenario awareness. Based on the inferred scenarios, a scenario-driven robustness adjustment strategy is developed within the dynamic covariance scaling-based RFGO framework, thereby ensuring consistency between the robustness parameter and the actual scenarios. Vehicle experiment results illustrated that the HSMM achieved recognition accuracies of 80.24%, 75.48%, and 96.55% in three different scenarios, respectively. Compared with fixed-parameter methods, the proposed method increased the proportion of high-accuracy epochs by 13.6%–27.1% and reduced abnormal epochs by 41.4%–51.4%, demonstrating improved accuracy and solution stability in complex environments.

title: The Fast Full analytic al Evaluation Method for Scattering Parameter Uncertainty Based on Multiple Hybrid cross Propagation Model
authors: Huanxin Li, Ziwei Li, Yuming Bai, Fenglin Yang, Xiaohai Cui, Zhijiao Chen, Shuo Chen, Wenze Yuan, Chongyang Zhang, Kejia Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Precise and rapid evaluation of scattering parameter (S-parameter) uncertainties is critical for microwave devices. This paper investigates uncertainty propagation from noise and nonideal calibration standards, proposing a multiple hybrid-cross propagation model (MHPM). The MHPM reveals that noise introduces uncertainty twice during calibration and measurement, interacting with uncertainties from nonideal standards through a hybrid-cross operation. Targeting this model and using the Short-Open-Load-Thru (SOLT) calibration example, we develop a fast fully-analytical evaluation method (FFEM) by expanding, decomposing, and computing the Jacobian matrix while accounting for correlated uncertainties. The method is validated by measuring uncertainties of 40 dB and 6 dB attenuators. Comparisons with the traditional Monte Carlo (MC) method confirm the FFEM’s reasonableness and credibility. Crucially, the FFEM avoids iterative computations with large random samples, achieving significantly faster speeds. The uncertainty matrix also enables analysis of covariances and Pearson correlation coefficients among different S-parameters.

title: An Explicit-Thinking LLM-Based Cognitive Apprenticeship Approach to Promote Students' Metacognition, Higher-Order Thinking Skills, and Learning Performance
authors: Yihua Zhong, Jili Chen, Changqin Huang, Xizhe Wang, Yixuan Chen, Tao He
abstract: With the rapid development of generative AI, large language models (LLMs) have been increasingly applied in education. Although LLMs demonstrate considerable potential in supporting student learning, conventional LLMs tend to provide direct answers without explicitly articulating their reasoning processes. This hinders students' ability to comprehend the underlying logic of problem-solving, thereby limiting opportunities for metacognitive engagement and the development of higher-order thinking skills. To address this issue, this study proposes a cognitive apprenticeship approach based on an explicit-thinking LLM that provides structured thinking support during LLM-based learning activities. By employing the modeling, coaching, and reflection mechanisms of cognitive apprenticeship, the approach guides students to internalize the LLM's reasoning processes, thereby fostering their higher-order thinking skills. A quasi-experimental design was adopted, involving 68 college students divided into experimental and control groups. Participants completed a writing-based learning task using either the explicit-thinking LLM or a conventional LLM, and the data were analyzed using a mixed-methods approach that included eye-tracking. The results showed that, compared to the control group, the experimental group (1) exhibited enhanced metacognitive regulation, (2) demonstrated superior performance in both problem-solving and critical thinking, and (3) achieved better learning outcomes when they devoted greater attention to the reasoning process generated by the explicit-thinking LLM. This study suggests that the explicit-thinking LLM enhances students' learning performance and higher-order thinking by making reasoning processes more transparent. It further emphasizes the essential role of cultivating students' critical and creative engagement with AI to effectively support the development of higher-order thinking skills.

title: Title of the Papers
authors: N/A
