title: A Retrieval-Augmented Generation-Powered Chatbot for Air Force Policy and Logistics Compliance
authors: Vedant Mahawar, Sanya Bhalla, John McKee, Mihai Boicu
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Recent personnel cuts mandated by the U.S. government have intensified pressure on U.S. Department of the Air Force (DAF) personnel and highlighted the need to reduce administrative overload. A specific challenge arises from the more than 11,400 regulatory publications that DAF personnel must comply with. To increase operational efficiency and keep up with the increased adoption of generative artificial intelligence tools the Air Force Research Laboratory developed the Non-Classified Internet Protocol Router Generative Pre-trained Transformer (NIPRGPT). NIPRGPT is a chatbot designed for use by U.S. Department of Defense personnel, capable of answering questions from uploaded documents through retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). However, NIPRGPT is slow, computationally intensive, prone to hallucination, and has a restrictive context window. To address these limitations, this work presents a lightweight RAG chatbot that employs semantic chunking, twostep retrieval with a bi-encoder and a cross-encoder, and a small language model (SLM) to generate human-like responses. The proposed system was evaluated on 71 regulatory questions drawn from five DAF publications, with both answer and citation correctness assessed by two researchers and an Air Force lieutenant colonel. An Intraclass Correlation Coefficient of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 8 0}$</tex> showed high inter-rater reliability, and the system achieved 99.15 % of NIPRGPT's accuracy while delivering a 12.34 -fold improvement in response latency. These results demonstrate that lightweight RAG systems can substantially aid with the reduction of administrative overload within the DAF.

title: Optimal Electric Vehicle Charging System and Wind Turbine Placement for Loss Control Using Integrated Optimization Technique in Distribution System
authors: Nur Eizzi Safia Ahmad Nasrudin, Ismail Musirin, Muhamad Hatta Hussain, Rahmatul Hidayah Salimin, Nur Farahiah Ibrahim, Fathiah Zakaria
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: The optimal planning of electric vehicle charging stations (EVCSs) and wind-based distributed generation (DG) in the distribution systems involve an efficient and strong optimization method to reduce the power losses. Poor location and size of EVCSs and Wind-DGs may enhance power losses and worsen the performance of a network. To overcome these issues, this paper proposes a new hybrid optimization method called Integrated Honey Badger-Evolutionary Programming Optimization (IHBEPO). The proposed algorithm integrates the global search power of Evolutionary Programming (EP) and the efficient local refinement of Honey Badger Optimization (HBO) to overcome the local trapping behavior of EP and slow convergence rate of HBO in multidimensional search spaces. The approach includes the modelling of EVCSs as controllable statical loads and wind turbines as the DG Type-III units injecting active and reactive power. The IHBEPO method is applied to the IEEE 33-bus radial distribution system at different load increment levels with installation of 3 and 5 EVCSs with 2 wind-DGs. The results of simulation indicate that IHBEPO has a low total power loss compared to standalone EP and HBO, indicating its efficiency and strength in the planning of EVCS and Wind-DGs in the distribution networks. During base condition, IHBEPO achieved 82.10% loss reduction as compared to EP with 55.14% and 78.69% with HBO for 3EVCSs and 2 WTs scenario, while in 5 EVCSs and 2 WTs scenario IHBEPO achieved 81.74% loss reduction, 43.10% with EP and 77.97% with HBO. This indicates the outstanding performance of the proposed IHBEPO over EP and HBO.

title: Development of Power System Analysis Simulator Using LabVIEW
authors: Nor Asiah Muhamad, Arif Rusyaidi Abdul Rahman, Swee Peng Ang, Sheik Mohammed Sulthan
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: This paper presents the development of a LabVIEW based simulator for power flow and fault studies in electrical power systems. The objective is to create an interactive and easy to use interface to enhance the understanding of power systems under various loading conditions. The simulation panel will include analysis using Newton Raphson method for load flow, integration of graphical representation to demonstrate the behavior of faults, two power network which are IEEE 2 bus system and 3 bus system, and the four types of faults included are Single Line to Ground, Line to Line, Double Line to Ground and 3 phase balanced fault. This LabVIEW based simulator achieves complete similarity in load flow and fault analysis results when compared to commercial simulators such as PowerWorld and MATLAB. Hence, the LabVIEW based simulator will serve as an educational tool for students and researchers to further develop a complex power system network.

title: GTPin: Enhancing Intel GPU Profiling With High-Level Binary Instrumentation
authors: Konstantin Levit-Gurevich, Alex Skaletsky, Ortal Geller, Moshe Goren, Rawan Ziadat, Rami Burstein, Tal Oved
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: As GPUs are increasingly used for high-performance and data-intensive applications, optimizing workloads and memory behavior remains a significant challenge due to complex memory hierarchies, high parallelism, and limited runtime observability. Binary instrumentation has emerged as a key technique for analyzing and profiling GPU workloads under these constraints. This paper introduces new technology within GTPin - the binary instrumentation framework for Intel GPUs - adding support for high-level instrumentation alongside the previous low-level ISA insertion approach. The new mechanism enables developers to write analysis routines in a high-level programming language (OpenCL C) and dynamically invoke them at any instruction during kernel execution. This capability enables the implementation of sophisticated profiling tools for conducting advanced on-the-fly analyses and studies on code running on Intel GPUs, such as cache modeling, memory pattern inspection, race detection, and more, while maintaining reasonably low overhead on real hardware.In this work, we describe the design of the technology, present usage examples, provide a unique overhead breakdown analysis quantifying the performance impact of both high-level and lowlevel instrumentation, and compare it with other existing GPU instrumentation frameworks. This comparison shows that even with high-level instrumentation, on average GTPin has lower overhead. These capabilities bring GTPin to parity with leading GPU binary instrumentation frameworks, thus allowing the community to investigate, research, and analyze all three major GPU vendors in a uniform way.

title: Simulation Study of Laser Stealth Dicing Process Based on Multi-Time-Step Pulses
authors: Wei Zeng, Zhuoran Li, Ranyun Wu, Xuesong Deng, Yanlong Sun, Taotao Chen
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: The laser stealth dicing process enables efficient separation of silicon by forming a modified layer inside the material through laser irradiation with multi-time-step pulses, and the associated multiphysics simulation has become a research hotspot. However, existing simulation models have mainly focused on the mechanism of single-time-step pulse interaction, making it difficult to accurately characterize the material modification behavior induced by actual multi-time-step pulses. In addition, the influence of key parameters, such as pulse spacing, laser power, and doping concentration, on modified layer width has not yet been clearly clarified. To address these issues, this study establishes, for the first time, a multiphysics coupled model for laser stealth dicing based on multi-time-step pulses, and systematically investigates the formation of the modified layer and the evolution of the coupled effects of key process parameters. The results show that the rapid meltingsolidification process induced by a single-time-step pulse generates a modified region and significant residual thermal stress inside the material, and that the superposition of stress fields among modified regions generated by multi-time-step pulses is the key to the formation of the modified layer. Further investigation revealed that increasing the laser average power enhances energy deposition within the material and leads to a wider modified layer, whereas a higher P-type doping concentration reduces the extent of thermally induced stress by altering the single-photon and two-photon absorption processes, thereby resulting in a narrower modified layer. The findings of this study provide a theoretical basis for optimizing the laser stealth dicing process of wafers.

title: GenAI-Powered Unified Framework for Deepfake and Malware Detection with Secure Digital Content Analysis
authors: Pilli Naveen, Srinivasulu Tharakesh, Satheesh Kumar M
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Deepfake media and malware-based cyberattacks have become much more common as digital media and online file-sharing platforms have grown exponentially. Due to the serious risks this presents to people, businesses, and governmental organizations, intelligent and automated security solutions are required. In order to detect possible deepfake manipulation and malware threats, this study presents an AI-powered unified detection framework that can analyze images, videos, files, and URLs. By combining machine learning-driven malware classification with deep learning-based visual forensics, the system generates a combined severity score that measures the degree of threat. Users can download a comprehensive security assessment report, upload content, and view real-time detection results via an online platform. An efficient and dependable solution is provided by experimental evaluation, which shows how well the suggested system detects manipulated media and malicious binaries.Automated security analysis tools are becoming increasingly important due to the emergence of sophisticated cyber threats, such as deepfake media manipulation and sophisticated malware. Deepfakes undermine the veracity of digital media and support disinformation campaigns by using deep learning techniques to produce lifelike synthetic images and videos. Simultaneously, malware embedded in executables, documents, and URLs remains a major cyberattack vector, leading to financial loss, system compromise, and data breaches.

title: Predicting Mental Health Treatment-Seeking Behavior with Explainable Feature Selection
authors: Challagundla Manasa Gayathri, Jaidev Sharma, Debanjali Bhattacharya
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Accurately identifying whether individuals will seek treatment for mental health conditions is crucial in devising timely and targeted interventions. Most work on the OSMI dataset has focused on standard classification pipelines, which do little to investigate how feature-space optimization influences predictive stability, interpretability, and performance. This study introduces a comparative framework that evaluates the effectiveness of two distinct feature-selection strategies—interpretability-driven feature importance using SHAP and dimensionality reduction using PCA for predicting treatment-seeking individuals. By examining how each method influences model performance, the framework provides insights into both the predictive utility of the selected features and the interpretability of the underlying machine learning models. Reduced feature representations obtained by the SHAP and PCA techniques were used to train a variety of machine learning classifiers. Experimental results showed highest accuracy of approximately 84%. Results from the current study supports the empirical evidence that explainability-driven feature refinement leads to more robust and efficient models, while providing a practical and scalable framework for mental health risk screening and workplace well-being analytics.

title: Two-Stage Distributionally Robust Day-Ahead Scheduling of a Grid-Connected Pv-Storage Microgrid Under Source-Load Uncertainty
authors: Xiaoyan Bao
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This paper proposes a two-stage Wasserstein distributionally robust day-ahead scheduling method for a gridconnected PV-storage microgrid under source-load fluctuations. The main challenge is that photovoltaic and load forecast errors, together with intertemporal operating constraints, may amplify redispatch requirements and make an economical deterministic schedule incur substantial real-time correction cost. To address this issue, a first-stage baseline scheduling model and a secondstage redispatch model are integrated in a data-driven DRO framework. In particular, to characterize the time-varying volatility of source-load fluctuations, an adaptive hourly Wasserstein radius is designed according to recent net-load variation levels, so that the ambiguity set contracts in low-volatility periods and expands in high-volatility periods. The resulting model is reformulated into a tractable deterministic mixed-integer program via strong duality and solved by Gurobi. Case studies on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 4}-\mathbf{h}$</tex> microgrid show that the proposed method achieves a favorable trade-off between economy and robustness. Compared with a uniform-radius DRO model, the adaptive-radius scheme reduces the day-ahead scheduling cost by 4.08 %.

title: Augmented Intelligence Prediction of Liver Cirrhosis Using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: V.S. Adithyan, R. M. Sakthivel Suriya, Ramkiran B, Vijayabhaskar B, Pavithra M
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Liver cirrhosis is a chronic disease with variable patient outcomes, making accurate prognosis prediction clinically challenging. This study proposes a machine learningbased framework for multi-class classification of cirrhosis patient outcomes into censored/alive (C), liver transplant (CL), and death (D). The dataset contains patient records with clinical, demographic, and biochemical features. Multiple machine learning models, including Logistic Regression, Support Vector Machines, Random Forest, Neural Networks, and XGBoost, were evaluated, followed by hyperparameter tuning and ensemble learning. A weighted soft voting ensemble combining XGBoost and Logistic Regression gives the best performance, attaining an accuracy of 91.15% on the full dataset. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of augmented intelligent ensemble learning for modeling complex clinical data and highlight its potential to support prognosis assessment in patients with liver cirrhosis.

title: Forge Detect: Deepfake Detection Framework for Image and Video Media
authors: Merlyn Mary Stephen, Rinny Anna Thomas, Shalin Ann Thomas, Sneha Mariam Samuel, Jinu Thomas
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Continued advancement of deep learning has remained of high importance that has improved visual convincing of altered generation capabilities of photos and videos, commonly referred to as deepfakes. Although creativity and business are both application areas of this technologies, their misuse might provoke serious issues regarding information honesty, civic trust and internet security. These bogus visual media are yet to be removed or spotted of content generated and methods of enhancing the same constantly ameliorate themselves. In this paper, ForgeDetect is presented, a universal deep fakes detector compatible with both images and videos. The model involves convolutional neural, spatial analysis networks, segmentation, techniques. In order to find places where manipulation has taken place and recurrent neural networks to project time-related trends. For image detection, the technique is aimed at the detection of spatial anomalies, thus no one recognizes altered faces and offers understandable descriptions of its findings. In the case of video detection, it is a combination of frame-by-frame, modelling of time sequences, time sequence analysis, and motion clues to detect dissimilarity between frames. The framework is also tested using common benchmark datasets like OpenForensics and FaceForensics++ respectively. Results obtained from the experiments performed reveal that the offered approach can be applied effectively identifies doctored visual information and offers interpreting results that can be applied in the real world and forensic investigations.

title: AI-Powered Web-Based System for Early Detection and Management of Alzheimer's Disease
authors: Sri Pavithra B, Shree Sathiyaa S K, Utthirakumari
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Alzheimer's Disease is a long-term neurological condition that slowly affects an individual's memory, reasoning, and everyday behavior. Although research is increasing, its early detection is yet problematic. In this paper, we introduce an online system using artificial intelligence for the early detection and management of Alzheimer's disease. The proposed system integrates multi-modal clinical data, such as medical history, MRI of the brain, and genetics, for thorough checking. Machine learning, deep learning, and large language model approaches analyze the data to estimate the risk for developing Alzheimer's disease and the probable stage of the disease as early, mild, and moderate. An easy-to-use, interactive web interface built with Python enables users to view diagnostic reports, comprehend the chances of progression, and get advice on the steps they may take. The proposed system will contribute to clinical decision-making, facilitate early treatment, and improve access to specialist care by integrating LLM outputs with clinical data, imaging, and genetic information. This system can greatly reduce the delay in diagnosis and help with early intervention, thus increasing the quality of life for the patients.

title: A Hybrid Privacy-Preserving Framework for Machine Learning in Edge Computing
authors: D B Srinivas, Rohan H C, Sinchan Patel H R, Rakshita Jadhav
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The shift of edge computing towards specialized machine learning (ML) applications still experiences a situation of essential tension between low-latency and security designed in a decentralized way. however, its decentralized and partially untrusted nature poses significant privacy challenges for machine learning applications involving sensitive user data. To address these challenges, this paper aims to design and evaluate a unified privacy-preserving learning framework that ensures strong data confidentiality while maintaining model utility and system efficiency in edge environments. Accordingly, This paper proposes a Unified Differential Privacy-Fully Homomorphic Encryption-Federated Learning (DP-FHE-FL) framework for privacy-preserving machine learning at the edge. In the proposed framework, local model gradients are perturbed using a Gaussian mechanism to achieve <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\epsilon, \delta)$</tex>-DP guarantees. The differentially private gradients are then encrypted with a fully homomorphic encryption scheme, allowing aggregation of directly encrypted updates without exposing any intermediate steps. The decryption is done after aggregation, which guarantees end-to-end client updates confidentiality. The framework is evaluated using the Adult Census Income dataset from the UCI(University of California, Irvine) Machine Learning Repository in a simulated nonIID(Independent and Identically Distributed) edge environment with 100 clients. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves a favorable privacy-utility tradeoff, maintaining competitive classification accuracy of up to 86 % while providing both statistical and cryptographic privacy guarantees. Comparative analysis with federated learning approaches employing standalone differential privacy or homomorphic encryption shows that the unified DP-FHE-FL framework offers superior robustness under strict privacy constraints. These results indicate that the proposed framework is well suited for privacy-sensitive edge applications requiring secure computation and formal privacy assurances.

title: Research on Multi source Fault Information Fusion Diagnosis Based on BERT and Knowledge Graph enUS
authors: Cui Shiming, Wang Youming, Qu Tingting, Feng Ji, Qingyi Wang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Facing scattered engine fault data across heterogeneous texts, this study integrates BERT with knowledge graphs for multi-source fault fusion. A BERT-BiLSTM-Attention -CRF model extracts structured triples (NER F1: 92.85%, RE F1: $89.81 \%)$. Combined with FMECA and FTA, a domain knowledge graph is built, enabling rapid fault localization and root-cause reasoning. This offers an interpretable solution for intelligent engine health management.

title: Research on an Adaptive Multi-layer Feature Fusion Method Based on Pre-trained Models for Student Feedback Sentiment Analysis
authors: Xintao Zhan, Lin Ma, Qiaoling Chen
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: In the practice of student affairs management and educational services in higher education institutions, student feedback texts are an important source of information for understanding students’ needs, emotional states, and changes in psychological conditions. Unlike general evaluative texts, student feedback often involves implicit emotional expressions, complex emotional directions, and diverse feedback intentions. This requires sentiment analysis models to be sensitive to implicit emotional cues and sentiment tendencies to determine the emotional polarity of student feedback information. To address these challenges, this paper proposes an adaptive multi-layer feature fusion sentiment analysis model (SLA-GF) for student feedback. The model is based on the sentiment knowledge-enhanced pretrained model SKEP, extracting sentence vector features from multiple high-level semantic representations, and achieving adaptive integration of multi-layer semantic information through inter-layer attention and gating fusion mechanisms. Experimental results on the SMILE-College student feedback dataset show that the proposed SLA-GF model outperforms various baseline models in accuracy and Macro-F1 scores, effectively analyzing the emotional tendencies in student feedback and providing reliable technical support for student affairs management and mental health-related work.

title: Crops Disease Detection Using Deep Learning
authors: C. Gokul, V. Karthick, M. Devaki
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: A deep learning-based system has been created to detect plant diseases in mountainous areas which are experiencing continuous climate change and unstable terrain. The system analyzes leaf photographs for disease indicators even before they become quite apparent thus giving the chance for early treatment and improved crop management. The system is totally automatic and does not require manual inspection or handcrafted features; it relies entirely on convolutional neural networks which directly extract the visual patterns necessary to tell diseased and healthy apart from images. This method allows very precise separation of healthy and diseased crops despite the changing lighting, humidity and temperature conditions that are common in the hills. The model is trained and validated on reference datasets of diverse crop species and different disease classes to guarantee robustness and generalization. The system is capable of providing in real-time disease detection through image analysis and can be linked to smart farming platforms for facilitating decision-making. The proposed solution is very suitable for hill station agriculture where hard access and fast disease spread necessitate automated and trustworthy monitoring. To put it shortly, the present study demonstrates the capability of deep learning to improve disease detection, reduce crop loss and thereby become a partner in the sustainable agriculture of the mountainous regions.

title: Study on Evolution Mechanism and Statistical Characteristics of Short-Sequence Abnormal Wear of Pantograph Carbon Strip Under Coupling of Multi-Source Environmental Stresses
authors: Feng Xu, Chong Du, Xiaoliang Wu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: The pantograph carbon strip is a core currentcollecting component of the urban rail transit pantographcatenary system, and its short-sequence abnormal wear under high humidity and tunnel forced convection seriously endangers operation safety and increases maintenance costs. Based on desensitized on-site data of 23 vehicle samples over three consecutive observation cycles, this paper conducts an empirical study via variance statistical analysis, revealing that the carbon strip wear rate exhibits a significant nonlinear jump in the late observation period, with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 0. 8 {\%}}$</tex> increase in the mean value and a sharp expansion of sample dispersion. A multi-physics field coupling theoretical framework of “aerodynamic disturbance-three-body abrasive wear - micro-arc ablation” is constructed to clarify the failure mechanism: tunnel forced convection intensifies abrasive particle intrusion and contact force disturbance, which collaborates with high-humidity water films to induce contact resistance mutation and high-frequency micro-arc pyrolysis, and even hinders the formation of self-lubricating films on new strips, leading to early rapid deterioration. Finally, engineering-based source governance measures for the key inducements and a dynamic early warning model based on the wear rate first derivative are proposed. The research provides theoretical and engineering support for the prevention and control of carbon strip abnormal wear and the intelligent operation and maintenance of pantograph-catenary systems under multi-source environmental stress coupling

title: Dual-Stage Fine-Tuned ResNet50 for Disaster and Earthquake Image Classification
authors: Gunjan Shandilya, G Sunil, Tashi Negi, Garima Jaitly, Neha Sharma, Sheifali Gupta
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The fast and accurate evaluation of disaster damage is essential to the proper emergency response and post-disaster recovery. The paper proposes a deep-learning-based model to classify damage image of multiple disasters using dual-stage fine-tuned ResNet50. The suggested solution is based on transfer learning on ImageNet- trained weights and data enhancement to enhance resilience in a wide range of disaster cases, such as earthquake, flood, fire, and landslide, among other types of damages. To achieve the objective of targeting domain specific feature refinement, a dual stage finetuning is used, with a more general model, ResNet50, frozen first to promote feature learning, and followed by a gradual unfreezing of higher levels. The outcomes of the experiments prove the classification accuracy of 99, the precision and recall of 98, and it can be stated that this classification has a strong discriminative power and a low number of inter-class confusion. Such findings indicate that the suggested framework is an effective and scalable solution to the automated assessment of disaster damage on images.

title: SecureNet Lite: An AI-Powered Cybersecurity Platform for Smart Web Threat Detection
authors: Nishanth R, Tadikonda Gaurav Bharadwaj, Suraj Prakash, Surya Rangan H P
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Cybersecurity risks including phishing, stolen passwords, and malicious login attempts remain a large threat to internet users and small groups that cannot afford top-class cybersecurity. This makes them of limited use. To solve this issue, we designed SecureNet Lite, an easy-to-use cybersecurity solution that combines password breach identification, phishing link classification, and intelligent login monitoring. We store passwords securely through SHA-1 hashing techniques that embrace k-anonymity. This feature allows us to identify breaches without exposing personal information. For phishing, we utilize an XGBoost machine learning algorithm that analyzes link attributes to identify both existing and novel phishing attempts. Moreover, an intelligent login tracker identifies unusual login activities by analyzing location, device, and time. Performance analysis indicates it performs greatly at phishing identification, safe password checking, and identification of unusual login details. SecureNet Lite is designed to be easy to use, lightweight, and has a Flask-based backend. SecureNet Lite proves to be a great tool for internet users, college students, and groups because it offers numerous levels of security.

title: A Cross-Domain Fault Diagnosis Method for Bearings Based on Multi-Channel Attention and CORAL Transfer Learning
authors: Xinwei Lin, Wanli Gao, Lei Qi
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To address the challenges of unlabeled targetdomain data, significant rotational speed variations, and evident cross-domain distribution shifts in high-speed train bearing fault diagnosis, this paper proposes a cross-domain fault diagnosis method that integrates dynamic window slicing, multi-channel attention modeling, and CORAL-based domain alignment. First, the signal segment length is adaptively adjusted according to the bearing rotational speed, and a multidimensional feature system consisting of time-domain, frequency-domain, time-frequencydomain, and entropy features is constructed. A cross-domain stable feature subset is then selected through feature validation. Second, a multi-channel attention neural network is developed to independently model and adaptively fuse multiple types of features, thereby improving fault identification performance in the source domain. Then, CORAL is employed to align the feature distributions between the source domain and the target domain, forming a transfer diagnosis framework for the unlabeled target domain. In addition, a target-domain self-training adaptation strategy combining pseudo-labeling and LoRA is further explored as an extension for lightweight target-domain adaptation. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves accuracies of 94.73% and 96.10% on fine-grained and coarse-grained classification tasks in the source domain, respectively. In the unlabeled target domain, CORAL alignment effectively improves prediction stability, indicating its potential to alleviate cross-domain statistical discrepancy under unlabeled target-domain settings.

title: RiderMate - Intelligent Pothole Alert System for Two-Wheeler Riders
authors: Apoorv Gupta
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The problem of potholes continues to be a critical safety issue on Indian roads, especially for two-wheeler riders, causing thousands of fatalities each year. India has 21 crore twowheelers with approximately 47% of Indian households owning one, making India the country with the largest two wheeler population globally. The paper describes the design and implementation of a Intelligent Pothole Alert System built specifically for two-wheelers. This hardware-enabled solution combines a Raspberry Pi computer and YOLOv5 deep learning model to detect potholes, a LiDAR sensor to estimate depth, an ESP32 microcontroller for handling the sensors and interfacing, and a mobile application using GPS technology for location and pothole reporting. Addressing significant limitations of existing detection systems, this device achieves depth-based severity classification, accurate GPS tagging, and facilitates reporting to civic maintenance authorities in a wireless mode. The deep learning-based device uses both the COCO dataset and published Kaggle dataset on potholes to assist the training, achieving a detection accuracy of 94.30% with very few false positives when tested in real-world scenarios. It is robust in terms of consistent performance in real-world variations such as rain, night-time use, and shadow. The system is compact, cost effective and scalable, making it suitable for deployment in India's large two-wheeler user-base leading to safer roads and smarter road infrastructure.

title: Dual-Stage CNN-Based Segmentation and Detection Framework for Precise Thermal Hotspot Localization in Solar Photovoltaic Modules
authors: Mohd Zulhamdy Ab Hamid, Kamarulazhar Daud, Muhammad Khusairi Osman, Iza Sazanita Isa, Mohd Shawal Jadin, Rosheila Darus
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Automated hotspot detection in solar photovoltaic (PV) modules faces significant challenges due to complex backgrounds, varying illumination, and low thermal contrast, which often lead to false detections and inaccurate localization. To address these limitations, this study proposes a dual-stage CNN-based framework that integrates U-Net for PV module segmentation and YOLOv11 for thermal hotspot detection. The framework aims to improve detection accuracy by first isolating regions of interest, minimizing background interference, and guiding precise localization. Image enhancement techniques were applied to refine segmentation quality. Quantitative evaluation showed strong model performance. In stage 1, the segmentation model achieved a high pixel accuracy (PA) of 98.95% and a mean Intersection over Union (mIoU) of 97.80%, demonstrating the model's excellent ability to accurately differentiate solar PV arrays from the background. In Stage 2, the detection model attained a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 95.25% and an F1-Score of 90.45%, demonstrating the model’s robust capability to precisely localize hotspots within the segmented PV regions. Qualitative results further validated the importance of segmentation, showing clearer hotspot localization and fewer misclassifications. This dual-stage approach offers a robust and scalable solution for real-world PV inspection, enhancing reliability in hotspot identification. The findings support future advancements in intelligent monitoring and predictive maintenance of PV systems.

title: Plane-Aware Depth Estimation with Fusion of Planar and Non-Planar Regions
authors: Weidong Zhang, Xiaoyu Qin, Ruiqi Zhao
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Monocular panoramic depth estimation aims to predict scene depth from a single 360° panoramic RGB image. Unlike conventional perspective images, panoramas provide a wider field of view but also introduce more severe distortions. In recent years, deep learning-based approaches have made remarkable progress in this field. However, most existing methods overlook the planar priors present in scenes. To address this limitation, we propose a decoupled depth estimation framework that utilizes an attention mechanism to separately process planar and free-form regions. Specifically, for detected planar regions, depth is derived by fitting planar parameters, while for free-form regions, depth is predicted through an adaptive learning strategy. The two depth components are then effectively fused to generate a complete and accurate panoramic depth map. Experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves superior performance across multiple benchmark panoramic datasets.

title: Speed Regulation System of PMSM Based on GA-Fuzzy PI
authors: Linsheng Li, Haolong Yuan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of traditional fuzzy PI controllers for Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSM), such as over-reliance on expert experience, low control accuracy, and time-consuming parameter tuning, this paper proposes an optimization scheme for fuzzy PI controllers based on Genetic Algorithm (GA). This scheme enhances the adaptability of expert experience in a specific control system through genetic algorithm, thereby improving the overall control accuracy. The genetic algorithm is used to optimize the weights of fuzzy rules and related parameters, and the performance of the optimized system is compared with that of the motor under the traditional control strategy through simulation. The simulation results show that under various operating conditions, the optimized fuzzy PI control system improves the response speed, shortens the electromagnetic torque fluctuation time, the overall robustness of the system is better, and the dynamic response effect is better.

title: Radar-based Vehicle Detection for Traffic Monitoring with High-Resolution Data
authors: Ché Habib, Mohammed Yunus Abdul Gaffar
abstract: This paper presents the development and evaluation of a multi-vehicle detection processor tailored for high-resolution radar data in traffic monitoring scenarios. The processor implements three Constant False Alarm Rate (CFAR) algorithms — CA-CFAR, OS-CFAR, and TM-CFAR — with an emphasis on minimizing false alarms and optimizing detection accuracy. An evaluation framework based on detection accuracy and false alarm count was employed to assess performance across recordings with differing vehicle scenarios. A key innovation is a combined detection framework that fuses time-domain and frequency-domain processing using logical operations to minimise false alarms without degrading detection accuracy. Performance is assessed on multiple measured radar recordings, with results demonstrating that CA-CFAR performs best for single-target scenarios while, TM-CFAR excelled in multi-target environments. The combined CA-CFAR and TM-CFAR approach, through a logical OR operation, provided the best overall balance of detection accuracy and low false alarm rate. This work establishes a practical foundation for evaluating CFAR-based vehicle detection and paves the way for future research on challenging radar traffic scenarios.

title: Exploring Classification Models and Input Representations for Arabic Readability Assessment
authors: Sarah F. Alhalees, Nagham A. Elshbrawi, Raya Y. Abu Aljamal, Fatimah M. Alsinan, Feryal E. Jadallah, Bayan Z. Barmem, Shahd A. Alahdal
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Assessing Arabic text readability is a complex challenge that is essential for the development of educational content and increasing accessibility. This paper introduces an Arabic readability classifier designed to align texts with readers’ proficiency levels. To support this task, we leverage the Bayyin dataset, a carefully compiled and preprocessed corpus of 46,410 sentences from diverse Arabic sources, annotated across six levels of difficulty, ranging from very simple text to highly advanced material, to support multi-level readability analysis. The study evaluates three distinct modeling approaches on this dataset: machine learning (SVM, Random Forest, XGBoost), deep learning (GNN, TextCNN, BiLSTM), and transformer-based models (AraBERTv2, CAMeLBERT-Mix, CAMeLBERT-MSA). The models were trained using various feature extraction techniques, including statistical features, TF-IDF, Word2Vec, and AraBERT embeddings, along with specific tokenization schemes (Word, D3Tok, Lex, D3Lex). Transformer-based architectures outperformed other models, effectively capturing the linguistic complexity needed for readability assessment. The best overall results were achieved by combining AraBERTv2 with the D3Tok input representation, yielding a 0.8800 Quadratic Weighted Kappa and 0.6607 Macro F1. The models distinguished levels one and six well, but intermediate levels remain challenging due to Arabic’s rich morphology and syntactic variations, which blur boundaries between levels. Overall, this work highlights how pairing a carefully curated Arabic dataset with advanced modeling techniques can improve multilevel readability assessment.

title: Detection of Bias in Recommender Systems Using Word Embeddings
authors: P V Vidya, Ajeesh Ramanujan, T K Thejwin
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Recommender systems enhance personalization but often amplify demographic and popularity biases. This study analyzes representational bias in embeddings from a LightFMbased hybrid recommender trained on the MovieLens $\mathbf{1 0 0 K}$ dataset. Exploratory analysis and t-SNE visualizations reveal clustering by gender, age, and genre, indicating implicit encoding of demographic and popularity-driven preferences. Despite strong ranking performance $(\mathrm{AUC}=0.92)$, the model exhibits low precision and recall due to sparse interactions. The results show that embedding-level analysis is a useful tool for bias detection. Future work will integrate fairness metrics, debiasing methods, and model comparisons to promote equitable recommendations.

title: Exploring Embedding Effectiveness in Course Recommendation: TF-IDF, Word2Vec, BERT, and Ensemble Learning
authors: Yves Matanga, Chunling Du, Etienne Van Wyk
abstract: With the rise of NLP, course recommender systems have become relevant tools to help universities suggest courses that are accurately tailored to learners’ needs. The current research explores the use of Word2Vec word embeddings, transformer-based embeddings, and TF-IDF matrices as feature extraction techniques to effectively capture information content from textual data in dense vector representations, enabling more accurate recommendations. The performance of these three feature engineering techniques is compared for a career recommendation problem of text classification on a university curriculum dataset and on an external user-based survey dataset. The findings of this paper highlight the relevance of dense embeddings to better capture semantic information and word closeness, with a particular observation of competitive results for the static Word2Vec embedding technique, achieving a 76.5% F1-score compared to 72.4% for BERT and 50.4% for TF-IDF on an external user dataset, undermining the need for more complex transformer embeddings for the task at hand. An ensemble voting classifier combining the top machine learning models in each embedding marginally improves the predictive accuracy (F1) to 78.9%.

title: A Hybrid Optimization Framework for Automatic Design of Arbitrary-Topology Matching Networks
authors: Jing Leng, Wei Feng, Hongtao Xu, Ye Lu
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: Traditional LC matching network (LCMN) circuit design heavily relies on expert experience to first determine the topology and then achieve broadband specifications by iterative simulations, which is time-consuming and labor intensive. To overcome these limitations, we propose a hybrid optimization framework for the automatic design of arbitrary-topology LCMN. This framework conveniently integrates various circuit topologies into design search space. It first utilizes the global search capability of simulated annealing (SA) to explore and generate a well-distributed population (P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf>) of design variables, and this optimized P<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</inf> is further used by non¬dominated sorting genetic algorithm II (NSGA-II) for efficient creation of LCMN design. The proposed framework has been demonstrated in three different LCMN circuit experiments, and the layout implementation has also been realized. This work provides a route for efficient design of future high-frequency (HF) circuits in an automated fashion.

title: A Detailed Review of Vein Enhancement System for Improved Vein Visualization
authors: Almaria Joseph, Alwin Tomy, Ayana Saji, Dhanush K Anil, Riya Cicily Ignatias
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Vein localization is a significant clinical challenge in cases of Peripheral Difficult Venous Access, where first-attempt cannulation failure rates of about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{2 4}$</tex> percentage lead to patient pain and complications such as hematoma or nerve injury. To address this, non-invasive Near-Infrared (NIR)–based vein detection systems have been developed, exploiting the strong absorption of NIR light (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$660-1000 \text{nm}$</tex>) by deoxygenated hemoglobin to generate high vein-to-tissue contrast. Research emphasizes wavelength optimization, with 830 nm performing better for dark skin tones, 850 nm for light skin tones, and 864 nm offering balanced performance across skin tones and BMI. Embedded systems employ denoising, CLAHE-based contrast enhancement, and signal processing methods such as Recursive Least Squares, along with advanced techniques including vein warming, deep learning models like Attention U-Net and GR-UNet, and reinforcement learning for real-time alignment. Supported by multithreaded processing, these systems achieve near real-time performance of around 37 FPS and detection accuracies up to 88 percentage for dorsal hand veins, making them practical, portable, and cost-effective tools for assisting clinicians during difficult cannulation procedures. This paper is a comprehensive review, and no independent hardware prototype was developed; all performance figures and system characteristics discussed are derived from previously published literature

title: Integration of Wireless Sensor Network with a CubeSat Satellite System for Enhanced Agricultural Monitoring
authors: Mayamiko Msonkho, Arno Barnard
abstract: This project aims to improve agricultural monitoring in African farming communities through the integration of cost-effective ground-based IoT devices that work with CubeSat satellite imaging. The proposed system integrates data from wireless sensor networks (WSNs) with high-resolution satellite imagery, offering farmers and agricultural researchers essential insights into crop health, soil conditions, and environmental factors, thereby facilitating data-driven decision-making for better resource utilization. This technology represents a substantial improvement over conventional techniques followed in most African farming communities, providing agricultural efficiency and resilience. The system consists of sensing ground nodes, connected in a mesh network—Meshtastic(R)—to a gateway node. Multiple gateway nodes connect to the satellite using a modified Time Division Multiple Access (TDMA) schema.

title: Total Dose and SEE Testing of the ISL74420M Radiation Tolerant Quad Clock Fanout IC
authors: N. W. van Vonno, W. H. Newman, L. G. Pearce, E. J. Thomson, M. L. Campanella
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: We report the results of total ionizing dose and singleevent effects testing performed by Renesas Electronics America on samples of the ISL74420M radiation tolerant Quad Clock Fanout IC. The part provides four synchronized clocks and is particularly useful in multiphase power converters. Multiple parts can be cascaded to create more than four synchronized clocks. Each of the four outputs can be set to a different frequency division and phase delay, which are set by selection pins or over an I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>C/SMBus interface. The part is fabricated in the Renesas 0.25 μm VIS25RT BCD mixed-signal process.

title: A Scalable RISC-V Symmetric Multiprocessor
authors: Tzu-Chen Yang, Ye Chen, Lin-En Yen, Yu-Ting Lee, Chun-Jen Tsai
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: In this paper, we present the design of a scalable RISC-V Symmetric Multiprocessor (SMP) for FPGAs for embedded systems. The number of processor cores of the system is configurable through an HDL parameter at synthesis time. The coherent memory subsystem of the processor adopts the snooping bus scheme and is composed of the private L1 caches of each core, a shared L2 cache of all cores, and the main DRAM memory. The proposed system supports FreeRTOS 11.2 for SMP scheduling. One of the key points of this research is to conduct analysis of the impact of the coherent memory events on the snooping bus as the number of cores increases. The scalability of the proposed design is only limited by the logic capacity of the FPGA devices. For this paper, the design has been verified using a Xilinx Kintex-7 XC7K325T FPGA device with up to 16 cores. The entire hardware and software source code of the system is open-source for other researchers to verify and improve upon.

title: A Real-Time Video-Based Accident Detection and Emergency Alert System Using Deep Learning
authors: Dharani V, Harini S, Hari Aravind A, R. Lalitha, K. Dhanalakshmi, P. Kabitha
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The recent advances in deep learning technology have improved the abilities of automated accident detectors. This paper proposes developing a low-cost accident detection system utilizing deep learning technology for the assessment of accident severity in videos. The system utilizes a YOLO Deep Neural Network that has been specifically designed to classify the events of the accidents based on the results of the accident patterns that are analyzed in the videos. The spatial evaluation for the events in the accidents requires an evaluation that utilizes the neural network model that is specifically optimized for the purpose of detecting objects during the evaluation process for consistency in the evaluation of the videos that contributes to the results for the classification evaluation. Evaluation for the videos is conducted through the OpenCV library. The occurrences of serious incidents are detected, the system autonomously records the important metadata of time stamps, geographic locations, and the captures of the evidential frames. The developed system provides a more efficient, accurate, and automated means of surveillance of the road safety, which has immense application in the highway monitoring system, intelligent traffic management system, and emergency response networks.

title: Performance Boost in RF HEMTs Through a Polarization-Enhanced 10-nm InAlGaN Barrier with T-Gate Process Integration
authors: Lung-Hsing Hsu, Ming-Hsuan Kao, Wen-Hsien Huang, Jia-Min Shieh, Chih-Yao Chang, Yen-Yang Hsieh, Hao-Chung Kuo, Chuan-Yeh Yu, Yu-Lun Chueh, Chang-Hong Shen
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: We optimize epitaxy and device architecture to improve the DC and RF performance of GaN-on-Si HEMTs simultaneously. The study explores quaternary InAlGaN/GaN barriers with calibrated Al compositions and a 1.5-nm AlN spacer to strengthen polarization coupling and 2DEG confinement and implements an air-surrounding T-gated (AS-TG) module that forms a 50 nm air gap to effectively reduce the equivalent dielectric constant of the passivation stack, thereby minimizing parasitic capacitances (C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">gs</inf> and C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">gd</inf>). Epitaxial tuning increases drive current, reduces on-resistance, and boosts g<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</inf>—while revealing the expected trade-off of more negative V<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</inf> and enlarged subthreshold swing under excessive polarization. Integrating AS-TG with a 0.15 μm T-gate raises f<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</inf> about 33% in the binary barrier. These results validate materials-to-device co-design as a practical path to high-efficiency, high-frequency GaN RF power amplification.

title: A LLM-Assisted High-Level Synthesis Code Transformation from C++ to SystemC
authors: Ray-Che Hsu, Hung-Yu Kao, Chih-Tsun Huang, Jing-Jia Liou
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: HLS (High Level Synthesis) is a design tool to use high level language (C/C++/SystemC) as the design entry to produce HDL codes. HLS can produce high-quality HDL designs, especially for signal processing algorithms and specialized AI workloads. To streamline the conversion process from C++ to synthesizable SystemC, we propose to assist designers in applying high-level synthesis more efficiently with Large Language Model (LLM). The flow covers two major components in the translation process: front-end language wrapper from C++ to synthesizable base SystemC and code transformation from base SystemC to vendor-support HLS SystemC.In the experimental results, with GPT-4.1, 276 codes are collected and translated into SystemC for HLS evaluation. By applying proper program repairing and interface model, 62.7% of generated RTL codes can pass full function and timing simulations with a commercial tool, which is much higher than a baseline at 12.7%.

title: Smart IoT E-Voting Framework with Anomaly Detection and Firebase Secure Data Management
authors: P. Vishnu Kumar, Kopparapu Venkata Naga Sai Srivalli, Yasaswini Gongati, Kuruva Hasini, S Sri Lakshmi Navya
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The paper proposes a Smart IoT-based electronic voting system to ensure the voting process is secure and properly managed. The proposed system is developed using an ESP32 microcontroller and fingerprint authentication to validate each voter's identity and avoid illegal voting. A GSM module is used to enable continuous real-time communication, making it possible for the system to work effectively even in remote locations. A GPS sensor is employed to track the location of voting. As soon as illegal voting is identified, the system automatically sends the location to higher authorities using an administrative mobile application. The Node-RED dashboard is utilized specifically for casting votes, making the voting process simple and user-friendly. It has a clear interface that enables voters to cast their votes without any confusion. Basic anomaly detection is done in the background to detect any unusual/suspicious voting activity. Firebase is utilized as the cloud service for securely storing voter information, voting data, and location information, ensuring that the data is accurate, up-to-date, and easily accessible throughout the entire voting process.

title: AI-Powered Digital Twin System for Enhancing Energy Efficiency in Industrial Process Control
authors: Monica K M, Marish Kumar P, Padmavathi M, K Mekala, Rashmi Pattan, Kasi Viswanathan
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: In the context of Industry 4.0, the focus of smart and efficient automation through the use of intelligence and the reduction of energy consumption becomes a prime requirement for modern industrial systems. In data-rich environments, the adaptability, scalability, and efficiency of traditional automation systems are often considered to be of lower value. In this context, this paper proposes the implementation of a Digital Twin-based framework with the inclusion of Federated Learning and artificial intelligence for the monitoring and automation of industrial process operations. The main aim of this research paper is to create a reliable automation system for controlling industrial operations with the simultaneous reduction of wastage of energy and improvement of the responsiveness of the overall system. In this context, the publicly available Energy Efficiency dataset was obtained from the Kaggle platform, and modeling was done using Python with TensorFlow, Pandas, and Matplotlib. The proposed method was able to attain an accuracy rate of 98.5%, which is higher than many existing benchmark methods. Various comparative graphs and graphical representations related to the energy parameters also depict the wide applicability of the proposed system, focusing on the importance of energy conservation. From the results, it is evident that the proposed system, which combines the concepts of Federated Learning and Digital Twins, is capable of providing better real-time insights, maintaining data privacy, and scaling up the automated industrial operations seamlessly. According to the researchers, the proposed method has significant potential to be implemented in the present industrial sector, focusing on the importance of efficient and environmentally friendly operations, overcoming the drawbacks of the conventional centralized monitoring methods. The results also help in the development of more intelligent automation, reducing the environmental hazards caused by industries

title: Development of a Double Scattering System for Radiation Effects
authors: Casey Corbridge, Vladimir Derenchuk, Jacob Shamblin
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: ProNova Solutions stands as a leading choice for proton testing, delivering exceptional convenience and efficiency. Enhancing its capabilities, the facility now incorporates double scattering technology to produce a 40 cm diameter beam with approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}-\mathbf{1 5} \boldsymbol{\%}$</tex> flatness. Additionally, ProNova has commissioned a precise 20 cm diameter beam with flatness up to 1%. Together with a dedicated cyclotron, these advancements ensure efficient and seamless testing for users.

title: Automated and Accessible Leukemia Detection from Microscopic Blood Films Using YOLO and Vision Transformers
authors: Andrew Blayama Stephen, Miracle James Olotuche, Mona Aman, Azizuddin Ahmed Biyabani
abstract: Leukemia remains a leading cause of cancer-related deaths, underscoring the urgent need for early and accessible diagnosis, especially in low-resource settings where expert analysis and advanced equipment are often lacking. To address this gap, we utilize a two-stage, resource-efficient diagnostic pipeline using low-cost microscopic blood film images. In the first stage, white blood cells (WBCs) are detected and their morphological attributes analyzed using object detection models. Building on the baseline model, the second stage classifies leukemia into five major subtypes. A fine-tuned You Only Look Once (YOLO)v5x model achieved a mean Average Precision 50 (mAP@50) of 47.87 for WBC detection, while a lightweight YOLOv11n variant offered faster inference with a mAP@50 of 46.56. Both models outperformed the baseline, demonstrating strong detection capabilities across varying computational demands. For subtype classification, a Vision Transformer (ViT)-based model reached 98% F1-score and 97% mAP. Our approach delivers a scalable, accurate, and accessible solution for leukemia diagnosis in underserved healthcare environments.

title: Joint Object Tracking and Intent Recognition
authors: Jiaming Liang, Bashar I. Ahmad, Simon Godsill
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: This paper presents a Bayesian framework for inferring the posterior of the augmented state of a target, incorporating its underlying goal or intent, such as any intermediate waypoints and/or the final destination. Thus, it is for joint object tracking and intent recognition. Several latent intent models are proposed here within a virtual leader formulation. They capture the influence of the target's hidden goal on its instantaneous behaviour. In this context, various motion models, including for highly maneuvering objects, are also considered. The a priori unknown target intent (e.g. destination) can dynamically change over time and take any value within the state space (e.g. a location or spatial region). A sequential Monte Carlo ( particle filtering) approach is introduced for the simultaneous estimation of the target's (kinematic) state and its intent. Rao-Blackwellisation is employed to enhance the statistical performance of the inference routine. Simulated data and real radar measurements are used to demonstrate the efficacy of the proposed techniques.

title: DDScatter: Enabling Delay-Doppler Modulation for Mmwave Radar-Backscatter Communication
authors: Zeming Yang, Bingbing Wang, Fengyuan Zhu, Linling Zhong, Wenhui Li, Yuanming Shi, Yunlong Lu, Haiming Jin, Xiaohua Tian
abstract: Millimeter-wave radar backscatter communication (Rad-BackCom) is emerging as a low-power, low-cost enabler for integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) in 6 G ambient IoT. Yet, existing mmWave Rad-BackCom designs face a hard trade-off: Delay-domain modulation offers higher rates but short range, while Doppler-domain modulation extends range but at extremely low throughput. We introduce DDScatter, a dual-function Rad-BackCom system that alleviates this trade-off by jointly exploiting Delay- and Doppler-domain spectral resources for two-dimensional Delay– Doppler (DD) modulation. The transmitted waveform is generated via 2D IDFT mapping, and demodulation is seamlessly performed using the native 2D DFT processing pipeline of mmWave radars. To address severe interference from environmental sensing echoes, we propose an inter-symbol matching filter (ISMF) algorithm that dynamically separates sensing and modulated components, enabling robust demodulation while doubling the SNR of sensing components. A prototype using commercial mmWave radars and COTS tags shows that DDScatter extends range by <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$4.5\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> over Delay-domain modulation and more than <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> data rate compared to Doppler-domain modulation, while preserving and enhancing sensing performance. DDScatter opens a path toward scalable, long-range, and high-efficiency ISAC for 6 G ambient IoT.

title: Online Learning of Modular Bayesian Deep Receivers: Single-Step Adaptation with Streaming Data
authors: Yakov Gusakov, Osvaldo Simeone, Tirza Routtenberg, Nir Shlezinger
abstract: Deep neural network (DNN)-based receivers offer a powerful alternative to classical model-based designs for wireless communication, especially in complex and nonlinear propagation environments. However, their adoption is challenged by the rapid variability of wireless channels, which makes pre-trained static DNN-based receivers ineffective, and by the latency and computational burden of online stochastic gradient descent (SGD)-based learning. In this work, we propose an online learning framework that enables rapid low-complexity adaptation of DNN-based receivers. Our approach is based on two main tenets. First, we cast online learning as Bayesian tracking in parameter space, enabling a single-step adaptation, which deviates from multi-epoch SGD. Second, we focus on modular DNN architectures that enable parallel, online, and localized variational Bayesian updates. Simulations with practical communication channels demonstrate that our proposed online learning framework can maintain a low error rate with markedly reduced update latency and increased robustness to channel dynamics as compared to traditional gradient descent based method.

title: Data-Driven Scalable FDI Attacks Detection for Cyber-Physical DC Microgrids
authors: Zhihua Wu, Chen Peng, Engang Tian, Yajian Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper proposes a data-driven detection framework for false data injection (FDI) attacks in cyber-physical DC microgrids. First, a novel data-driven characterization framework for cyber-physical DC microgrids is presented, eliminating the need for restrictive assumptions such as the invertibility of stacked data matrices and quasi-stationary linear approximations. Subsequently, a data-driven L∞ estimator is designed for real-time monitoring of the states of distributed generation units (DGUs), capable of adapting to noise-corrupted measurements. Meanwhile, a two-stage model-free scalable (TSMFS) attack detection framework is proposed. In the offline stage, historical data are leveraged to construct a set of linear inequalities whose solution yields a residual generator. The online stage generates detection residuals exclusively from received measurements, without relying on coupling information from neighboring DGUs, thus endowing the scheme with inherent scalability. Finally, experiments validate the performance of the data-driven estimator and the effectiveness of the proposed TSMFS detection framework.

title: 3D-FGRNet: 3D-Feature Guided Refinement Network for SAR Change Detection
authors: Bin Cui, Peng Zhu, Hong Fang, Yao Peng, Wenmei Li, Tao Xie
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) change detection is crucial for numerous remote sensing applications, yet it faces significant challenges including speckle noise and the scarcity of reliable ground truth labels. Traditional unsupervised SAR change detection methods often struggle with noise robustness and the effective generation of high-quality pseudo-labels for subsequent learning. This paper proposes a novel unsupervised framework, the 3D-feature guided refinement network (3D-FGRNet), specifically designed to overcome these limitations. Our approach first introduces a dual-domain constrained ratio (DDCR) technique to construct a robust difference image by integrating spatial and difference domain information, thereby significantly enhancing noise resistance. Subsequently, a boundary-aware algorithm is developed to extract high-confidence, spatially refined pseudo-labels from a three-dimensional feature space. These pseudo-labels then guide the training of a wavelet-based bi-dimensional aggregation network (WBANet) to generate precise change detection maps. Extensive experiments on three distinct datasets demonstrate that the proposed 3D-FGRNet shows superior efficacy and robustness compared to existing methods, making a significant contribution to the field of remote sensing.

title: HierRelTriple: Guiding Indoor Layout Generation with Hierarchical Relationship Triplet Losses
authors: Kaifan Sun, Bingchen Yang, Peter Wonka, Jun Xiao, Haiyong Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: We present a hierarchical triplet-based indoor relationship learning method, coined HierRelTriple, with a focus on spatial relationship learning. Existing approaches often depend on attention-based network designs and manually defined training objectives using handcrafted spatial rules or simplified pairwise relationships. However, these methods fail to capture complex, multi-object relationships found in real scenarios, leading to overcrowded or physically implausible arrangements. We introduce HierRelTriple, a hierarchical framework for modeling relational triplets, which first partitions functional regions and then automatically extracts three levels of spatial relationships: object-to-region (O2R), object-to-object (O2O), and corner-to-corner (C2C). By representing these relationships as geometric triplets and employing approaches based on Delaunay Triangulation to establish spatial priors, we derive IoU-based losses between denoised and ground-truth triplets and integrate them seamlessly into the diffusion denoising process. The joint formulation of inter-object distances, angular orientations, and spatial relationships enhances the physical realism of the generated scenes. Extensive experiments on unconditional layout synthesis, floorplan-conditioned layout generation, and scene rearrangement demonstrate that HierRelTriple improves spatial-relation metrics by over 15% and substantially reduces collisions and boundary violations compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: Hyperspectral Compressed Reconstruction for Anomaly Detection Based on Structured Spectral Dual-Tensor Coupled Decomposition
authors: Dan Li, Yanyan Lv, Fanqiang Kong, Qiang Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Hyperspectral imaging provides critical information for physical target characterization and anomaly sensing through high-dimensional spectral measurements. However, in practical imaging systems, constrained by sensor bandwidth, storage capacity, and energy consumption, hyperspectral data are often acquired in a compressed measurements. This makes it difficult to obtain accurate and reliable anomaly detection results from such incomplete measurements. Existing methods typically follow a sequential framework, in which compressed sensing reconstruction (CSR) is performed first, followed by hyperspectral anomaly detection (HAD). The two processes hinder their potential for synergistic optimization. To this end, we propose an integrated model called Structured Spectral Dual-Tensor Coupled Decomposition (SSDTCD), which achieves synchronized optimization of CSR and HAD tasks from compressed measurements. The model enhances the joint representational capability among spectral bands through a Structured Coupled Spectral Low-Rank Decomposition (SCSLRD), thereby improving the robustness of sparse reconstruction and the accuracy of HAD. Furthermore, a Dual-Tensor Sparse-Enhanced Decomposition (DTSED) is incorporated to achieve coupled optimization of low-rank structures and sparse components. Experiments on different datasets show that SSDTCD outperforms several state-of-the-art methods in both reconstruction accuracy and HAD precision. Moreover, it maintains stable and reliable sensing capability even under extremely low sampling rates, validating its effectiveness and practical value under constrained measurement conditions.

title: Frequency-Aware Causal Regularization for Multiple Instance Learning in Whole Slide Image Classification
authors: Dawei Fan, Lifang Wei, Mingyue Han, Tao Xu, Xuemei Qiu, Yanping Chen, Changcai Yang, Riqing Chen
abstract: Whole slide image (WSI) classification is a critical task in computational pathology and is aimed at providing automated diagnostic support through high-resolution tissue image analysis. In weakly supervised WSI classification scenarios, the main challenge concerns the traditional multiple instance learning (MIL) methods, which rely on instance-level embeddings aggregated by an attention-based pooling mechanism. These methods often depend on data-driven statistical correlations, leading to misalignments between their attention allocation schemes and histopathological diagnostic regions and reducing the resulting prediction reliability. To address this, we propose frequency-aware causal regularized multiple instance learning (FC-MIL), an innovative framework combining that combines frequency-aware attention (FAA) and causal regularization (CR). FAA extracts more granular, fine-grained histological textures by jointly modeling spatial- and frequency- domain features, whereas CR introduces feature-level counterfactual perturbations as an intervention-inspired regularizer in the latent space, encouraging the model to rely less on spurious correlations and more on invariant pathological cues. Experimental results obtained on four WSI datasets show that FC-MIL outperforms the state-of-the-art MIL methods in terms of both accuracy and interpretability. Our source code is available at https://github.com/7FFDW/FCMIL.

title: The Evolving Sentiments in Professional Communication: A Diachronic and Cross-Disciplinary Study of Academic Writing
authors: Hua Wang, Qingshun He
abstract: Background: The strategic use of positive and negative language is important to stance and credibility construction in professional communication. Academic writing is a primary mode of expert-to-expert communication, where linguistic sentiments reflect authorial negotiation and knowledge presentation. Literature review: While prior research suggested a linguistic positivity bias in specific contexts, diachronic trends in positivity and negativity across disciplines remains unexplored, particularly regarding how such shifts reshape communicative functions. Research questions: RQ1. What are the diachronic trends of linguistic positivity and negativity in academic writing across soft and hard sciences? RQ2. How do communicative functions of sentiment-laden sentences evolve across the two disciplinary groups? Methodology: A corpus of 3000 articles published between 1974 and 2023, from soft and hard sciences was compiled. Computational sentiment analysis was combined with manual rhetorical coding to identify sentiment trends and communicative functions. Results: Soft sciences become more neutral over time. Hard sciences remain stable in negative sentiment, but show an increase in positive sentiment scores. Functionally, soft sciences favor positive evaluations when introducing prior studies and attenuate sentiment in conclusions. Hard sciences increasingly introduce prior work critically and frame conclusions positively. Conclusion: These findings reveal evolving disciplinary norms in professional communication, where sentiment is strategically employed to construct knowledge and persuade peers. Writers may amplify innovation through positive framing or signal research gaps through critique. Such insights inform professional communication pedagogy by guiding students to balance critique and promotion to enhance professional authority and disciplinary alignment. Future research may examine sentiment patterns in other professional genres, such as corporate social responsibility reports and popular scientific texts.

title: Different Damp-Heat-Induced Series Resistance Degradation Behaviors in Fielded Crystalline Silicon Photovoltaic Modules Due to Difference in Bill of Materials
authors: Elizabeth C. Palmiotti, John S. Mangum, Steve W. Johnston, Rebecca B. Wai, Chun-Sheng Jiang, William B. Hobbs, Michael G. Deceglie, Timothy J Silverman, Ingrid Repins, E. Ashley Gaulding
abstract: This case study investigates mono-crystalline silicon modules from underperforming portions of a utility-scale photovoltaic power plant. Field-collected I-V curves and electroluminescence imaging suggested that increased series resistance was a primary factor driving module degradation. Selected modules were removed from the field for further analysis, including incremental damp heat accelerated testing, which confirmed a progression in series resistance degradation. Two distinct cell degradation behaviors became apparent during the investigation. Cross-sectional scanning electron microscopy (with elemental analysis) and scanning spreading resistance microscopy identified key differences between the two degradation mechanisms, primarily grid finger width and contact resistance. Additionally, the study highlights the reliability implications of retest requirements in International Electrotechnical Commission 61215 for material changes and how they may have mitigated the degradation observed at this site.

title: Intelligent Health Management for an Autonomous Lunar Rover
authors: Nour El-Din Safwat, Kavindu Ranasinghe, Kester Leibhardt, Lauren Edwards, Roberto Sabatini
abstract: The increasing demand for autonomous robotic systems in extended space exploration missions necessitates robust health management frameworks to ensure operational reliability in challenging and extreme extraterrestrial environments. This paper proposes an Integrated Vehicle Health Management (IVHM) framework designed to support NASA's RESOURCE Lunar Mission, with a focus on enabling the sustained operation of an autonomous lunar polar rover tasked with exploiting ice deposits at the lunar poles. The framework employs artificial intelligence in fault detection and diagnostic reasoning modules, using real-time sensor data to identify, predict, and mitigate subsystem faults. This approach enhances system reliability, safety, and mission success rates while reducing operational costs. Emphasis is placed on mission-critical communication and navigation subsystems, evaluated through digital twin simulations and structured fault injection techniques under nominal and degraded conditions. An Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) is employed as the IVHM reasoning engine to deliver interpretable fault diagnosis, early degradation detection, and rapid subsystem reconfiguration in dynamic operational scenarios. The results demonstrate the feasibility and efficacy of the proposed IVHM framework in advancing autonomous fault management for planetary exploration vehicles, providing a pathway for safer, more resilient, and cost-effective space missions.
