title: Efficient Federated Learning in IoV: Trajectory-Aware Communication Modeling and Incentive Mechanism
authors: Chen Chen, Chenyang Cao, Ji Feng, Bowen Li, Quan Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) in the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) faces key challenges such as high communication overhead, strong node heterogeneity, and highly dynamic path connectivity, which impede model convergence efficiency and system scalability. To address these issues, this paper proposes a trajectory-aware communication modeling and incentive-driven Federated Learning framework (TCM-FL). Specifically, the system extracts multi-dimensional QoS features based on real vehicle trajectories from the LuST dataset, calculates node communication trustworthiness scores using a Random Forest model, constructs a delay-weighted communication graph to support RSU-level path analysis and regional division, and introduces a joint weighting strategy considering communication quality and node contribution in the aggregation stage to enhance model training effectiveness. Additionally, a decentralized reward mechanism implemented via smart contracts encourages high-quality participation, utilizing the Gini coefficient and the standard deviation of regional contributions as fairness evaluation metrics. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms baseline schemes in terms of communication efficiency, model performance, and incentive fairness. Specifically, the classification accuracy reached 0.92, the average path length remained stable, the Gini coefficient dropped to 0.177, and regional contribution differences converged significantly. Ablation studies and baseline comparisons further verify the effectiveness and overall advantages of each core module.

title: CodeNER: Code Prompting for Named Entity Recognition
authors: Sungwoo Han, Hyeyeon Kim, Jingun Kwon, Hidetaka Kamigaito, Manabu Okumura
abstract: Recent studies have explored various approaches for treating candidate named entity spans as both source and target sequences in named entity recognition (NER) by leveraging large language models (LLMs). Although previous approaches have successfully generated candidate named entity spans with suitable labels, they rely solely on the input context information when using LLMs, particularly, ChatGPT. However, NER inherently requires capturing detailed labeling requirements with input context information. To address this issue, we propose a novel method that leverages code-based prompting to improve the capabilities of LLMs in understanding and performing NER. By embedding code within prompts, we provide detailed Beginning-Inside-Outside (BIO) schema instructions for labeling, thereby exploiting the ability of LLMs to comprehend long-range scopes in programming languages. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed code-based prompting method outperforms conventional text-based prompting on ten benchmarks across English, Arabic, Finnish, Danish, and German datasets, indicating the effectiveness of explicitly structuring NER instructions. Furthermore, our in-depth analysis showed that the performance improvement of the proposed method stems from code-based structuring and BIO-guided processing rather than from simple entity-label descriptions. Moreover, the method maintained its effectiveness or demonstrated additional performance improvements when combined with Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting and few-shot settings.

title: Fuzzy logic-based prescribed-time adaptive intelligent tracking control for uncertain nonlinear systems with deception attacks: A novel stability criterion
authors: Chong Li, Jianwei Xia, Ju H. Park, Jing Zhang, Hao Shen
abstract: In this paper, the challenging problem of prescribed-time (PT) adaptive intelligent tracking control is investigated for the nonlinear uncertain systems with external disturbance under sensor deception attacks. Firstly, since the existence of sensor deception attacks, the states and output of the systems in the network channel are unavailable. To compensate for the attack gains and achieve PT tracking control under deception attacks, we substitute the gains generated by deception attacks and the PT adjustment function into the designed coordinate transformation, and apply the Nussbaum function technique to process the attack gains in the controller design process. Secondly, based on the cited definition of the PT adjustment function, a novel stability criterion is proposed and proven. Then, utilizing the available compromised states, the corresponding PT adaptive intelligent tracking controller is constructed using backstepping method, and the fuzzy logic system (FLS) is applied in the design process to approximate the unknown continuous function, avoiding the “complexity explosion” problem caused by repeated differentiation and thus reducing the computational burden to a certain extent. Further, according to the proposed stability lemma, it is demonstrated that the designed control strategy is capable of enabling the systems output to achieve zero-error tracking within the prescribed time considering external disturbances and deception attacks. At last, the effectiveness of the designed control scheme is illustrated by MATLAB simulation results.

title: Nanobody-Based Detection of Ywhag Protein as a Cerebrospinal Fluid (Csf) Diagnostic Biomarker for Alzheimer's Disease: an in Silico Study
authors: Rehman Arif, Gaurav Sharma
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Alzheimer's disease (AD) is one of the most aggressive forms of neurodegenerative disorders and remains a leading cause of cognitive decline worldwide. Despite significant research, the detection of specific biomarkers remains a major challenge. Recently, the YWHAG protein has emerged as a potential biomarker associated with disease progression and cognitive impairment in AD patients. In this study, we propose a novel approach using nanobodies-small, single-domain antibody fragments-for the targeted detection of YWHAG. Due to their compact size and high affinity, nanobodies can cross the plasma membrane and be engineered for diagnostic imaging and therapeutic delivery. This strategy may enable earlier detection and improved monitoring of AD progression. We hypothesize that the nanobody 4 S11 may be a highly effective candidate for detecting the YWHAG protein, a potential biomarker for AD. The three-dimensional structure of the YWHAG protein was predicted using AlphaFold, while 17 nanobody structures were obtained from the Protein Data Bank. These nanobodies were subjected to molecular docking simulations against YWHAG to evaluate binding interactions. Nanobody 7MFV demonstrated the strongest binding affinity and most stable interaction with the YWHAG protein. Binding energy calculations further supported these findings, indicating that 7MFV exhibits the most favorable interaction profile. This study highlights a promising direction for the early detection and monitoring of cognitive decline in AD through nanobodybased diagnostics.

title: Column Twisted Reed-Solomon Codes as MDS Codes
authors: Wei Liu, Jinquan Luo, Puyin Wang, Dengxin Zhai
abstract: In this paper, we study column twisted Reed-Solomon(TRS) codes. We establish some sufficient conditions for these codes to be MDS and show that the dimension of their Schur square codes is 2k. Consequently, these TRS codes are shown to be not equivalent to Reed-Solomon(RS) codes. Moreover, our construction offers more flexible parameters than existing twisted generalized Reed-Solomon(TGRS) code designs. For a large odd prime power q, systematically constructed TGRS codes are known to be limited to length q+1/2 . By contrast, our column TRS construction supports code lengths up to q+3/2 . Finally, we present the dual codes of column TRS codes. Overall, this work introduces a new method for constructing MDS codes by appending column vectors to some generator matrix of an RS code.

title: Design and Evaluation of a Fast Dynamic On-State Resistance Measuring Circuit for GaN Power Devices
authors: Honglang Zhang, Hui Li, Yuhua Cheng, Shan Yin, Yingzhe Wu, Chen Song, Jinshu Lin, Xi Liu, Minghai Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: In recent years, the gallium nitride high-electron-mobility transistor (GaN HEMT) has been regarded as a promising candidate for high power density and high efficiency applications such as data center, motor drive, and consumer electronics. However, the dynamic on-resistance (RDS,on) degradation could lead to additional conduction loss and unexpected reliability issues. Thus, the fast and accurate characterization for this parameter is crucial. This paper presents a dynamic RDS,on measuring circuit with an optimized diode-based clamping circuit for the fast on-state voltage (VDS,on) measurement. The dynamic response time of the proposed clamping circuit is comprehensively analyzed and evaluated in this paper, and the guidelines for circuit design are given, which are validated by simulations and double pulse test experiments. The experimental results indicate that the proposed clamping circuit could reduce the dynamic response time by nearly 30% compared with the conventional method, and also achieve a faster dynamic response compared with the previous study. Then, the dynamic RDS,on measurement based on the proposed clamping circuit with different drain-source current sensing methods under the testing conditions of continuous operation, hard switching, and forward conduction is proposed. The details and evaluations of circuit designs are presented. The experimental results demonstrate that both methods could extract the dynamic RDS,on at 1 μs after the turn-on transient with a tolerance within 1.5%, and with dynamic response times of 47 ns and 144 ns, respectively.

title: Generative Structural Refinement via Importance-Aware Hypergraph Learning for Robust Higher-Order Representations
authors: Kai Yin, Yuxiang Zhao, Hao Chi, Hao Huang
abstract: Hypergraph modeling of higher-order interactions is essential in fields such as emerging consumer electronics ecosystems. However, robust hypergraph representation learning remains challenging due to the complexity of multi-way relationships and the prevalence of structural noise. Although conventional Hypergraph Neural Networks (HGNNs) often incorporate attention mechanisms, they typically rely on local feature aggregation over the observed incidence structure. This dependence can limit their ability to learn noise-invariant structural representations, rendering the models vulnerable to structural perturbations and distribution shifts. To address these limitations, we propose Importance-aware Hypergraph Contrastive Learning (IHCL), a robust, generative-driven framework that integrates importance-guided structural perturbations with contrastive learning. IHCL quantifies hyperedge importance by combining four complementary signals: hyperedge degree, intra-edge feature variance, structural centrality, and feature centrality. Guided by these importance scores, IHCL progressively masks noise-sensitive hyperedges and enforces representation consistency between the original and generatively perturbed hypergraphs through contrastive learning. By effectively filtering out noisy connections while preserving discriminative higher-order patterns, IHCL substantially improves robustness of complex interaction systems. Theoretically, we show that the proposed importance-aware masking reduces expected representation shift and yields a tighter InfoNCE-based mutual-information lower bound than random masking under the same budget. Extensive experiments on eight topologically complex stress-test datasets demonstrate that IHCL outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, exhibiting superior stability under structural noise and diverse data distributions.

title: CaFTRA: Frequency-Domain Correlation-Aware Feedback-Free MIMO Transmission and Resource Allocation for 6 G and Beyond
authors: Bo Qian, Hanlin Wu, Jiacheng Chen, Yunting Xu, Xiaoyu Wang, Haibo Zhou, Yusheng Ji
abstract: The fundamental design of wireless systems toward AI-native 6 G and beyond are driven by the need for ever-increasing demand of mobile data traffic, extreme spectral efficiency and adaptability across diverse service scenarios. To overcome the limitations posed by feedback-based multiple-input and multiple-output (MIMO) transmission, we propose a novel frequency-domain Correlation-aware Feedback-free MIMO Transmission and Resource Allocation (CaFTRA) framework tailored for fully-decoupled radio access networks (FD-RAN) to meet the emerging requirements of AI-Native 6 G and beyond. By leveraging artificial intelligence (AI), CaFTRA eliminates the need for real-time channel state information (CSI) feedback during online MIMO operation by predicting CSI from user geolocation. We introduce a learnable-queries-driven Transformer network for geolocation-to-CSI mapping, which utilizes multi-head attention and learnable query embeddings to accurately capture frequency-domain correlations among resource blocks (RBs), thereby significantly improving CSI prediction precision. Once base stations (BSs) adopt feedback-free transmission, their downlink transmission coverage can be significantly expanded due to the elimination of frequent uplink feedback. To fully explore the possibility of such extensive coverage with multi-BS cooperation, we apply a low-complexity many-to-one matching theory-based algorithm, which is proved to converge to a stable matching within limited iterations. Simulation results based on the Vienna 5 G system-level simulator show that CaFTRA achieves stable matching convergence and significant gains in spectral efficiency and user fairness over representative 5 G baselines, while remaining effective across different deployment scenario and carrier band through scenario-specific retraining.

title:
Enhanced DC Flashover Performance of Insulators in C
                    <sub>4</sub>
                    F
                    <sub>7</sub>
                    N/CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Gas Environment by Gradient Ozone Treatment
authors: Tianyu Qi, He Gao, Xiwen Liu, Heng Zhang, Lei Zhang, Meng Chen, Zhen Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: As gas-insulated equipment advances toward higher voltage levels and environmentally benign applications, SF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sub> is increasingly restricted because of its substantial global warming potential (GWP). To address this issue, the environmentally friendly mixed gas, C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>F<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7</sub>N/CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>, emerges as a promising substitute, greatly alleviating the greenhouse effect of SF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">6</sub>. However, the overall insulation performance unavoidably decreases, resulting in a higher susceptibility of insulators to surface flashover. To enhance the DC surface flashover withstand capability of insulators in C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>F<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7</sub>N/CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>, this paper employs ozone treatment as a facile and operationally simple surface modification method to form a surface conductivity gradient, which regulates the electric-field distribution and surface charge density in a coordinated manner, thereby improving DC flashover performance. Based on finite element simulations and experimental measurements, a three-layer non-uniform gradient ozone treatment is identified as the optimal solution. The results indicate that ozone gradient modification is capable of modulating the surface conductivity profile of insulators, while simultaneously improving electric-field uniformity and charge dissipation characteristics. Consequently, the maximum electric field reduces from 6.18 kV/mm to 1.26 kV/mm, the electric-field non-uniformity reduces from 3.29 to 1.22, the surface charge density reduces by 82.30%, and the DC flashover voltage rises by 21%, all in 10% C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>F<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7</sub>N/90% CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>.This study provides a feasible technical pathway with good process compatibility and simple implementation for improving the insulation performance of GIS insulators under C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sub>F<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">7</sub>N/CO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> mixed gas conditions.

title: Simulated lightning strikes on larix gmelinii: Breakdown behavior and interaction mechanisms in lightning-induced forest fire
authors: Shu Li, Duanzhou Peng, Jinru Sun, Yongqiang Guo, Aoyu Wang, Xiaomeng Liu, Xueling Yao, Pengle Cheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Standing-tree, the forest combustibles most susceptible to lightning strikes, play a pivotal role in the ignition and propagation of forest fires. To investigate their electrical response and damage mechanisms, an experimental approach was developed to examine both local and overall dielectric breakdown behaviors, and simulated lightning discharge experiments were conducted. This study focuses on the breakdown and failure characteristics of Larix gmelinii trees from the Daxinganling region of China under direct lightning current injection. The results demonstrate that breakdown strength decreases radially from heartwood to bark, with axial strength being substantially lower than radial strength. As moisture content increases, the breakdown site gradually shifts outward from the core toward the periphery until full saturation is achieved. Lightning-induced damage originates from arc ablation, Joule heating, electric breakdown, and thermal expansion stress, manifesting in two primary damage modes: (1) central fragmentation caused by internal arc expansion, and (2) epidermal peeling driven by peripheral thermal stress. The dominant damage type is determined by the competition between internal carbonization expansion and surface flashover development. The characteristic damage patterns observed on standing-tree provide crucial evidence and theoretical insights for predicting lightning-triggered smoldering and fire initiation in forest ecosystems.

title: ResNeXt-50 for Thermographic PV Defect Classification: A Comparative Convolution Neural Network (CNN) Study
authors: Muhammad Arif Zakaria, Shahrani Shahbudin, Farah Yasmin Abdul Rahman, Marianah Masri, Zuriati Janin
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Photovoltaic (PV) panels are essential in solar energy systems, yet their efficiency can be reduced by defects caused by environmental exposure, manufacturing flaws, shipping, or installation errors. Accurate detection of such defects is critical to ensuring reliable energy output. This study investigates Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architectures for classifying PV module defects using thermographic images, focusing on Residual Neural Networks (ResNet) and Visual Geometry Group (VGG) models. Four ResNet variants; ResNet-18, ResNet-34, ResNet-50, and the advanced ResNeXt-50 were compared with VGG-16 and VGG-19. Performance was evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. Results show that ResNeXt-50 achieved the highest performance, with 81.9% accuracy, 81.4% precision, 81.9% recall, and 81.5% F1-score, outperforming all other architectures. These findings highlight the effectiveness of advanced CNN models in enhancing PV defect classification, supporting improved monitoring and maintenance strategies for solar energy systems.

title: AgriVenture: Comparative Evaluation of Allocation Algorithms for Fair and Efficient Agricultural Distribution
authors: Arshad Alam, Tresa Maria Josylin, Aruna N, Kritayush Singh Basnyat, Harsh Kumar Kurmi, Divya Arnav Dev
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Fair distribution of agricultural produce remains as challenge in agrifood logistics due to the geographical dispersion of farmers, unpredictable market demand, and unequal production capacities. AgriVenture is a computational framework for evaluating different allocation algorithms under realistic agricultural conditions. This study compares six algorithms—Greedy, Max-Flow, Min-Cost Max-Flow, Hungarian, Fair Hungarian, and Fair Min-Cost Max-Flow—using synthetic datasets that model different demand levels, perishability constraints, and farmer heterogeneity. Performance is measured using demand satisfaction, supply utilization, fairness to small farmers, allocation variance, and transportation distance. Experimental findings reveal that cost-optimized algorithms reduce logistical distance, whereas fairness-enhanced variants improve the representation of small farmers with minimal efficiency loss. The approach offers practical insights into selecting an optimal allocation technique for different agricultural contexts and serves as a foundation for future deployment and visualization-driven research.

title: AI-Driven Dyslexia Assistive Reader: A Comprehensive Tool for Text Extraction, Simplification, and Accessibility
authors: K. Saranya, Lekhanthika Mathan R, R. Praveena
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The given paper provides the description of an AIs-assisted Dyslexia Reader that could serve people with dyslexia by facilitating the process of reading and comprehending the text. The system incorporates Optical Character Recognition (OCR) to draw out writings in the images and documents, natural language processing to simplify writings and text to speech (TTS) to aid the audio needs. It has multi-language support, real-time camera OCR, dyslexiafriendly display options, and amazing accessibility controls such as font customization, background themes and highlighting of complex words in a text. Export abilities allow the user to store processed material to be used in academics and business. The architecture of the tool integrates a Python-Flask backend and modern frontend technologies, which will promote the fluent and user-friendly experience. This was assessed using modern web technologies and evaluation carried done by performance analysis of transcription accuracy and text simplification. The results after experiment proved better readability and understanding. It is an assistive reader that helps dyslexic users read more fluently, better, and comprehend more, as well as to be used in education and accessibility in the workplace.

title: Secure EHR System using Edge and Blockchain
authors: T Varshitha, Sanagapalli Rithvika, Sangireddy Sasivardhan Reddy, Dondu Mahesh Reddy, Bala Gangadhara Gutam, I Sapthami
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Internet usage brought digitalization of data, it is widely using in health care industry also for Electronic Health Records (EHRs) management. It is becoming challenge to provide security, transparency, and privacy-preserving for Electronic Health Records (EHRs). To avoid said issues the present study is developing a auditable system with data integrity, confidentiality, and verifiability through a hybrid integration of edge computing and blockchain technology. The system process the data and encrypt at local edge nodes to minimize the exposure during transfer to cloud environment, it reduces the latency time and achieves tamper resistance and accountability. The system validates each transaction which is recorded on a blockchain ledger, where maintains smart contracts with access permissions, it performs trails automatically. A privacy-preserving agreement ensures trust, reputation, expertise metrics verifies the transactions without revealing patient data. Homomorphic encryption and differential privacy mechanism are used for better security and compliance with healthcare regulations such as HIPAA and GDPR. In testing we prove that the system provides accuracy, works with low latency, strong protection against unauthorized access in distributed healthcare networks. The proposed Proof of Trust and Reputation (PoTR) mechanism outperforms PoW and PoS models with an average latency of 0.24 s, throughput of 142 TPS, energy consumption of 110 J, and data integrity of 99.6%, according to experimental evaluation using MATLAB and Hyperledger Fabric.

title: Bias and Explainability in Facial Emotion Recognition Across Gender
authors: Rogerant Tshibangu, Jules R. Tapamo
abstract: Facial Emotion Recognition (FER) systems are increasingly deployed in affective computing, human-computer interaction, and psychological diagnostics. Despite their technical progress, such systems often exhibit performance disparities across demographic groups particularly along gender and racial lines raising concerns about fairness, generalizability, and ethical use.This study proposes a cross-dataset framework to systematically audit gender bias in FER models using both distributional and performance-based metrics. We leverage symmetric Kullback-Leibler Divergence (KLD) to compare softmax output distributions between male and female predictions and quantify emotion-specific prediction discrepancies. Additionally, we compute per-emotion accuracy gaps to measure performance divergence.The analysis is conducted on two demographically annotated evaluation sets: MUTFER2024, a racially homogeneous dataset of Black South African youth, and RAF-DB, a racially diverse public benchmark. A CNN model trained on FER2013 is used to simulate deployment in unfamiliar demographic settings.Results show consistently higher bias scores in MUTFER2024 compared to RAF-DB, suggesting that gender disparities are amplified in racially unfamiliar populations. Emotions such as Angry, Happy, and Surprised demonstrate the highest divergence across groups. These findings provide new empirical evidence that gender bias in FER is not uniform but depends on racial composition a critical insight for fairness-aware AI.Our work introduces a reproducible methodology for intersectional bias auditing in FER, contributes region-specific evaluation data, and supports the development of more inclusive and trustworthy affective systems.

title: Probabilistic Customer Lifetime Value Modeling Using RFM Features and Pareto/NBD—Gamma Estimation
authors: G. Kalpana Devi, K. Shilpa, Md. Kaif, Mutyala Rajeshwari, Kanigiri Swarateja, R. Sai Krishna
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Predicting Customer Lifetime Value is a crucial task for customer-centric decision-making, which allows organizations to better their marketing strategies, enhance their retention efforts, and optimize resource allocation. This paper presents an interpretable, data-driven framework for predicting CLV by using the Recency, Frequency, and Monetary behavioral attributes of RFM derived from large online retail transaction datasets. The proposed system couples probabilistic customer behavioral modeling using the Pareto/NBD model to forecast future purchase frequency and the probability of customer retention, with the Gamma-Gamma model for estimating the expected monetary value of each transaction. Both components are combined to compute the lifetime value of each customer for a specified time period. The proposed framework is evaluated on a real-world online retail dataset consisting of over one million transactions across multiple countries. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach effectively captures heterogeneous purchasing patterns, identifies high-value customers, and provides robust CLV estimates even when transaction and revenue distributions are highly skewed. The results highlight the suitability of the proposed RFM-based probabilistic model for scalable and interpretable CLV forecasting in e-commerce and customer relationship management systems.

title: Unveiling the Hidden Patterns of Oral Cancer Through VGG16: A Deep Learning Framework for Early Detection And Prognosis
authors: C. Rachana, O. Roopa Devi
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Oral cancer is one of the malignant neoplasm's that develop most frequently worldwide. Oral cancer progression is correlated with several associated risks. Oral cancer is a board condition which impacts the neck & head region's oral cavity. To develop a more effective treatment strategy for oral cancer at the earliest stages, experts need to first examine oral malignant lesions. Heavy drinking and smoking are the key risk factors for the development of oral squamous cell carcinoma (OSCC). Oral cancer seems to be more common in men than in women. Relapses of the disease can occasionally happen even after oral cancer has been effectively treated, so quick identification and classification of oral cancer risk levels are important. A computer-aided diagnostic system developed using deep learning has been successful in many applications and can quickly and accurately diagnose oral cancer lesions. Transfer learning can effectively manage the difficulty of obtaining large training datasets in medical image classification by retrieving the characteristics from a collection of natural images and adapting them to new image datasets. In the present study, a transfer learning method is used to classify histopathology pictures of oral squamous cell cancer (OSCC).to create an efficient deep learning-based computer-aided system. The pertained model vgg16 is used to accurately classify the classes of OSCC and Normal. The proposed approach achieved an accuracy of 95% both in training and validation and achieved accuracy of 99% to test normal images and 95% to test on OSCC images.

title: CycleSync+: A Personalized Digital Wellness System for Cycle-Phase Detection, Nutrition Guidance, and Mood-Driven Recommendations
authors: K. S. G. Yeseswini, Shilpita V, Leekhith Nunna, Sreejith S, M. Gurupriya
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: CycleSync+ is a digital wellness system designed to provide phase-aware nutrition and lifestyle guidance across the menstrual cycle. The system determines menstrual phases using rule-based segmentation derived from user-reported cycle information and historical logs. Daily inputs related to mood, symptoms, sleep, and nutrition are analyzed to generate contextual wellness recommendations aligned with physiological changes across cycle phases. Unlike conventional period-tracking applications that focus primarily on date prediction and symptom recording, CycleSync+ emphasizes explainable, phase-specific guidance grounded in established clinical and nutritional research. The system is implemented using a three-tier architecture comprising a user interface layer, a backend application layer, and a persistent data layer. Core backend modules include cycle phase detection, mood analytics, and a rule-based recommendation engine that integrates phase and mood context to produce personalized outputs. Functional evaluation indicates that the system consistently generates phase-aligned recommendations with low processing latency. Overall, CycleSync+ demonstrates how rule-based inference and structured data pipelines can be applied to deliver transparent and interpretable digital menstrual wellness support. Future work will explore wearable data integration, offline access, and adaptive personalization using datadriven models.

title:
Enabling Affordable Chip Design with Open Chiplet Atlas
                    <sup>TM</sup>
                    Ecosystem
authors: Zhimin Chen, Wei-Han Lien
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: The rising non-recurring engineering cost of advanced semiconductor design has driven interest in chiplet-based architectures to improve reuse and reduce development cost. However, adoption remains limited by the lack of a mature and open chiplet ecosystem, including gaps in system level interoperability, reference designs, compliance validation, and reusable merchant chiplets. This paper examines these challenges and presents a practical, product driven approach to ecosystem development. Building on this approach, the Open Chiplet Atlas ecosystem is introduced as an open, vendor neutral framework that standardizes chiplet behavior beyond die-to-die interfaces. The Open Chiplet Atlas Architecture and its open-source reference framework provide reusable non-application functionality such as system management, security, boot, and debug, enabling interoperable and scalable multi-vendor chiplet-based systems.

title: Achieving a Dielectric Constant of 60 in $\text{Zr}_{x} \text{Hf}_{1-x} \mathrm{O}_{2}$: A Candidate for DRAM?
authors: Pramoda Vishnumurthy, Richard Ganser, Alfred Kersch, Thomas Mikolajick, Uwe Schroeder, Dong-Gyu Kim, Chang Hwa Jung, Hanjin Lim
abstract: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{Zr}_{\mathrm{x}} \text{Hf}_{1-\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{O}_2$</tex> with an optimized Hf-to-Zr ratio has demonstrated a dielectric constant exceeding 55 within the DRAM operating voltage range. This enhancement can reduce the power consumption of DRAM and is expected to meet the growing demands driven by the rapid expansion of AI services. This work investigates the Hf-to-Zr composition in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6 \text{nm} \text{Zr}_{\mathrm{x}} \text{Hf}_{1-\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{O}_{2}$</tex> metal-insulator-metal capacitors to achieve a high dielectric constant. The physical mechanisms responsible for dielectric enhancement are analyzed, and comprehensive reliability characterizations are conducted, including voltage polarity-dependent dielectric constant loss, retention behavior, and temperature stability. These results define the usable operating conditions and practical limitations of dielectric constant enhancement observed in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{Z r}_{\mathbf{x}} \mathbf{H f}_{\mathbf{1}}, { }_{x} \mathrm{O}_{2}$</tex> for DRAM applications.

title: An Intelligent IoT-Enabled Precision Farming Robotic System for AI-Based Crop Health Monitoring and Smart Agricultural Management
authors: Varsha P. Hotur, Farah Kowsar, Sindhu K., M B Girish, Pavithra G., Swapnil S. Ninawe
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: In this agricultural sector has been a significant contributor to the Indian economy for many years. It accounts for approximately 18% of the country’s gross domestic product (GDP). Precision agriculture has witnessed advancements through the integration of robotics and the Internet of Things (IoT). In the case of selective harvesting of crops like tomatoes and apples, conventional farming methods require extensive labour to handpick ripened fruits individually. To address this, we propose the use of a robot that can assist farmers in labor-intensive tasks such as selective crop harvesting, qualitative segregation, and providing real-time information on crop health, soil nutrition, and crop shelf-life detection. The data collected is analyzed, processed, and transmitted to the farmer through an Android application. The robot is equipped with the capability to differentiate between ripe and unripe fruits/vegetables and selectively harvest only the ripe ones. Furthermore, the quality of the harvested crop is assessed based on weight, color, and overall health to ensure proper grading before transferring them to designated containers. Any spoiled or overripe fruits/vegetables are removed to prevent any negative impact on plant growth. The proposed robot features a harvesting arm that can reach this fruits/vegetable & plucking them from trees or from the plants, subsequently transferring it to the appropriate container. Users can assign specific tasks to the device according to their requirements using the accompanying mobile application.

title: Deep Learning Based Real-Time Sign Language & Gesture Translator
authors: Baba Fakruddin Ali B H, Raghavendra Patil G E, Swastik Anand, Tushar Pandey, Utkarsh Krishna Tripathi, Saksham Srivastava
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Communication between non-signers and sign language users is still exceedingly difficult, especially in everyday settings where assistive technology or interpreters are not easily accessible. Because they rely on strict feature extraction approaches or find it difficult to capture the fluid, dynamic nature of sign language, current gesture-recognition techniques frequently fail in real-world settings. In this work, we provide a real-time gesture and sign language translation system that uses cutting-edge computer vision and machine learning approaches to close this communication gap. under order to replicate real-world use, our dataset contains a variety of hand forms, motion sequences, and contextual clues that were gathered under a range of lighting and backdrop situations. To find the best dependable method for both static and dynamic gesture detection, we assessed a number of deep learning architectures, such as CNN, LSTM, and transformer-based models.

title: A Novel Sparse-IMU Convolutional Neural Network for Real-Time Hip Kinematics in Exoskeleton-Augmented Gait
authors: Lauren Low
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Accurate estimation of sagittal-plane hip flexion angles is critical for controlling wearable robotic devices and monitoring gait rehabilitation. While full-body arrays of inertial measurement units (IMUs) provide high accuracy, they present barriers to clinical and field use due to setup complexity, user burden, and cost. This study evaluated whether a reduced-sensor configuration, using only bilateral thigh-mounted IMUs (ThighOnly), could achieve comparable performance to a full-body IMU array (Full-body) when paired with a convolutional neural network (CNN) for hip kinematics estimation. Three participants (S01-S03) performed treadmill walking on a NordicTrack T 6.5 Si under two conditions: unassisted walking (No Exo) and walking with a Hypershell Go X hip exoskeleton (With Exo). Ground-truth hip angles were measured using the Noraxon MyoMotion system. Separate CNN models were trained for each configuration and condition, and accuracy was evaluated using root mean square error RMSE (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${}^{\circ}$</tex>) and Pearson's correlation coefficient (r). Across participants, Thigh-Only achieved a mean RMSE of 3.07° in No Exo, only 0.24° higher than the Full-Body configuration (2.83°). Correlations remained high (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r \geq 0.94$</tex>). In the Exo condition, Thigh-Only averaged 3.97° versus 3.47° for Full-body. RMSE increased for both configurations in Exo-assisted walking, suggesting gait variability from powered assistance, with subtle left-right asymmetries. These findings show that a minimal IMU setup can match or closely approach full-body accuracy, offering a practical, low-burden alternative for real-time joint angle prediction. Reduced-sensor CNN models hold promise for scalable, user-friendly applications in assistive robotics, rehabilitation, and gait monitoring.

title: SleepSense AI: A Hybrid Machine Learning Framework for Stress Level Prediction Using Physiological Sleep Parameters
authors: Varuni C V, Praveena Kumari M K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The quality of stress, which is increasingly recognised as a critical psychological and physiological issue in contemporary lifestyles, is greatly influenced by sleep and the underlying biological cycles. Traditional stress assessment methods are based on subjective, non-scalable self-reporting and clinical evaluations. This study introduces SleepSense AI, a hybrid machine learning system that predicts stress levels the following day based on physiological traits learnt from sleep. The SaYoPillow dataset is used to develop and evaluate a number of machine learning and deep learning models. A hybrid CNN-LSTM based model achieves better generalisation across stress levels (0–4). The system, which offers real-time stress prediction and personalised recommendations, is implemented using a Django-based web application.

title: Assessment of Ground Flash Density in Southern Malaysia and Implications for Renewable Energy
authors: Muhamad Haziq Azraei Abdul Aziz, Dalina Johari, Faranadia Abdul Haris, Siti Zaliha Mohammad Noor, Zuhaila Mat Yasin, Mohd Muzafar Ismail
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Lightning is known to pose significant threat to electrical infrastructure, particularly in tropical regions where thunderstorm occurrence is frequent. This paper presents an investigation of lightning behavior in Southern Malaysia based on data obtained from the Lightning Location System (LLS). The main goal is to estimate the ground flash density in the study area and assess its implication for renewable energy installations. For the study, lightning data for a period of three years from 2023 to 2025 were obtained from Tenaga Nasional Berhad Research (TNBR). Cloud-to-ground (CG) lightning occurring within a 10 km radius of reference location were analyzed and their distribution as well as the peak current characteristics were determined. Subsequently, the ground flash density (GFD) of the study area was estimated. The results indicate that lightning activity in the study area is dominated by negative CG events, representing about 95% of the total recorded occurrences. The maximum peak current recorded was 215 kA for negative CG and 140 kA for positive CG flashes. Although less frequent, positive CG flashes exhibited higher geometric and arithmetic mean peak currents compared with negative CG. Based on the estimated flash counts, the ground flash density ranges between approximately 6 and 12 flashes/km<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>/year, which indicate moderate to high lightning exposure typical of tropical regions. In conclusion, since the area can be considered as lightning prone, renewable energy installations built in the study area require appropriate lightning protection and surge protection measures to alleviate the risk.

title: Multi-Objective Optimization Design of Non-Uniform Stator Slots for Induction Motors Based on NSGA-II
authors: Xianguo Liu, Meijie Han
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This paper investigates the modulation mechanism and multi-objective optimization of non-uniform stator slots to suppress torque ripple in induction motors. Analytical models reveal that effective air-gap harmonic amplitudes depend fundamentally on the slot-opening-to-tooth-pitch ratio. Consequently,a topology featuring symmetrically distributed asymmetric slots within the phase belt is proposed to suppress high-order harmonics and achieve a more sinusoidal magnetomotive force waveform. Based on these insights, a multi-objective optimization targeting efficiency, torque ripple, and slot fill factor is performed on a 7.5 kW motor utilizing a 3D Pareto front. Finite element analysis confirms that the optimized design reduces torque ripple from 3.88 % to 3.21 % while maintaining an average torque of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$24.62 ~\mathrm{N} \cdot ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>. This micro-tuning of slot dimensions successfully decouples pulsating torque components from fundamental electromechanical energy conversion, minimizing vibration and noise excitation sources without compromising baseline performance.

title: Fuzzy Logic Controller Assisted Variable Step Size P&O MPPT Algorithm in Partially Shaded PV System
authors: Siti Nur Hajar Mohd Tarmizi, Muhammad Iqbal Zakaria, Najwa Nasuha Mahzan, Mohd Abdul Talib Mat Yusoh, Muhammad Nizam Kamarudin, Nabil El Fezazi
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Renewable energy sources such as solar photovoltaic (PV) have emerged as sustainable alternatives to fossil fuels. By controlling the module voltage and current, Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) is crucial for optimizing PV efficiency; however, environmental factors such as partial shading conditions (PSC) significantly reduce system performance. Conventional MPPT methods, including Perturb and Observe (P&O) and Incremental Conductance (INC), often fail to track the global maximum power point (GMPP) under highly non-linear characteristics. This paper proposes an improved MPPT algorithm that integrates a variable step-size P&O fuzzy logic controller (FLC) to enhance energy extraction under PSC. MATLAB/Simulink modelling with a DC–DC boost converter was used to evaluate the proposed method against conventional INC. The results demonstrate that the hybrid algorithm effectively maintains operation at the global MPP, particularly under partial shading.

title: Mobility Patterns in Underwater Sensor Networks
authors: Lyzandra D'Souza, Sivakumar V., Adesh N. D., Rudresh Shirwaikar
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Underwater Wireless Sensor Networks (UWSNs) operates in complex aquatic environments where energy constraints, unpredictable mobility, and acoustic communication delays pose significant challenges to efficient sensing and communication. This study addresses the problem of selecting appropriate node mobility models to enhance network performance—specifically energy consumption, coverage, and network lifetime. Three mobility strategies were investigated: Brownian random motion, Depth-Adjustable mobility, and Ocean Current-Based Drift. Each model was implemented in a simulated underwater environment using the Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG) Reinforcement Learning(RL) algorithm. Simulations results indicate that the Depth-Adjustable Mobility Model consistently delivers the highest network lifetime. Its controlled vertical adjustments enable energy-efficient operation without significant loss in coverage. The Ocean Current-Based Drift Model offered a balanced performance, benefiting from adaptive current-following strategies. However, it lagged behind the depth-adjusted model in overall longevity. The Brownian Motion Model, proved to be the least efficient—exhibiting moderate energy consumption and reduced lifetime due to its inherently chaotic motion and lack of structured routing logic. The study finds that controlled mobility, especially depth-adjustable movement, is optimal for long-term and energy-sensitive underwater deployments. But models that simulate random or purely environmental drift are less effective for structured monitoring tasks.

title: Toward a Formal Definition of Small Language Models: A Multi-Criteria Framework, Taxonomy and Practical Implications
authors: Yale Yu, Xinjian Qiang, Jianpeng Che, Weijuan Wen, Qiujun Lan, Austin Smith, Yiwen Qiang
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Small Language Models (SLMs) are increasingly adopted for edge and single-node deployments across enterprise and education contexts, driven by cost, latency, and the need for reliable, traceable, and governable AI outputs. However, the field lacks a formal, falsifiable definition distinguishing SLMs from medium/large models (MLMs/LLMs). We propose an operational definition grounded in five axes: parameter scale, data adequacy, deployability, efficiency envelope, and capability sufficiency. Building on scaling laws and motivated by concerns over hallucination and operational trust, we formalize conditions under which a model qualifies as an SLM and present a taxonomy spanning Micro-Small-Medium-Large bands. We introduce a practical classifier, enumerate algorithmic traits (quantization, distillation, efficient attention), and analyze implications for constructing reliable, retrieval-augmented systems. This framework aims to standardize discourse, enable comparable research, and guide the principled engineering of verifiable, efficient, and domain-grounded AI systems, supporting the transition toward “AI for Engineering”.

title: Adaptive Fuzzy Control for Structual Uncertain Non-Strict Feedback Systems
authors: Zhuoliang Yang, Chunli Zhang, Shihao Dong
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This paper investigates the adaptive tracking control problem for a class of third-order non-strict-feedback nonlinear systems with unknown smooth nonlinear terms. To preserve the original non-strict-feedback structure of the system, the unknown functions are assumed to remain dependent on the full state variables. During the controller design, following the recursive framework of the backstepping method, fuzzy logic system approximators are constructed for the unknown nonlinear terms arising at each design stage. On this basis, a fuzzy scaling strategy with a denominator structure is introduced to provide a unified upper-bound estimation for the unknown nonlinear terms, such that the denominator terms in the first two design steps depend only on the current subsystem states and do not involve the control input, thereby avoiding the algebraic loop problem in the first two steps of the design. In addition, under the adaptive backstepping control framework, the control laws and adaptive update laws are established. Lyapunov stability analysis shows that all signals in the system remain bounded and that the tracking error converges to a sufficiently small neighborhood of zero. Numerical simulation results confirm that the proposed scheme can effectively track the reference signal and yield satisfactory control performance.

title: License Plate Recognition in Traffic System Using CNN Alogithm
authors: S. Sharmila Devi, Abisheik Jerome. S, Azath. N, Abinayashree. B
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The necessity for automated traffic monitoring and intelligent surveillance systems has grown due to the quick increase in the number of cars. In real-time situations, manual license plate inspection is inefficient, time-consuming, and prone to mistakes. Using a Raspberry Pi and a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture, this research suggests a low-cost, real-time License Plate Recognition (LPR) system. The Raspberry Pi camera module takes pictures of cars, and the license plate area is identified and isolated using preprocessing methods based on OpenCV. To identify alphanumeric characters from segmented license plate photos, a CNN model that has been specially trained is used. Without the need for additional processing resources, the system functions as a stand-alone embedded solution. High character recognition accuracy and dependable performance under various lighting and environmental situations are shown by experimental assessments. Applications like automatic parking, security monitoring, toll collecting, and smart traffic management are all excellent fits for the proposed system.

title: A Novel Approach to Part-of-Speech Tagging for Kannada Proverbs by using an Optimized Multi-Layer Perceptron
authors: Jamuna Nikhil, Kiran Kumar B S, Kavya T G
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Part-of-Speech (POS) tagging is a foundational task in Natural Language Processing (NLP), supporting higher-level applications such as Named Entity Recognition (NER), Machine Translation (MT) and Information Retrieval (IR) etc. Kannada proverbs are traditional sayings that convey cultural wisdom and moral lessons. Tagging these kind of words are highly challenging as they are idiomatic, figurative and carry richest collections of linguistic traits. For a language like Kannada, which is basically low resourced and agglutinative in nature, the traditional approaches such as rule-based models and statistical systems have limited ability in capturing contextual meaning and fail in noisy or morphologically rich language settings. Although deep learning architectures Bidirectional Long Short Term Memory (BiLSTM), Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), transformers achieve state-of-the-art performance, they demand heavy computation and memory, which restricts their use in low-resource environments. This paper proposes an optimized Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) based PoS tagging framework for Kannada proverbs, which is lightweight, faster, and efficient while maintaining competitive accuracy and it is the first attempt in Kannada language history to try out PoS tagging for Kannada proverbs. The proposed model is empirically tested with manually attributed proverb datasets and able to achieve an accuracy of 98%.

title: Comparative Study of Large Language Models for Neural Architecture Search in Federated Learning with PSO
authors: Hloniphani Maluleke, Clement N. Nyirenda
abstract: In this study, we explore how Large Language Models, such as GPT-4, Qwen-3, DeepSeek, Gemini 2.5, Gemma-3, and LLaMA-3 can help design better neural network architectures using a two-level Particle Swarm Optimisation framework. We replicate the original FedGPT-4 PSO NAS study and extend it by comparing different LLMs as architecture generators. Our simulation results show that LLM-based NAS models consistently outperform traditional federated learning methods such as FedAvg and FedPSO, particularly in terms of accuracy and robustness. Our findings are particularly relevant for real-world deployments where internet connections are slow or unreliable, like in rural areas of South Africa. Our results suggest that using LLMs to guide model design can improve performance in federated learning while still protecting data privacy.

title: Enhanced Mobile Broadband Validation over a Slice-Aware 5G Testbed for EdTech Applications
authors: Lusani Mamushiane, Thoriso Makhosa, Steve Motsotso, Hlabi Kobo
abstract: This work demonstrates an education-focused 5G architecture that delivers comparable streaming quality to geographically separated learners over the same network slice. A centralized core with distributed user-plane functions (UPFs) near Limpopo and Cape Town was evaluated using controlled TCP/UDP traffic. Results show stable, site-independent streaming performance at each campus and fairness when both sites transmit simultaneously on the shared slice. TCP experiments with increasing concurrency exhibited predictable capacity sharing without degrading cross-site experience, while UDP baselines reflected low jitter and minimal loss consistent with smooth video playback. These findings indicate that a single logical slice, anchored by regional UPFs, can provide consistent Quality of Experience across provinces, supporting equitable access to digital learning. Practical tooling constraints limited large-scale parallel UDP evaluation, suggesting future validation with multi-device trials and alternative traffic generators.

title: Transfer Learning Challenges in Multilingual Racism Detection: A Case Study with XLM-RoBERTa
authors: Ikram El Miqdadi, Soufiane Hourri, Jamal Kharroubi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: We investigate multilingual racism detection in Arabic, French, and English social media using XLM-RoBERTa with frozen layers. Two early stopping criteria, validation loss and validation F1-score, are compared across datasets with varying class balance. For English and French, F1-based stopping accelerates training while maintaining performance comparable to lossbased stopping. For the imbalanced Arabic dataset, however, F1- based stopping fails to capture minority class patterns, whereas loss-based stopping yields modest yet informative results. These findings highlight the critical role of dataset balance in selecting early stopping strategies for frozen multilingual models.

title: PLC Programming Design and Configuration Simulation Implementation of Multi-Elevator Group Control System
authors: Junnan Tian, Jing Liu, Ming Cai, Yuncai Yan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To address the control requirements for cooperative operation of multiple elevators in high-rise buildings, this paper designs and implements a multi-elevator group control system based on the Siemens S7-1200 PLC and the TIA Portal development environment. Through modular programming, the system achieves centralized processing of hall call signals, management of car floor selections, and a shortest-distance scheduling algorithm, effectively improving dispatching efficiency and passenger response speed. A simulation test platform for elevator operation is constructed using configuration software, intuitively displaying elevator operating status, dispatching allocation, and safety protection functions, providing reliable support for the verification and optimization of the control program. The system not only demonstrates good stability and real-time performance but also effectively reduces passenger waiting time and improves overall operational efficiency by optimizing the call allocation strategy. Simulation test results under normal and typical peak call conditions show that the designed system meets the expected control indicators and possesses strong applicability and expansion potential. Furthermore, fault response tests for key sensors such as light curtains verify the basic robustness of the system. This work offers practical technical support and a solution reference for intelligent elevator control in high-rise buildings.

title: Revolutionizing Vaccine Transport: An IoT-Enabled Smart Cold Storage Box for Real-Time Temperature Monitoring
authors: Saranya S, Kaviya P, Rohita M, Sanju Shree Varshini K K, Shree Harini J J
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Throughout their lives, vaccines–interventions that can save lives–need to be kept in carefully monitored, low-temperature environments. Traditional cold storage systems can suffer from limited data visibility, unreliable power supply, and no automated monitoring in remote and resource-constrained locations. This study proposes an Internet of Things (IoT) and renewable energy integration-based smart cold storage system to provide continuous vaccine preservation between 2°C and 8°C, as recommended by the WHO. It makes use of GSM/Wi-Fi modules, sensors, a cloud dashboard, and a NodeMCU microcontroller. Real hardware results validate the design, as the hardware prototype successfully maintains constant temperature control, sends out instant digital alerts, and enables cloud-based monitoring of vaccine storage conditions. The remedy resolves long-standing issues with vaccine transportation and storage while ensuring sustainability, scalability, and real time monitoring.

title: Multi-Language Support Systems in Salesforce Using Agentforce and LLMs
authors: Rohini Isarapu Google, Sharathchandra Gowda
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Multilingual customer support has become a new trend in the current world due to globalization, especially in companies that are using Salesforce’s Agentforce. Many industries’ everyday customer service platforms do not support multiple languages in their queries, and as such, they slow down to provide potentially incomprehensible translations. The solution presented in this paper consists of incorporating Large Language Models (LLMs) into Salesforce’s Agentforce, allowing on-the-spot and highly scalable multilingual CS services for clients worldwide. This includes advanced natural language processing (NLP) skills. LLMs can provide translations, creation, and accurate replies in multiple languages that can help improve client satisfaction in terms of customer service. This paper evaluates the architecture of the system, possible integration methods, and effectiveness indicators, such as accuracy, possibility to expand, and response time, and proves that this approach can considerably enhance the support effectiveness. The solution also points to scenarios in which enterprises may require other languages in addition to those addressed in the solution. It also encapsulates how Salesforce’s Agentforce, launched at Dreamforce 2024, is changing how real-time support is delivered and actual ROI from the customer’s point of view.

title: Crop Recommendation System using Machine Learning
authors: Nanda H Hanchinal, Geeta R. Bharamagoudar, Shobha R. Bharamagoudar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Efficient crop selection is critical for improving agricultural productivity and sustainability. This paper presents an intelligent crop recommendation system that integrates Internet of Things (IoT) sensing with machine learning to support data-driven farming decisions. The proposed system analyzes soil and environmental parameters, including nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium (NPK), pH, moisture, temperature, humidity, and rainfall. Real-time data collected through IoT sensors is combined with historical climate data to enhance prediction performance. Multiple machine learning models—Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, Multi-Layer Perceptron, and XGBoost—are evaluated within an ensemble framework to recommend suitable crops. Experimental results demonstrate an accuracy of approximately 90%, indicating the effectiveness of the proposed approach in improving crop selection and promoting sustainable agriculture.

title: Benchmarking Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Turkish Historical Texts: The “Multilingual Paradox” in Embedding Models
authors: Mehmet Aksoy, Halil Yigit, Zeynep Hilal Kilimci
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in general-purpose tasks; however, they often suffer from hallucinations and a lack of factual consistency, particularly when processing politically and historically charged texts. While Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) offers a promising solution by grounding generation in external knowledge, standard architectures often underperform in morphologically rich languages like Turkish, a challenge also observed in Arabic contexts. This study presents the first comprehensive RAG benchmark for Turkish historical texts, utilizing “Nutuk,” a seminal political discourse by Mustafa Kemal ATATÜRK. To overcome data scarcity in this low-resource domain, a synthetic Question-Answering (QA) dataset of 600 pairs was constructed using Claude-3.5, a methodology validated for enhancing model performance in low-resource languages. 90 configurations involving 5 embedding models (BGE-M3, XLM-RoBERTa, LaBSE, MPNet, MiniLM), 6 chunking strategies, and 3 chunk sizes (128, 256, 512 tokens) were systematically evaluated across 54,000 individual tests. Experimental results reveal a significant “Multilingual Paradox”: despite being optimized for multilingual tasks, XLM-RoBERTa exhibited the lowest performance (MRR: 0.077), whereas BGE-M3 achieved state-of-the-art results (MRR: 0.379). This disparity is attributed to the contrastive learning objectives employed by BGE-M3, which provide superior semantic separation in vector space. Furthermore, statistical tests (Friedman $\chi^{2}=69.11, p\lt0.001$) confirmed that model selection is the dominant factor in retrieval performance, while the proposed “Keyword-aware” chunking strategy proved optimal for semantic integrity. This work provides a reproducible baseline and theoretical insights for historical RAG applications in nonEnglish languages. Scope. Although RAG includes both retrieval and generation, this benchmark is designed to isolate retrieval effects under a controlled generator and prompt. We report downstream generation metrics (e.g., ROUGE and BERTScore), while deeper generation error-mode analysis is deferred to future work. Data. The QA set is synthetically generated and humanverified for grounding references. Nonetheless, the dataset size (600 pairs) and stylistic regularities of synthetic questions may introduce evaluation bias; results should be interpreted as a baseline rather than a definitive ranking for all Turkish historical QA distributions.

title: Characterizing Cache-Asymmetric CPU Topologies on AMD 3D V-Cache Processors
authors: Sunwoo Kim, Eunbi Jeong, Myung Kuk Yoon
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: In chiplet-based processors, integrating 3D V-Cache into a subset of core complex dies (CCDs) naturally creates cache asymmetry across them. Such asymmetry can lead to nonintuitive performance behavior across different CPU topology configurations, depending on the workload. This work analyzes the performance implications of cache-asymmetric processors by evaluating the AMD 3D V-Cache processor under various topologies. Our evaluation results first show that simply using more cores or threads can sometimes degrade performance due to contention in shared resources. In addition, leveraging a CCD equipped with large V-Cache can underperform compared to a standard CCD with a smaller cache capacity because of its lower clock frequency. Therefore, neither maximizing available cores or threads nor relying on the V-Cache CCD is consistently optimal, emphasizing the need for effective topology decisions through comprehensive workload characterization.

title: Real Time Hardware-Aided Prediction of Electric Vehicle Battery States using Operational Data from Onboard Systems
authors: Raghav Vyas, Maheswaran A S, Jayarama Pradeep
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The increasing popularity of electric vehicles requires the implementation of “intelligent” systems that are capable of facilitating battery energy management and motor performance in efficient ways. Current battery state prediction accuracy, including state of charge, state of health, and remaining useful life, is a complex process, particularly under varying driving conditions. To address the limitations encountered using traditional battery state models, the present work focuses on the implementation of a novel approach to battery state prediction based on machine learning, employing real-time hardware implementation for brushless direct current motor intelligence. In contrast to the global trend, where machine learning-based battery state prediction employs the process of machine learning using offline data, the proposed approach utilizes the capabilities of machine learning using live data, thereby facilitating the performance of battery state prediction using live machine learning-based approaches, resulting in the efficient adaptation of battery state during varying conditions.

title: Greedy Algorithm for Building Sparse Linear Regression Models
authors: Nuru Nabuuso, Vladimir Estivill-Castro
abstract: The growing reliance on Artificial Intelligence (AI) in high-stakes domains has heightened concerns over the interpretability of machine learning models. While AI systems using large models often achieve high predictive accuracy, their "black-box" nature undermines accountability, trust, ethical and legal compliance. Explainable AI (XAI) addresses this challenge by prioritising model transparency, often through feature selection to enhance interpretability. Traditional methods like LASSO, stepwise selection methods, and PCA face limitations in high-dimensional settings, struggling to balance interpretability, and computational efficiency.This paper introduces a greedy hybrid feature selection approach for sparse linear modelling, explicitly constraining models to a maximum of three features to enforce interpretability. We evaluate this method against classical techniques, assessing trade-offs between accuracy, computational efficiency, and interpretability. Our results demonstrate that the constrained hybrid approach maintains competitive predictive performance while significantly improving model transparency. By explicitly prioritising sparsity, our method offers a practical solution for domains where understanding model decisions is as critical as accuracy itself.

title: Automated Detection of Chest Diseases from Chest X- Ray Using Efficientnet
authors: Swathi Yalavarthy, Alla Jyothi, Bethapudi Surekha, Yammila Sumanjali, Mekapothula Varsha
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Chest diseases, including pneumonia, cardiomegaly, pleural effusion, and oedema, continue to be significant causes of morbidity and mortality on a global scale. Chest X-ray (CXR) imaging is extensively utilised for diagnostic purposes owing to its affordability and widespread availability; nevertheless, manual analysis is labour-intensive and subject to inter-observer inconsistency. To overcome these challenges, this paper introduces an automated deep learning framework for multi-label detection of chest diseases utilising EfficientNet. The proposed system is trained and assessed on the MIMICCXR dataset, which comprises a substantial collection of annotated chest X-ray images depicting various thoracic conditions. Image preprocessing and data augmentation methods are employed to enhance robustness, and the EfficientNet architecture is fine-tuned for multi-label classification utilising binary cross-entropy loss with logits and the OneCycle learning rate schedule. Accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and confusion matrix analysis are used to assess the model's performance. ResNet-18, DenseNet- 121, and Swin Transformer models are compared. Overall, the findings show that EfficientNet is a very useful and comprehensible model for automatically identifying chest conditions from Xray pictures. By facilitating quick, precise, and reliable screening, the suggested framework may help radiologists make better clinical decisions in actual healthcare environments.

title: An Improved Path Planning Algorithm Based on Progressive Target Bias and Smoothing Strategy for Mobile Robots
authors: Chao Ding, Silei Han, Min Qu, Jiasen Tian, Xiao Tan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Path planning serves as a core technology for the autonomous navigation of mobile robots and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), playing a pivotal role in enhancing navigation efficiency and safety in complex environments. Aiming at the shortcomings of the traditional Progressive Target Bias and Smoothing Improved RRT (PSBI-RRT) algorithm, such as fixed target bias probability, poor path smoothness, and low search efficiency in dense obstacle environments, this paper proposes an improved PSBI-RRT algorithm integrated with an adaptive target-biased sampling strategy and a hybrid path smoothing strategy. Firstly, an adaptive target-biased sampling strategy is designed to dynamically adjust the bias probability according to the minimum distance between two bidirectional random trees, and a collision prediction mechanism is added to reduce invalid sampling operations. Secondly, a hybrid path smoothing strategy combining greedy pruning and third-order B-spline fitting is adopted to optimize the path length and smoothness simultaneously. Comparative experiments between the proposed algorithm and the traditional PSBI-RRT algorithm are conducted using MATLAB R2024a. The experimental results demonstrate that the improved algorithm maintains a success rate of over <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$9 5 \%$</tex> in all test environments. In comparison with the traditional algorithm, the average path length is shortened by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.1 \%-6.3 \%$</tex>, the path smoothness is improved by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$42.9 \%-46.4 \%$</tex>, and the average planning time is significantly reduced. The proposed algorithm exhibits strong adaptability to complex environments and possesses high practical value for the autonomous navigation of mobile robots and UAVs.

title: Theodosian: A Deep Dive into Memory-Hierarchy-Centric FHE Acceleration
authors: Wonseok Choi, Hyunah Yu, Jongmin Kim, Hyesung Ji, Jaiyoung Park, Jung Ho Ahn
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: Fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) enables secure computation on encrypted data, mitigating privacy concerns in cloud and edge environments. However, due to its high compute and memory demands, extensive acceleration research has been pursued across diverse hardware platforms, especially GPUs. In this paper, we perform a microarchitectural analysis of CKKS, a popular FHE scheme, on modern GPUs. Focusing on the memory hierarchy, we demonstrate that dominant kernels remain bound by the on-chip L2 cache despite its high bandwidth, exposing a persistent inner memory wall beyond the conventional off-chip DRAM bottleneck. Further, we reveal that the overall CKKS throughput is constrained by low per-kernel hardware utilization, caused by insufficient intra-kernel parallelism. Motivated by these findings, we introduce Theodosian, a set of complementary, memory-aware optimizations that improve cache efficiency and reduce runtime overheads. Theodosian achieves $\mathbf{1. 4 5}-\mathbf{1. 8 3} \times$ performance improvements over a highly optimized baseline, Cheddar, across representative CKKS workloads. On an RTX 5090, we reduce the bootstrapping latency for 32,768 complex numbers from 22.1 ms to 15.2 ms, and further to 12.8 ms with additional algorithmic optimizations, establishing a new state-of-the-art GPU performance to the best of our knowledge.
